text: I'm selfish, impatient and a little insecure. I make mistakes, I am out of control and at times hard to handle. But if you can't handle me at my worst, then you sure as hell don't deserve me at my best.
author: Marilyn Monroe

text: You've gotta dance like there's nobody watching,Love like you'll never be hurt,Sing like there's nobody listening,And live like it's heaven on earth.
author: William W. Purkey

text: You know you're in love when you can't fall asleep because reality is finally better than your dreams.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: A friend is someone who knows all about you and still loves you.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: Darkness cannot drive out darkness: only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate: only love can do that.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: We accept the love we think we deserve.
author: Stephen Chbosky

text: It is better to be hated for what you are than to be loved for what you are not.
author: Andre Gide

text: As he read, I fell in love the way you fall asleep: slowly, and then all at once.
author: John Green

text: The opposite of love is not hate, it's indifference. The opposite of art is not ugliness, it's indifference. The opposite of faith is not heresy, it's indifference. And the opposite of life is not death, it's indifference.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: It is not a lack of love, but a lack of friendship that makes unhappy marriages.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Love all, trust a few, do wrong to none.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Have you ever been in love? Horrible isn't it? It makes you so vulnerable. It opens your chest and it opens up your heart and it means that someone can get inside you and mess you up.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, while loving someone deeply gives you courage.
author: Lao Tzu

text: There is never a time or place for true love. It happens accidentally, in a heartbeat, in a single flashing, throbbing moment.
author: Sarah Dessen

text: Love is that condition in which the happiness of another person is essential to your own.
author: Robert A. Heinlein

text: You love me. Real or not real?"I tell him, "Real.
author: Suzanne Collins

text: Love looks not with the eyes, but with the mind,And therefore is winged Cupid painted blind.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Love is like the wind, you can't see it but you can feel it.
author: Nicholas Sparks

text: If you can make a woman laugh, you can make her do anything.
author: Marilyn Monroe

text: You don't love someone because they're perfect, you love them in spite of the fact that they're not.
author: Jodi Picoult

text: We’re all a little weird. And life is a little weird. And when we find someone whose weirdness is compatible with ours, we join up with them and fall into mutually satisfying weirdness—and call it love—true love.
author: Robert Fulghum

text: Love never dies a natural death. It dies because we don't know how to replenish its source. It dies of blindness and errors and betrayals. It dies of illness and wounds; it dies of weariness, of witherings, of tarnishings.
author: Anais Nin

text: There is nothing I would not do for those who are really my friends. I have no notion of loving people by halves, it is not my nature.
author: Jane Austen

text: The real lover is the man who can thrill you by kissing your forehead or smiling into your eyes or just staring into space.
author: Marilyn Monroe

text: If I had a flower for every time I thought of you...I could walk through my garden forever.
author: Alfred Tennyson

text: When someone loves you, the way they talk about you is different. You feel safe and comfortable.
author: Jess C. Scott

text: Dumbledore watched her fly away, and as her silvery glow faded he turned back to Snape, and his eyes were full of tears."After all this time?""Always," said Snape.
author: J.K. Rowling

text: To die will be an awfully big adventure.
author: J.M. Barrie

text: A lady's imagination is very rapid; it jumps from admiration to love, from love to matrimony in a moment.
author: Jane Austen

text: So it's not gonna be easy. It's going to be really hard; we're gonna have to work at this everyday, but I want to do that because I want you. I want all of you, forever, everyday. You and me... everyday.
author: Nicholas Sparks

text: Just when you think it can't get any worse, it can. And just when you think it can't get any better, it can.
author: Nicholas Sparks

text: Tis better to have loved and lost, Than never to have loved at all.
author: Alfred Tennyson

text: I love you as certain dark things are to be loved, in secret, between the shadow and the soul.
author: Pablo Neruda

text: The one you love and the one who loves you are never, ever the same person.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: Some people don't understand the promises they're making when they make them," I said."Right, of course. But you keep the promise anyway. That's what love is. Love is keeping the promise anyway.
author: John Green

text: Love is needing someone. Love is putting up with someone's bad qualities because they somehow complete you.
author: Sarah Dessen

text: When I despair, I remember that all through history the way of truth and love have always won. There have been tyrants and murderers, and for a time, they can seem invincible, but in the end, they always fall. Think of it--always.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: One is loved because one is loved. No reason is needed for loving.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: When we love, we always strive to become better than we are. When we strive to become better than we are, everything around us becomes better too.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: We love the things we love for what they are.
author: Robert Frost

text: I would die for you. But I won't live for you.
author: Stephen Chbosky

text: In vain have I struggled. It will not do. My feelings will not be repressed. You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.
author: Jane Austen

text: Perhaps all the dragons in our lives are princesses who are only waiting to see us act, just once, with beauty and courage. Perhaps everything that frightens us is, in its deepest essence, something helpless that wants our love.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: You are, and always have been, my dream.
author: Nicholas Sparks

text: The only thing worse than a boy who hates you: a boy that loves you.
author: Markus Zusak

text: So, I love you because the entire universe conspired to help me find you.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: Where there is love there is life.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Never love anyone who treats you like you're ordinary.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I would always rather be happy than dignified.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: Once upon a time there was a boy who loved a girl, and her laughter was a question he wanted to spend his whole life answering.
author: Nicole Krauss

text: Nobody has ever measured, not even poets, how much the heart can hold.
author: Zelda Fitzgerald

text: Some women choose to follow men, and some women choose to follow their dreams. If you're wondering which way to go, remember that your career will never wake up and tell you that it doesn't love you anymore.
author: Lady Gaga

text: We loved with a love that was more than love.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

text: And so the lion fell in love with the lamb…" he murmured. I looked away, hiding my eyes as I thrilled to the word."What a stupid lamb," I sighed."What a sick, masochistic lion.
author: Stephenie Meyer

text: This is a good sign, having a broken heart. It means we have tried for something.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: Every heart sings a song, incomplete, until another heart whispers back. Those who wish to sing always find a song. At the touch of a lover, everyone becomes a poet.
author: Plato

text: A guy and a girl can be just friends, but at one point or another, they will fall for each other...Maybe temporarily, maybe at the wrong time, maybe too late, or maybe forever
author: Dave Matthews Band

text: The more I know of the world, the more I am convinced that I shall never see a man whom I can really love. I require so much!
author: Jane Austen

text: Eleanor was right. She never looked nice. She looked like art, and art wasn't supposed to look nice; it was supposed to make you feel something.
author: Rainbow Rowell

text: He's more myself than I am. Whatever our souls are made of, his and mine are the same.
author: Emily Bronte

text: I have decided to stick to love...Hate is too great a burden to bear.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: What's meant to be will always find a way
author: Trisha Yearwood

text: Out beyond ideas of wrongdoing and rightdoing there is a field.I'll meet you there.When the soul lies down in that grassthe world is too full to talk about.
author: Jalaluddin Rumi

text: They say a person needs just three things to be truly happy in this world: someone to love, something to do, and something to hope for.
author: Tom Bodett

text: Two people in love, alone, isolated from the world, that's beautiful.
author: Milan Kundera

text: The heart was made to be broken.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: This above all: to thine own self be true, And it must follow, as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man.
author: William Shakespeare

text: For the two of us, home isn't a place. It is a person. And we are finally home.
author: Stephanie Perkins

text: You could have had anything else in the world, and you asked for me."She smiled up at him. Filthy as he was, covered in blood and dirt, he was the most beautiful thing she'd ever seen."But I don't want anything else in the world.
author: Cassandra Clare

text: Love is so short, forgetting is so long.
author: Pablo Neruda

text: The world is indeed full of peril, and in it there are many dark places; but still there is much that is fair, and though in all lands love is now mingled with grief, it grows perhaps the greater.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: The very essence of romance is uncertainty.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: What I want is to be needed. What I need is to be indispensable to somebody. Who I need is somebody that will eat up all my free time, my ego, my attention. Somebody addicted to me. A mutual addiction.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: There is always some madness in love. But there is also always some reason in madness.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: If all else perished, and he remained, I should still continue to be; and if all else remained, and he were annihilated, the universe would turn to a mighty stranger.
author: Emily Bronte

text: Man may have discovered fire, but women discovered how to play with it.
author: Candace Bushnell

text: And, in the endThe love you takeis equal to the love you make.
author: Paul McCartney

text: What I need is the dandelion in the spring. The bright yellow that means rebirth instead of destruction. The promise that life can go on, no matter how bad our losses. That it can be good again.
author: Suzanne Collins

text: Love does not consist of gazing at each other, but in looking outward together in the same direction.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: Love is a fire. But whether it is going to warm your hearth or burn down your house, you can never tell.
author: Joan Crawford

text: I can feel Peeta press his forehead into my temple and he asks, 'So now that you've got me, what are you going to do with me?' I turn into him. 'Put you somewhere you can't get hurt.
author: Suzanne Collins

text: If you remember me, then I don't care if everyone else forgets.
author: Haruki Murakami

text: I don't trust people who don't love themselves and tell me, 'I love you.' ... There is an African saying which is: Be careful when a naked person offers you a shirt.
author: Maya Angelou

text: It was love at first sight, at last sight, at ever and ever sight.
author: Vladimir Nabokov

text: Love doesn't just sit there, like a stone, it has to be made, like bread; remade all the time, made new.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love.
author: Anonymous

text: If pain must come, may it come quickly. Because I have a life to live, and I need to live it in the best way possible. If he has to make a choice, may he make it now. Then I will either wait for him or forget him.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: Be careful of love. It'll twist your brain around and leave you thinking up is down and right is wrong.
author: Rick Riordan

text: If you love somebody, let them go, for if they return, they were always yours. If they don't, they never were.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: I love you. Remember. They cannot take it
author: Lauren Oliver

text: Do you think I'm pretty?I think you're beautifulBeautiful?You are so beautiful, it hurts sometimes.
author: Richelle Mead

text: When love is not madness it is not love.
author: Pedro Calderon de la Barca

text: Love does not begin and end the way we seem to think it does. Love is a battle, love is a war; love is a growing up.
author: James     Baldwin

text: A woman's heart should be so hidden in God that a man has to seek Him just to find her.
author: Max Lucado

text: Declarations of love amuse me. Especially when unrequited.
author: Cassandra Clare

text: What's this?" he demanded, looking from Clary to his companions, as if they might know what she was doing there."It's a girl," Jace said,recovering his composure. "Surely you've seen girls before, Alec. Your sister Isabelle is one.
author: Cassandra Clare

text: Love is an irresistible desire to be irresistibly desired.
author: Robert Frost

text: I was smiling yesterday,I am smiling today and I will smile tomorrow.Simply because life is too short to cry for anything.
author: Santosh Kalwar

text: It is good to love many things, for therein lies the true strength, and whosoever loves much performs much, and can accomplish much, and what is done in love is well done.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: If I can stop one heart from breaking, I shall not live in vain.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: When the power of love overcomes the love of power, the world will know peace.
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: I think... if it is true that there are as many minds as there are heads, then there are as many kinds of love as there are hearts.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: It is a curious thought, but it is only when you see people looking ridiculous that you realize just how much you love them.
author: Agatha Christie

text: Hate the sin, love the sinner.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Of all forms of caution, caution in love is perhaps the most fatal to true happiness.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: One love, one heart, one destiny.
author: Bob Marley

text: I loved her against reason, against promise, against peace, against hope, against happiness, against all discouragement that could be.
author: Charles Dickens

text: If you gave someone your heart and they died, did they take it with them? Did you spend the rest of forever with a hole inside you that couldn't be filled?
author: Jodi Picoult

text: It's not the face, but the expressions on it. It's not the voice, but what you say. It's not how you look in that body, but the thing you do with it. You are beautiful.
author: Stephenie Meyer

text: You know, when it works, love is pretty amazing. It's not overrated. There's a reason for all those songs.
author: Sarah Dessen

text: What Is Love? I have met in the streets a very poor young man who was in love. His hat was old, his coat worn, the water passed through his shoes and the stars through his soul
author: Victor Hugo

text: All I ever wanted was to reach out and touch another human being not just with my hands but with my heart.
author: Tahereh Mafi

text: To me, Fearless is not the absense of fear. It's not being completely unafraid. To me, Fearless is having fears. Fearless is having doubts. Lots of them. To me, Fearless is living in spite of those things that scare you to death.
author: Taylor Swift

text: I love you like a fat kid loves cake!
author: Scott Adams

text: It isn't possible to love and part. You will wish that it was. You can transmute love, ignore it, muddle it, but you can never pull it out of you. I know by experience that the poets are right: love is eternal.
author: E.M. Forster

text: Gravitation is not responsible for people falling in love.
author: Albert Einstein

text: We waste time looking for the perfect lover, instead of creating the perfect love.
author: Tom Robbins

text: They say when you are missing someone that they are probably feeling the same, but I don't think it's possible for you to miss me as much as I'm missing you right now
author: Edna St. Vincent Millay

text: Any fool can be happy. It takes a man with real heart to make beauty out of the stuff that makes us weep.
author: Clive Barker

text: Welcome to the wonderful world of jealousy, he thought. For the price of admission, you get a splitting headache, a nearly irresistable urge to commit murder, and an inferiority complex. Yippee.
author: J.R. Ward

text: You couldn't relive your life, skipping the awful parts, without losing what made it worthwhile. You had to accept it as a whole--like the world, or the person you loved.
author: Stewart O'Nan

text: What is hell? I maintain that it is the suffering of being unable to love.
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

text: Never close your lips to those whom you have already opened your heart.
author: Charles Dickens

text: Love is not affectionate feeling, but a steady wish for the loved person's ultimate good as far as it can be obtained.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: The most painful thing is losing yourself in the process of loving someone too much, and forgetting that you are special too.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: Find what you love and let it kill you.
author: Charles Bukowski

text: The man of knowledge must be able not only to love his enemies but also to hate his friends.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Look after my heart - I've left it with you.
author: Stephenie Meyer

text: Things we lose have a way of coming back to us in the end, if not always in the way we expect.
author: J.K. Rowling

text: One cannot think well, love well, sleep well, if one has not dined well.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: I would like to be the air that inhabits you for a moment only. I would like to be that unnoticed and that necessary.
author: Margaret Atwood

text: And then he gives me a smile that just seems so genuinely sweet with just the right touch of shyness that unexpected warmth rushes through me.
author: Suzanne Collins

text: He does something to me, that boy. Every time. It’s his only detriment. He steps on my heart. He makes me cry.
author: Markus Zusak

text: I want to do with you what spring does with the cherry trees.
author: Pablo Neruda

text: Have enough courage to trust love one more time and always one more time.
author: Maya Angelou

text: You can't measure the mutual affection of two human beings by the number of words they exchange.
author: Milan Kundera

text: Sometimes it's a form of love just to talk to somebody that you have nothing in common with and still be fascinated by their presence.
author: David Byrne

text: How do you spell 'love'?" - Piglet"You don't spell it...you feel it." - Pooh
author: A.A. Milne

text: Tonight I can write the saddest linesI loved her, and sometimes she loved me too.
author: Pablo Neruda

text: A DEFINITION NOT FOUND IN THE DICTIONARY Not leaving: an act of trust and love,often deciphered by children
author: Markus Zusak

text: One day you will kiss a man you can't breathe without, and find that breath is of little consequence.
author: Karen Marie Moning

text: Maybe...you'll fall in love with me all over again.""Hell," I said, "I love you enough now. What do you want to do? Ruin me?""Yes. I want to ruin you.""Good," I said. "That's what I want too.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: Was it hard?" I ask.Letting go?"Not as hard as holding on to something that wasn't real.
author: Lisa Schroeder

text: I'm saying that I'm a moody, insecure, narrow-minded, jealous, borderline homicidal bitch, and I want you to promise me that you're okay with that, because it's who I am, and you're what I need.
author: Jeaniene Frost

text: Then I realize what it is. It's him. Something about him makes me feel like I am about to fall. Or turn to liquid. Or burst into flames.
author: Veronica Roth

text: Art and love are the same thing: It’s the process of seeing yourself in things that are not you.
author: Chuck Klosterman

text: Letting go doesn't mean that you don't care about someone anymore. It's just realizing that the only person you really have control over is yourself.
author: Deborah Reber

text: Promise me you'll never forget me because if I thought you would, I'd never leave.
author: A.A. Milne

text: I wasn't actually in love, but I felt a sort of tender curiosity.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: When I am with you, we stay up all night.When you're not here, I can't go to sleep.Praise God for those two insomnias!And the difference between them.
author: Jalaluddin Rumi

text: I loved you like a man loves a woman he never touches, only writes to, keeps little photographs of.
author: Charles Bukowski

text: You yourself, as much as anybody in the entire universe, deserve your love and affection
author: Sharon Salzberg

text: Tears shed for another person are not a sign of weakness. They are a sign of a pure heart.
author: Jose N. Harris

text: It is the time you have wasted for your rose that makes your rose so important.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: There is only one page left to write on. I will fill it with words of only one syllable. I love. I have loved. I will love.
author: Audrey Niffenegger

text: Happiness is holding someone in your arms and knowing you hold the whole world.
author: Orhan Pamuk

text: Sometimes when I look at you, I feel I'm gazing at a distant star. It's dazzling, but the light is from tens of thousands of years ago.Maybe the star doesn't even exist any more. Yet sometimes that light seems more real to me than anything.
author: Haruki Murakami

text: A half-read book is a half-finished love affair.
author: David Mitchell

text: To love oneself is the beginning of a lifelong romance.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I like flaws. I think they make things interesting.
author: Sarah Dessen

text: Someone I loved once gave me a box full of darkness. It took me years to understand that this too, was a gift.
author: Mary Oliver

text: I'm oxygen and he's dying to breathe.
author: Tahereh Mafi

text: Every one of us is, in the cosmic perspective, precious. If a human disagrees with you, let him live. In a hundred billion galaxies, you will not find another.
author: Carl Sagan

text: I'm not sentimental--I'm as romantic as you are. The idea, you know,is that the sentimental person thinks things will last--the romanticperson has a desperate confidence that they won't.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: I'm on Aslan's side even if there isn't any Aslan to lead it. I'm going to live as like a Narnian as I can even if there isn't any Narnia.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: A purpose of human life, no matter who is controlling it, is to love whoever is around to be loved.
author: Kurt Vonnegut Jr.

text: It is easy to love people in memory the hard thing is to love them when they are there in front of you.
author: John Updike

text: Love can change a person the way a parent can change a baby- awkwardly, and often with a great deal of mess.
author: Lemony Snicket

text: When I saw you I fell in love, and you smiled because you knew.
author: Arrigo Boito

text: You know how they say you only hurt the ones you love? Well, it works both ways.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: Be with me always - take any form - drive me mad! only do not leave me in this abyss, where I cannot find you! Oh, God! it is unutterable! I can not live without my life! I can not live without my soul!
author: Emily Bronte

text: What she had realized was that love was that moment when your heart was about to burst.
author: Stieg Larsson

text: Sorrow is how we learn to love. Your heart isn't breaking. It hurts because it's getting larger. The larger it gets, the more love it holds.
author: Rita Mae Brown

text: Friendship marks a life even more deeply than love. Love risks degenerating into obsession, friendship is never anything but sharing.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: One word frees us of all the weight and pain of life:That word is love.
author: Sophocles

text: Respect was invented to cover the empty place where love should be.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: I guess that’s just part of loving people: You have to give things up. Sometimes you even have to give them up.
author: Lauren Oliver

text: The more one judges, the less one loves.
author: Honore de Balzac

text: The heart has its reasons which reason knows not.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: Time was passing like a hand waving from a train I wanted to be on. I hope you never have to think about anything as much as I think about you.
author: Jonathan Safran Foer

text: Sometimes love means letting go when you want to hold on tighter.
author: Melissa Marr

text: Holding Eleanor's hand was like holding a butterfly. Or a heartbeat. Like holding something complete, and completely alive.
author: Rainbow Rowell

text: Trust your heart if the seas catch fire, live by love though the stars walk backward.
author: E.E. Cummings

text: The love of learning, the sequestered nooks,And all the sweet serenity of books
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

text: From childhood's hour I have not been. As others were, I have not seen. As others saw, I could not awaken. My heart to joy at the same tone. And all I loved, I loved alone.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

text: Come sleep with me: We won't make Love, Love will make us.
author: Julio Cortazar

text: People have forgotten this truth," the fox said. "But you mustn’t forget it. You become responsible forever for what you’ve tamed. You’re responsible for your rose.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: Nothing takes the taste out of peanut butter quite like unrequited love.
author: Charles M. Schulz

text: When someone is in your heart, they're never truly gone. They can come back to you, even at unlikely times.
author: Mitch Albom

text: Ever has it been that love knows not its own depth until the hour of separation.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: Don't cry over someone who wouldn't cry over you.
author: Lauren Conrad

text: Absence diminishes small loves and increases great ones, as the wind blows out the candle and fans the bonfire.
author: Francois de La Rochefoucauld

text: Being with you never felt wrong. It's the one thing I did right. You're the one thing I did right.
author: Becca Fitzpatrick

text: I wonder how many people don't get the one they want, but end up with the one they're supposed to be with.
author: Fannie Flagg

text: How do I love thee? Let me count the ways.I love thee to the depth and breadth and heightMy soul can reach
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

text: When you trip over love, it is easy to get up. But when you fall in love, it is impossible to stand again.
author: Albert Einstein

text: This is where it all begins. Everything starts here, today.
author: David Nicholls

text: To be fully seen by somebody, then, and be loved anyhow - this is a human offering that can border on miraculous.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: I’ve been fighting to be who I am all my life. What’s the point of being who I am, if I can’t have the person who was worth all the fighting for?
author: Stephanie  Lennox

text: I want to be in a relationship where you telling me you love me is just a ceremonious validation of what you already show me.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: In your light I learn how to love. In your beauty, how to make poems. You dance inside my chest where no-one sees you, but sometimes I do, and that sight becomes this art.
author: Jalaluddin Rumi

text: True love is usually the most inconvenient kind.
author: Kiera Cass

text: Watch out for intellect,because it knows so much it knows nothingand leaves you hanging upside down,mouthing knowledge as your heartfalls out of your mouth.
author: Anne Sexton

text: We're staying together," he promised. "You're not getting away from me. Never again.
author: Rick Riordan

text: The sunlight claps the earth, and the moonbeams kiss the sea: what are all these kissings worth, if thou kiss not me?
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

text: Even After All this timeThe Sun never says to the Earth,"You owe me."LookWhat happensWith a love like that,It lights the whole sky.
To lose balance sometimes for love is part of living a balancedlife.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: If you love something set it free, but don't be surprised if it comes back with herpes.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: My wish is that you may be loved to the point of madness.
author: Andre Breton

text: The things we love destroy us every time, lad. Remember that.
author: George R.R. Martin

text: Throw your dreams into space like a kite, and you do not know what it will bring back, a new life, a new friend, a new love, a new country.
author: Anais Nin

text: I sought to hear the voice of God and climbed the topmost steeple, but God declared: "Go down again - I dwell among the people.
author: John Henry Newman

text: If we have no peace, it is because we have forgotten that we belong to each other.
author: Mother Teresa

text: sometimes you don't need a goal in life, you don't need to know the big picture. you just need to know what you're going to do next!
author: Sophie Kinsella

text: I won't ever leave you, even though you're always leaving me.
author: Audrey Niffenegger

text: I love you as certain dark things are loved, secretly, between the shadow and the soul.
author: Stephanie Perkins

text: We are not trapped or locked up in these bones. No, no. We are free to change. And love changes us. And if we can love one another, we can break open the sky.
author: Walter Mosley

text: You have to kiss a lot of frogs before you find your prince
author: E.L. James

text: About all you can do in life is be who you are. Some people will love you for you. Most will love you for what you can do for them, and some won't like you at all.
author: Rita Mae Brown

text: For my part, I prefer my heart to be broken. It is so lovely, dawn-kaleidoscopic within the crack.
author: D.H. Lawrence

text: The world was collapsing, and the only thing that really mattered to me was that she was alive.
author: Rick Riordan

text: I was always hungry for love. Just once, I wanted to know what it was like to get my fill of it -- to be fed so much love I couldn't take any more. Just once.
author: Haruki Murakami

text: The course of true love never did run smooth.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Romance is the glamour which turns the dust of everyday life into a golden haze.
author: Elinor Glyn

text: In a perfect world, you could fuck people without giving them a piece of your heart. And every glittering kiss and every touch of flesh is another shard of heart you’ll never see again.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: The human heart is a strange vessel. Love and hatred can exist side by side.
author: Scott Westerfeld

text: For where all love is, the speaking is unnecessary
author: Diana Gabaldon

text: Someday you're gonna look back on this moment of your life as such a sweet time of grieving. You'll see that you were in mourning and your heart was broken, but your life was changing...
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: sex is the consolation you have when you can't have love
author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez

text: It is possible to be in love with you just because of who you are.
author: Maggie Stiefvater

text: The music in his laughter had a way of rounding off the missing notes in her soul.
author: Gloria Naylor

text: If he loved with all the powers of his puny being, he couldn't love as much in eighty years as I could in a day.
author: Emily Bronte

text: I bet you could sometimes find all the mysteries of the universe in someone's hand.
author: Benjamin Alire Saenz

text: More smiling, less worrying. More compassion, less judgment. More blessed, less stressed. More love, less hate.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Each of us is born with a box of matches inside us but we can't strike them all by ourselves
author: Laura Esquivel

text: I almost wish we were butterflies and liv'd but three summer days - three such days with you I could fill with more delight than fifty common years could ever contain.
author: John Keats

text: And next time you're planning to injure yourself to get me attention, just remember that a little sweet talk works wonders.
author: Cassandra Clare

text: Love is how you stay alive, even after you are gone.
author: Mitch Albom

text: If only she could be so oblivious again, to feel such love without knowing it, mistaking it for laughter.
author: Markus Zusak

text: It's not how much we give but how much love we put into giving.
author: Mother Teresa

text: The highest function of love is that it makes the loved one a unique and irreplaceable being.
author: Tom Robbins

text: I know enough to know that no woman should ever marry a man who hated his mother.
author: Martha Gellhorn

text: We are told that people stay in love because of chemistry, or because they remain intrigued with each other, because of many kindnesses, because of luck. But part of it has got to be forgiveness and gratefulness.
author: Ellen Goodman

text: And what would humans be without love?"RARE, said Death.
author: Terry Pratchett

text: I have feelings too. I am still human. All I want is to be loved, for myself and for my talent.
author: Marilyn Monroe

text: Love is not the absence of logicbut logic examined and recalculatedheated and curved to fitinside the contours of the heart
author: Tammara Webber

text: True love is not so much a matter of romance as it is a matter of anxious concern for the well-being of one's companion.
author: Gordon B. Hinckley

text: Just because you're beautiful and perfect, it's made you conceited.
author: William Goldman

text: Sometimes God allows what he hates to accomplish what he loves.
author: Joni Eareckson Tada

text: You are the one girl that made me risk eveything for a future worth having.
author: Simone Elkeles

text: By the time you swear you're his,Shivering and sighing.And he vows his passion is,Infinite, undying.Lady make note of this --One of you is lying.
author: Dorothy Parker

text: Anxiety is love's greatest killer. It makes others feel as you might when a drowning man holds on to you. You want to save him, but you know he will strangle you with his panic.
author: Anais Nin

text: I'll fight it. I'll fight it for you. Don't you worry about me, Hazel Grace. I'm okay. I'll find a way to hang around and annoy you for a long time.
author: John Green

text: That's what people do who love you. They put their arms around you and love you when you're not so lovable.
author: Deb Caletti

text: Let yourself be drawn by the stronger pull of that which you truly love.
author: Jalaluddin Rumi

text: The heart is the toughest part of the body. Tenderness is in the hands.
author: Carolyn Forche

text: The soul is healed by being with children.
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

text: Love has nothing to do with what you are expecting to get - only with what you are expecting to give - which is everything
author: Katharine Hepburn

text: I wondered what happened when you offered yourself to someone, and they opened you, only to discover you were not the gift they expected and they had to smile and nod and say thank you all the same.
author: Jodi Picoult

text: When it's gone, you'll know what a gift love was. You'll suffer like this. So go back and fight to keep it.
author: Ian McEwan

text: I told you. You don't love someone because of their looks or their clothes or their car. You love them because they sing a song only your heart can understand.
author: L.J. Smith

text: True love comes quietly, without banners or flashing lights. If you hear bells, get your ears checked.
author: Erich Segal

text: The really important kind of freedom involves attention, and awareness, and discipline, and effort, and being able truly to care about other people and to sacrifice for them, over and over, in myriad petty little unsexy ways, every day.
author: David Foster Wallace

text: If you can love someone with your whole heart, even one person, then there's salvation in life. Even if you can't get together with that person.
author: Haruki Murakami

text: In youth, it was a way I had,To do my best to please.And change, with every passing ladTo suit his theories.But now I know the things I knowAnd do the things I do,And if you do not like me so,To hell, my love, with you.
author: Dorothy Parker

text: Love is the extremely difficult realization that something other than oneself is real.
author: Iris Murdoch

text: I belong to my beloved, and my beloved is mine.
author: Jamie McGuire

text: Yours is the light by which my spirit's born: - you are my sun, my moon, and all my stars.
author: E.E. Cummings

text: It's bullshit to think of friendship and romance as being different. They're not. They're just variations of the same love. Variations of the same desire to be close.
author: Rachel Cohn

text: If my love were an ocean,there would be no more land.If my love were a desert,you would see only sand.If my love were a star-late at night, only light.And if my love could grow wings,I'd be soaring in flight.
author: Jay Asher

text: Love, it never dies. It never goes away, it never fades, so long as you hang on to it. Love can make you immortal
author: Gayle Forman

text: It's hard being left behind. (...) It's hard to be the one who stays.
author: Audrey Niffenegger

text: Why are old lovers able to become friends? Two reasons. They never truly loved each other, or they love each other still.
author: Whitney Otto

text: and he suddenly knew that if she killed herself, he would die. Maybe not immediately, maybe not with the same blinding rush of pain, but it would happen. You couldn't live for very long without a heart.
author: Jodi Picoult

text: Moments, when lost, can't be found again. They're just gone.
author: Jenny Han

text: What are the chances you’d ever meet someone like that? he wondered. Someone you could love forever, someone who would forever love you back? And what did you do when that person was born half a world away? The math seemed impossible.
author: Rainbow Rowell

text: I wonder if fears ever really go away, or if they just lose their power over us.
author: Veronica Roth

text: Trust no friend without faults, and love a woman, but no angel.
author: Doris Lessing

text: There is always something left to love.
author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez

text: We must love one another or die
author: W.H. Auden

text: If a thing loves, it is infinite.
author: William Blake

text: Be the reason someone smiles. Be the reason someone feels loved and believes in the goodness in people.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: Love is a better master than duty.
author: Albert Einstein

text: It doesn't matter who you are or what you look like, so long as somebody loves you.
author: Roald Dahl

text: After all this time?""Always...
author: J.K. Rowling

text: There is nothing more truly artistic than to love people.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: In love, no question is ever preposterous.
author: Andre Brink

text: Love, n. A temporary insanity curable by marriage.
author: Ambrose Bierce

text: No woman wants to be in submission to a man who isn't in submission to God!
author: T.D. Jakes

text: I love mankind, he said, "but I find to my amazement that the more I love mankind as a whole, the less I love man in particular.
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

text: You only need one man to love you. But him to love you free like a wildfire, crazy like the moon, always like tomorrow, sudden like an inhale and overcoming like the tides. Only one man and all of this.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: The greatest happiness of life is the conviction that we are loved -- loved for ourselves, or rather, loved in spite of ourselves.
author: Victor Hugo

text: Happily ever after, or even just together ever after, is not cheesy,” Wren said. “It’s the noblest, like, the most courageous thing two people can shoot for.
author: Rainbow Rowell

text: What greater gift than the love of a cat.
author: Charles Dickens

text: We are all worthy of one another.
author: Edward P. Jones

text: Love is an abstract noun, something nebulous. And yet love turns out to be the only part of us that is solid, as the world turns upside down and the screen goes black.
author: Martin Amis

text: Love cannot be reduced to a catalogue of reasons why, and a catalogue of reasons cannot be put together into love.
author: Eleanor Catton

text: To say that one waits a lifetime for his soulmate to come around is a paradox. People eventually get sick of waiting, take a chance on someone, and by the art of commitment become soulmates, which takes a lifetime to perfect.
author: Criss Jami

text: Lovers alone wear sunlight.
author: E.E. Cummings

text: Oh no. Don't smile. You'll kill me. I stop breathing when you smile.
author: Tessa Dare

text: Because, if you could love someone, and keep loving them, without being loved back . . . then that love had to be real. It hurt too much to be anything else.
author: Sarah Cross

text: The great tragedy of life is not that men perish, but that they cease to love.
author: W. Somerset Maugham

text: Do I love you? My God, if your love were a grain of sand, mine would be a universe of beaches.
author: William Goldman

text: I think it is all a matter of love the more you love a memory the stronger and stranger it becomes
author: Vladimir Nabokov

text: There's a difference between really loving someone and loving the idea of her.
author: Gillian Flynn

text: He is beauty, inside and out.He is the silver lining in a world of darkness.He is my light.
author: Marie Lu

text: Nothing ever happens like you imagine it will
author: John Green

text: I think we ought to live happily ever after.
author: Diana Wynne Jones

text: Unless you love someone, nothing else makes sense.
author: E.E. Cummings

text: With him, life was routine; without him, life was unbearable.
author: Harper Lee

text: Snape's patronus was a doe,' said Harry, 'the same as my mother's because he loved her for nearly all of his life, from when they were children.
author: J.K. Rowling

text: Love and compassion are necessities, not luxuries. Without them, humanity cannot survive.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

text: If you can capture a woman's imagination, then you will have her. But imagination is a strange creature. It needs time and distance to function properly.
author: Kathleen Tessaro

text: It’s dark now and I am very tired. I love you, always. Time is nothing.
author: Audrey Niffenegger

text: Every atom of your flesh is as dear to me as my own: in pain and sickness it would still be dear.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: You're like a song that I heard when I was a little kid but forgot I knew until I heard it again.
author: Maggie Stiefvater

text: If I were to live a thousand years, I would belong to you for all of them. If we were to live a thousand lives, I would want to make you mine in each one.
author: Michelle Hodkin

text: I don’t know what they are called, the spaces between seconds– but I think of you always in those intervals.
author: Salvador Plascencia

text: Love sucks. Sometimes it feels good. Sometimes it's just another way to bleed.
author: Laurell K. Hamilton

text: I'm not sure this is a world I belong in anymore. I'm not sure that I want to wake up.
author: Gayle Forman

text: Heaven would never be heaven without you.
author: Richard Matheson

text: I have never found anybody who could stand to accept the daily demonstrative love I feel in me, and give back as good as I give.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: I'm saying I'm in love with you! I've been in love with you this whole bleeding year!
author: Stephanie Perkins

text: I know my heart will never be the sameBut I'm telling myself I'll be okay
author: Sara Evans

text: What would you do if I kissed you right now?"I stared at his beautiful face and his beautiful mouth and I wanted nothing more than to taste it. "I would kiss you back.
author: Michelle Hodkin

text: Love recognizes no barriers. It jumps hurdles, leaps fences, penetrates walls to arrive at its destination full of hope.
author: Maya Angelou

text: We always see our worst selves. Our most vulnerable selves. We need someone else to get close enough to tell us we’re wrong. Someone we trust.
author: David Levithan

text: The moment you stop to think about whether you love someone, you've already stopped loving that person forever.
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafon

text: Do what you love, love what you do, and with all your heart give yourself to it.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: The best things in life make you sweaty.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

text: So it’s true, when all is said and done, grief is the price we pay for love.
author: E.A. Bucchianeri

text: When in a relationship, a real man doesn't make his woman jealous of others, he makes others jealous of his woman.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: The day the power of love overrules the love of power, the world will know peace.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Those sweet lips. My, oh my, I could kiss those lips all night long.Good things come to those who wait.
author: Jess C. Scott

text: Believe in a love that is being stored up for you like an inheritance, and have faith that in this love there is a strength and a blessing so large that you can travel as far as you wish without having to step outside it.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: We're all a little weird. And life is a little weird. And when we find someone whose weirdness is compatible with ours, we join up with them and fall into mutually satisfying weirdness — and call it love — true love.
author: Robert Fulghum

text: It's funny how you can forget everything except people loving you. Maybe that's why humans find it so hard getting over love affairs. It's not the pain they're getting over, it's the love.
author: Melina Marchetta

text: Parents were the only ones obligated to love you from the rest of the world you had to earn it.
author: Ann Brashares

text: There ain't no way you can hold onto something that wants to go, you understand? You can only love what you got while you got it.
author: Kate DiCamillo

text: It's not that you should never love something so much that it can control you.It's that you need to love something that much so you can never be controlled.It's not a weakness.It's your best strength.
author: Patrick Ness

text: You are my life now.
author: Stephenie Meyer

text: Every true love and friendship is a story of unexpected transformation. If we are the same person before and after we loved, that means we haven't loved enough.
author: Elif Shafak

text: If I die, I will wait for you, do you understand? No matter how long. I will watch from beyond to make sure you live every year you have to its fullest, and then we’ll have so much to talk about when I see you again… (Bones)
author: Jeaniene Frost

text: Please Mia," he implores. "Don't make me write a song.
author: Gayle Forman

text: I may not always be with you But when we're far apartRemember you will be with meRight inside my heart
author: Marc Wambolt

text: Morning without you is a dwindled dawn.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: The Bible tells us to love our neighbors, and also to love our enemies; probably because generally they are the same people.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: I know that the whole point—the only point—is tofind the things that matter, and hold on to them, and fight for them, and refuse tolet them go.
author: Lauren Oliver

text: How can I be reasonable? To me our love was everything and you were my whole life. It is not very pleasant to realize that to you it was only an episode.
author: W. Somerset Maugham

text: It's the imperfections that make things beautiful
author: Jenny Han

text: Remember that the best relationship is one in which your love for each other exceeds your need for each other.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

text: I don't care if you're black, white, straight, bisexual, gay, lesbian, short, tall, fat, skinny, rich or poor. If you're nice to me, I'll be nice to you. Simple as that.
author: Robert Michaels

text: One day spent with someone you love can change everything.
author: Mitch Albom

text: His heart danced upon her movements like a cork upon a tide. He heard what her eyes said to him from beneath their cowl and knew that in some dim past, whether in life or revery, he had heard their tale before.
author: James Joyce

text: There is no love sincerer than the love of food.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: Love is like a brick. You can build a house, or you can sink a dead body.
author: Lady Gaga

text: Sleep my little baby-ohSleep until you wakenWhen you wake you'll see the worldIf I'm not mistaken...Kiss a loverDance a measure,Find your nameAnd buried treasure...Face your lifeIts pain, Its pleasure,Leave no path untaken.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together,but do so with all your heart.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: She wasn't doing a thing that I could see, except standing there leaning on the balcony railing, holding the universe together.
author: J.D. Salinger

text: I will not try to convince you to love me, to respect me, to commit to me. I deserve better than that; I AM BETTER THAN THAT...Goodbye.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: I envy people that know love. That have someone who takes them as they are.
author: Jess C. Scott

text: I don't care what you say to me. I care what you share with me.
author: Santosh Kalwar

text: Some people feel like they don't deserve love. They walk away quietly into empty spaces, trying to close the gaps of the past.
author: Jon Krakauer

text: The great thing to remember is that though our feelings come and go God's love for us does not.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Keep your best wishes, close to your heart and watch what happens
author: Tony DeLiso

text: Ye are Blood of my Blood, and Bone of my Bone,I give ye my Body, that we Two might be One.I give ye my Spirit, 'til our Life shall be Done.
author: Diana Gabaldon

text: He's not your prince charming if he doesn't make sure you know that you're his princess.
author: Demi Lovato

text: In this world, it is too common for people to search for someone to lose themselves in. But I am already lost. I will look for someone to find myself in.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: Her heart was a secret garden and the walls were very high.
author: William Goldman

text: I love you more than I think I should.
author: Becca Fitzpatrick

text: The voice of Love seemed to call to me, but it was a wrong number.
author: P.G. Wodehouse

text: I belong to the people I love, and they belong to me--they, and the love and loyaty I give them, form my identity far more than any word or group ever could.
author: Veronica Roth

text: Don't leave a piece of jewelry at his house so you can go back and get it later he may be with his real girlfriend.
author: Amy Sedaris

text: Love, the poet said, is woman's whole existence.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: Falling for him would be like cliff diving. It would be either the most exhilarating thing that ever happened to me or the stupidest mistake I’d ever make.
author: Colleen Houck

text: One day, when I am a braver man, I will tell her these things, and then I will look her in the eye tell her I love her and ask her to be only mine. But until that day, we're just friends.
author: Charlie Huston

text: In secret we met -In silence I grieve,That thy heart could forget,Thy spirit deceive.If I should meet theeAfter long years,How should I greet thee? -With silence and tears
author: George Gordon Byron

text: nothing in this world was more difficult than love.
author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez

text: She is a friend of my mind. She gather me, man. The pieces I am, she gather them and give them back to me in all the right order.
author: Toni Morrison

text: All my heart is yours, sir: it belongs to you; and with you it would remain, were fate to exile the rest of me from your presence forever.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: If I love you, what business is it of yours?
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: There is love in holding and there is love in letting go.
author: Elizabeth Berg

text: Being with you and not being with you is the only way I have to measure time.
author: Jorge Luis Borges

text: I've always loved you, and when you love someone, you love the whole person, just as he or she is, and not as you would like them to be.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: After all these years, I see that I was mistaken about Eve in the beginning; it is better to live outside the Garden with her than inside it without her.
author: Mark Twain

text: If you love a flower that lives on a star, it is sweet to look at the sky at night. All the stars are a-bloom with flowers...
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: Love is a luxury.""No. Love is an element."An element. Like air to breathe, earth to stand on.
author: Laini Taylor

text: I have little left in myself -- I must have you. The world may laugh -- may call me absurd, selfish -- but it does not signify. My very soul demands you: it will be satisfied, or it will take deadly vengeance on its frame.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: The future for me is already a thing of the past -You were my first love and you will be my last
author: Bob Dylan

text: No human relation gives one possession in another—every two souls are absolutely different. In friendship or in love, the two side by side raise hands together to find what one cannot reach alone.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: If you spend your time hoping someone will suffer the consequences for what they did to your heart, then you're allowing them to hurt you a second time in your mind.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: One ought to hold on to one's heart; for if one lets it go, one soon loses control of the head too.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Life is a series of pulls back and forth... A tension of opposites, like a pull on a rubber band. Most of us live somewhere in the middle. A wrestling match...Which side win? Love wins. Love always wins
author: Mitch Albom

text: I mean, at the end of the day, what the hell does it matter who I end up with if it can't be you?
author: Tabitha Suzuma

text: Kissing - and I mean like, yummy, smacking kissing - is the most delicious, most beautiful and passionate thing that two people can do, bar none. Better than sex, hands down.
author: Drew Barrymore

text: I want to try making things right because picking up the pieces is way better than leaving them the way they are.
author: Simone Elkeles

text: Each relationship between two persons is absolutely unique. That is why you cannot love two people the same. It simply is not possible. You love each person differently because of who they are and the uniqueness that they draw out of you.
author: William Paul Young

text: But if I hadn't fallen, I wouldn't have met you.
author: Becca Fitzpatrick

text: Let me die the moment my love dies. Let me not outlive my own capacity to love. Let me die still loving, and so, never die.
author: Mary Zimmerman

text: And all I loved, I loved alone.
author: Edgar Allan Poe

text: The power of finding beauty in the humblest things makes home happy and life lovely.
author: Louisa May Alcott

text: Love makes you want to be a better man—right, right. But maybe love, real love, also gives you permission to just be the man you are.
author: Gillian Flynn

text: Stephen kissed me in the spring,Robin in the fall,But Colin only looked at meAnd never kissed at all.Stephen’s kiss was lost in jest,Robin’s lost in play,But the kiss in Colin’s eyesHaunts me night and day.
author: Sara Teasdale

text: I'm a little pencil in the hand of a writing God, who is sending a love letter to the world.
author: Mother Teresa

text: Moving on is easy. It's staying moved on that's trickier.
author: Katerina Stoykova Klemer

text: Though lovers be lost, love shall not; And death shall have no dominion.
author: Dylan Thomas

text: Live your truth. Express your love. Share your enthusiasm. Take action towards your dreams. Walk your talk. Dance and sing to your music. Embrace your blessings. Make today worth remembering.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: Forever and ever, kid, until you're sick and tired of seeing me.
author: Marie Lu

text: Maybe there isn’t such a thing as fate. Maybe it’s just the opportunities we’re given, and what we do with them. I’m beginning to think that maybe great, epic romances don’t just happen. We have to make them ourselves.
author: Marissa Meyer

text: I miss him in so many ways, but right now I miss him in the way you always miss someone when you're single among a room full of couples.
author: Emily Giffin

text: Love is our true destiny. We do not find the meaning of life by ourselves alone - we find it with another.
author: Thomas Merton

text: How we need another soul to cling to, another body to keep us warm. To rest and trust; to give your soul in confidence: I need this, I need someone to pour myself into.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: If I were a tree, I would have no reason to love a human.
author: Maggie Stiefvater

text: The story of life is quicker than the wink of an eye, the story of love is hello and goodbye...until we meet again
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: Christian, you are the state lottery, the cure for cancer, and the three wishes from Aladdin's lamp all rolled into one
author: E.L. James

text: Each day brings new opportunities, allowing you to constantly live with love—be there for others—bring a little light into someone's day. Be grateful and live each day to the fullest.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: I knew they would kill me when they found out, but…” He struggled for words, releasing a sharp breath. “I think I realized that I would rather die because I betrayed them, than live because I betrayed you.
author: Marissa Meyer

text: He suddenly recalled from Plato's Symposium: People were hermaphrodites until God split then in two, and now all the halves wander the world over seeking one another. Love is the longing for the half of ourselves we have lost.
author: Milan Kundera

text: If I know what love is, it is because of you.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: Don’t sacrifice yourself too much, because if you sacrifice too much there’s nothing else you can give and nobody will care for you.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: To love someone means to see them as God intended them.
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

text: Where there is great love, there are always miracles.
author: Willa Cather

text: Did I really want to stay on this road longer, knowing it was only going to end in devastation?
author: Becca Fitzpatrick

text: I have seen the best of you, and the worst of you, and I choose both.
author: Sarah Kay

text: One of the things that makes me who I am is the loyalty I have to people I hold close to my heart.
author: Simone Elkeles

text: Love is the strangest, most illogical thing in the world.
author: Jennifer E. Smith

text: I don't love you anymore", she whispered. "I don't love you at all." His throat closed. "It's all right, sweetheart. I love you enough for both of us.
author: Susan Elizabeth Phillips

text: Weeping is not the same thing as crying. It takes your whole body to weep, and when it's over, you feel like you don't have any bones left to hold you up.
author: Sarah Ockler

text: In the darkness, two shadows, reaching through the hopeless, heavy dusk. Their hands meet, and light spills in a flood like a hundred golden urns pouring out of the sun.
author: Madeline Miller

text: To love means loving the unlovable. To forgive means pardoning the unpardonable. Faith means believing the unbelievable. Hope means hoping when everything seems hopeless.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: They're in love. Fuck the war.
author: Thomas Pynchon

text: I wish you could be kissed, Jane,' he said. 'Because I would beg just one off you. Under all this.' He flailed an arm toward the stars.
author: Maggie Stiefvater

text: You’ll never be able to find yourself if you’re lost in someone else.
author: Colleen Hoover

text: It's the kind of kiss that inspires stars to climb into the sky and light up the world.
author: Tahereh Mafi

text: You are my heart, my life, my entire existence." -Ash
author: Julie Kagawa

text: True love is the best thing in the world, except for cough drops.
author: William Goldman

text: Maybe I was destined to forever fall in love with people I couldn’t have. Maybe there’s a whole assortment of impossible people waiting for me to find them. Waiting to make me feel the same impossibility over and over again.
author: Carol Rifka Brunt

text: Woman is sacred the woman one loves is holy.
author: Alexandre Dumas

text: There is no intensity of love or feeling that does not involve the risk of crippling hurt. It is a duty to take this risk, to love and feel without defense or reserve.
author: William S. Burroughs

text: People should fall in love with their eyes closed.
author: Andy Warhol

text: Actually that’s my secret — I can’t even talk about you to anybody because I don’t want any more people to know how wonderful you are.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: If you don't receive love from the ones who are meant to love you, you will never stop looking for it.
author: Robert Goolrick

text: You’re just another story I can’t tell anymore.
author: pleasefindthis

text: There is nothing that is going to make people hate you more, and love you more, than telling the truth.
author: Stefan Molyneux

text: A fit, healthy body—that is the best fashion statement
author: Jess C. Scott

text: When you forgive, you love. And when you love, God’s light shines upon you.
author: Jon Krakauer

text: But when I do feel all the strength go out of me, and I fall to my knees beside the table and I think I cry, then, or at least I want to, and everything inside me screams for just one more kiss, one more word, one more glance, one more.
author: Veronica Roth

text: You’ve changed me forever. And I’ll never forget you.
author: Kiera Cass

text: Are you upset little friend? Have you been lying awake worrying? Well, don't worry...I'm here. The flood waters will recede, the famine will end, the sun will shine tomorrow, and I will always be here to take care of you.
author: Charles M. Schulz

text: The only creatures that are evolved enough to convey pure love are dogs and infants.
author: Johnny Depp

text: My heart only ever had one thought, one want. One need. Despite all, in spite of all...All my heart has ever wanted is you.
author: Stephanie Laurens

text: Choose your love. Love your choice.
author: Thomas S. Monson

text: The only love that I really believe in is a mother’s love for her children.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: As lovers, the difference between men and women is that women can love all day long, but men only at times.
author: W. Somerset Maugham

text: Love is scary: it changes it can go away. That's the part of the risk. I don't want to be scared anymore.
author: Jenny Han

text: Then I discovered that being related is no guarantee of love!
author: Stieg Larsson

text: Damn you, spoilt creature; I shan’t make you love me any the more by giving myself away like this.
author: Vita Sackville-West

text: Love is a serious mental disease.
author: Plato

text: Life is funny. Things change, people change, but you will always be you, so stay true to yourself and never sacrifice who you are for anyone.
author: Zayn Malik

text: some moments are nice, some arenicer, some are even worthwritingabout.
author: Charles Bukowski

text: You're gorgeous, you old hag, and if I could give you just one gift ever for the rest of your life it would be this. Confidence. It would be the gift of confidence. Either that or a scented candle
author: David Nicholls

text: Do I love you because you're beautiful, or are you beautiful because I love you?
author: Richard Rodgers

text: For one human being to love another human being: that is perhaps the most difficult task that has been given to us, the ultimate, the final problem and proof, the work for which all other work is merely preparation.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: If you truly want to be respected by people you love, you must prove to them that you can survive without them.
author: Michael Bassey Johnson

text: You don’t take away my choices. You are my choice.
author: Colleen Houck

text: My soul sees its equal in you.
author: Renee Ahdieh

text: Love is being honest with yourself at all timesbeing honest with the other person at all timestelling, listening, respecting the truthand never pretendingLove is the source of reality
author: Susan Polis Schutz

text: I can't set my hopes too high,'Cause every hello ends with a goodbye.
author: Demi Lovato

text: When you love someone, its never over. You move on, because you have to but you take them with you in your heart
author: Elizabeth Chandler

text: A kiss is a lovely trick designed by nature to stop speech when words become superfluous.
author: Ingrid Bergman

text: I want love to conquer all. But love can't conquer anything. It can't do anything on it's own.It relies on us to do the conquering on its behalf.
author: David Levithan

text: I am certain of nothing but the holiness of the Heart's affections and the truth of the Imagination.
author: John Keats

text: Of all our games, love's play is the only one which threatens to unsettle the soul...
author: Marguerite Yourcenar

text: I wish you to know that you have been the last dream of my soul.
author: Charles Dickens

text: This is what happened in love. One of you cried a lot and then both of you grew sarcastic.
author: Lorrie Moore

text: Limitless undying love which shines around me like a million suns it calls me on and on across the universe.
author: John Lennon

text: you're an expert at sorry and keeping the lines blurry
author: Taylor Swift

text: I love her, and that's the beginning and end of everything.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: What about when someone doesn't need a shoulder? What if they need the arms?
author: Stephen Chbosky

text: If you love something so much let it go. If it comes back it was meant to be
author: if it doesn't it never was

text: I want everything with you, America. I want the holidays and the birthdays, the busy season and lazy weekends. I want peanut butter fingertips on my desk. I want inside jokes and fights and everything. I want a life with you.
author: Kiera Cass

text: I will follow you to the ends of the world.
author: Khaled Hosseini

text: He did not care if she was heartless, vicious and vulgar, stupid and grasping, he loved her. He would rather have misery with one than happiness with the other.
author: W. Somerset Maugham

text: And I wondered if love was too weak a word for what he felt, what he’d done for me. For what I felt for him.
author: Sarah J. Maas

text: I love you,’ he whispered, and kissed my brow. ‘Thorns and all.
author: Sarah J. Maas

text: The saddest thing about love, Joe, is that not only the love cannot last forever, but even the heartbreak is soon forgotten.
author: William Faulkner

text: I love you enough to never make you choose.
author: Katie McGarry

text: Wait. You think I'm going to die? That's why you slept with me?
author: Richelle Mead

text: For it was not into my ear you whispered, but into my heart. It was not my lips you kissed, but my soul.
author: Judy Garland

text: It is an absolute human certainty that no one can know his own beauty or perceive a sense of his own worth until it has been reflected back to him in the mirror of another loving, caring human being.
author: John Joseph Powell

text: Until one has loved an animal, a part of one's soul remains unawakened.
author: Anatole France

text: But grief makes a monster out of us sometimes . . . and sometimes you say and do things to the people you love that you can't forgive yourself for.
author: Melina Marchetta

text: You may choose to look the other way but you can never say again that you did not know.
author: William Wilberforce

text: I don't want to hurt you or anybody so please forget about me. Just try. Find yourself a better friend.
author: Nina LaCour

text: As your lover describes you, so you are.
author: Jeanette Winterson

text: Sometimes we love with nothing more than hope. Sometimes we cry with everything except tears.
author: Gregory David Roberts

text: Be the one who nurtures and builds. Be the one who has an understanding and a forgiving heart one who looks for the best in people. Leave people better than you found them.
author: Marvin J. Ashton

text: At the end of the day it's about how much you can bear, how much you can endure. Being together, we harm nobody; being apart, we extinguish ourselves.
author: Tabitha Suzuma

text: First best is falling in love. Second best is being in love. Least best is falling out of love. But any of it is better than never having been in love.
author: Maya Angelou

text: To the world you may be one person but to one person you may be the world.
author: Bill Wilson

text: When is a monster not a monster? Oh, when you love it.
author: Caitlyn Siehl

text: If you could only love enough, you could be the most powerful person in the world.
author: Emmet Fox

text: I drive around the streetsan inch away from weeping,ashamed of my sentimentality andpossible love.
author: Charles Bukowski

text: Would you destroy Something perfect in order to make it beautiful?
author: Gerard Way

text: Don't ever think I fell for you, or fell over you. I didn't fall in love, I rose in it.
author: Toni Morrison

text: Dare to love yourselfas if you were a rainbowwith gold at both ends.
author: Aberjhani

text: He loved her, of course, but better than that, he chose her, day after day. Choice: that was the thing.
author: Sherman Alexie

text: Living with him is like being told a perpetual story: his mind is the biggest, most imaginative I have ever met. I could live in its growing countries forever.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: I don't care if you danced naked on the roof of the Little Palace with him. I love you, Alina, even the part of you that loved him.
author: Leigh Bardugo

text: Missing someone, they say, is self-centered.I self-center you more than ever.
author: Sasa Stanisic

text: The way you walk, way you talk, way you say my name; it's beautiful, wonderful, don't you ever change.
author: Taylor Swift

text: Neither love nor evil conquers all, but evil cheats more.
author: Laurell K. Hamilton

text: When people fall in love, they burst into flames.
author: Jandy Nelson

text: So I placed my heart under lock and key To take some time, and take care of me But I turn around and you're standing here
author: Deborah Cox

text: Love is not a maybe thing, you know when you love someone.
author: Lauren Conrad

text: Did you think you could stop me? I'll burn the world down to save her.
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout

text: If I have learned anything in this long life of mine, it is this: in love we find out who we want to be; in war we find out who we are.
author: Kristin Hannah

text: Even on my weakest daysI get a little bit stronger
author: Sara Evans

text: Would he find her?Without question.Would he save her?Always.
author: Lauren Kate

text: To fall in love with God is the greatest romance; to seek him the greatest adventure; to find him, the greatest human achievement.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: Love Jo all your days, if you choose, but don't let it spoil you, for it's wicked to throw away so many good gifts because you can't have the one you want.
author: Louisa May Alcott

text: You were you,and I was I;we were twobefore our time.I was yoursbefore I knew,and you have always been mine too.
author: Lang Leav

text: A heart filled with anger has no room for love.
author: Joan Lunden

text: I didn't want to kiss you goodbye — that was the trouble — I wanted to kiss you good night — and there's a lot of difference.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: When he died, all things soft and beautiful and bright would be buried with him.
author: Madeline Miller

text: Failed relationships can be described as so much wasted make-up.
author: Marian Keyes

text: To be brave is to love someone unconditionally, without expecting anything in return. To just give. That takes courage, because we don't want to fall on our faces or leave ourselves open to hurt.
author: Madonna

text: There are so many things that demand to be said. Where did you go? Do you ever think about me? You've ruined me. Are you okay? But of course, I can't say any of that.
author: Gayle Forman

text: You cannot be with someone just because you don’t want to hurt him. You have your own happiness to think about.
author: Melissa de la Cruz

text: I don't like it when I outweigh my men.
author: Patricia Briggs

text: I'll find you. There is no place in this world you can hide from me. I'm watching.Love, Dimitri
author: Richelle Mead

text: Last time I saw you, I said that it hurt too much to love you. But I was wrong about that. The truth is it hurts too much not to love you.
author: P.C. Cast

text: To love is to recognize yourself in another.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: You can live your whole life not realizing that what you're looking for is right in front of you.
author: David Nicholls

text: It does not matter how long you are spending on the earth, how much money you have gathered or how much attention you have received. It is the amount of positive vibration you have radiated in life that matters,
author: Amit Ray

text: When you love you wish to do things for. You wish to sacrifice for. You wish to serve.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: Oh, love isn't there to make us happy. I believe it exists to show us how much we can endure.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: A busy, vibrant, goal-oriented woman is so much more attractive than a woman who waits around for a man to validate her existence.
author: Mandy Hale

text: How do you know when it's over?" "Maybe when you feel more in love with your memories than with the person standing in front of you.
author: Gunnar Ardelius

text: And you can't love, not fully, unless you are loved in return.
author: Lauren Oliver

text: And I can fight only for something that I love, love only what I respect, and respect only what I at least know.
author: Adolf Hitler

text: The truest form of love is how you behave toward someone, not how you feel about them.
author: Steve Hall

text: The fact is…I’m in love with you, and I have been for some time.
author: Colleen Houck

text: He sighed and then focused his eyes right on me. It was like drowning, drowning in seas of green. There was nothing in the world except for those eyes."I want to kiss you, Rose," he said softly. "And I want you to want me too.
author: Richelle Mead

text: Anne laughed."I don't want sunbursts or marble halls, I just want you.
author: L.M. Montgomery

text: I'm the kind of boy that can fall in love with any girl because I love with the heart, not the eyes.
author: Niall Horan

text: If having a soul means being able to feel love and loyalty and gratitude, then animals are better off than a lot of humans.
author: James Herriot

text: Just because I'm not forever by your side doesn't mean that's not precisely where I want to be.
author: Stephanie Laurens

text: Can officially confirm that the way to a man's heart these days is not through beauty, food, sex, or alluringness of character, but merely the ability to seem not very interested in him.
author: Helen Fielding

text: She's my kitten, and no one else's.
author: Jeaniene Frost

text: Work without love is slavery.
author: Mother Teresa

text: Then he made one last effort to search in his heart for the place where his affection had rotted away, and he could not find it.
author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez

text: Love, like fire, goes out without fuel.
author: Mikhail Lermontov

text: Love, when spelled backwards and read phonetically, reads evil
author: Eminem

text: I threw myself into that fire, threw myself into it, into him, and let myself burn.
author: Sarah J. Maas

text: If somebody says 'I love you' to me, I feel as though I had a pistol pointed at my head. What can anybody reply under such conditions but that which the pistol holder requires? 'I love you, too'.
author: Kurt Vonnegut Jr.

text: But you're the only one, Scarlet. You'll always be the only one.
author: Marissa Meyer

text: If you love deeply, you're going to get hurt badly. But it's still worth it.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: …There is the heat of Love, the pulsing rush of Longing, the lover’s whisper, irresistible—magic to make the sanest man go mad.
author: Homer

text: There can be no deep disappointment where there is not deep love.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Why is there ever this perverse cruelty in humankind, that makes us hurt most those we love best?
author: Jacqueline Carey

text: There comes a time in your life when you have to choose to turn the page, write another book or simply close it.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Often it is the most deserving people who cannot help loving those who destroy them.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: You know that place between sleep and awake, that place where you still remember dreaming? That’s where I’ll always love you. That’s where I’ll be waiting.
author: J.M. Barrie

text: After all, damn it, what does being in love mean if you can't trust a person.
author: Evelyn Waugh

text: And in the end, we were all just humans.. drunk on the idea that love, only love, could heal our brokenness.
author: Christopher Poindexter

text: You can obsess and obsess over how things ended—what you did wrong or could have done differently—but there's not much of a point. It's not like it'll change anything. So really, why worry?
author: Jess Rothenberg

text: You asked why I couldn't forgive you," Nick said, very quietly, and I jumped a little. "It was because you were the love of my life, Harper. And you didn't want to be. That's hard to let go.
author: Kristan Higgins

text: Sex without love is a meaningless experience, but as far as meaningless experiences go its pretty damn good.
author: Woody Allen

text: His breath hitched, and he regarded me hungrily. “You’re playing with fire, you know that?” “That’s weird, considering you’re an ice prin—” I didn’t get any further, as Ash leaned in and kissed me.
author: Julie Kagawa

text: Blood of my Blood," he whispered, "and bone of my bone. You carry me within ye, Claire, and ye canna leave me now, no matter what happens, You are mine, always, if ye will it or no, if ye want me or nay. Mine, and I wilna let ye go.
author: Diana Gabaldon

text: Love is always open arms. If you close your arms about love you will find that you are left holding only yourself.
author: Leo Buscaglia

text: Love is a promise, love is a souvenir, once given never forgotten, never let it disappear.
author: John Lennon

text: You made me feel alive again.
author: Julie Kagawa

text: It is a defect of God's humor that he directs our hearts everywhere but to those who have a right to them.
author: Tom Stoppard

text: The minute I stopped trying to find the right girl, and started trying to become the right guy...the girl came.
author: Jonathan Antin
