text: Don't go looking for boys in the darkThey will say pretty things thenleave you with scars.Do go looking for boys in the parkFor that is where the true gentlemen are.
author: Anna Godbersen

text: Every normal man must be tempted, at times, to spit on his hands, hoist the black flag, and begin slitting throats.
author: H.L. Mencken

text: Everybody is a main character to someone...
author: Amy Harmon

text: A true gentleman is one that apologizes anyways, even though he has not offended a lady intentionally. He is in a class all of his own because he knows the value of a woman's heart.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Love one another, but make not a bond of love:Let it rather be a moving sea between the shores of your souls.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: Unrequited love is the infinite curse of a lonely heart.
author: Christina Westover

text: I am the most miserable person who ever lived," he said... "You are young, and in love," said Primus. "Every young man in your position is the most miserable young man who ever lived.
author: Neil Gaiman

text: Only time can heal your broken heart. Just as only time can heal his broken arms and legs.
author: Jim Henson

text: Before, I wanted to say: "I found love!" But now, I want to say: "I found a person. And he belongs to me and I belong to him.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: The eye through which I see God is the same eye through which God sees me; my eye and God's eye are one eye, one seeing, one knowing, one love.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: I do my best thinking at night when everyone else is sleeping. No interruptions. No noise. I like the feeling of being awake when no one else is.
author: Jennifer Niven

text: Why are you leaving me?He wrote, I do not know how to live.I do not know either but I am trying.I do not know how to try.There were some things I wanted to tell him. But I knew they would hurt him. So i buried them and let them hurt me
author: Jonathan Safran Foer

text: You were the missing piece of my soul, the breath in my lungs, and the blood in my veins.
author: J.A. Redmerski

text: Sometimes I feel like we're a knot, too tangled to be taken apart.
author: Kiera Cass

text: I knew I loved you before I met youI think I dreamed you into lifeI knew I loved you before I met youI have been waiting all my life
author: Savage Garden

text: I feel like a part of my soul has loved you since the beginning of everything.Maybe we’re from the same star.
author: Emery Allen

text: You looove me. (holds out arms) You love me this much.
author: James Patterson

text: I’m here not because I am supposed to be here, or because I’m trapped here, but because I’d rather be with you than anywhere else in the world.
author: Richard Bach

text: He looked so lost, so soulful, so lonely. I wanted him to kiss me now. I wanted to let him know I was his for all eternity.
author: Ellen Schreiber

text: I want you to be weak. As weak as I am.
author: Milan Kundera

text: Each time you happen to me all over again.
author: Edith Wharton

text: There is no time for cut-and-dried monotony. There is time for work. And time for love. That leaves no other time.
author: Coco Chanel

text: What I cannot love, I overlook.
author: Anais Nin

text: A mighty pain to love it is,And 't is a pain that pain to miss;But of all pains, the greatest painIt is to love, but love in vain.
author: Abraham Cowley

text: It's a strange thing to discover and to believe that you are loved when you know that there is nothing in you for anybody but a parent or a God to love.
author: Graham Greene

text: This time when we kiss, I feel it in the pit of my stomach, I feel it in my heart. And I realize love isn't about sex. It's about connection.
author: Ellen Hopkins

text: If you love someone but rarely make yourself available to him or her, that is not true love.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Would you mind repeating that? I'm afraid I might have lost my wits altogether and just hallucinated what I've longed to hear.
author: Jeaniene Frost

text: Loving someone is giving them the power to break your heart, but trusting them not to.
author: Julianne Moore

text: Autumn is the hardest season. The leaves are all falling, and they're falling likethey're falling in love with the ground.
author: Andrea Gibson

text: Close your eyes and I'll kiss you, Tomorrow I'll miss you.
author: Paul McCartney

text: Things that matter are not easy. Feelings of happiness are easy. Happiness is not. Flirting is easy. Love is not. Saying you’re friends is easy. Being friends is not.
author: David Levithan

text: He was my North, my South, my East and West,My working week and my Sunday rest,My noon, my midnight, my talk, my song;I thought that love would last forever: I was wrong.
author: W.H. Auden

text: That's how you know you love someone, I guess, when you can't experience anything without wishing the other person were there to see it, too.
author: Kaui Hart Hemmings

text: That's when you know for sure somebody loves you. They figure out what you need and they give it to you -- without you asking.
author: Adriana Trigiani

text: When you feel someone else's pain and joy as powerfully as if it were your own, then you know you really loved them.
author: Ann Brashares

text: When God knows you're ready for the responsibility of commitment, He'll reveal the right person under the right circumstances.
author: Joshua Harris

text: ..."better to have loved and lost" bullshit. Don't show me paradise and then burn it down.
author: Harlan Coben

text: I desire to be with you. I miss you. I feel lonely when I can't see you. I am obsessed with you, fascinated by you, infatuated with you. I hunger for your taste, your smell, the feel of your soul touching mine.
author: Jack Llawayllynn

text: Annabeth pressed her lips to Percy’s ear. “I love you.”She wasn’t sure he could hear her—but if they died, she wanted those to be her last words.
author: Rick Riordan

text: For women, the best aphrodisiacs are words. The G-spot is in the ears. He who looks for it below there is wasting his time.
author: Isabel Allende

text: They've got no idea what happiness is, they don't know that without this love there is no happiness or unhappiness for us--there is no life.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: Love cannot be forced, love cannot be coaxed and teased. It comes out of heaven, unasked and unsought.
author: Pearl S. Buck

text: Love is too precious to be ashamed of.
author: Laurell K. Hamilton

text: This I want to believe implicitly: Man was born for love and revolution.
author: Osamu Dazai

text: I will not wait to love as best as I can. We thought we were young and that there would be time to love well sometime in the future. This is a terrible way to think. It is no way to live, to wait to love.
author: Dave Eggers

text: I am my heart’s undertaker. Daily I go and retrieve its tattered remains, place them delicately into its little coffin, and bury it in the depths of my memory, only to have to do it all again tomorrow.
author: Emilie Autumn

text: Like some wines our love could neither mature nor travel.
author: Graham Greene

text: He was contemplation and enthusiasm. Ambition and strong coffee. I could have looked at him forever.
author: E. Lockhart

text: The heart can think of no devotionGreater than being shore to the ocean-Holding the curve of one position,Counting an endless repetition.
author: Robert Frost

text: So long as we love we serve; so long as we are loved by others, I would almost say that we are indispensable; and no man is useless while he has a friend.
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

text: Love is a striking example of how little reality means to us.
author: Marcel Proust

text: Hope for love, pray for love, wish for love, dream for love…but don’t put your life on hold waiting for love.
author: Mandy Hale

text: Do you believe a man can truly love a woman and constantly betray her?Never mind physically but betray her in his mind,in the very "poetry of his soul".Well,it's not easy but men do it all the time.
author: Mario Puzo

text: The more boys I meet the more I love my dog.
author: Carrie Underwood

text: I hug him tightly. “I can’t imagine my life without you, Christian. I love you so much it frightens me.” “Me, too,” he breathes. “My life would be empty without you. I love you so much.
author: E.L. James

text: Absence makes the heart grow fonder, but it sure makes the rest of you lonely.
author: Charles M. Schulz

text: Hate looks like everybody else until it smiles
author: Tahereh Mafi

text: and when all the wars are over, a butterfly will still be beautiful.
author: Ruskin Bond

text: Tears are words the heart can't express
author: Gerard Way

text: I think you can only be truly mad at someone you really love.
author: E.L. James

text: Wanting him to come back before anyone notices part of the world has not moved since he left.
author: Brian Andreas

text: Love is illogical, love had consequences--I did this to myself, and I should be able to take it.
author: Marie Lu

text: Your naked body should only belong to those who fall in love with your naked soul.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: I would love to saythat youmake meweak in the kneesbutto be quite upfrontand completelytruthfulyoumake my bodyforgetit has kneesat all.
author: Tyler Knott Gregson

text: Love must not entreat,' she added, 'or demand. Love must have the strength to become certain within itself. Then it ceases merely to be attracted and begins to attract.
author: Hermann Hesse

text: Falling in love you remain a child; rising in love you mature. By and by love becomes not a relationship, it becomes a state of your being. Not that you are in love - now you are love.
author: Osho

text: I wish I had a boyfriend. I wish he lived in the wardrobe on a coat hanger. Whenever I wanted, I could get him out and he'd look at me the way boys do in films, as if I'm beautiful.
author: Jenny Downham

text: I don't remember who said this, but there really are places in the heart you don't even know exist until you love a child.
author: Anne Lamott

text: Two words. Three vowels. Four constenants. Seven letters. It can either cut you open to the core and leave you in ungodly pain or it can free your soul and lift a tremendous weight off you shoulders. The phrase is: It's over.
author: Maggi Richard

text: Everyone always wants to know how you can tell when it's true love, and the answer is this: when the pain doesn't fade and the scars don't heal, and it's too damned late.
author: Jonathan Tropper

text: If you aren't the woman I think you are, then this isn't the world I thought it was.
author: Arthur Golden

text: You who suffer because you love, love still more. To die of love, is to live by it.
author: Victor Hugo

text: Sometimes two people have to fall apart, to realize how much they need to fall back together.
author: Colleen Hoover

text: Freely we serveBecause we freely love, as in our willTo love or not; in this we stand or fall.
author: John Milton

text: We come to love not by finding a perfect person, but by learning to see an imperfect person perfectly.
author: Sam Keen

text: I got my heart's desire, and there my troubles began.
author: Lev Grossman

text: Relationships are all about trust and equality. If one person shares, then the other person should share, too.
author: Sophie Kinsella

text: Life is a gift. Don't forget to live it.
author: Nicola Yoon

text: That night when you kissed me, I left a poem in your mouth, and you can hear some of the lines every time you breathe out.
author: Andrea Gibson

text: Love who you love while you have them. That's all you can do. Let them go when you must. If you know how to love, you'll never run out.
author: Ann Brashares

text: Were you always such a stubborn, blind, obtuse girl?”“Are you calling me stupid?”“Yes, but in a more poetic way!”“Well, here’s a poem for you. Get lost!
author: Colleen Houck

text: Know that love is truly timeless.
author: Mary M. Ricksen

text: I want someone who is fierce and will love me until death and knows that love is as strong as death, and be on my side forever and ever. I want someone who will destroy and be destroyed by me.
author: Jeanette Winterson

text: Even when I'm dead, I'll swim through the Earth,like a mermaid of the soil, just to be next to your bones.
author: Jeffrey McDaniel

text: How could I have ever been ashamed of loving Dante Quintana?
author: Benjamin Alire Saenz

text: I have always found that actively lovingsaves one from a morbid preoccupationwith the shortcomings of society.
author: Alan Paton

text: You are the only one who has understood even a whisper of me, and I will tell you that I am the only person who has understood even a whisper of you.
author: Jonathan Safran Foer

text: I have so much in me, and the feeling for her absorbs it all; I have so much, and without her it all comes to nothing.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: In itself, homosexuality is as limiting as heterosexuality: the ideal should be to be capable of loving a woman or a man; either, a human being, without feeling fear, restraint, or obligation.
author: Simone de Beauvoir

text: We're all golden sunflowers inside.
author: Allen Ginsberg

text: Hope is not about proving anything. It's about choosing to believe this one thing, that love is bigger than any grim, bleak shit anyone can throw at us.
author: Anne Lamott

text: When you miss someone....it’s weird…your body doesn’t function normally..as it should. Because I miss you, and my heart…it’s not steady…my soul it sings numb. Fingers are cold…like you…your soul.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: The opposite of love is not hate, its apathy, when you simply don't bother about that person!
author: Amish Tripathi

text: Those whom we most love are often the most alien to us.
author: Christopher Paolini

text: Where you go, I shall go; where you die, I shall die, and there will I be buried.
author: Rosamund Hodge

text: Love makes us such fools.
author: Leslye Walton

text: With you, intimacy colours my voice.even ‘hello’ sounds like ‘come here'.
author: Warsan Shire

text: A friend of mine once defined love as finding someone you can talk to late into the night
author: James Patterson

text: And yet another moral occurs to me now: Make love when you can. It's good for you.
author: Kurt Vonnegut Jr.

text: Your kids require you most of all to love them for who they are, not to spend your whole time trying to correct them.
author: Bill Ayers

text: It’s strange how dreams get under your skin and give your heart a test for what’s real and what’s imaginary.
author: Jason Mraz

text: It is better to lose your pride with someone you love rather than to lose that someone you love with your useless pride.
author: John Ruskin

text: Submission is not about authority and it is not obedience
author: it is all about relationships of love and respect.

text: Cut the ending. Revise the script. The man of her dreams is a girl.
author: Julie Anne Peters

text: Most men claim to desire driven, independent and confident women. Yet when confronted with such a creature reverence often evolves into resent. For just like women, men need to be needed.
author: Tiffany Madison

text: Would you rather live one perfect day over and over or live your life with no perfect days but just decent ones?
author: Jenny Han

text: The words ‘I Love You’ kill, and resurrect millions, in less than a second.
author: Aberjhani

text: Love is like breathing. You take it in and let it out.
author: Wally Lamb

text: It ought to make us feel ashamed when we talk like we know what we're talking about when we talk about love.
author: Raymond Carver

text: Hate is... It's too easy. Love. Love takes courage.
author: Hannah Harrington

text: It's like I'm thirteen again and he's my crush. All I'm aware of in this entire roomful of people is him. Where he is, what he's doing, who he's talking to.
author: Sophie Kinsella

text: I love you because no two snowflakes are alike, and it is possible, if you stand tippy-toe, to walk between the raindrops.
author: Nikki Giovanni

text: When it comes to men, deal with them as they are, not how you’d like them to be.
author: Greg Behrendt

text: You can't hurt me the way you think you can. But even if you could? I would rather die with the taste of you on my tongue than live and never touch you again. I'm in love with you, Mara. I love you. No matter what you do.
author: Michelle Hodkin

text: I'm guilty of giving people more chances than they deserve but when I'm done, I'm done.
author: Turcois Ominek

text: Some feelings sink so deep into the heart that only loneliness can help you find them again. Some truths are so painful that only shame can help you live with them. Some things are so sad that only your soul can do the crying for them.
author: Gregory David Roberts

text: Do you really think anyone in this world, compares to you in my eyes?
author: S.C. Stephens

text: People wait around too long for love. I'm happy with all of my lusts!
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: Heart, we will forget him,You and I, tonight!You must forget the warmth he gave,I will forget the light.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: What's a fuck when what I want is love?
author: Henry Miller

text: You honestly expect me to breathe in a world without air?
author: Renee Ahdieh

text: I don't want to be somebody's crush. If somebody likes me, I want them to like the real me, not what they think I am.
author: Stephen Chbosky

text: Nobody ever gets what they want when it comes to love.
author: Scott Turow

text: It hurts to breathe. It hurts to live. I hate her, yet I do not think I can exist without her.
author: Charlotte Featherstone

text: I love you," I said, and stabbed him.
author: Sarah J. Maas

text: It's one thing to fall in love. It's another to feel someone else fall in love with you, and to feel a responsibility toward that love.
author: David Levithan

text: How can something so wrong feel so right?
author: Tabitha Suzuma

text: I don't think you can define love.
author: Harry Styles

text: I thought you were gone forever, I thought you’d walked away from everything, because I failed, because I destroyed the only thing that ever mattered to me. I waited for you to come, but you didn’t.
author: Alexandra Adornetto

text: Let me love you, but don't love me back. Do love me and let me hate you for a while. Let me feel like I have some control, because I know I never do.
author: Ann Brashares

text: Absence is to love what wind is to fire; it extinguishes the small, it inflames the great.
author: Roger de Rabutin de Bussy

text: Makakapili ka ng lugar na uupuan mo, pero hindi mo mapipili ang taong uupo sa puwang sa tabi mo... Ganyan ang senaryo sa bus. Ganyan din sa pag-ibig... Lalong 'di mo kontrolado kung kelan siya bababa.
author: Bob Ong

text: If equal affection cannot be,Let the more loving one be me.
author: W.H. Auden

text: Five minutes are enough to dream a whole life, that is how relative time is.
author: Mario Benedetti

text: This time I wouldn't forget him, because I couldn't ever forgive him - for breaking my heart twice.
author: James Patterson

text: Thought I couldn't live without youIt's gonna hurt when it heals tooEven though I really love youI'm gonna smile cause I deserve toQuickly I'm learning to love againAll I know is I'mma be ok
author: Leona Lewis

text: I'm not really sure why. But... do you stop loving someone just because they betray you? I don't think so. That's what makes the betrayal hurt so much - pain, frustration, anger... and I still loved her. I still do.
author: Brandon Sanderson

text: Well my music was different in high school; I was singing about love—you know, things I don't care about anymore.
author: Lady Gaga

text: If you have the woman you love, what more do you need? Well, besides an alibi for the time of her husband’s murder.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo

text: Love is like a friendship caught on fire. In the beginning a flame, very pretty, often hot and fierce, but still only light and flickering. As love grows older, our hearts mature and our love becomes as coals, deep-burning and unquenchable.
author: Bruce Lee

text: You know that when I hate you, it is because I love you to a point of passion that unhinges my soul.
author: Julie de Lespinasse

text: The only way love can last a lifetime is if it's unconditional. The truth is this: love is not determined by the one being loved but rather by the one choosing to love.
author: Stephen Kendrick

text: Someone once wrote that a novel should deliver a series of small astonishments. I get the same thing spending an hour with you.
author: E. Lockhart

text: Love God and He will enable you to love others even when they disappoint you.
author: Francine Rivers

text: It was brave," countered Issa. "It was rare. It was love, and it was beautiful.
author: Laini Taylor

text: You're like a grey sky. You're beautiful, even though you don't want to be.
author: Jasmine Warga

text: There will never be no love at all.
author: Bob Marley

text: I love you and it's getting worse.
author: Joseph E. Morris

text: I believe in love the verb, not the noun.
author: Greg Behrendt

text: Dogs have given us their absolute all. We are the center of their universe. We are the focus of their love and faith and trust. They serve us in return for scraps. It is without a doubt the best deal man has ever made.
author: Roger A. Caras

text: I love you, in my mind where my thoughts reside, in my heart where my emotions live, and in my soul where my dreams are born. I love you.
author: Dee Henderson

text: A kiss on the beach when there is a full moon is the closest thing to heaven.
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

text: I...will never...regret you.
author: Colleen Hoover

text: She did not believe he could have really gone, because for her, to leave the person you loved was impossible.
author: Jodi Lynn Anderson

text: I crossed a thousand leagues to come to you, and lost the best part of me along the way. Don't tell me to leave.
author: George R.R. Martin

text: Confession is not betrayal. What you say or do doesn't matter only feelings matter. If they could make me stop loving you-that would be the real betrayal.
author: George Orwell

text: Those who really love you don't mean to hurt you and if they do, you can't see it in their eyes but it hurts them too.
author: Holly Black

text: Lover? I don't know. I don't know if she loves me. I don't know if I love her. All I can say is, she's the one I think about. All the time. She's the voice I want to hear. She's the face I hope to see.
author: Sophie Kinsella

text: Let me wake up next to you, have coffee in the morning and wander through the city with your hand in mine, and I'll be happy for the rest of my fucked up little life.
author: Charlotte Eriksson

text: I don't understand the point of being together if you're not the happiest.
author: Gillian Flynn

text: As the light begins to intensify, so does my misery, and I wonder how it is possible to hurt so much when nothing is wrong.
author: Tabitha Suzuma

text: Love is honesty. Love is a mutual respect for one another.
author: Simone Elkeles

text: I have been loving you a little more every minute since this morning.
author: Victor Hugo

text: How could I be sleeping with this particular man.... Surely only true love could justify my lack of taste.
author: Margaret Atwood

text: Don't marry a rich man. Marry a good man. He will spend his life trying to keep you happy. No rich man can buy that!
author: Staness Jonekos

text: He offered her the world. She said she had her own.
author: Monique Duval

text: The right thing at the wrong tme is the wrong thing.
author: Joshua Harris

text: She was bored. She loved, had capacity to love, for love, to give and accept love. Only she tried twice and failed twice to find somebody not just strong enough to deserve it, earn it, match it, but even brave enough to accept it.
author: William Faulkner

text: Sometimes I sit alone under the stars and think of the galaxies inside my heart and truly wonder if anyone will ever want to make sense of all that I am
author: Christopher Poindexter

text: Love is a rebellious bird that nobody can tame.
author: Veronica Rossi

text: I don't let nobody see me wishin' he was mine
author: Taylor Swift

text: There are only two reasons why you leave someone you're still in love with - either it's the right thing to do, or it's the only thing to do.
author: Tiffany Reisz

text: If a writer falls in love with you, you can never die.
author: Mik Everett

text: When you know my love, my love will warm you.
author: Ruby Dee

text: Someday, after mastering the winds, the waves, the tides and gravity, we shall harness for God the energies of love, and then, for a second time in the history of the world, man will have discovered fire.
author: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin

text: Love is—a shade of what I feel.
author: Renee Ahdieh

text: People die, I think, but your relationship with them doesn't. It continues and is ever-changing.
author: Jandy Nelson

text: Love is the only force capable of transforming an enemy to a friend.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: I imagine a line, a white line, painted on the sand and on the ocean, from me to you.
author: Jonathan Safran Foer

text: We are not held back by the love we didn't receive in the past, but by the love we're not extending in the present.
author: Marianne Williamson

text: Love is a snowmobile racing across the tundra and then suddenly it flips over, pinning you underneath. At night, the ice weasels come.
author: Matt Groening

text: I need the thing that happens when your brain shuts off and your heart turns on.
author: Elizabeth Wurtzel

text: Every day People straighten up the hair, why not the heart?
author: Ernesto Che Guevara

text: A weed is but an unloved flower.
author: Ella Wheeler Wilcox

text: Stronger than lover's love is lover's hate. Incurable, in each, the wounds they make.
author: Euripides

text: I never want to be away from you again, except at work, in the restroom or when one of us is at a movie the other does not want to see.
author: Lemony Snicket

text: The whole image is that eternal suffering awaits anyone who questions God's infinite love. That's the message we're brought up with, isn't it? Believe or die! Thank you, forgiving Lord, for all those options.
author: Bill Hicks

text: He drew a circle that shut me out-Heretic , rebel, a thing to flout.But love and I had the wit to win:We drew a circle and took him In !From the poem " Outwitted
author: Edwin Markham

text: To find out if she really loved me, I hooked her up to a lie detector. And just as I suspected, my machine was broken.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo

text: A truth should exist,it should not be usedlike this. If I love youis that a fact or a weapon?
author: Margaret Atwood

text: ...I told him a story of two people. Two people who shouldn't have met, and who didn't like each other much when they did, but who found they were the only two people in the world who could possibly have understood each other.
author: Jojo Moyes

text: The only queer people are those who don't love anybody.
author: Rita Mae Brown

text: You have my whole heart. You always did.
author: Cormac McCarthy

text: You get use to someone—start to like them, even—and they leave. In the end, everyone leaves.
author: Rachel Ward

text: The Heart wants what it wants - or else it does not care
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Wisdom is knowing I am nothing,Love is knowing I am everything,and between the two my life moves.
author: Nisargadatta Maharaj

text: Love is a possible strength in an actual weakness.
author: Thomas Hardy

text: Hatred would have been easier. With hatred, I would have known what to do. Hatred is clear, metallic, one-handed, unwavering; unlike love.
author: Margaret Atwood

text: It shattered something inside me that hadn't been broken before.
author: E. Lockhart

text: You have to, take a deep breath. and allow the music to flow through you. Revel in it, allow yourself to awe. When you play allow the music to break your heart with its beauty.
author: Kelly White

text: A man in love can do extraordinary things, I don’t care if you’re an angel, you’re my angel, and I won’t let you go.
author: Alexandra Adornetto

text: When you find love, you take it. You grab it with both hands and you do everything in your power not to let it go. You can’t just walk away from it and expect it to linger until you’re ready for it.
author: Colleen Hoover

text: Men build too many walls and not enough bridges.
author: Joseph Fort Newton

text: A woman should soften but not weaken a man.
author: Sigmund Freud

text: Because isn’t that the point of every relationship: to be known by someone else, to be understood? He gets me. She gets me. Isn’t that the simple magic phrase?
author: Gillian Flynn

text: sometimes no matter how many eyelashes or dandelion seeds you blow, no matter how much of your heart you tear out and slap on your sleeve, it just ain't gonna happen.
author: Melissa Jensen

text: Love me like you'll never see me again.
author: Alicia Keys

text: A best friend is the only one that walks into your life when the world has walked out.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: I have loved you all my life, Mal," I whispered through my tears. "There is no end to our story.
author: Leigh Bardugo

text: When you fish for love, bait with your heart, not your brain.
author: Mark Twain

text: That’s love: Two lonely persons keep each other safe and touch each other and talk to each other.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Just because love don't look the way you think it should, don't mean you don't have it.
author: Leslye Walton

text: She was the kind of girlfriend God gives you young, so you'll know loss the rest of your life.
author: Junot Diaz

text: I want YOU to be the one wanting me first. Pushing me first. Kissing me first. Don't be careful with me," he said. "Because I won't be careful with you.
author: Michelle Hodkin

text: Love takes off the masks we fear we cannot live without and know we cannot live within.
author: James     Baldwin

text: Before I fellin love with words,with setting skies and singing birds—it was you I fellin love with first.
author: Lang Leav

text: I wanted to be his life preserver, the thing that would keep him afloat. Instead, he became my anchor. And I’m tired of drowning.
author: Amanda Grace

text: If love is blind, then maybe a blind person that loves has a greater understanding of it.
author: Criss Jami

text: You might be looking for reasons but there are no reasons.
author: Nina LaCour

text: The truth was, he now belonged only to my past, and it was time I begin to accept it, as much as it hurt to do so.
author: Tammara Webber

text: What if I were smiling and running into your arms? Would you see then what I see now?
author: Jon Krakauer

text: What is love? two souls and one flesh friendship? two bodies and one soul.
author: Joseph Roux

text: If it weren't for music, I would think that love is mortal.
author: Mark Helprin

text: He's around the twist,' said Azalea. 'Breaking all the windows? He's mad.' to break all the windows in the house and drown yourself in a bucket but don't actually do it, well, that's love.
author: Heather Dixon

text: People do the damndest things when they’re in love.
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout

text: Making love to me is amazing. Wait, I meant: making love, to me, is amazing. The absence of two little commas nearly transformed me into a sex god.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo

text: Never, never, never. I am never going to forget you.
author: Alexandra Bracken

text: True beauty is something that attacks, overpowers, robs, and finally destroys.
author: Yukio Mishima

text: Never forget that justice is what love looks like in public.
author: Cornel West

text: And the story of love is a long sad tale ending in graves.
author: Jack Kerouac

text: And if you couldn't be loved, the next best thing was to be let alone.
author: L.M. Montgomery

text: All the most powerful emotions come from chaos -fear,anger,love- especially love. Love is chaos itself. Think about it! Love makes no sense. It shakes you up and spins you around. And then, eventually , it falls apart.
author: Kirsten Miller

text: Sometimes good things fall apart, so better things can fall together.
author: Jessica Howell

text: Define yourself radically as one beloved by God. This is the true self. Every other identity is illusion.
author: Brennan Manning

text: Love is hard to find, hard to keep, and hard to forget.
author: Alysha Speer

text: Looking at beauty in the world, is the first step of purifying the mind.
author: Amit Ray

text: The first stab of love is like a sunset, a blaze of color -- oranges, pearly pinks, vibrant purples...
author: Anna Godbersen

text: Learn to deal with the fact that you are not a perfect person but you are a person that deserves respect and honesty.
author: Pandora Poikilos

text: Whoever loves becomes humble. Those who love have , so to speak , pawned a part of their narcissism.
author: Sigmund Freud

text: If I had a camera,' I said, 'I'd take a picture of you every day. That way I'd remember how you looked every single day of your life.
author: Nicole Krauss

text: The only way of knowing a person is to love them without hope.
author: Walter Benjamin

text: Better than chocolate, being with you last night. Silly me, I thought that nothing was better than chocolate.
author: E. Lockhart

text: Living simply makes loving simple.
author: bell hooks

text: Love the trees until their leaves fall off, then encourage them to try again next year.
author: Chad Sugg

text: Physics isn't the most important thing. Love is.
author: Richard Feynman

text: A warrior does not give up what he loves, he finds the love in what he does
author: Dan Millman

text: And Max, the king of all wild things, was lonely and wanted to be where someone loved him best of all.
author: Maurice Sendak

text: Your absence has not taught me to be alone, it merely has shown that when together we cast a single shadow on the wall.
author: Doug Fetherling

text: Remember how it was when we kissed? Armfuls and armfuls of light thrown right at us. A rope dropping down from the sky. How can the word love and the word life even fit in the mouth?
author: Jandy Nelson

text: Perfect love is the most beautiful of all frustrations because it is more than one can express.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: Who saidI can't wear my ConverseWith my dress, well babyThat's just me!
author: Demi Lovato

text: I believe that every single event in life happens in an opportunity to choose love over fear.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: I cannot compromise my respect for your love. You can keep your love, I will keep my respect.
author: Amit Kalantri

text: Why is the measure of love loss?
author: Jeanette Winterson

text: The end of a relationship is not always a failure. Sometimes all the love in the world is not enough to save something. In these cases, it is not a matter of fault from either person. Some things cannot be, it's as simple as that.
author: Ashly Lorenzana

text: My love is pizza shaped. Won’t you have a slice? It’s circular, so there’s enough to go around.
author: Dora J. Arod

text: Dance. Smile. Giggle. Marvel. TRUST. HOPE. LOVE. WISH. BELIEVE. Most of all, enjoy every moment of the journey, and appreciate where you are at this moment instead of always focusing on how far you have to go.
author: Mandy Hale

text: I wore your promise on my finger for one yearI'll wear your name on my heart til I dieBecause you were my boy, you were my only boy forever.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: In love, one and one are one.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: I would rather a romantic relationship turn into contempt than turn into apathy. The passion in the extremities make it appear as though it once meant something. We grow from hot or cold, but lukewarm is the biggest insult.
author: Criss Jami

text: I love you not only for what you are, but for what I am when I am with you. I love you not only for what you have made of yourself, but for what you are making of me. I love you for the part of me that you bring out.
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

text: None of us can choose where we shall love...
author: Susan  Kay

text: Remembering. Forgetting. I'm not sure which is worse.
author: Kelley Armstrong

text: Loving's pretty easy. It's letting someone love you that's hard.
author: Rita Mae Brown

text: They said that love was terrifying and tender, wild and sweet, and none of it made any sense.But now I knew that every mad word was true.
author: Rosamund Hodge

text: Do people look the same when they go to heaven, mommy?""I don't know. I don't think so.""Then how do people recognize each other?""I don't know, sweetie. They just feel it. You don't need your eyes to love, right?
author: R.J. Palacio

text: Fight for the only thing she knew was good enough, noble enough, powerful enough to be worth risking everything... Love.
author: Lauren Kate

text: ...love is the sum of our choices, the strength of our commitments, the ties that bind us together.
author: Emily Giffin

text: For there is no friend like a sisterIn calm or stormy weather; To cheer one on the tedious way, To fetch one if one goes astray,To lift one if one totters down, To strengthen whilst one stands
author: Christina Rossetti

text: The language of love letters is the same as suicide notes.
author: Courtney Love

text: Love is illogical. You fall into it like a manhole. Then you're just stuck. You die in love more than you live in love.
author: Tarryn Fisher

text: Tell me then, does love make one a fool or do only fools fall in love?
author: Orhan Pamuk

text: Nothing in life is worth,turning your back on,if you love it.
author: Albert Camus

text: Eternity is in love with the productions of time.
author: William Blake

text: You are my butterfly and refuse to set you free.
author: Shannon Hale

text: He promised her that he would give her everything, everything she wanted, as men in love always do. And she trusted him despite herself, as women in love always do.
author: Philippa Gregory

text: Love as thou wilt
author: Jacqueline Carey

text: But everyone disappears, no matter who loves them.
author: Dave Eggers

text: Let me tell ya. You gotta pay attention to signs. When life reaches out with a moment like this it's a sin if you don't reach back... I'm telling you.
author: Matthew Quick

text: True friends are those who came into your life, saw the most negative part of you, but are not ready to leave you, no matter how contagious you are to them.
author: Michael Bassey Johnson

text: Love is not blind that is the last thing that it is. Love is bound and the more it is bound the less it is blind.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: Those who have never known the deep intimacy and the intense companionship of happy mutual love have missed the best thing that life has to give.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: But, my God, it's so beautiful when the boy smiles
author: Anna Nalick

text: There are so many men, all endlessly attempting to sweep me off my feet. And there is one of you, trying just the opposite. Making sure my feet are firm beneath me, lest I fall.
author: Patrick Rothfuss

text: And maybe that was love. Being so vulnerable and allowing someone else in so far they could hurt you, but they also give you everything.
author: Christine Feehan

text: You can never be wise and be in love at the same time.
author: Bob Dylan

text: There is one friend in the life of each of us who seems not a separate person, however dear and beloved, but an expansion, an interpretation, of one's self, the very meaning of one's soul.
author: Edith Wharton

text: I love you, Marks. My heart is completely and utterly yours. And unfortunately for you, the rest of me comes with it.
author: Lisa Kleypas

text: Neither a lofty degree of intelligence nor imagination nor both together go to the making of genius. Love, love, love, that is the soul of genius.
author: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart

text: Depression is the flaw in love. To be creatures who love, we must be creatures who can despair at what we lose, and depression is the mechanism of that despair.
author: Andrew Solomon

text: Love: the sickest of Irony’s sick jokes. The place where logic and order go to die.
author: Christopher Moore

text: Jealous?""Maybe.""No reason. I like my ladies with a pulse.
author: Rachel Caine

text: I’m a fake fact factory. The things I make are the things I make up. Also, as a side business, I make love. Actually, I just made that up.
author: Dora J. Arod

text: Truth without love is brutality, and love without truth is hypocrisy.
author: Warren W. Wiersbe

text: ...the opposite of love is not hate -- it's apathy. It's not giving a damn. If somebody hates me, they must "feel" something ... or they couldn't possibly hate. Therefore, there's some way in which I can get to them.
author: Leo Buscaglia

text: I had to touch you with my hands, I had to taste you with my tongue; one can't love and do nothing.
author: Graham Greene

text: All the problem of women, starts with men. All the problem of men, ends with women.
author: Santosh Kalwar

text: No, this trick won't work... How on earth are you ever going to explain in terms of chemistry and physics so important a biological phenomenon as first love?
author: Albert Einstein

text: She was bendable light: she shone around every corner of my day.
author: Jerry Spinelli

text: But hurry, let's entwine ourselves as one, our mouth broken, our soul bitten by love, so time discovers us safely destroyed.
author: Federico Garcia Lorca

text: I never lie," I said offhand. "At least not to those I don't love.
author: Anne Rice

text: Love, like everything else in life, should be a discovery, an adventure, and like most adventures, you don’t know you’re having one until you’re right in the middle of it.
author: E.A. Bucchianeri

text: But why, why, why can't people just say what they mean?
author: Graeme Simsion

text: Nothing else wounds so deeply and irreparably. Nothing else robs us of hope so much as being unloved by one we love
author: Clive Barker

text: It seems to me now that the plain state of being human is dramatic enough for anyone you don't need to be a heroin addict or a performance poet to experience extremity. You just have to love someone.
author: Nick Hornby

text: Love is not finding someone to live with. It's finding someone you can't live without.
author: Rafael Ortiz

text: Soul meets soul on lovers lips.
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

text: But whatever However Whenever this ends I want you to know That right now I love you forever
author: Andrea Gibson

text: Well, it's simple to love someone," she said. "But it's hard to know when you need to say it out loud.
author: Rebecca Stead

text: I make love with a focus and intensity that most people reserve for sleep.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo

text: Nothing had changed. I was the stupid one again. I was the girl who never understood who she was to people.
author: Carol Rifka Brunt

text: One's life has value so long as one attributes value to the life of others, by means of love, friendship, and compassion
author: Simone de Beauvoir

text: Don't be reckless with other people's hearts. Don't put up with people who are reckless with yours.
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: We shouldn't teach great books we should teach a love of reading. Knowing the contents of a few works of literature is a trivial achievement. Being inclined to go on reading is a great achievement.
author: B.F. Skinner

text: Love is a drama of contradictions.
author: Franz Kafka

text: Friendship often ends in love. But love in friendship never.
author: Charles Caleb Colton

text: When I first met you, that's what I remember. I looked up at the sky and thought, I'm going to love this person because even the sky looks different.
author: Margaret Stohl

text: Perhaps all romance is like that not a contract between equal parties but an explosion of dreams and desires that can find no outlet in everyday life. Only a drama will do and while the fireworks last the sky is a different colour.
author: Jeanette Winterson

text: The thing with your heart's desire is that your heart doesn't even know what it desires until it turns up.
author: Daniel Handler

text: Her heart had grown so familiar to the pain of life without him, that to respond now seemed too large a pleasure she could not endure. If pain was love, then she loved fiercely. Yet knew she could not be near that boy again.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: Real friendship or love is not manufactured or achieved by an act of will or intention. Friendship is always an act of recognition.
author: John O'Donohue

text: Americans... are forever searching for love in forms it never takes, in places it can never be. It must have something to do with the vanished frontier.
author: Kurt Vonnegut Jr.

text: There are some experiences in life they haven't invented the right words for.
author: Lisa Kleypas

text: I think Heaven will be like a first kiss.
author: Sarah Addison Allen

text: Her desires are mine. Her wishes are mine. Should even the world stand against her, my blade will be at her side. And should it fail to protect her, let my own existence be forfeit." - Ash
author: Julie Kagawa

text: The greatest thing you'll ever learn is just to love and be loved in return.
author: Nat King Cole

text: Love is not primarily a relationship to a specific person; it is an attitude, an ordination of character which determines the relatedness of the person to the whole world as a whole, not toward one object of love
author: Erich Fromm

text: The moment our lips met, I knew with pure and piercing certainty that I would have waited for him forever.
author: Leigh Bardugo

text: Dexter, I love you so much. So, so much, and I probably always will. I just don't like you anymore. I'm sorry.
author: David Nicholls

text: One rarely falls in love without being as much attracted to what is interestingly wrong with someone as what is objectively healthy.
author: Alain de Botton

text: You don’t love because: you love despite; not for the virtues, but despite the faults.
author: William Faulkner

text: You've got to have someone who loves your body. Who doesn't define you, but sees you. Who loves what he sees. Who you don't have to struggle to be good enough for.
author: Deb Caletti

text: Millions of people are suffering: they want to be loved but they don't know how to love. And love cannot exist as a monologue; it is a dialogue, a very harmonious dialogue.
author: Osho

text: Follow me, reader! Who told you that there is no true, faithful, eternal love in this world! May the liar's vile tongue be cut out! Follow me, my reader, and me alone, and I will show you such a love!
author: Mikhail Bulgakov

text: Friendship is selfless love, care, respect, and honor not a profitable opportunity.
author: Santosh Kalwar

text: Love lasts about seven years. That's how long it takes for the cells of the body to totally replace themselves.
author: Francoise Sagan

text: You had to be willing to fight in order for a love story to last a life time.
author: Cristina Marrero

text: If I had a single flower for every time I think about you, I could walk forever in my garden.
author: Claudia Adrienne Grandi

text: People who love themselves, don’t hurt other people. The more we hate ourselves, the more we want others to suffer.
author: Dan Pearce

text: Love is when the other person's happiness is more important than your own.
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

text: If you enter this world knowing you are loved and you leave this world knowing the same, then everything that happens in between can be dealt with.
author: Michael  Jackson

text: It’s hard to communicate anything exactly and that’s why perfect relationships between people are difficult to find.
author: Gustave Flaubert

text: Your truest friends are the ones who will stand by you in your darkest moments--because they're willing to brave the shadows with you--and in your greatest moments--because they're not afraid to let you shine.
author: Nicole Yatsonsky

text: Love, whether newly born or aroused from a deathlike slumber, must always create sunshine, filling the heart so full of radiance, that it overflows upon the outward world.
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

text: He looks at her and smiles. "You're sort of dangerous, you know?" She stares at him. "Me?" "Yeah," he says sitting back. "I'm way too honest with you.
author: Jennifer E. Smith

text: May you live as long as you wish and love as long as you live.
author: Robert A. Heinlein

text: That kind of love should've been able to beat sickness. That kind of love should've conquered anything.
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout

text: When you leave,weary of me,without a word I shall gently let you go.
author: Kim Sowol

text: It's easy to look back and see it, and it's easy to give the advice. But the sad fact is, most people don't look beneath the surface until it's too late.
author: Wendelin Van Draanen

text: It's usually the selfish people who are loved the most. They do what you deny yourself, and you love them for it. You give them your heart.
author: Saul Bellow

text: How many loved your moments of glad grace,  And loved your beauty with love false or true;  But one man loved the pilgrim soul in you, And loved the sorrows of your changing face.
author: W.B. Yeats

text: No matter how bad your heart is broken, the world doesn't stop for your grief.
author: Faraaz Kazi

text: I can do pain. That’s the easy part of life. It’s everything else, happiness, laughter, love, that’s fucking complicated.
author: Jessica Sorensen

text: All I want from you is to trust me with what little you can, and grow in loving people around you with the same love I share with you. It's not your job to change them, or to convince them. You are free to love without an agenda.
author: William Paul Young

text: I'm beginning to think that maybe it's not just how much you love someone. Maybe what matters is who you are when you're with them.
author: Anne Tyler

text: No one compares to you, but there's no you, except in my dreams tonight.
author: Lana Del Rey

text: Self-talk reflects your innermost feelings.
author: Asa Don Brown

text: To fall in love is easy, even to remain in it is not difficult; our human loneliness is cause enough. But it is a hard quest worth making to find a comrade through whose steady presence one becomes steadily the person one desires to be.
author: Anna Louise Strong

text: If you press me to say why I loved him, I can say no more than because he was he, and I was I.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: It's good to let God pick a man for you. We don't do so well when we pick them ourselves. They end up lipsticks in a drawer, all those wrong colors you thought looked so good in the package.
author: Deb Caletti

text: The question, love, is whether you want me enough to take the risk.
author: Lisa Kleypas

text: I'm yours for ever--for ever and ever. Here I stand; I'm as firm as a rock. If you'll only trust me, how little you'll be disappointed. Be mine as I am yours.
author: Henry James

text: In the absence of love, we began slowly but surely to fall apart.
author: Marianne Williamson

text: But you cant shut everyone out. I mean you have to have someone to love. . .someone to hold on to. . . someone--
author: Hubert Selby Jr.

text: My love is meatloaf flavored. I just wish my meatloaf was also meatloaf flavored.
author: Dora J. Arod

text: It's so much easier to convince yourself you're madly in love with someone when you know nothing about him.
author: Megan McCafferty

text: I love you. I love you. I send this message through my fingers and into his, up his arm and into his heart. Hear me. I love you. And I'm sorry to leave you.
author: Jenny Downham

text: I finally know the difference between pleasing and loving, obeying and respecting. It has taken me so many years to be okay with being different, and with being this alive, this intense. (xxvi)
author: Eve Ensler

text: They are orphans again, with no true home but each other and whatever life they can make together on the other side of the sea.
author: Leigh Bardugo

text: If you love me, Henry, you don’t love me in a way I understand.
author: Richard Siken

text: Love is one of the true mysteries,' he said at last. 'The truest and the deepest of all. One thing, Maerad: to love is never wrong. It may be disastrous; it may never be possible; it may be the deepest agony. But it is never wrong.
author: Alison Croggon

text: If I were rain, That joins sky and earth that otherwise never touch,Could I join two hearts as well?
author: Tite Kubo

text: Happiness is part of who we are. Joy is the feeling
author: Tony DeLiso

text: We live in a dark and romantic and quite tragic world.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: Time is too slow for those who wait, too swift for those who fear, too long for those who grieve, too short for those who rejoice, but for those who love, time is eternity.
author: Henry Van Dyke

text: In a universe of ambiguity, this kind of certainty comes only once, and never again, no matter how many lifetimes you live.
author: Robert James Waller

text: How 'bout a shot of truth in that denial cocktail.
author: Jennifer Salaiz

text: I take no joy in mead nor meat, and song and laughter have become suspicious strangers to me. I am a creature of grief and dust and bitter longings. There is an empty place within me where my heart was once.
author: George R.R. Martin

text: And I didn't choose it, Kat. I chose you.
author: Ally Carter

text: We met less than a week ago and in that time I've done nothing but lie and cheat and betray you. I know. But if you give me a chance...all I want is to protect you. To be near you. For as long as I'm able.
author: Marissa Meyer

text: Those we love never truly leave us, Harry. There are things that death cannot touch.
author: Jack Thorne

text: He brought out the worst in me, and was the best thing that ever happened to me.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: You can be in love and you can be in a relationship. But they’re not always the same thing.
author: pleasefindthis

text: Can't you see? Every step I have taken, since I was that child on the bridge, has been to bring myself closer to you.
author: Arthur Golden

text: I could’ve sworn I was telling the truth when I told you I didn’t miss you.
author: pleasefindthis

text: I am troubled, immeasurablyby your eyes. I am struck by the featherof your soft reply.The sound of glassspeaks quick, disdainand concealswhat your eyes fightto explain.
author: Jim Morrison

text: I was his exception, and as much as I had tried to fight my feelings, he was mine.
author: Jamie McGuire

text: ...Despite the mayhem that followed, Bruno found that he was still holding Shmuel's hand in his own and nothing in the world would have persuaded him to let go.
author: John Boyne

text: You smiled and talked to me of nothing and I felt that for this I had been waiting long.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: I only have so much willpower, Helen," he whispered. "And since you apparently sleep in the most ridiculously transparent tank top I've ever seen, I'm going to have to ask you to get under the covers before I do something stupid.
author: Josephine Angelini

text: Nobody knows you.You don't know yourself.And I, who am half in love with you,What am I in love with?My own imaginings?
author: D.H. Lawrence

text: I like being the girl nobody can have.
author: Miley Cyrus

text: Perhaps watching someone you love suffer can teach you even more than suffering yourself can.
author: Dodie Smith

text: Sometimes loneliness makes the loudest noise.
author: Aaron Ben-Ze'ev

text: and yet her name was like a summons to all my foolish blood.
author: James Joyce

text: Every life needs a little space. It leaves room for good things to enter it.
author: Sarah Addison Allen

text: Two minds with but a single thought, two hearts that beat as one.
author: Jasper Fforde

text: Popular culture is a place where pity is called compassion, flattery is called love, propaganda is called knowledge, tension is called peace, gossip is called news, and auto-tune is called singing.
author: Criss Jami

text: Loving someone always requires you to not love others.
author: Koushun Takami

text: The heart of man is very much like the sea, it has its storms, it has its tides and in its depths it has its pearls too
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: I didn't care who kissed you first as long as I kissed you last."-George from If We Kiss
author: Rachel Vail

text: I hold it true, whate'er befall;I feel it when I sorrow most;'Tis better to have loved and lostThan never to have loved at all.
author: Alfred Tennyson

text: Can he love her? Can the soul really be satisfied with such polite affections? To love is to burn - to be on fire, like Juliet or Guinevere or Eloise...
author: Emma Thompson

text: Love is a combination of care, commitment, knowledge, responsibility, respect and trust.
author: bell hooks

text: There's only one thing that can heal the heart... Only one... It's love, Gaara.
author: Masashi Kishimoto

text: It is very difficult to live among people you love and hold back from offering them advice.
author: Anne Tyler

text: I haven’t been very impressed lately.By people,or places,or the way someone said he loved me and then slowly changed his mind.
author: Charlotte Eriksson

text: When male authors write love stories, the heroine tends to end up dead.
author: Susan Elizabeth Phillips

text: he placed his handson my mindbefore reachingfor my waistmy hipsor my lipshe didn't call mebeautiful firsthe called meexquisite - how he touches me
author: Rupi Kaur

text: You stay safe, You love. You survive. You laugh and cry and struggle and sometimes you fail and sometimes you succeed. You Push.
author: Carrie Ryan

text: Emotion without reason lets people walk all over you
author: reason without emotion is a mask for cruelty.

text: Love never dies.
author: Lauren Kate

text: Everything is clearer when you're in love.
author: John Lennon

text: I love you, even if there isn’t any me, or any love, or even any life. I love you.
author: Zelda Fitzgerald

text: Love only grows by sharing. You can only have more for yourself by giving it away to others.
author: Brian Tracy

text: how sad and bad and mad it was - but then, how it was sweet
author: Robert Browning

text: But you've slipped under my skin, invaded my blood and seized my heart.
author: Maria V. Snyder

text: I thought I was going to sweat five years before I'd feel like this.""I wanted it as much as you did. I've just got a hell of a poker face" I smiled, pressing my lips against his.
author: Jamie McGuire

text: You're my Star, a stargazer too,and I wish that I were Heaven,with a billion eyes to look at you!
author: Plato

text: I want him to see the flowers in my eyes and hear the songs in my hands.
author: Francesca Lia Block

text: Only in the agony of parting do we look into the depths of love.
author: George Eliot

text: Is it possible not to ever know your type-not to even know you have a type-until quite suddenly you do?
author: Jennifer E. Smith

text: I love you not only for what you are, but for what I am when I am with you
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning

text: The truth was...he was in love with her. Totally over-the-line, no-going-back, not-even-dead-would-he-part kind of shit.
author: J.R. Ward

text: I am two fools, I know,For loving, and for saying so.
author: John Donne

text: Give me the Love that leads the wayThe Faith that nothing can dismayThe Hope no disappointments tireThe Passion that'll burn like fireLet me not sink to be a clodMake me Thy fuel, Flame of God
author: Amy Carmichael

text: You have to be very fond of men. Very, very fond. You have to be very fond of them to love them. Otherwise they're simply unbearable.
author: Marguerite Duras

text: But you're so busy changing the worldJust one smile can change all of mine
author: Jack Johnson

text: I really don't even know you, and yet, in my life, you are forever entangled; to my history, inextricably bound.
author: Sarah Ockler

text: Find a woman who makes you feel more alive. She won't make life perfect but she'll make it infinitely more interesting. And then love her with all that's in you.
author: Gayle Roper

text: Daddy," I whispered, feeling my own breath hitch in my throat. "I love you."Just when I was sure he was asleep, the one corner of his mouth lifted in a smile. "I knew that," he murmured. "Always knew that.
author: Morgan Matson

text: I can't sleep, I can't eat, I can't do anything but think about him. At night I dream of him, all day I wait to see him, and when I do see him my heart turns over and I think I will faint with desire.
author: Philippa Gregory

text: People Die...Beauty Fades...Love Changes...And You Will Always Be Alone
author: L.J. Smith

text: The heart is a strange beast and not ruled by logic.
author: Maria V. Snyder

text: You were a summer gift, one I'll always treasure. You were a dream I never wanted to wake up from. You opened my eyes to things I'll never really see. You're the best thing that will ever happen to me.
author: Ellen Hopkins

text: The best use of life is love. The best expression of love is time. The best time to love is now.
author: Rick Warren

text: Knowing how to be solitary is central to the art of loving. When we can be alone, we can be with others without using them as a means of escape.
author: bell hooks

text: I can't promise not to make you mad. I can't promise that I won't hurt you. All I can promise is that I want you in my life, and I'll do anything to keep you there.
author: Chelsea M. Cameron

text: Get up, Shahrzad al-Khayzuran. You kneel before no one. Least of all me.
author: Renee Ahdieh

text: But what can be done, the one who loves must share the fate of the one he loves.
author: Mikhail Bulgakov

text: There is love of course. And then there's life, its enemy.
author: Jean Anouilh

text: Every time I close my eyes, it's like a dark paradise. No one compares to you, but there's no you, except in my dreams tonight
author: Lana Del Rey

text: Human relationships always help us to carry on because they always presuppose further developments, a future - and also because we live as if our only task was precisely to have relationships with other people.
author: Albert Camus

text: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
author: Anonymous

text: Often you don't know whether a woman is friend, enemy or lover until it is too late. Sometimes, she is all three.
author: Robert Jordan

text: I love you. You’re mine. I’ll kill any bastard who tries to take you from me.
author: Samantha Young

text: Tucker: Why would you tell me now if it's against the rules?Clara: Because I love you.
author: Cynthia Hand

text: You don’t need scores of suitors. You need only one… if he’s the right one.
author: Louisa May Alcott

text: I will find you.In the farthest corner, I will find you.
author: Mary E. Pearson

text: True love is born from understanding.
author: Gautama Buddha

text: Real museums are places where Time is transformed into Space.
author: Orhan Pamuk

text: If loneliness was a choice, what was the other option? To settle for second-best and try to be happy with that? And was that fair to the person you settled for?
author: Lisa Kleypas

text: We need to help people to discover the true meaning of love. Love is generally confused with dependence. Those of us who have grown in true love know that we can love only in proportion to our capacity for independence.
author: Fred Rogers

text: People who meet in airports are seventy-two percent more likely to fall for each other than people who meet anywhere else.
author: Jennifer E. Smith

text: I'm telling you this for one reason and one reason only: No matter how sure you are of someone's love, it's always nice to hear it.
author: Mike Gayle

text: After all, my erstwhile dear,My no longer cherished,Need we say it was not love,Just because it perished?
author: Edna St. Vincent Millay

text: Life is like a game of chess.To win you have to make a move.Knowing which move to make comes with IN-SIGHTand knowledge, and by learning the lessons that areacculated along the way.We become each and every piece within the game called life!
author: Allan Rufus

text: If a man whistles at you, don't turn around. You are a lady not a dog.
author: Niall Horan

text: I have loved to the point of madness; that which is called madness, that which to me, is the only sensible way to love.
author: Francoise Sagan

text: It was rather beautiful: the way he put her insecurities to sleep. The way he dove into her eyes and starved all the fears and tasted all the dreams she kept coiled beneath her bones.
author: Christopher Poindexter

text: And we all know love is a glass which makes even a monster appear fascinating.
author: Alberto Moravia

text: Because love, love is never finished. It circles and circles, the memories out of order and not always complete.
author: Sara Zarr

text: What does the brain matter compared with the heart?
author: Virginia Woolf

text: God's definition of what matters is pretty straightforward. He measures our lives by how we love.
author: Francis Chan

text: You are afraid of it because it is stronger than you you hate it because you are afraid of it you love it because you cannot subdue it to your will. Only the unsubduable can be loved.
author: Yevgeny Zamyatin

text: We all have an old knot in the heart we wish to untie.
author: Michael Ondaatje

text: That’s what it feels like when you touch me. Like millions of tiny universes being born and then dying in the space between your finger and my skin. Sometimes I forget.
author: Iain Thomas

text: Love is like quicksilver in the hand. Leave the fingers open and it stays. Clutch it and it darts away.
author: Dorothy Parker

text: ...when you decide you want to be with someone forever, you want forever to start right now.
author: Alexandra Potter

text: That must be love: when everything else in the world could implode and you wouldn't care as long as you had that one person standing beside you.
author: Katie McGarry

text: I'll always want him. Until every sun goes dark in every sky, until I am nothing more than long-forgotten cosmic dust, I will want him. And even then I suspect my particles will long for his.
author: Ann Aguirre

text: Me quota ang pag-ibig. Sa bawat limang umiibig, isa lang ang magiging maligaya. Ang iba, iibig sa di sila iniibig. Iibig nang di natututo. O iibig sa wala. O di iibig kailanman.
author: Ricky Lee

text: I miss you.I miss you. I miss you, I miss you.Be ready, because when I see you, I'll never let you go again.
author: Veronica Rossi

text: He said that we belonged together because he was born with a flower and I was born with a butterfly and that flowers and butterflies need each other for survival.
author: Gemma Malley

text: Loving you forever can't be wrong. Even though you're not here, can't move on
author: Lana Del Rey

text: I love you so much that nothing can matter to me - not even you...Only my love- not your answer. Not even your indifference
author: Ayn Rand

text: You can love somebody without it being like that. You keep them a stranger, a stranger who's a friend.
author: Truman Capote

text: I asked you here tonight because when you realise you want to spend the rest of your life with somebody, you want the rest of your life to start as soon as possible.
author: Graeme Simsion

text: The purpose of relationship is not to have another who might complete you, but to have another with whom you might share your completeness.
author: Neale Donald Walsch

text: The knowledge that she would never be loved in return acted upon her ideas as a tide acts upon cliffs.
author: Thornton Wilder

text: I loved him as we always love for the first time
author: with idolatry and wild passion.

text: It is that we are never so defenseless against suffering as when we love, never no helplessly unhappy as when we have lost our loved object of its love.
author: Sigmund Freud

text: You said move on, where do I go?
author: Katy Perry

text: Infantile love follows the principle: "I love because I am loved." Mature love follows the principle: "I am loved because I love." Immature love says: "I love you because I need you." Mature love says: "I need you because I love you.
author: Erich Fromm

text: I understood that as much as I had resisted the outside, as much as I had constricted my life, as much as I had closed and narrowed the channels into me, there were still many takers for the quiet heart.
author: Steve Martin

text: I don't want to live, I want to love first and live incidentally.
author: Zelda Fitzgerald

text: Love's gift cannot be given, it waits to be accepted.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: Love cannot live where there is no trust.
author: Edith Hamilton

text: Thank you...for gracing my life with your lovely presence, for adding the sweet measure of your soul to my existence.
author: Richard Matheson

text: The thing is, that world doesn't exist. All growing up means is that your realize no one will come along to fix things. No one will come along to save you.
author: Elizabeth Scott

text: You can give without loving, but you cannot love without giving.
author: Amy Carmichael

text: Love is like the sea. It's a moving thing, but still and all, it takes its shape from the shore it meets, and it's different with every shore.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: That's what I love about Aibileen, she can take the most complicated things in life and wrap them up so small and simple, they'll fit right in your pocket.
author: Kathryn Stockett

text: Everything want to be loved. Us sing and dance and holler, just trying to be loved.
author: Alice Walker

text: It's a very brave thing to fall in love. You have to be willing to trust somebody else with your whole being, and that's very difficult, really difficult and very brave.
author: Nicole Kidman

text: There’s something about arriving in new cities, wandering empty streets with no destination. I will never lose the love for the arriving, but I'm born to leave.
author: Charlotte Eriksson

text: I'll come and make love to you at five o'clock. If I'm late start without me.
author: Tallulah Bankhead

text: Sometimes, remembering hurts too much.
author: Jess Rothenberg

text: It always struck me in years after how bizarre it was, how two people could look at one another with such tenderness and complete love, and how quickly that could dissolve into nothing but bitterness.
author: Hannah Harrington

text: the world is not a pleasant place to be without someone to hold and be held by.
author: Nikki Giovanni

text: My father gave me the greatest gift anyone could give another person, he believed in me.
author: Jim Valvano

text: I cut myself because you wouldn't let me cry.I cried because you wouldn't let me speak.I spoke because you wouldn't let me shine.I shone because I thought you loved me...
author: Emilie Autumn

text: It never gets easier, missing you. And sometimes I wonder if it ever will.
author: Heather Brewer

text: Waiting is erotic
author: Irene Nemirovsky

text: Love, and you shall be loved.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: I don’t understand the hatred and fear of gays and bisexuals and lesbians…it’s a concept I honestly cannot grasp. To me, it’s not who you love…a man, a woman, what have you…it’s the fact that you love. That is all that truly matters.
author: Al Pacino

text: I look at you, and I think about you, and ... I don't know. No one has ever confounded me the way you do.
author: Stephanie Perkins

text: Choice or no, my heart is his.
author: Beth Revis

text: If you listen to the wind very carefully, you'll be able to hear me whisper my love for you.
author: Andrew Davidson

text: The fiercest anger of all, the most incurable,Is that which rages in the place of dearest love.
author: Euripides

text: He experienced the singular pleasure of watching people he loved fall in love with other people he loved.
author: Hanya Yanagihara

text: A man always finds it hard to realize that he may have finally lost a woman's love, however badly he may have treated her.
author: Arthur Conan Doyle

text: Some people feel love in their hearts, Julie. Some of us feel it all way into our souls. We're the ones who can't forget.
author: Judith McNaught

text: If you have reasons to love someone, you don’t love them.
author: Slavoj Zizek

text: Better to put your heart on the line, risk everything, and walk away with nothing than play it safe. Love is a lot of things, but “safe” isn’t one of them.
author: Mandy Hale

text: So many believe that it is love that grows, but it is the knowing that grows and love simply expands to contain it.
author: William Paul Young

text: Love is blind, they say--but isn't it more that love makes us see too much? Isn't it more that love floods our brain with sights and sounds, so that everything looks bigger, brighter, more lovely than ever before?
author: Susan  Fletcher

text: Falling into ruin was a bit like falling in love: Both descents stripped you bare and left you as you were at your core. And both endings are equally painful.
author: J.R. Ward

text: I want him to love me as much as I love him.
author: L.J. Smith

text: Faith goes up the stairs that love has built and looks out the windows which hope has opened.
author: Charles Haddon Spurgeon

text: Don't be afraid to lose him, because if a man truly loves you, he's not going anywhere
author: Steve Harvey

text: He is the cheese to my macaroni.
author: Diablo Cody

text: Just knowing you exist changed the world for me.
author: Tiffanie DeBartolo

text: The room fell quiet. And as I read down the list of over one hundred and fifty eight-grade boys, I realized that to me, there had only ever been one boy.
author: Wendelin Van Draanen

text: You have within you,right now,everything you need to deal with whatever the world can throw at you.
author: Brian Tracy

text: Falling In Love Is Like Owning A Dog.Throw things away and love will bring them back,again, and again, and again.But most of all, love needs love, lots of it.And in return, love loves you and never stops.
author: Taylor Mali

text: ...some things are better left in the past. And true things are destined to repeat themselves.
author: Suzanne Young

text: I want you. I want you so badly I can’t stand it. When you left, it felt like the world got darker. Like I couldn’t truly see anything. Couldn’t feel anything.
author: Rachel Vincent

text: It is love, not reason, that is stronger than death.
author: Thomas Mann

text: A gentleman holds my hand.A man pulls my hair.A soulmate will do both.
author: Alessandra Torre

text: To love someone enough to let them go, you had to let them go forever or you did not love them that much.
author: Diana Wynne Jones

text: Love is a game of tic-tac-toe,constantly waitingfor the next x or o.
author: Lang Leav

text: What sexual preference do you hope she has?” “Happiness.” Isnt that cool?
author: Francesca Lia Block

text: Love is happiness, but only when you believe it will last forever. Even though every time it turns out to be a lie, it’s only faith that gives love its strength and its joy.
author: Sergei Lukyanenko

text: Ah me, but where are now the songs I sangWhen life was sweet because you call’d them sweet?
author: Christina Rossetti

text: Soulmate" is an overused term, but a true soul connection is very rare, and very real.
author: Hilary Duff

text: I am the poet of the poor, because I was poor when I loved; since I could not give gifts, I gave words.
author: Ovid

text: I don't understand how I can know so little about love and how it works. How I can be so bad at it when it's all I've ever wanted.All I've ever known is about leaving or being left.
author: Carrie Ryan

text: I think sometimes when we find love we pretend it away, or ignore it, or tell ourselves we’re imagining it. Because it is the most painful kind of hope there is.
author: Rae Carson

text: Dustfinger still clearly remembered the feeling of being in love for the first time. How vulnerable his heart had suddenly been! Such a trembling, quivering thing, happy and miserably unhappy at once.
author: Cornelia Funke

text: To love makes one solitary.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: True love always makes a man better, no matter what woman inspires it.
author: Alexandre Dumas

text: There are all kinds of ways for a relationship to be tested, even broken, some, irrevocably; it’s the endings we’re unprepared for.
author: Katherine Owen

text: When what you want is a relationship, and not a person, get a dog.
author: Deb Caletti

text: After all, a woman who doesn't love cats is never going to be make a man happy.
author: Orhan Pamuk

text: You can give without loving, but you can never love without giving.
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

text: I wanted to tell her that I loved her, and not in the complicated way I loved our parents, but in a simple way I never had to think about. I loved her like breathing.
author: Brenna Yovanoff

text: The heart makes its choices without weighing the consequences. It doesn't look ahead to the lonely nights that follow.
author: Tess Gerritsen

text: —I don't want to lose you—Then why are you the one that keeps letting go?
author: Alexandra Bracken

text: Kisses open doors, I've noticed. That one gesture can unlock secrets, ease open feelings. It can't be prevented--these kisses just are. It's how they work. They break into basements you never knew you had.
author: Susan  Fletcher

text: What’s wrong is that every morning and every night, I lie in bed wondering why you’re not beside me.
author: K.A. Tucker

text: We all know interspecies romance is weird.
author: Tim Burton

text: Everything happens for a reason and, something better will come along for me!
author: Selena Gomez

text: May I kiss you then? On this miserable paper? I might as well open the window and kiss the night air.
author: Franz Kafka

text: And I got out of there without punching anyone, kicking anyone, or breaking down in tears. Some days the small victories are all you achieve.
author: Molly Ringle

text: I love you for who you are, not who the world thinks you should be.
author: Libba Bray

text: Mine was the twilight and the morning. Mine was a world of rooftops and love songs.
author: Roman Payne

text: I know what I have given you... I do not know what you have received.
author: Antonio Porchia

text: I wish the night would end,I wish the day'd begin,I wish it would rain or snow,or the wind would blow,or the grass would grow,I wish I had yesterday,I wish there were games to play...
author: V.C. Andrews

text: He didn't marry you to become king. He became king because he wanted to marry you.
author: Megan Whalen Turner

text: He didn't mind how he looked to other people, because the nursery magic had made him Real, and when you are Real shabbiness doesn't matter.
author: Margery Williams Bianco

text: Three years? That's a thousand tomorrows, ma'am.
author: Karen Kingsbury

text: I love him wholly and unconditionally and without reservation. I love him enough to sacrifice a friendship. I love him enough to accept my own happiness and use it, in turn, to make him happy back.
author: Emily Giffin
