text: Losing your life is not the worst thing that can happen. The worst thing is to lose your reason for living.
author: Jo Nesbø

text: As far as we can discern, the sole purpose of human existence is to kindle a light of meaning in the darkness of mere being.
author: C.G. Jung

text: EARLY BIRDOh, if you’re a bird, be an early birdAnd catch the worm for your breakfast plate.If you’re a bird, be an early early bird--But if you’re a worm, sleep late.
author: Shel Silverstein

text: All the world's a stage and most of us are desperately unrehearsed.
author: Sean O'Casey

text: Life is more or less a lie, but then again, that's exactly the way we want it to be.
author: Bob Dylan

text: Moral wounds have this peculiarity - they may be hidden, but they never close; always painful, always ready to bleed when touched, they remain fresh and open in the heart.
author: Alexandre Dumas

text: Life, although it may only be an accumulation of anguish, is dear to me, and I will defend it.
author: Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley

text: Those who will not reason, are bigots, those who cannot, are fools, and those who dare not, are slaves.
author: George Gordon Byron

text: Life is nothing without a little chaos to make it interesting.
author: Amelia Atwater-Rhodes

text: Our battered suitcases were piled on the sidewalk again; we had longer ways to go. But no matter, the road is life
author: Jack Kerouac

text: I don't know why we are here, but I'm pretty sure that it is not in order to enjoy ourselves.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: Writing is a form of therapy; sometimes I wonder how all those who do not write, compose, or paint can manage to escape the madness, melancholia, the panic and fear which is inherent in a human situation.
author: Graham Greene

text: The world changes, we do not, therein lies the irony that kills us.
author: Anne Rice

text: I think that you’ve got to make something that pleases you and hope that other people feel the same way.
author: Thomas Keller

text: The cost of oblivious daydreaming was always this moment of return, the realignment with what had been before and now seemed a little worse.
author: Ian McEwan

text: If the path before you is clear, you're probably on someone else's.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: The beautiful is always bizarre.
author: Charles Baudelaire

text: Shine your light and make a positive impact on the world
author: there is nothing so honorable as helping improve the lives of others.

text: People were always getting ready for tomorrow. I didn't believe in that. Tomorrow wasn't getting ready for them. It didn't even know they were there.
author: Cormac McCarthy

text: There will be time, there will be timeTo prepare a face to meet the faces that you meet.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: Science is organized knowledge. Wisdom is organized life.
author: Will Durant

text: You need to find a way to live your life, that it doesn't make a mockery of your values.
author: Bill Ayers

text: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff. We are a way for the universe to know itself.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Never respond to an angry person with a fiery comeback, even if he deserves it...Don't allow his anger to become your anger.
author: Bohdi Sanders

text: What I'm not sure about, is if our lives have been so different from the lives of the people we save. We all complete. Maybe none of us really understand what we've lived through, or feel we've had enough time.
author: Kazuo Ishiguro

text: Dogs are not our whole life, but they make our lives whole.
author: Roger A. Caras

text: When a man learns to love, he must bear the risk of hatred.
author: Masashi Kishimoto

text: Character — the willingness to accept responsibility for one's own life — is the source from which self-respect springs.
author: Joan Didion

text: Happiness was but the occasional episode in a general drama of pain.
author: Thomas Hardy

text: After all, what's a life, anyway? We're born, we live a little while, we die.
author: E.B. White

text: To move, to breathe, to fly, to float,To gain all while you give,To roam the roads of lands remote,To travel is to live.
author: Hans Christian Andersen

text: The best way to predict your future is to create it.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Just remember that the things you put into your head are there forever, he said. You might want to think about that.You forget some things, dont you?Yes. You forget what you want to remember and you remember what you want to forget.
author: Cormac McCarthy

text: We are unusual and tragic and alive.
author: Dave Eggers

text: I do not believe anyone can be perfectly well, who has a brain and a heart
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

text: I know it's not easy for you, living this life, but try to remember, always try to remember, you're not the only one with troubles.
author: Laini Taylor

text: Your "I CAN" is more important than your IQ.
author: Robin S. Sharma

text: When a stargirl cries, she sheds not tears but light.
author: Jerry Spinelli

text: We lead our lives like water flowing down a hill, going more or less in one direction until we splash into something that forces us to find a new course.
author: Arthur Golden

text: We all have two lives. The second one starts when we realize that we only have one.
author: Tom Hiddleston

text: Winners are not afraid of losing. But losers are. Failure is part of the process of success. People who avoid failure also avoid success.
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki

text: How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
author: Annie Dillard

text: You were made by God and for God and until you understand that, life will never make sense.
author: Rick Warren

text: Let me give you a tip on a clue to men's characters: the man who damns money has obtained it dishonorably
author: the man who respects it has earned it.

text: The universe may not always play fair, but at least it's got a hell of a sense of humor.
author: Candace Bushnell

text: Life is hard. After all, it kills you.
author: Katharine Hepburn

text: But we can't go back. We can only go forward.
author: Libba Bray

text: Civilized men are more discourteous than savages because they know they can be impolite without having their skulls split, as a general thing.
author: Robert E. Howard

text: Hobbes: Do you think there's a God?Calvin: Well, somebody's out to get me!
author: Bill Watterson

text: I have known the joy and pain of friendship. I have served and been served. I have made some good enemies for which I am not a bit sorry. I have loved unselfishly, and I have fondled hatred with the red-hot tongs of Hell. That's living.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: Enjoy life. There's plenty of time to be dead.
author: Hans Christian Andersen

text: I am ready for whatever's coming. I expect nothing but to be let down or turned away. I am alone. Goddamn. The shit hurts sometimes, but I realize what I am, what I have become.
author: Henry Rollins

text: Clocks slay time... time is dead as long as it is being clicked off by little wheels
author: only when the clock stops does time come to life.

text: Whether we fall by ambition, blood, or lustLike diamonds we are cut with our own dust
author: John Webster

text: There are three deaths. The first is when the body ceases to function. The second is when the body is consigned to the grave. The third is that moment, sometime in the future, when your name is spoken for the last time.
author: David Eagleman

text: In the end, it wasn't death that surprised her but the stubbornness of life.
author: Jeffrey Eugenides

text: Look. I have a strategy. Why expect anything? If you don’t expect anything, you don’t get disappointed.
author: Patricia McCormick

text: You can't be brave if you've only had wonderful things happen to you.
author: Mary Tyler Moore

text: Life is a blank canvas, and you need to throw all the paint on it you can.
author: Danny Kaye

text: This is how the entire course of a life can be changed: by doing nothing.
author: Ian McEwan

text: Drink your tea slowly and reverently, as if it is the axis on which the world earth revolves - slowly, evenly, without rushing toward the future.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: My destination is no longer a place, rather a new way of seeing.
author: Marcel Proust

text: Your time is way too valuable to be wasting on people that can't accept who you are.
author: Turcois Ominek

text: Oh, you hate your job? Why didn't you say so?There's a support group for that. It's called EVERYBODY, and they meet at the bar.
author: Drew Carey

text: Maybe everyone is just waiting for someone else to save them.
author: Margaret Peterson Haddix

text: We are all connected; To each other, biologically. To the earth, chemically. To the rest of the universe atomically.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: Sometimes I just think depression's one way of coping with the world. Like, some people get drunk, some people do drugs, some people get depressed. Because there's so much stuff out there that you have to do something to deal with it.
author: Ned Vizzini

text: When defeat comes, accept it as a signal that your plans are not sound, rebuild those plans, and set sail once more toward your coveted goal.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: A man is like a novel: until the very last page you don't know how it will end. Otherwise it wouldn't even be worth reading.
author: Yevgeny Zamyatin

text: What do you most value in your friends?Their continued existence.
author: Christopher Hitchens

text: The goal of life is to make your heartbeat match the beat of the universe, to match your nature with Nature.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: You can get all A's and still flunk life.
author: Walker Percy

text: People rarely bring flowers to a suicide.
author: Jennifer Niven

text: Life is problems. Living is solving problems.
author: Raymond E. Feist

text: Push your boundaries beyond the ordinary
author: be that “extra” in “extraordinary.

text: You are -- your life, and nothing else.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: All knowledge is worth having.
author: Jacqueline Carey

text: Mistakes are, after all, the foundations of truth, and if a man does not know what a thing is, it is at least an increase in knowledge if he knows what it is not.
author: C.G. Jung

text: Wisdom is having things right in your lifeand knowing why.
author: William Stafford

text: Normal is an illusion. What is normal for the spider is chaos for the fly.
author: Charles Addams

text: When you make a choice, you change the future.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: There's no advantage to hurrying through life." -Shikamaru Nara
author: Masashi Kishimoto

text: It's good to do uncomfortable things. It's weight training for life.
author: Anne Lamott

text: Despair is the price one pays for self-awareness. Look deeply into life, and you'll always find despair.
author: Irvin D. Yalom

text: It's important in life if you don't give a shit. It can help you a lot.
author: George Carlin

text: Selling my soul would be a lot easier if I could just find it.
author: Nikki Sixx

text: A boo is a lot louder than a cheer.
author: Lance Armstrong

text: Do your own thing on your own terms and get what you came here for
author: Oliver James

text: Art is unquestionably one of the purest and highest elements in human happiness. It trains the mind through the eye, and the eye through the mind. As the sun colors flowers, so does art color life.
author: John Lubbock

text: One swallow does not make a summer,neither does one fine day; similarly one day or brief time of happiness does not make a person entirely happy.
author: Aristotle

text: All that happens means something
author: nothing you do is ever insignificant.

text: Before I can live with other folks I’ve got to live with myself. The one thing that doesn’t abide by majority rule is a person’s conscience.
author: Harper Lee

text: When I lost my sight, Werner, people said I was brave. When my father left, people said I was brave. But it is not bravery; I have no choice. I wake up and live my life. Don't you do the same?
author: Anthony Doerr

text: You are a child of the universe, no less than the trees and the stars; you have a right to be here. And whether or not it is clear to you, no doubt the universe is unfolding as it should.
author: Max Ehrmann

text: That’s how I know they are dreams. Because the simple and plain and everyday things are the ones that we can never have. (Cassia Reyes)
author: Ally Condie

text: If you try to change it, you will ruin it. Try to hold it, and you will lose it.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Stay hungry, stay young, stay foolish, stay curious, and above all, stay humble because just when you think you got all the answers, is the moment when some bitter twist of fate in the universe will remind you that you very much don't.
author: Tom Hiddleston

text: Books can be dangerous. The best ones should be labeled ‘This could change your life’.
author: Helen Exley

text: I only want to write. And there's no college for that except life.
author: Dodie Smith

text: Life is infinitely stranger than anything which the mind of man could invent.
author: Arthur Conan Doyle

text: A joke is a very serious thing.
author: Winston S. Churchill

text: Life will hit you hard in the face, wait for you to get back up just so it can kick you in the stomach. But getting the wind knocked out of you is the only way to remind your lungs how much they like the taste of air.
author: Sarah Kay

text: Well, I like to eat, sleep, drink, and be in love.I like to work, read, learn, and understand life.
author: Langston Hughes

text: I believe in looking reality straight in the eye and denying it.
author: Garrison Keillor

text: I think the highest and lowest points are the important ones. Anything else is just...in between.
author: Jim Morrison

text: Spending time with God is the key to our strength and success in all areas of life. Be sure that you never try to work God into your schedule, but always work your schedule around Him.
author: Joyce Meyer

text: I had rather be shut up in a very modest cottage with my books, my family and a few old friends, dining on simple bacon, and letting the world roll on as it liked, than to occupy the most splendid post, which any human power can give.
author: Thomas Jefferson

text: Changing is what people do when they have no options left.
author: Holly Black

text: All that is important is this one moment in movement. Make the moment important, vital, and worth living. Do not let it slip away unnoticed and unused.
author: Martha Graham

text: Always forgive, but never forget, else you will be a prisoner of your own hatred, and doomed to repeat your mistakes forever.
author: Wil Zeus

text: All sacrifice and suffering is redemptive. It is used to either teach the individual or to help others. Nothing is by chance.
author: A.J. Russell

text: If you can't do anything about it, laugh like hell.
author: David Cook

text: I suppose sooner or later in the life of everyone comes a moment of trial. We all of us have our particular devil who rides us and torments us, and we must give battle in the end.
author: Daphne du Maurier

text: Life is but a day
author: A fragile dew-drop on its perilous wayFrom a tree’s summit.

text: Life is too short when you think of the length of death
author: Sean Mangan

text: My point is, life is about balance. The good and the bad. The highs and the lows. The pina and the colada.
author: Ellen DeGeneres

text: I've come to believe that all my past failure and frustration were actually laying the foundation for the understandings that have created the new level of living I now enjoy.
author: Anthony Robbins

text: My life is a reading list.
author: John Irving

text: A bend in the road is not the end of the road…Unless you fail to make the turn.
author: Helen Keller

text: I feel sorry for anyone who is in a place where he feels strange and stupid.
author: Lois Lowry

text: When we're connected to others, we become better people.
author: Randy Pausch

text: By the way, when Oprah Winfrey is suggesting you may have overextended yourself, you need to examine your fucking life.
author: Tina Fey

text: There is no more profitable investment than investing in yourself. It is the best investment you can make
author: you can never go wrong with it. It is the true way to improve yourself to be the best version of you and lets you be able to best serve those around you.

text: Hopes are like hair ornaments. Girls want to wear too many of them. When they become old women they look silly wearing even one.
author: Arthur Golden

text: We never live
author: we are always in the expectation of living.

text: I think computer viruses should count as life ... I think it says something about human nature that the only form of life we have created so far is purely destructive. We've created life in our own image.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: Because horror on Earth is real and it is every day. It is like a flower or like the sun
author: it cannot be contained.

text: I have noticed that even those who assert that everything is predestined and that we can change nothing about it still look both ways before they cross the street
author: Stephen Hawking

text: Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety, after all it is only in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
author: Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu-Talib A.S

text: For a day, just for one day,Talk about that which disturbs no oneAnd bring some peace into yourBeautiful eyes.
author: The Subject Tonight Is Love: 60 Wild and Sweet Poems of Hafiz

text: Another world is not only possible, she is on her way. On a quiet day, I can hear her breathing.
author: Arundhati Roy

text: We can only be said to be alive in those moments when our hearts are conscious of our treasures.
author: Thornton Wilder

text: The Lord prefers common-looking people. That is why he made so many of them.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Life is a long lesson in humility.
author: J.M. Barrie

text: Beauty is a whore, I like money better.
author: Michael Cunningham

text: We should live our lives as though Christ was coming this afternoon.
author: Jimmy Carter

text: Forget injuries, never forget kindnesses.
author: Confucius

text: Be careful the stories you're telling yourself about your current circumstances
author: a head full of negative thoughts has no space for positive ones.

text: Nobody wants to be here and nobody wants to leave.
author: Cormac McCarthy

text: I still find each day too short for all the thoughts I want to think, all the walks I want to take, all the books I want to read, and all the friends I want to see.
author: John Burroughs

text: ... And these childrenthat you spit onas they try to change their worldsare immune to your consultations.They're quite awareof what they're going through...
author: David Bowie

text: Pain or love or danger makes you real again....
author: Jack Kerouac

text: Here’s to freedom, cheers to art. Here’s to having an excellent adventure and may the stopping never start.
author: Jason Mraz

text: You don't ask people with knives in their stomachs what would make them happy
author: happiness is no longer the point. It's all about survival

text: The moment a little boy is concerned with which is a jay and which is a sparrow, he can no longer see the birds or hear them sing.
author: Eric Berne

text: While we try to teach our children all about life, our children teach us what life is all about.
author: Angela Schwindt

text: Life is a drama full of tragedy and comedy. You should learn to enjoy the comic episodes a little more.
author: Jeannette Walls

text: And though you think the world is at your feet, it can rise up and tread on you.
author: Ian McEwan

text: I'd found out that if you pushed people away hard enough, they tended to go.
author: Morgan Matson

text: It is hard work and great art to make life not so serious.
author: John Irving

text: The life where nothing was ever unexpected. Or inconvenient. Or unusual. The life without colour, pain or past.
author: Lois Lowry

text: I don’t chase after anyone, If you wanna walk out of my life, then I’ll hold the fucking door open for you.
author: Wiz Khalifa

text: One of the greatest journeys in life is overcoming insecurity and learning to truly not give a shit.
author: J.A. Konrath

text: Live or die, but don't poison everything.
author: Anne Sexton

text: I hope you live a life you’re proud of. If you find that you’re not, I hope you have the strength to start all over again.
author: Eric Roth

text: She had been given a wonderful gift: life. Sometimes it was cruelly taken away too soon, but it's what you did with it that counted, not how long it lasted.
author: Cecelia Ahern

text: Never lose faith in yourself, and never lose hope; remember, even when this world throws its worst and then turns its back, there is still always hope.
author: Pittacus Lore

text: What strange phenomena we find in a great city, all we need do is stroll about with our eyes open. Life swarms with innocent monsters.
author: Charles Baudelaire

text: It is not worth the while to let our imperfections disturb us always.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: This work was strictly voluntary, but any animal who absented himself from it would have his rations reduced by half.
author: George Orwell

text: It is in vain to say human beings ought to be satisfied with tranquility: they must have action
author: and they will make it if they cannot find it.

text: It is not that we have so little time but that we lose so much. ... The life we receive is not short but we make it so
author: we are not ill provided but use what we have wastefully.

text: Droll thing life is -- that mysterious arrangement of merciless logic for a futile purpose. The most you can hope from it is some knowledge of yourself -- that comes too late -- a crop of inextinguishable regrets.
author: Joseph Conrad

text: That's the problem with life. You never get enough time to stare at your ceiling and try to figure out what's going on.
author: Leila Sales

text: The true hypocrite is the one who ceases to perceive his deception, the one who lies with sincerity.
author: Andre Gide

text: It is wise to direct your anger towards problems -- not people
author: to focus your energies on answers -- not excuses.

text: Power does not corrupt. Fear corrupts... perhaps the fear of a loss of power.
author: John Steinbeck

text: Give a bowl of rice to a man and you will feed him for a day. Teach him how to grow his own rice and you will save his life.
author: Confucius

text: You often meet your fate on the road you take to avoid it.
author: Goldie Hawn

text: Creo que todos tenemos un poco de esa bella locura que nos mantiene andando cuando todo alrededor es tan insanamente cuerdo.
author: Julio Cortazar

text: Some beautiful paths can't be discovered without getting lost.
author: Erol Ozan

text: What I want is so simple I almost can't say it: elementary kindness.
author: Barbara Kingsolver

text: you might want to decide fast. We live in a dangerous world. If you see a chance to be happy, you have to fight for it, so later you have no regrets.
author: Ilona Andrews

text: Life seems to go on without effort when I am filled with music.
author: George Eliot

text: Fate loves the fearless.
author: James Russell Lowell

text: I am not good at noticing when I'm happy, except in retrospect.
author: Tana French

text: Rise to the occassion which is life!
author: Virginia Euwer Wolff

text: I never ask a man what his business is, for it never interests me. What I ask him about are his thoughts and dreams.
author: H.P. Lovecraft

text: The trick is to enjoy life. Don't wish away your days, waiting for better ones ahead.
author: Marjorie Pay Hinckley

text: There are some values that you should never compromise on to stay true to yourself
author: you should be brave to stand up for what you truly believe in even if you stand alone.

text: Truth is not something outside to be discovered, it is something inside to be realized.
author: Osho

text: Art is the reason I get up in the morning, but the definition ends there. It doesn't seem fair that I'm living for something I can't even define.
author: Ani DiFranco

text: I believe when life gives you lemons, you should make lemonade...and try to find someone whose life has given them vodka, and have a party.
author: Ron White

text: Alice had got so much into the way of expecting nothing but out-of-the-way things to happen, that it seemed quite dull and stupid for life to go on in the common way.
author: Lewis Carroll

text: Life—the way it really is—is a battle not between good and bad, but between bad and worse
author: Joseph Brodsky

text: Be so good they can't ignore you.
author: Steve Martin

text: Never compete with someone who has nothing to lose.
author: Baltasar Gracian

text: But the only way never to do the wrong thing is never to do anything.
author: Jim Butcher

text: I don't pray really, because I don't want to bore God.
author: Orson Welles

text: That’s life for you. All the happiness you gather to yourself, it will sweep away like it’s nothing. If you ask me I don’t think there are any such things as curses. I think there is only life. That’s enough.
author: Junot Diaz

text: Everything has its wonders, even darkness and silence, and I learn, whatever state I may be in, therein to be content
author: Helen Keller

text: To live is to be musical, starting with the blood dancing in your veins. Everything living has a rhythm. Do you feel your music?
author: Michael  Jackson

text: No matter where you are, you're always a bit on your own, always an outsider.
author: Banana Yoshimoto

text: Disappointment came to me,and booted me,and bruised and hurt me,but that's how people grow up.
author: Morrissey

text: For beautiful eyes, look for the good in others; for beautiful lips, speak only words of kindness; and for poise, walk with the knowledge that you are never alone.
author: Sam Levenson

text: It can take years to mold a dream. It takes only a fraction of a second for it to be shattered.
author: Mary E. Pearson

text: The absence of the will to live is, alas, not sufficient to make one want to die.
author: Michel Houellebecq

text: Your time may come. Do not be too sad, Sam. You cannot be always torn in two. You will have to be one and whole, for many years. You have so much to enjoy and to be, and to do.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Go for it, while you can. I know you have it in you. And I can't promise you'll get everything you want, but I can promise nothing will change if you don't try.
author: J.M. Darhower

text: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life, and see if I could not learn what it had to teach, and not, when I came to die, discover that I had not lived.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Along with the idea of romantic love, she was introduced to another--physical beauty. Probably the most destructive ideas in the history of human thought. Both originated in envy, thrived in insecurity, and ended in disillusion.
author: Toni Morrison

text: The most important relationship in your life is the relationship you have with yourself. Because no matter what happens, you will always be with yourself.
author: Diane Von Furstenberg

text: Life’s greatest gift is the freedom it leaves you to step out of it whenever you choose.
author: Andre Breton

text: all of us have a place in history. mine is clouds.
author: Richard Brautigan

text: The deepest wounds aren't the ones we get from other people hurting us. They are the wounds we give ourselves when we hurt other people.
author: Isobelle Carmody

text: To live is to war with trolls.
author: Henrik Ibsen

text: Life is partly what we make it, and partly what it is made by the friends we choose.
author: Tehyi Hsieh

text: People are going to say what they want to say and think what they want to think, and I can't change their minds.
author: Hilary Duff

text: Never forget:we walk on hell,gazing at flowers.
author: Kobayashi Issa

text: There is no escape—we pay for the violence of our ancestors.
author: Frank Herbert

text: The world will give you that once in awhile, a brief timeout; the boxing bell rings and you go to your corner, where somebody dabs mercy on your beat-up life.
author: Sue Monk Kidd

text: Man starts over again everyday, in spite of all he knows, against all he knows.
author: Emil M. Cioran

text: You look ridiculous if you danceYou look ridiculous if you don't danceSo you might as welldance.
author: Gertrude Stein

text: No matter how hard life is, don't lose hope
author: Zayn Malik

text: Sometimes life happens.
author: Katie McGarry

text: I like shape very much. A novel has to have shape, and life doesn't have any.
author: Jean Rhys

text: When all is said and done, the life of faith is nothing if not an unending struggle of the spirit with every available weapon against the flesh.
author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

text: Focus on making yourself better, not on thinking that you are better.
author: Bohdi Sanders

text: You can be young without money, but you can't be old without it.
author: Tennessee Williams

text: Have you ever hoped for something? And held out for it against all the odds? Until everything you did was ridiculous?
author: Ali Shaw

text: Do not look back and grieve over the past, for it is gone; and do not be troubled about the future, for it has yet to come. Live in the present, and make it so beautiful that it will be worth remembering
author: Ida Scott Taylor McKinney

text: Why did I write? Because I found life unsatisfactory.
author: Tennessee Williams

text: I run after her, not really giving chase. I’m running because I can, because I must.Because I want to see how far I can go before I have to stop.
author: Libba Bray

text: Whatever is going to happen will happen, whether we worry or not.
author: Ana Monnar

text: I am old enough to know only too well my good and bad qualities, which were often one in the same.
author: Lisa See

text: My only regrets are the moments when i doubted myself and took the safe route. Life is too short to waste time being unhappy.
author: Dan Howell

text: Time ticks by; we grow older. Before we know it, too much time has passed and we've missed the chance to have had other people hurt us. To a younger me this sounded like luck; to an older me this sounds like a quiet tragedy.
author: Douglas Coupland

text: One who makes himself a worm cannot complain afterwards if people step on him.
author: Immanuel Kant

text: A feeling is no longer the same when it comes the second time. It dies through the awareness of its return. We become tired and weary of our feelings when they come too often and last too long.
author: Pascal Mercier

text: ... millions long for immortality who don't know what to do with themselves on a rainy Sunday afternoon.
author: Susan Ertz

text: If people make fun of you, that probably means you're doing something right.
author: Evanescence

text: We're all made of stories. When they finally put us underground, the stories are what will go on. Not forever, perhaps, but for a time. It's a kind of immortality, I suppose, bounded by limits, it's true, but then so's everything.
author: Charles de Lint

text: You need to spend time crawling alone through shadows to truly appreciate what it is to stand in the sun.
author: Shaun Hick

text: A man travels the world over in search of what he needs and returns home to find it.
author: George Moore

text: There’s a really stupid saying: When life hands you a lemon, make lemonade. Well, I have a better saying: When life hands you a lemon, shove that lemon up its stupid butt.
author: Gena Showalter

text: Don't Worry Be Happy
author: Meher Baba

text: The end is in the beginning and yet you go on.
author: Samuel Beckett

text: You enter the forestat the darkest point,where there is no path.Where there is a way or path,it is someone else's path.You are not on your own path.If you follow someone else's way,you are not going to realizeyour potential.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you will ever have.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Two wrongs don't make a right, but don't three lefts make a right? Two wrongs don't make a right, but don't two negatives make a positive?
author: Andrew Clements

text: See, the poor dream all their lives of getting enough to eat and looking like the rich. And what do the rich dream of?? Losing weight and looking like the poor.
author: Aravind Adiga

text: If after I die, people want to write my biography, there is nothing simpler. They only need two dates: the date of my birth and the date of my death. Between one and another, every day is mine.
author: Fernando Pessoa

text: I can’t really act the way I want to act, or say what I want to say all the time. And a lot of times, I cover that up with a smile.
author: Hilary Duff

text: I am afraid. Not of life, or death, or nothingness, but of wasting it as if I had never been.
author: Daniel Keyes

text: Life doesn't make any sense, and we all pretend it does. Comedy's job is to point out that it doesn't make sense, and that it doesn't make much difference anyway.
author: Eric Idle

text: I don’t want life to imitate art. I want life to be art.
author: Carrie Fisher

text: I simply regard romantic comedies as a subgenre of sci-fi, in which the world created therein has different rules than my regular human world.
author: Mindy Kaling

text: I do not love men: I love what devours them.
author: Andre Gide

text: There's more to life than love.
author: Ramon Bautista

text: Desire makes life happen. Makes it matter. Makes everything worth it. Desire is life. Hunger to see the next sunrise or sunset, to touch the one you love, to try again. 'Hell would be waking up and wanting nothing,' he agrees.
author: Karen Marie Moning

text: The fact that life has no meaning is a reason to live --moreover, the only one.
author: Emil M. Cioran

text: Sometimes you have to be a bitch to get things done.
author: Madonna

text: Everybody know death is inevitably coming, but it never fails to catch everybody by surprise everytime one is going
author: Hlovate

text: Crap.It's all crap.Living is crap.Life has no meaning.None. Nowhere to be found.Crap.Why doesn't anybody realize this?
author: K-Ske Hasegawa

text: Rise and rise again until lambs become lions
author: Robin Hood

text: Mathematics expresses values that reflect the cosmos, including orderliness, balance, harmony, logic, and abstract beauty.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: But I can hardly sit still. I keep fidgeting, crossing one leg and then the other. I feel like I could throw off sparks, or break a window--maybe rearrange all the furniture.
author: Raymond Carver

text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment
author: Jim Rohn

text: I don't judge people. It blurs out the center of my attention,my focus, myself.
author: Toba Beta

text: Let others lead small lives, but not you.. Let others argue over small things, but not you.. Let others cry over small hurts, but not you.. Let others leave their future in someone else's hands, but not you.
author: Jim Rohn

text: LearningTo believe you are magnificent. And gradually to discover that you are not magnificent. Enough labor for one human life.
author: Czesław Miłosz

text: Totally without hope one cannot live. To live without hope is to cease to live. Hell is hopelessness. It is no accident that above the entrance to Dante's hell is the inscription: "Leave behind all hope, you who enter here.
author: Jurgen Moltmann

text: If you don’t like the road you’re walking, start paving another one.
author: Dolly Parton

text: Life is like an onion; you peel it off one layer at a time, and sometimes you weep.
author: Carl Sandburg

text: The way I define happiness is being the creator of your experience, choosing to take pleasure in what you have, right now, regardless of the circumstances, while being the best you that you can be.
author: Leo Babauta

text: There is no such thing as work-life balance. Everything worth fighting for unbalances your life.
author: Alain de Botton

text: It is not so much the major events as the small day-to-day decisions that map the course of our living. . . Our lives are, in reality, the sum total of our seemingly unimportant decisions and of our capacity to live by those decisions.
author: Gordon B. Hinckley

text: For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.
author: Aristotle

text: I will be stronger than my sadness.
author: Jasmine Warga

text: Let your life lightly dance on the edges of Time like dew on the tip of a leaf.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: I can't. You can't rewrite the past.
author: Jay Asher

text: I know I wrote letters to people with no address on this earth, I know that you are dead. But I hear you. I hear all of you. We were here. Our lives matter.
author: Ava Dellaira

text: Our most significant opportunities will be found in times of greatest difficulty.
author: Thomas S. Monson

text: While there's life, there's hope.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: Autobiography begins with a sense of being alone. It is an orphan form.
author: John Berger

text: People don't always want to be with people. It gets tiring.
author: Emma Donoghue

text: I would not have you descend into your own dream. I would have you be a conscious citizen of this terrible and beautiful world.
author: Ta-Nehisi Coates

text: Anything can happen in life, especially nothing.
author: Michel Houellebecq

text: I need you to be happy. I need one of us to be happy.
author: Holly Black

text: Do nothing, and nothing happens. Life is about decisions. You either make them or they're made for you, but you can't avoid them.
author: Mhairi McFarlane

text: Life is short. Focus on what really matters most
author: you should change your priorities over time.

text: We are all ordinary. We are all boring. We are all spectacular. We are all shy. We are all bold. We are all heroes. We are all helpless. It just depends on the day.
author: Brad Meltzer

text: Don't blow off another's candle for it won't make yours shine brighter.
author: Jaachynma N.E. Agu

text: I want to see beauty. In the ugly, in the sink, in the suffering, in the daily, in all the days before I die, the moments before I sleep.
author: Ann Voskamp

text: If a man is to live, he must be all alive, body, soul, mind, heart, spirit.
author: Thomas Merton

text: Life has but one true charm: the charm of the game. But what if we’re indifferent to whether we win or lose?
author: Charles Baudelaire

text: Each time we don't say what we wanna say, we're dying.
author: Yoko Ono

text: I wonder if I'll ever have to decide which is worse, life as we're living or no life at all.
author: Susan Beth Pfeffer

text: Instead of focusing on how much you can accomplish, focus on how much you can absolutely love what you’re doing.
author: Leo Babauta

text: If you can spend a perfectly useless afternoon in a perfectly useless manner, you have learned how to live
author: Lin Yutang

text: Demasiada cordura puede ser la peor de las locuras, ver la vida como es y no como debería de ser.Too much sanity may be madness. And maddest of all, to see life as it is and not as it should be.
author: Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra

text: You know, life fractures us all into little pieces. It harms us, but it's how we glue those fractures back together that make us stronger.
author: Carrie Jones

text: I want to keep my dreams, even bad ones, because without them, I might have nothing all night long.
author: Joseph Heller

text: Having perfected our disguise, we spend our lives searching for someone we don’t fool.
author: Robert Brault

text: I know there is no straight road No straight road in this world Only a giant labyrinth Of intersecting crossroads
author: Federico Garcia Lorca

text: There's no benchmark for how life's "supposed" to happen. There is no ideal world for you to wait around for. The world is always just what it is now, it's up to you how you respond to it.
author: Isaac Marion

text: Self-consciousness is the enemy of all art, be it acting, writing, painting, or living itself, which is the greatest art of all.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: To leave, after all, was not the same as being left.
author: Anita Shreve

text: Real life is only ever just real life. Messy. What it means depends on how you look at it. The only thing you’ve got to do is find a way to live there.
author: Patrick Ness

text: I am afraid to show you who I really am, because if I show you who I really am, you might not like it--and that's all I got.
author: Sabrina Ward Harrison

text: When all else fails, there's always delusion.
author: Conan O'Brien

text: Home is where somebody notices when you are no longer there.
author: Aleksandar Hemon

text: And meanwhile time goes about its immemorial work of making everyone look and feel like shit.
author: Martin Amis

text: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
author: Epictetus

text: If you don't learn to laugh at life it'll surely kill you, that I know.
author: Brom

text: Besides the noble art of getting things done, there is the noble art of leaving things undone. The wisdom of life consists in the elimination of non-essentials.
author: Lin Yutang

text: Dance above the surface of the world. Let your thoughts lift you into creativity that is not hampered by opinion.
author: Red Haircrow

text: You don't put your life into your books, you find it there.
author: Alan Bennett

text: People are the most difficult thing in the world to change
author: Gena Showalter

text: If you cannot find peace within yourself, you will never find it anywhere else.
author: Marvin Gaye

text: My hope still is to leave the world a bit better than when I got here.
author: Jim Henson

text: We all make choices, but in the end our choices make us.
author: Ken Levine

text: How easy it was to capitalize on a person’s own bent for self-destruction; how simple to nudge them into non-being, then to stand back and shrug and agree that it had been the inevitable result of a chaotic, catastrophic life.
author: Robert Galbraith

text: Life keeps throwing me lemons because I make the best lemonade...
author: King James Gadsden

text: We have to lie to ourselves to live. Otherwise, we'd go crazy.
author: Patrick Ness

text: Life is at its best when everything has fallen out of place, and you decide that you're going to fight to get them right, not when everything is going your way and everyone is praising you.
author: Thisuri Wanniarachchi

text: Create your own miracles
author: do what you think you cannot do.

text: Be still, sad heart! and cease repining;Behind the clouds is the sun still shining;Thy fate is the common fate of all,Into each life some rain must fall
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

text: I hope you will go out and let stories, that is life, happen to you, and that you will work with these stories... water them with your blood and tears and your laughter till they bloom, till you yourself burst into bloom.
author: Clarissa Pinkola Estes

text: It’s probably my job to tell you life isn’t fair, but I figure you already know that. So instead, I’ll tell you that hope is precious, and you’re right not to give up.
author: C.J. Redwine

text: What a waste my life would be without all the beautiful mistakes I've made.
author: Alice Bag

text: If people do not believe that mathematics is simple, it is only because they do not realize how complicated life is.
author: John von Neumann

text: All paths are present, always... and we can but choose among them.
author: Jacqueline Carey

text: I think, that if the world were a bit more like ComicCon, it would be a better place.
author: Matt Smith

text: In wisdom gathered over time I have found that every experience is a form of exploration.
author: Ansel Adams

text: The connections we make in the course of a life--maybe that's what heaven is.
author: Fred Rogers

text: The life you have left is a gift. Cherish it. Enjoy it now, to the fullest. Do what matters, now.
author: Leo Babauta

text: Life takes a bit of time and a lot of relationship.
author: William Paul Young

text: Those who have failed to work toward the truth have missed the purpose of living.
author: Gautama Buddha

text: Sometimes you need a little crisis to get your adrenaline flowing and help you realize your potential.
author: Jeannette Walls

text: Muscles aching to work, minds aching to create - this is man.
author: John Steinbeck

text: People who say that yesterday was better than today are ultimately devaluing their own existence.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: A stumble may prevent a fall.
author: Thomas Fuller

text: Strange, I thought, how you can be living your dreams and your nightmares at the very same time.
author: Ransom Riggs

text: In the haunted house of life, art is the only stair that doesn't creak.
author: Tom Robbins

text: It frightens me, the awful truth, of how sweet life can be...
author: Bob Dylan

text: What kind of life can you have in a house without books?
author: Sherman Alexie

text: It's paradoxical that the idea of living a long life appeals to everyone, but the idea of getting old doesn't appeal to anyone.
author: Andy Rooney

text: The behavior of a human being in sexual matters is often a prototype for the whole of his other modes of reaction in life.
author: Sigmund Freud

text: The past can teach us, nurture us, but it cannot sustain us. The essence of life is change, and we must move ever forward or the soul will wither and die.
author: Susanna Kearsley

text: I've been embarrassing myself since about birth.
author: Phil Lester

text: Each new day is a blank page in the diary of your life. The secret of success is in turning that diary into the best story you possibly can.
author: Douglas Pagels

text: If you haven't confidence in self, you are twice defeated in the race of life. With confidence, you have won even before you have started.
author: Marcus Garvey

text: One of the tragic things I know about human nature is that all of us tend to put off living. We are all dreaming of some magical rose garden over the horizon - instead of enjoying the roses that are blooming outside our windows today.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: There are a lot of things I wish I would have done, instead of just sitting around and complaining about having a boring life.
author: Kurt Cobain

text: Life is full of grief, to exactly the degree we allow ourselves to love other people.
author: Orson Scott Card

text: Suspect each moment, for it is a thief, tiptoeing away with more than it brings.
author: John Updike

text: If fate doesn't make you laugh, you just don't get the joke.
author: Gregory David Roberts

text: To succeed in life, you need three things: a wishbone, a backbone and a funnybone.
author: Reba McEntire

text: Whether we like it or not, the one justification for the existence of all religions is death, they need death as much as we need bread to eat.
author: Jose Saramago

text: Life is a winking light in the darkness.
author: Hayao Miyazaki

text: Don't wish it was easier wish you were better. Don't wish for less problems wish for more skills. Don't wish for less challenge wish for more wisdom
author: Jim Rohn

text: When we fail to set boundaries and hold people accountable, we feel used and mistreated. This is why we sometimes attack who they are, which is far more hurtful than addressing a behavior or a choice.
author: Brene Brown

text: In my many years I have come to a conclusion that one useless man is a shame, two is a law firm, and three or more is a congress.
author: Peter Stone

text: It’s always about timing. If it’s too soon, no one understands. If it’s too late, everyone’s forgotten.
author: Anna Wintour

text: Hurt is a part of life. To be honest, I think hurt is a part of happiness, that our definition of happiness has gotten very narrow lately, very nervous, a little afraid of this brawling, fabulous, unpredictable world.
author: Julian Gough

text: Life is such a glorious trauma, is it not?
author: J.R. Ward

text: How can I be substantial if I do not cast a shadow? I must have a dark side also If I am to be whole
author: C.G. Jung

text: Dogs are great. Bad dogs, if you can really call them that, are perhaps the greatest of them all.
author: John Grogan

text: That woman doesn't have the sense God gave a retarded flea.
author: Nora Roberts

text: Life is a moderately good play with a badly written third act.
author: Truman Capote

text: Well, sometimes I worry that my whole life will be based about what's comfortable and easy. I'll care too much about what makes me feel good to ever really reach for anything. And then I worry that even if I do, I won't succeed.
author: Julia Hoban

text: It is beautiful, it is endless, it is full and yet seems empty. It hurts us.
author: Jackson Pearce

text: The world is wide, and I will not waste my life in friction when it could be turned into momentum.
author: Frances E. Willard

text: Life consists of two days, one for you one against you. So when it's for you don't be proud or reckless, and when it's against you be patient, for both days are test for you.
author: Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu-Talib A.S

text: Tried living in the real world instead of a shell, but I was bored before I even began.
author: Morrissey

text: What I am telling you, before you begin my story, is this -- two things: I crave truth. And I lie.
author: Tana French

text: My formula for life is very simple: in the morning, wake up; at night, go to sleep. In between I try and occupy myself as best I can.
author: Cary Grant

text: Too late, I found you can't wait to become perfect, you got to go out and fall down and get up with everybody else.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: You can't do anything about the length of your life, but you can do something about its width and depth.
author: H.L. Mencken

text: Bring something incomprehensible into the world!
author: Gilles Deleuze

text: Sometimes it's better to stop trying to make sense of things. Life isn't clear cut, there are always gray areas.
author: Alexandra Adornetto

text: I said "Somebody should do something about that." Then I realized I am somebody.
author: Lily Tomlin

text: We are the cosmos made conscious and life is the means by which the universe understands itself.
author: Brian Cox

text: I’m not a bad person. I haven’t killed anyone. I (rarely) lie. I don’t kick little puppies. So why do people look at me as if the world would be a better place without me?
author: Gena Showalter

text: Like gravity, karma is so basic we often don't even notice it.
author: Sakyong Mipham

text: Keep your friends close but your enemies closer.
author: Mario Puzo

text: Your hand can seize today, but not tomorrow; and thoughts of your tomorrow are nothing but desire. Don’t waste this breath, if your heart isn’t crazy, since "the rest of your life" won’t last forever.
author: Omar Khayyam

text: Spend the afternoon, you can't take it with you.
author: Annie Dillard

text: He found himself understanding the wearisomeness of this life, where every path was an improvisation and a considerable part of one's waking life was spent watching one's feet.
author: William Golding

text: Or, rather, let us be more simple and less vain.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: Slow down and enjoy life. It's not only the scenery you miss by going too fast - you also miss the sense of where you are going and why.
author: Eddie Cantor

text: Life is the farce we are all forced to endure.
author: Arthur Rimbaud

text: Everybody's damaged by something.
author: Emma Donoghue

text: You're always with yourself, so you might as well enjoy the company.
author: Diane Von Furstenberg

text: You are never alone. You are eternally connected with everyone.
author: Amit Ray

text: The friends you make in college are friends you'll have for life, even if you don't talk for years at a time.
author: Jessica Park

text: If one has no sense of humor, one is in trouble.
author: Betty White

text: A lot of the conflict you have in your life exists simply because you're not living in alignment
author: you're not be being true to yourself.

text: Life is like a play: it's not the length, but the excellence of the acting that matters.
author: Seneca

text: You'd think, 'What if I make a mistake today, I'll regret it'. I don’t believe in regret, I feel everything leads us to where we are and we have to just jump forward, mean well, commit and just see what happens.
author: Angelina Jolie

text: In one drop of water are found all the secrets of all the oceans
author: in one aspect of You are found all the aspects of existence.

text: But instead of spending our lives running towards our dreams, we are often running away from a fear of failure or a fear of criticism.
author: Eric Wright

text: The spirit of a man is constructed out of his choices.
author: Irvin D. Yalom

text: Memory can make a thing seem to have been much more than it was.
author: Marilynne Robinson

text: For those who believe, no proof is necessary. For those who don't believe, no proof is possible.
author: Stuart Chase

text: No matter how qualified or deserving we are, we will never reach a better life until we can imagine it for ourselves and allow ourselves to have it.
author: Richard Bach

text: Because there was a hunger in me to see everything and do everything. I wanted to be everyone I saw. I wasn't enough for me. Can you understand that?
author: Sidney Sheldon

text: Leonard asks me if there's anything I need to know before he dies, I think about it for a minute, turn to him, say what's the meaning of life, Leonard? He laughs, says that's an easy one, my son, it's whatever you want it to be.
author: James Frey

text: Everyone is lonely, we have to remember that life is to be lived one day at a time. You cannot worry about the past or future. Happiness is in the now.
author: Claudia Gray

text: When I look at my old pictures, all I can see is what I used to be but am no longer. I think: What I can see is what I am not.
author: Aleksandar Hemon

text: The future is meant for those who are willing to let go of the worst parts of the past. When you cannot take two steps without turning around to inspect your footsteps, you are getting nowhere fast.
author: Corey Taylor

text: All our knowledge has its origin in our perceptions
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Don't leave it all unsaid,somewhere in the wasteland of your head.
author: Morrissey

text: You couldnt erase everything that caused you pain with recollection.Every memory was valuable
author: even the bad ones

text: Find ecstasy in life
author: the mere sense of living is joy enough.

text: A quitter never wins-and-a winner never quits.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: I had a dream about you last night. The champagne was non-alcoholic. You didn't notice, and laughed at my jokes anyway.
author: Michael  Summers

text: A celibate clergy is an especially good idea, because it tends to suppress any hereditary propensity toward fanaticism.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Life is ours to be spent, not tobe saved.
author: D.H. Lawrence

text: Many people pray to be kept out of unexpected problems. Some people pray to be able to confront and overcome them.
author: Toba Beta

text: Live all you can: it's a mistake not to. It doesn't matter what you do in particular, so long as you have had your life. If you haven't had that, what have you had?
author: Henry James

text: What keeps life fascinating is the constant creativity of the soul.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: Why not just live in the moment, especially if it has a good beat?
author: Goldie Hawn

text: Poor creatures. What did we do to you? With all our schemes and plans?
author: Kazuo Ishiguro

text: There are two primary choices in life: to accept conditions as they exist, or accept the responsibility for changing them
author: Denis Waitley

text: I tried to contain myself... but I escaped!
author: Gary Paulsen

text: Scars show us where we have been, they do not dictate where we are going.
author: David Rossi

text: Life is half delicious yogurt, half crap, and your job is to keep the plastic spoon in the yogurt.
author: Scott Adams

text: Life makes fools of all of us sooner or later. But keep your sense of humor and you'll at least be able to take your humiliations with some measure of grace. In the end, you know, its our own expectations that crush us.
author: Paul Murray

text: No, I regret nothing, all I regret is having been born, dying is such a long tiresome business I always found.
author: Samuel Beckett

text: ... you don't have to wait for someone to treat you bad repeatedly. All it takes is once, and if they get away with it that once, if they know they can treat you like that, then it sets the pattern for the future.
author: Jane Green

text: Life is grace. Sleep is forgiveness. The night absolves. Darkness wipes the slate clean, not spotless to be sure, but clean enough for another day's chalking.
author: Frederick Buechner

text: Stop asking God to bless what you're doing. Find out what God's doing. It's already blessed.
author: Bono

text: Each day is God's gift to you. What you do with it is your gift to Him.
author: T.D. Jakes

text: The reason why the universe is eternal is that it does not live for itself
author: it gives life to others as it transforms.

text: Know who you are, and be it. Know what you want, and go out and get it!
author: Carroll Bryant

text: The past can not be cured.
author: Elizabeth I

text: There is one who remembers the way to your door: Life you may evade, but Death you shall not.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: Some people insist that 'mediocre' is better than 'best.' They delight in clipping wings because they themselves can't fly. They despise brains because they have none.
author: Robert A. Heinlein

text: Life sucks, then you die. Then it sucks again.
author: Tonya Hurley

text: I think when you become a parent you go from being a star in the movie of your own life to the supporting player in the movie of someone else's.
author: Craig Ferguson

text: You can play it safe, and I wouldn't blame you for it. You can continue as you've been doing, and you'll survive, but is that what you want? Is that enough?
author: J.M. Darhower

text: We cannot, after all, judge a biography by its length, by the number of pages in it; we must judge by the richness of the contents...Sometimes the 'unfinisheds' are among the most beautiful symphonies.
author: Viktor E. Frankl

text: People are not lazy, they simply have impotent goals..that is..goals that do not inspire them.
author: Anthony Robbins

text: Being born is like being kidnapped. And then sold into slavery.
author: Andy Warhol

text: I'm not sure if there's one right place I'm supposed to be, he said, but I know a couple of wrong places I'd give a second try in a heartbeat.
author: Brian Andreas

text: Accidents don't happen to people who take accidents as a personal insult.
author: Mario Puzo

text: War is not for winning, Masha," sighed Koschei, reading the tracks of supply lines, of pincer strategies, over her shoulder. "It is for surviving.
author: Catherynne M. Valente

text: Come clean with a child heartLaugh as peaches in the summer windLet rain on a house roof be a songLet the writing on your facebe a smell of apple orchards on late June.
author: Carl Sandburg

text: Vital lives are about action. You can't feel warmth unless you create it, can't feel delight until you play, can't know serendipity unless you risk.
author: Joan Erickson

text: Real life is messy, inconsistent, and it's seldom when anything ever really gets resolved. It's taken me a long time to realize that.
author: Alan Moore

text: To live greatly, we must develop the capacity to face trouble with courage, disappointment with cheerfulness, and triumph with humility.
author: Thomas S. Monson

text: You are always a little bit wrong
author: Hank Green

text: Don't be afraid of the shadows, that only means there's a light nearby.
author: Evanescence

text: Birthdays could be such a bummer when you were older than the country you lived in.
author: Lynsay Sands

text: Life would be unbearably dull if we had answers to all our questions.
author: Jim Butcher

text: All the stuff you can’t wait to get away from, until it’s not there anymore, and then you miss it like crazy.
author: Morgan Matson

text: Life is full of beauty. Notice it. Notice the bumble bee, the small child, and the smiling faces. Smell the rain, and feel the wind. Live your life to the fullest potential, and fight for your dreams.
author: Ashley  Smith

text: Oh, for the years I have not lived, but only dreamed of living.
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

text: it takes as much courage to have tried and failed as it does to have tried and succeded.
author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

text: Even a happy life cannot be without a measure of darkness, and the word happy would lose its meaning if it were not balanced by sadness.
author: C.G. Jung

text: It's amazing how quickly nature consumes human places after we turn our backs on them. Life is a hungry thing.
author: Scott Westerfeld

text: When life doesn't meet your expectations, it was important to take it with grace.
author: Patricia Briggs

text: it always sound scarier when a hollerer talk soft.
author: Kathryn Stockett

text: I feel no need for any other faith than my faith in the kindness of human beings. I am so absorbed in the wonder of earth and the life upon it that I cannot think of heaven and angels.
author: Pearl S. Buck

text: Decide what you want to be....Pay the Price ...And be what you want to be.
author: John A. Widtsoe

text: No matter what happened yesterday it is insignificant when compared to what lies within the core of your being today.
author: Sandy Brewer

text: The longer we live, the more weight we carry in our hearts.
author: Ai Yazawa

text: You just have to go after what you want and if it doesn't want you back then so be it
author: it doesn't deserve you anyways.

text: Hope is not a feeling of certainty that everything ends well. Hope is just a feeling that life and work have a meaning.
author: Vaclav Havel

text: The only certainty life contains is death.
author: Patricia Briggs

text: You're only young once, but you can be immature forever
author: Germaine Greer

text: This new day has greeted us with no rules
author: unconditional opportunity. Do not dilute the power of this new day with the hardship of yesterday. Greet this day the way it has greeted you

text: I had a dream about you last night.. you were holding a pine cone and introducing him as Gerald.
author: Nicole McKay

text: It’s life. You don’t figure it out. You just climb up on the beast and ride.
author: Rebecca Wells

text: There are times in our lives when we have to realize our past is precisely what it is, and we cannot change it. But we can change the story we tell ourselves about it, and by doing that, we can change the future.
author: Eleanor Brown

text: You can't regret the life you didn't lead.
author: Junot Diaz

text: Love changes things
author: hope allows us to endure until the change arrives.

text: It's faith in something and enthusiasm for something that makes a life worth living.
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes Sr.

text: It is always betterto avenge dear ones than to indulge in mourning.For every one of us, living in this worldmeans waiting for our end. Let whoever canwin glory before death. When a warrior is gone,that will be his best and only bulwark.
author: Seamus Heaney

text: For one day there will be nothing left, yet we will have something. -me-I tried to wait for my life to begin. Something has to happen. Like an amazing boy. I know he's out there. I just have to find him.
author: Susane Colasanti

text: ..because never in my life have I ever been picked when there was another alternative.
author: Jasmine Warga

text: You have every right to a beautiful life.
author: Selena Gomez

text: That's not a bad word...hate and war are bad words, but fuck isn't.
author: Judy Blume

text: Don't let your luggage define your travels, each life unravels differently.
author: Shane L. Koyczan

text: I realize that life is risks. It’s acknowledging the past but looking forward. It’s taking a chance that we willmake mistakes but believing that we all deserve to be forgiven.
author: Carrie Ryan

text: The spiritual life does not remove us from the world but leads us deeper into it
author: Henri J.M. Nouwen

text: Life is a spark between two identical voids, the darkness before birth and the one after death.
author: Irvin D. Yalom

text: Life is life. You choose to live it or you do not.
author: Carrie Ryan

text: We must all suffer from one of two pains: the pain of discipline or the pain of regret. The difference is discipline weighs ounces while regret weighs tons.
author: Jim Rohn

text: The ego relies on the familiar. It is reluctant to experience the unknown, which is they very essence of life.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: This is the strangest life I have ever known.
author: Jim Morrison

text: In life, there are no mistakes, only lessons.
author: Vic Johnson

text: Fuck it... That's really the attitude that keeps a family together, it's not "we love each other", it's just "fuck it, man.
author: Louis C.K.

text: There is no thing as a single-issue struggle because we do not live single-issue lives.
author: Audre Lorde

text: There is nothing perfect...only life.
author: Sue Monk Kidd

text: Some say life is the thing, but I prefer reading.
author: Ruth Rendell

text: God put us here, on this carnival ride. We close our eyes never knowing where it'll take us next.
author: Carrie Underwood

text: You will receive everything you need when you stop asking for what you do not need
author: Nisargadatta Maharaj

text: The best way to prepare for life is to begin to live.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: When nothing is sure, everything is possible.
author: Margaret Drabble

text: Atticus had said it was the polite thing to talk to people about what they were interested in, not about what you were interested in.
author: Harper Lee

text: We need people in our lives with whom we can be as open as possible. To have real conversations with people may seem like such a simple, obvious suggestion, but it involves courage and risk.
author: Thomas  Moore

text: You know, people ask me. They say 'Dan, three years later do you really want to be drawing cat whiskers on your face?' but they don't understand. The cat whiskers, they come from within.
author: Dan Howell

text: Everything will be alright in the end so if it is not alright it is not the end.
author: Deborah Moggach

text: It takes no more time to see the good side of life than to see the bad.
author: Jimmy Buffett

text: They say that not matter how old you become, when you are with your siblings, you revert back to childhood.
author: Karen   White

text: If you think something is missing in your life, it is probably YOU...
author: Robert Holden

text: Life is a compromise of what your ego wants to do, what experience tells you to do, and what your nerves let you do.
author: Walter Bagehot

text: It's okay. We aren't in the same class. Just don't forget that some of us watch the sunset too.
author: S.E. Hinton

text: Dreamers are mocked as impractical. The truth is they are the most practical, as their innovations lead to progress and a better way of life for all of us.
author: Robin S. Sharma

text: Life is not fair get used to used to it
author: Bill Gates

text: Many things are not as they seem: The worst things in life never are.
author: Jim Butcher

text: The Way is not in the sky
author: the Way is in the heart.

text: Life starts out with everyone clapping when you take a poo and goes downhill from there.
author: Sloane Crosley

text: Life is something that happens when you can't get to sleep.
author: Fran Lebowitz

text: I am still in the land of the dying
author: I shall be in the land of the living soon. (his last words)

text: Being faithful in the smallest things is the way to gain, maintain, and demonstrate the strength needed to accomplish something great.
author: Alex  Harris

text: Paper is more patient than man.
author: Anne Frank

text: It is your responsibility to find fault with me, it is mine to hear you out. But don't expect me to change.
author: John Irving

text: Yesterday was a closed book, tomorrow, however, was another story.
author: Cecelia Ahern

text: I need to stop fantasizing about running away to some other life and start figuring out the one I have.
author: Holly Black

text: Only when you accept that one day you'll die can you let go, and make the best out of life. And that's the big secret. That's the miracle.
author: Gabriel Ba

text: To pay attention, this is our endless and proper work.
author: Mary Oliver

text: I can chase you, and I can catch you,but there is nothing I can do to make you mine.
author: Morrissey

text: The path to success is to take massive, determined action.
author: Anthony Robbins

text: Before I can tell my life what I want to do with it, I must listen to my life telling me who I am.
author: Parker J. Palmer

text: Don't set your goals by what other people deem important.
author: Jaachynma N.E. Agu

text: The people I distrust most are those who want to improve our lives but have only one course of action.
author: Frank Herbert

text: I am my own experiment. I am my own work of art.
author: Madonna

text: I want to drag knives over my skin, just to feel something other than shame, but I'm not even brave enough for that
author: Paula Hawkins

text: A life isn't measured in hours and minutes. It's the quality, not the length.
author: Gabrielle Zevin

text: It's impossible to go through life unscathed. Nor should you want to. By the hurts we accumulate, we measure both our follies and our accomplishments.
author: Christopher Paolini

text: Our life is made by the death of others.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: ...Life is much simpler if you don’t notice anything....
author: Tom Upton

text: Life… For some it is battle for others its dance...For rich it is party for poor it is chance!
author: Nino Varsimashvili

text: Every path is the right path. Everything could've been anything else.﻿ And it would have just as much meaning.
author: Mr. Nobody

text: I had a dream about you. I was sitting on your couch, relating my succession of ideas on subconscious influence. I asked you what they meant, and you told me that free associations were a bad way to advance my political career.
author: Bauvard

text: Every ounce of my cynicism is supported by historical precedent.
author: Glen Cook

text: Oh, God of Dust and Rainbows,Help us to seeThat without the dust the rainbowWould not be.
author: Langston Hughes

text: Hope is such a beautiful word, but it often seems very fragile. Life is still being needlessly hurt and destroyed.
author: Michael  Jackson

text: You know my name, not my story. You've heard what I've done, but not what I've been through.
author: Jonathan Anthony Burkett

text: The heart may freeze, or it can burn. The pain will ease and I can learn. There is no future, there is no past. I live this moment as, my last.
author: Jonathan Larson

text: To be heroic is to be courageous enough to die for something
author: to be inspirational is to be crazy enough to live a little.

text: Atheism, a religion dedicated to its own sense of smug superiority.
author: Stephen Colbert

text: Be a true Heart, not a follower.
author: Ed Sheeran

text: Properly practiced, knitting soothes the troubled spirit, and it doesn't hurt the untroubled spirit either.
author: Elizabeth Zimmermann

text: Life is a perpetual yesterday for us.
author: Alice Sebold

text: Desire is half of life
author: indifference is half of death.

text: In examining disease, we gain wisdom about anatomy and physiology and biology. In examining the person with disease, we gain wisdom about life.
author: Oliver Sacks

text: Sometimes you just have those days where everything goes wrong. But sometimes, and totally unexpectedly, something can go right.
author: Leila Sales

text: life and love are very precious when both are in full bloom.
author: Louisa May Alcott

text: You live it forward, but understand it backward.
author: Abraham Verghese

text: But I have my life, I’m living it. It’s twisted, exhausting, uncertain, and full of guilt, but nonetheless, there’s something there.
author: Banana Yoshimoto

text: If you want something you can have it, but only if you want everything that goes with it, including all the hard work and the despair, and only if you're willing to risk failure.
author: Philip Pullman

text: Lottery: A tax on people who are bad at math.
author: Ambrose Bierce

text: We started off trying to set up a small anarchist community, but people wouldn't obey the rules.
author: Alan Bennett

text: You wanted to feel alive, right? It doesn't matter if you're monster or human. Living hurts.
author: Victoria Schwab

text: May all that have life be delivered from suffering
author: Gautama Buddha

text: The whole house seemed to exhale a melancholy breath of emptiness
author: Michael Chabon

text: Art and life are subjective. Not everybody's gonna dig what I dig, but I reserve the right to dig it.
author: Whoopi Goldberg

text: When you're the most happening person at the party, it's time to leave
author: Kelly Cutrone

text: If her enemies were Brigan's friends and her friends were Brigan's enemies, then the two of them could walk through the world arm in arm and never be hit by arrows again.
author: Kristin Cashore

text: The greatest wealth is health
author: Virgil

text: Remembering that you are going to die is the best way I know to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Letting go is the lesson. Letting go is always the lesson. Have you ever noticed how much of our agony is all tied up with craving and loss?
author: Susan Gordon Lydon

text: The difference between school and life? In school, you're taught a lesson and then given a test. In life, you're given a test that teaches you a lesson.
author: Tom Bodett

text: It doesn't matter who you are, or where you come from, or how much money you've got in your pocket. You have your own destiny and your own life ahead of you.
author: Lady Gaga

author: Wendy Wasserstein

text: Oh sorry, I was taking life seriously.
author: Bill Hicks

text: The young always have the same problem - how to rebel and conform at the same time. They have now solved this by defying their parents and copying one another.
author: Quentin Crisp

text: I think of life as a good book. The further you get into it, the more it begins to make sense.
author: Harold S. Kushner

text: If you want to be a new man you have to stay in new places, and do new things, with people who never knew you before. If you go back to the same old ways, what else can you be but the same old person?

text: Kindness, I’ve discovered, is everything in life.
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer

text: One day can change your life. One day can ruin your life. All life is is three or four big days that change everything.
author: Beverly Donofrio

text: It is in your moments of decision that your destiny is shaped.
author: Anthony Robbins

text: You will be fine,' the fortune teller says. 'There may be decisions to make and surprises in store. Life takes us to unexpected places sometimes. The future is never set in stone, remember that.
author: Erin Morgenstern

text: Look, everyone talks about the unknown like it's some big scary thing, but it's the familiar that's always bothered me. It's heavy, builds up around you like rocks, until it's walls and a ceiling and a cell.
author: V.E. Schwab

text: Life’s too short to drink crappy coffee and cry over boys who don’t care.
author: Unknown

text: That's all you can do in this world, no matter how strong the current beats against you, or how heavy your burden, or how tragic your love story. You keep going.
author: Robyn Schneider

text: I have given my word that only death will take me from you.
author: Philippa Gregory

text: Our sweetest songs are those of saddest thought.
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

text: She smiled and said with an ecstatic air: "It shines like a little diamond","What does?""This moment. It is round, it hangs in empty space like a little diamond; I am eternal.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: I am a person who is unhappy with things as they stand. We cannot accept the world as it is. Each day we should wake up foaming at the mouth because of the injustice of things.

text: There had been times when he knew, somewhere in him, that he would get used to it, whatever it was, because he had learnt that some hard things became softer after a very little while.

text: We can regard our life as a uselessly disturbing episode in the blissful repose of nothingness.
author: Arthur Schopenhauer

text: You can't have living without dying. So you can't call it living, what we got. We just are, we just be, like rocks beside the road.
author: Natalie Babbitt

text: Being liked for the way you looked is worse than not being liked at all.

text: I don't understand a thing about this world: about people, and why they do the things they do. The more I find out, the more I uncover, the more I know, the less I understand.
author: Craig Silvey

text: I saw the world from the stars' point of view, and it looked unbearably lonely.
author: Shaun David Hutchinson

text: The world is merciless, and it's also very beautiful.
author: Hajime Isayama

text: Love me when I least deserve it, because that's when I really need it.
author: Swedish Proverb

text: The main facts in human life are five: birth, food, sleep, love and death.
author: E.M. Forster

text: A 'no' does not hide anything, but a 'yes' very easily becomes a deception.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: Nothing in this world compares to the comfort and security of having someone just hold your hand.

text: This worldthat was our homefor a brief spellnever brought us anythingbut pain and grief
author: its a shame that not one of our problemswas ever solved.We departwith a thousand regretsin our hearts.

text: She laughed for her wasted, difficult life that never had to be wasted or difficult in the first place.
author: Leslye Walton

text: The world's a puzzle; no need to make sense out of it." - Socrates
author: Dan Millman

text: Silence is argument carried out by other means.
author: Ernesto Che Guevara

text: I finally figured out that not every crisis can be managed. As much as we want to keep ourselves safe, we can't protect ourselves from everything. If we want to embrace life, we also have to embrace chaos.
author: Susan Elizabeth Phillips

text: dont be afraid of death, be afraid of the unlived life.
author: Natalie Babbitt

text: Now I've gone for too longLiving like I'm not aliveSo I'm going to start over tonightBeginning with you and I
author: Hayley Williams

text: We remember the past, live in the present, and write the future.
author: Shaun David Hutchinson

text: You will have to make up for the smallness of your size by your courage and selfless devotion to duty, for it is not life that matters, but the courage, fortitude and determination you bring to it.
