text: The only thing you have to do in this life is die," said Mrs. Pinsky..."everything else is a choice.
author: Karen Thompson Walker

text: Tomorrow belongs to those who can hear it coming
author: David Bowie

text: it was when I stopped searching for home within others and lifted the foundations of home within myself I found there were no roots more intimate than those between a mind and body that have decided to be whole.
author: Rupi Kaur

text: Toby Zeigler: There's literally no one in the world I don't hate right now.
author: Aaron Sorkin

text: You know why love stories have happy endings?” I shake my head.“Because they end too early,” she continues. “They always end right at the kiss. You never have to see all the bullshit that comes later. You know, Life.
author: Hank Moody

text: If my answers frighten you then you should cease asking scary questions.
author: Quentin Tarantino

text: It is our failure to become our perceived ideal that ultimately defines us and makes us unique. It's not easy, but if you accept your misfortune and handle it right your perceived failure can become a catalyst for profound re-invention.
author: Conan O'Brien

text: There are some choices you can only make once. You can't go back to where you made a choice and then take the other one.
author: Mary Hoffman

text: Son, are you happy? I don't mean to pry,but do you dream of Heaven?Have you ever wanted to die?
author: Tim Burton

text: Oh threats of Hell and Hopes of Paradise! Life flies
author: One thing is certain and the rest is Lies -The Flower that once has blown forever dies.

text: If you're gonna burn a bridge behind you, make sure you've crossed it first.
author: Quentin R. Bufogle

text: Letting him goThere is a particular kind of suffering to be experienced when you love something greater than yourself. A tender sacrifice. Like the pained silence felt in the lost song of a mermaid
author: or the bent and broken feet of a dancing ballerina. It is in every considered step I am taking in the opposite direction of you.

text: Life' wrote a friend of mine, 'is a public performance on the violin, in which you must learn the instrument as you go along.
author: E.M. Forster

text: The power of God is with you at all times; through the activities of mind, senses, breathing, and emotions; and is constantly doing all the work using you as a mere instrument.
author: Anonymous

text: The mind is a wonderful servant, but a terrible master.
author: Robin S. Sharma

text: Dreams are hopeful because they exist as pure possibility. Unlike memories, which are fossils, long dead and buried deep.
author: Shaun David Hutchinson

text: After a long, labored sigh, I said, “She was really happy when I got there. I’m pretty sure she was suicidal when I left.”“You do have that effect on people.
author: Darynda Jones

text: The rain fluctuates between drizzle and torrential. It messes with your mind. It makes you think things will always be like this, never getting better, always letting you down right when you though the worst was over.
author: Susane Colasanti

text: Living might mean taking chances, but they're worth taking. Loving might be a mistake. but its worth making
author: Reba McEntire

text: The problem with the designated driver program, it's not a desirable job, but if you ever get sucked into doing it, have fun with it. At the end of the night, drop them off at the wrong house.
author: Jeff Foxworthy

text: That’s the duty of the old, to be anxious on behalf of the young. And the duty of the young is to scorn the anxiety of the old.
author: Philip Pullman

text: One of the inescapable encumbrances of leading an interesting life is that there have to be moments when you almost lose it.
author: Jimmy Buffett

text: What was the point of even having a conversation when words couldn't be trusted?
author: James Dashner

text: You didn't just cheat on me
author: you cheated on us. You didn't just break my heart

text: I had a dream about you last night... you were a giant slinky and I watched you fall down the stairs.
author: Amy Sommers

text: Those who don't jump will never fly.
author: Leena Ahmad Almashat

text: I had a dream about you last night. I could fly. I was going to use this power to impress you, but you were too heavy to carry, so I won you over with my personality instead
author: Michael  Summers

text: End? No, the journey doesn't end here. Death is just another path. One that we all must take.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: In a well-ordered universe...
author: Morgan Matson

text: If you own this story you get to write the ending.
author: Brene Brown

text: In his face there came to be a brooding peace that is seen most often in the faces of the very sorrowful or the very wise. But still he wandered through the streets of the town, always silent and alone.
author: Carson McCullers

text: We do not need to eat animals, wear animals, or use animals for entertainment purposes, and our only defense of these uses is our pleasure, amusement, and convenience.
author: Gary L. Francione

text: We think you'll find that every woman in her heart of hearts longs for three things: to be romanced, to play an irreplaceable role in a great adventure, and to unveil beauty. That's what makes a woman come alive.
author: John Eldredge

text: I want to Live! Not Die, Not Hide, LIVE!
author: Margaret Peterson Haddix

text: It’s never overreacting to ask for what you want and need.
author: Amy Poehler

text: Do not forget that the value and interest of life is not so much to do conspicuous things...as to do ordinary things with the perception of their enormous value.
author: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin

text: Between living and dreaming there is a third thing. Guess it.
author: Antonio Machado

text: All teachings are mere references. The true experience is living your own life. Then, even the holiest of words are only words.
author: Ming-Dao Deng

text: Life is a dream from which we all must wake before we can dream again.
author: Robert Jordan

text: Life is hell, at some point we all just have to get used to it.
author: Alysha Speer

text: So I would choose to be with you,That's if the choice were mine to make,But you can make decisions too,And you can have this heart to break
author: Billy Joel

text: There is something incredibly nostalgic and significant about the annual cascade of autumn leaves.
author: Joe L. Wheeler

text: there isn't enough of anythingas long as we live. But at intervalsa sweetness appears and, given a chanceprevails.
author: Raymond Carver

text: Our lives were just beginning, our favorite moment was right now, our favorite songs were unwritten.
author: Rob Sheffield

text: Things will get easier, people's minds will change, and you should be alive to see it.
author: Ellen DeGeneres

text: Sometimes we love with nothing more than hope. Sometimes we cry with everything except tears. In the end that’s all there is: love and its duty, sorrow and its truth. In the end that’s all we have - to hold on tight until the dawn
author: Gregory David Roberts

text: Life is doubt,And faith without doubt is nothing but death.
author: Miguel de Unamuno

text: You are the only you ... You are the best you. You will always be the second best anyone else.
author: Leo Buscaglia

text: Pray for the dead and fight like hell for the living.
author: Mary Harris Jones

text: Whether you like it or not, you are committed to the human endeavor. I cannot ally myself with such a purely negative goal as avoidance of suffering. Suffering is a chance you take by the fact of being alive.
author: William S. Burroughs

text: We are star stuff harvesting sunlight.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Life lived in the absence of the psychedelic experience that primordial shamanism is based on is life trivialized, life denied, life enslaved to the ego.
author: Terence McKenna

text: Life is neither static nor unchanging. With no individuality, there can be no change, no adaptation and, in an inherently changing world, any species unable to adapt is also doomed.
author: Jean M. Auel

text: Most people gaze neither into the past nor the future
author: they explore neither truth nor lies. They gaze at the television.

text: You have succeeded in life when all you really WANT is only what you really NEED.
author: Vernon Howard

text: To escape fear, you have to go through it, not around.
author: Richie Norton

text: BRODIE:Hell hath no fury like a woman scorned for SEGA.
author: Kevin Smith

text: You can never know about about your own destiny: are the people you meet there to play a part on your oun destiny, or do you exist just to play a role in theirs?
author: Libba Bray

text: You may be disappointed if you fail, but you are doomed if you don’t try.
author: Beverly Sills

text: Even as your body betrays you, your mind denies it.
author: Sara Gruen

text: Dedicate yourself to what gives your life true meaning and purpose
author: make a positive difference in someone's life.

text: A sailor chooses the wind that takes the ship from a safe port. Ah, yes, but once you're abroad, as you have seen, winds have a mind of their own. Be careful, Charlotte, careful of the wind you choose.
author: Avi

text: Self-love is a good thing but self-awareness is more important. You need to once in a while go ‘Uh, I’m kind of an asshole.
author: Louis C.K.

text: Imagine no limitations
author: decide what's right and desirable before you decide what's possible.

text: Learning the truth has become my life's love.
author: Dan Brown

text: Be yourself. Don't worry about what other people are thinking of you, because they're probably feeling the same kind of scared, horrible feelings that everyone does.
author: Phil Lester

text: Clearly, America has no shortage of metaphorical opportunities for the poor.
author: Stephen Colbert

text: When it rains it pours. Maybe the art of life is to convert tough times to great experiences: we can choose to hate the rain or dance in it.
author: Joan Marques

text: Until death it is all life
author: Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra

text: Women only cut their hair in times of crisis... It's somethin' a woman always has the power to do, even when she loses control over everything else. Cuttin' hair is a cry for help.
author: Bella Pollen

text: Yet if I was asked to do this again - in fact, if I was ever asked to repeat any of my experiences - I'd have to say, fuck it, bring them on. I've no regrets.This is what it means to be alive.
author: John Barrowman

text: There are always loose ends in real life.
author: Robert Galbraith

text: No lake so still but it has its wave.No circle so perfect but that it has its blur.I would change things for you if I could
author: As I can't you must take them as they are.

text: But fate, as Merlin always taught us, is inexorable. Life is a jest of the Gods, Merlin liked to claim, and there is no justice. You must learn to laugh, he once told me, or else you'll just weep yourself to death.
author: Bernard Cornwell

text: There's a sorrow and pain in everyone's life, but every now and then there's a ray of light that melts the loneliness in your heart and brings comfort like hot soup and a soft bed.
author: Hubert Selby Jr.

text: True life lies in laughter, love and work.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: Sure it could get rough sometimes, but life wasn't a Hollywood movie. Shit happened. You fought, you screamed, and somehow you worked like hell to get out the other side still intact.
author: Samantha Young

text: my friends don't seem to be friends at all but people whose phone numbers I haven't lost.
author: Nick Hornby

text: Nalaman kong habang lumalaki ka, maraming beses kang madadapa. Bumangon ka man ulit o hindi, magpapatuloy ang buhay, iikot ang mundo, at mauubos ang oras.
author: Bob Ong

text: Life can take so many twists and turns. You can’t ever count yourself out. Even if you’re really afraid at some point, you can’t think that there’s no room for you to grow and do something good with your life.
author: Portia de Rossi

text: Because if you take a risk, you just might find what you're looking for.
author: Susane Colasanti

text: Know that the same spark of life that is within you, is within all of our animal friends, the desire to live is the same within all of us...
author: Rai Aren

text: When we cannot bear to be alone, it means we do not properly value the only companion we will have from birth to death - ourselves.
author: Eda J. LeShan

text: If you have nothing in life but a good friend, you're rich.
author: Michelle Kwan

text: Sometimes problems don’t require a solution to solve them
author: instead they require maturity to outgrow them.

text: I welcome questions. I hate assumptions.
author: Red Haircrow

text: Happiness is not the absence of problems
author: it's the ability to deal with them.

text: No one escapes from life alive.
author: Michael Crichton

text: You never really know what's coming. A small wave, or maybe a big one. All you can really do is hope that when it comes, you can surf over it, instead of drown in its monstrosity.
author: Alysha Speer

text: I don’t see myself being special; I just see myself having more responsibilities than the next man. People look to me to do things for them, to have answers.
author: Tupac Shakur

text: All I know is I make sense to me- it's other people who seem complicated.
author: Tara Kelly

text: At least one thing was consistent about her life: It just kept on getting more complicated.
author: Scott Westerfeld

text: I've come to know that what we want in life is the greatest indication of who we really are.
author: Richard Paul Evans

text: This is not the end, this is not even the beginning of the end, this is just perhaps the end of the beginning.
author: Winston S. Churchill

text: Stop trying to be less of who you are. Let this time in your life cut you open and drain all of the things that are holding you back.
author: Jennifer Elisabeth

text: I am not my body. My body is nothing without me.
author: Tom Stoppard

text: All life is only a set of pictures in the brain, among which there is no difference betwixt those born of real things and those born of inward dreamings, and no cause to value the one above the other.
author: H.P. Lovecraft

text: It's better to have a few faithful friends than numerous shallow friendships.
author: Jonathan Anthony Burkett

text: There are two kinds of secrets. The ones we keep from others and the ones we keep from ourselves.
author: Frank Warren

text: Life is painful and disappointing. It is useless, therefore, to write new realistic novels. We generally know where we stand in relation to reality and don’t care to know any more.
author: Michel Houellebecq

text: I had a dream about you last night... you kept meowing at people and licking yourself it was not unlike you normally.
author: Nicole McKay

text: Most of us have the good or bad fortune of seeing our lives fall apart so slowly we barely notice.
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafon

text: Life itself is the proper binge.
author: Julia Child

text: when we were kidslaying around the lawnon ourbellieswe often talkedabouthowwe'd like todieandwe allagreed on thesamething
author: we'd alllike to diefucking(althoughnone of ushaddone anyfucking)and nowthatwe are hardlykidsany longerwe think moreabouthownot todieandalthoughwe'rereadymost ofuswouldprefer todo italoneunder thesheetsnowthatmost ofushave fuckedour livesaway.

text: Everything that had happened was shockingly beautiful, enough to make you crazy.
author: Banana Yoshimoto

text: The soul is like an uninhabited worldthat comes to life only whenGod lays His headagainst us.
author: Thomas Aquinas

text: My name is Jimmy,but my friends just call methe hideous penguin boy.
author: Tim Burton

text: Be happy -- if you're not even happy, what's so good about surviving?
author: Tom Stoppard

text: If you say that you never lie in life,you honestly insult my intelligence.
author: Toba Beta

text: Middle age has been defined as what happens when a person's broad mind and narrow waist change places.
author: A.C. Grayling

text: Let us work without reasoning,' said Martin; 'it is the only way to make life endurable.
author: Voltaire

text: We are the sum of all people we have ever met
author: you change the tribe and the tribe changes you.

text: I could just remember how my father used to say that the reason for living was to get ready to stay dead a long time.
author: William Faulkner

text: Love doesn't make the world go 'round
author: love is what makes the ride worthwhile.

text: Things don't always work out the way we hope. You just have to pick yourself up and find a new direction to go in.
author: Joelle Charbonneau

text: It’s too bad if a heart lacks fire,and is deprived of the light of a heart ablaze.The day on which you arewithout passionate loveis the most wasted day of your life.
author: Omar Khayyam

text: Life is boring. People are vengeful. Good things always end. We do so many things and we don’t know why, and if we do find out why, it’s decades later and knowing why doesn’t matter any more.
author: Douglas Coupland

text: This autumn-why am I growing old?bird disappearing among clouds.
author: Basho Matsuo

text: You don't win races by wishing, you win them by running faster than everyone else does.
author: Philip Pullman

text: Whenever I see an ambulance, I like to think there is a baby being born, rather than a death.
author: Phil Lester

text: I know I'm an acquired taste - I'm anchovies. And not everybody wants those hairy little things.
author: Tori Amos

text: at your weakest, you end up showing more strength; at your lowest, you are suddenly lifted higher than you’ve ever been. They all border one another, these opposites and show how quickly we can be altered.
author: Cecelia Ahern

text: You know, considering your IQ, you're really socially retarded sometimes.
author: Shannon Delany

text: Everybody was sorry. Sorry was easy. Sorry was for suckers.
author: Gretchen McNeil

text: All art is but dirtying the paper delicately.
author: John Ruskin

text: Love changes what is probable and makes unlikely things possible.
author: Ally Condie

text: Would you like to know your future?If your answer is yes, think again. Not knowing is the greatest life motivator.So enjoy, endure, survive each moment as it comes to you in its proper sequence -- a surprise.
author: Vera Nazarian

text: I feel very adventurous. There are so many doors to be opened, and I'm not afraid to look behind them.
author: Elizabeth   Taylor

text: Each player must accept the cards life deals him or her; but once they are in hand, he or she alone must decide how to play the cards in order to win the game.
author: Voltaire

text: Still, he figured, sometimes you've got to do what you've got to do, and then sometimes you've just got to run like hell after it's done.
author: Derek Landy

text: I'm a fountain of blood. In the shape of a girl.
author: Bjork

text: They had, finally, the only thing anyone really wants in life: someone to hold your hand when you die.
author: Lorrie Moore

text: Be curious about the world in which you live. Look things up. Chase down every reference. Go deeper than anybody else--that's how you'll get ahead.
author: Austin Kleon

text: Heaven is an idea constructed by man to help him cope with the fact that life on earth is both brutally short and, paradoxically, far too long.
author: Andrew Davidson

text: Because no one needs to live for ever. I think that sometimes you can outstay your welcome.
author: Gemma Malley

text: We may run, walk, stumble. drive, or fly, but let us never lose sight of the reason for the journey, or miss a chance to see a rainbow on the way.
author: Gloria Gaither

text: Timendi causa est nescire - Ignorance is the cause of fear.
author: Seneca

text: I hate when people say 'I see'. It doesn't mean anything and I think it's hostile. Whenever anyone tells me 'I see' I think they're really saying 'Fuck you'.
author: Peter Cameron

text: I’ll never second guess the things that I have done. I’ve got too much left to say and too much to become.
author: Alex Gaskarth

text: No matter how many people believe or don’t believe in you, you must bethe ultimate believer in yourself!
author: Pablo

text: Destiny is a lie. Destiny is justification for atrocity. It is the means by which murderers armour themselves against reprimand. It is a word intended to stand in place of ethics, denying all moral context.
author: Steven Erikson

text: To build up a library is to create a life. It's never just a random collection of books.
author: Carlos Maria Dominguez

text: That is life... to begin again when everything is lost!
author: A.J. Cronin

text: I think people would live a bit longer if they didn't know how old they were. Age puts restrictions on things.
author: Karl Pilkington

text: Life is dear to every living thing
author: the worm that crawls upon the ground will struggle for it.

text: Children and dogs are the messengers of God some of us do not deserve them
author: Ginnetta Correli

text: Your nightmares follow you like a shadow, forever.
author: Aleksandar Hemon

text: My father chose my name , and my last name was chosen by my ancestors . That’s enough, I myself choose my way
author: Ali Shariati

text: You make mistakes, mistakes don't make you
author: Maxwell Maltz

text: Goals are the road maps that guide you to your destination. Cultivate the habit of setting clearly-defined written goals
author: they are the road maps that guide you to your destination.

text: You said we all want there to be more than this! Well, there's always more than this. There's always something you don't know.
author: Patrick Ness

text: It is said that power corrupts, but actually it's more true that power attracts the corruptible. The sane are usually attracted by other things than power.
author: David Brin

text: The dead do not hurt you
author: only the living do.

text: Life is but a memory Happened long ago. Theatre full of sadness For a long forgotten show.
author: Nick  Drake

text: Some of us weren't born for rewards, Froi. We were born for sacrifices.
author: Melina Marchetta

text: Remember that life is not measured by the number of breaths we take, but by the moments that take our breath away!
author: Vicki Corona

text: It was a once in a lifetime thing. I hate to think it but I bet it's true. It's too bad for us that our once in a lifetime happened when were too young to handle it.
author: Scott Spencer

text: How unhappy does one have to be before living seems worse than dying?
author: Deborah Curtis

text: Men acquire a particular quality by constantly acting a particular way... you become just by performing just actions, temperate by performing temperate actions, brave by performing brave actions.
author: Aristotle

text: Killers aren't always assassins. Sometimes, they don't even have blood on their hands.
author: Ruta Sepetys

text: So since I'm still here livin',I guess I will live on.I could've died for love--But for livin' I was born.
author: Langston Hughes

text: Maybe if I forgot things once in a while, we'd all be a little bit happier.
author: Jay Asher

text: Dead, we are revealed in our true dimensions, and they are surprisingly modest.
author: Michael Cunningham

text: In life, more than in anything else, it isn’t easy to end up alive.
author: Roman Payne

text: We have become, by the power of a glorious evolutionary accident called intelligence, the stewards of life's continuity on earth. We did not ask for this role, but we cannot abjure it. We may not be suited to it, but here we are.
author: Stephen Jay Gould

text: For someone like myself in whom the ability to trust others is so cracked and broken that I am wretchedly timid and am forever trying to read the expression on people's faces.
author: Osamu Dazai

text: Learn to drive?""Never," said Quentin. "My mission in life is to be a passenger.
author: Diana Wynne Jones

text: What we are reluctant to touch often seems the very fabric of our salvation.
author: Don DeLillo

text: That is the challenge Companion. To take what has happened to you and learn from it. Nothing is quite so destructive as pity, especially self-pity. No event in life is so terrible that one cannot rise above it.
author: Robin Hobb

text: I look back to where my life had been. It's always risky to think of letting go. That's why this is the perfect ending. Nothing left to reconcile.
author: Loretta Ellsworth

text: When you expect nothing from the world - not the light of the sun, the wet of water, nor the air to breathe - everything is a wonder and every moment a gift.
author: Michael J. Sullivan

text: All the lives I could live, all the people I will never know, never will be, they are everywhere. That is all that the world is.
author: Aleksandar Hemon

text: Be where you are
author: otherwise you will miss your life.

text: Has it ever struck you that life is all memory, except for the one present moment that goes by you so quick you hardly catch it going?
author: Tennessee Williams

text: Don't be trapped by dogma — which is living with the results of other people's thinking.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Life is better than death, I believe, if only because it is less boring, and because it has fresh peaches in it.
author: Alice Walker

text: In my life so far, I have discovered that there are really only two kinds of people: those who are for you, and those who are against you. Learn to recognize them, for they are often and easily mistaken for each other.
author: Lemmy Kilmister

text: He hated to think of his own life stretching ahead of him that way, a long succession of days and nights that were fine - not good, not bad, not great, not lousy, not exciting, not anything.
author: Robert Cormier

text: I had a dream about you. You were lost in a daydream, when I walked in and you began screaming. But I know that could never actually happen. In real life I only enter people’s nightmares.
author: Bauvard

text: Once you leave out all the bullshit they teach you in school, life gets really simple.
author: George Carlin

text: There are no second chances in life, except to feel remorse.
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafon

text: Only the person who has experienced light and darkness, war and peace, rise and fall, only that person has truly experienced life.
author: Stefan Zweig

text: Life isn't a book. There's no guarantee of a happy ending.
author: Kristin Cast

text: We all know we're going to die
author: what's important is the kind of men and women we are in the face of this.

text: Pack a pillow and blanket and see as much of the world as you can.You will not regret it.
author: Jhumpa Lahiri

text: But in that moment I understood what they say about nostalgia, that no matter if you're thinking of something good or bad, it always leaves you a little emptier afterward.
author: John Corey Whaley

text: Sometimes, some lies that spoken with high confidencecould be more receptive than facts that spoken with doubt.
author: Toba Beta

text: It kills me how these days everyone has clinical justification for their strangeness.
author: Miguel Syjuco

text: Go boldly and honestly through the world. Learn to love the fact that there is nobody else quite like you.
author: Daniel Radcliffe

text: Life was a swarm of accidents waiting in the treetops, descending upon any living thing that passed, ready to eat them alive. You swam in a river of chance and coincidence. You clung to the happiest accidents- the rest you let float by.
author: David Wroblewski

text: Not life, but good life, is to be chiefly valued.
author: Socrates

text: Such a simple concept, yet so true: that which we manifest is before us; we are the creators of our own destiny. Be it through intention or ignorance, our successes and our failures have been brought on by none other than ourselves.
author: Garth Stein

text: Things don't matter, people do
author: Rosie Thomas

text: A live body and a dead body contain the same number of particles. Structurally, there's no discernible difference. Life and death are unquantifiable abstracts. Why should I be concerned?
author: Alan Moore

text: Never say 'I can't.' 'I can't' is a limit, and life is about breaking through limits. Say 'I will' instead.
author: Heather Vogel Frederick

text: You must draw from that strength. You must increase it. And you must hold on to it because sometimes that small glimmer of inner strength is all that we have to help us press forward through the darkness.
author: Morgan Rhodes

text: How can one change one's entire life and build a new one on one moment of love? And yet, that's what you make me want to close my eyes and do.
author: Greta Garbo

text: To put meaning in one's life may end in madness,But life without meaning is the tortureOf restlessness and vague desire--It is a boat longing for the sea and yet afraid.
author: Edgar Lee Masters

text: But he won’t let the pain blot out the humor no more’n he’ll let the humor blot out the pain.
author: Ken Kesey

text: Silence before being born, silence after death: life is nothing but noise between two unfathomable silences.
author: Isabel Allende

text: There is no better high than discovery.
author: Edward O. Wilson

text: AZRAEL:No pleasure, no rapture, no exquisite sin greater... than central air.
author: Kevin Smith

text: Nothing ever becomes real till experienced – even a proverb is no proverb until your life has illustrated it
author: John Keats

text: Enjoy your youth.You'll never be younger thanyou are at this very moment.
author: Chad Sugg

text: Your greatest self has been waiting your whole life
author: don't make it wait any longer.

text: Let yourself be open and life will be easier. A spoon of salt in a glass of water makes the water undrinkable. A spoon of salt in a lake is almost unnoticed.
author: Buddha Siddhartha Guatama Shakyamuni

text: I'm a completely worthless woman and no man should risk his life for me.
author: Greta Garbo

text: If we do not find anything very pleasant, at least we shall find something new.
author: Voltaire

text: The best laid schemes o' mice an' men gang aft agley.
author: Robert Burns

text: if youre never scared, or embarased, or hurt, it means you never take any chances.
author: Julia Sorel

text: To treat a person like a carpet, it is necessary that one do the walking, and one allow himself to be walked on.--Shin'a'in saying
author: Mercedes Lackey

text: Life is a journey, Frannie darling," Feagan had once told me. "Choose well those with whom you travel."As always, I've followed Feagan's counsel.
author: Lorraine Heath

text: I saw my earlier selves as different people, acquaintances I had outgrown. I wondered how I could ever have been some of them.
author: Roger Zelazny

text: But spending your life concentrating on death is like watching a whole movie and thinking only about the credits that are going to roll at the end. It’s a mistake of emphasis.
author: Nicholson Baker

text: Surely the earth can be savedby all the peoplewho insiston love.
author: Alice Walker

text: Lo esperado no sucede. Es lo inesperado lo que acontece.
author: Ernesto Sabato

text: And life is what we make it.Always has been, always will be.
author: Grandma Moses

text: Some things take so longBut how do I explainWhen not too many peopleCan see we're all the sameAnd because of all their tearsYour eyes can't hope to seeThe beauty that surrounds themNow, isn't it a pity
author: George Harrison

text: It would be so nice if something made sense for a change.
author: Lewis Carroll

text: Love is forever. If it wasn't, it wouldn't be love. The world is beautiful. If it wasn't, it wouldn't be the world.
author: Rick Yancey

text: The preparation of good food is merely another expression of art, one of the joys of civilized living…
author: Dione Lucas

text: Forgiveness is not something you do for someone else; it's something you do for yourself. To forgive is not to condone, it is to refuse to continue feeling bad about an injury.
author: Jim Beaver

text: Sometimes it seemed to him that his life was delicate as a dandelion. One little puff from any direction, and it was blown to bits.
author: Katherine Paterson

text: She cracked a smile. "So what's your poison" He sighed dramatically, and let the truth tumble off his tongue. "Life." "Ah," she said ruefully. "That'll kill you.
author: Victoria Schwab

text: CLEMENTINE: This is it, Joel. It's going to be gone soon.JOEL: I know.CLEMENTINE: What do we do?JOEL: Enjoy it.
author: Charlie Kaufman

text: Do more listening than talking
author: talk more about them than about you.

text: The shape of my life is, of course, determined by many things; my background and childhood, my mind and its education, my conscience and its pressures, my heart and its desires.
author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

text: Live for each second without hesitation
author: Elton John

text: It’s funny how you can live somewhere your whole life, but not really see it.
author: Kami Garcia

text: Sin sorpresas, aunque sean disagredables, nadie estaria dispuesto a vivir.
author: David Olguin

text: I think the only answer is to live life to the fullest while you can and collect memories like fools collect money. Because in the end, that's all you have - happy memories.
author: Sarah Strohmeyer

text: You are where you need to be. Just take a deep breath.
author: Lana Parrilla

text: I had a dream about you last night. The best day of my life was when I taught you how to juggle, but the best day of yours was when you taught someone else.
author: Michael  Summers

text: Life is more fun if you play games.
author: Roald Dahl

text: Life is not an easy matter…. You cannot live through it without falling into frustration and cynicism unless you have before you a great idea which raises you above personal misery, above weakness, above all kinds of perfidy and baseness.
author: Leon Trotsky

text: I can do this… I can start over. I can save my own life and I’m never going to be alone as long as I have stars to wish on and people to still love.
author: Jennifer Elisabeth

text: Thinking there is something better out there in the world, something worth more than you already have- of all my mistakes,that was my biggest.
author: Louise Candlish

text: Truth is beauty
author: Lisi Harrison

text: Waking up is not a selfish pursuit of happiness, it is a revolutionary stance, from the inside out, for the benefit of all beings in existence.
author: Noah Levine

text: No matter where life takes me, you'll find me with a smile. Presumed to be happy, always laughing like a child. I never thought life could be this sweet! It's got me cheesing from cheek to cheek!
author: Mac Miller

text: There really is only one ending to any story. Human life ends in death. Until then, it keeps going and gets complicated and there's loss. Everything involves loss; every relationship ends in one way or another.
author: Charlie Kaufman

text: To live is to be haunted.
author: Philip K. Dick

text: Remember to breathe. It is after all, the secret of life.
author: Gregory Maguire

text: That's what I do. Watch movies and read. Sometimes I even pretend to write, but I'm not fooling anyone. Oh, and I go to the mailbox.
author: Nicole Krauss

text: There are no such things as curses
author: only people and their decisions

text: Many people say I'm the best women's soccer player in the world. I don't think so. And because of that, someday I just might be.
author: Mia Hamm

text: Few men realize that their life, the very essence of their character, their capabilities and their audacities, are only the expression of their belief in the safety of their surroundings.
author: Joseph Conrad

text: My reasons to liveWere my reasons to dieBut at least they were mineNow I've freedom unboundCut the laces of life
author: Emilie Autumn

text: In a person's lifetime there may be not more than half a dozen occasions that he can look back to in the certain knowledge that right then, at that moment, there was room for nothing but happiness in his heart.
author: Ernestine Gilbreth Carey

text: My mother always said 'Don't bother other people.' I think that's good advice.
author: Amy Sedaris

text: Things Happen and once they start happening you pretty much just to hold on for dear life and see where they drop you when they stop.
author: Meg Rosoff

text: When you live your life by poor standards, you inflict damage on everyone who crosses your path, especially those you love.
author: Jordan Belfort

text: If everyone started off the day singing, just think how happy they'd be.
author: Lauren Myracle

text: In the end, the end of a life only matters to friends, family, and other folks you used to know. For everyone else, it's just another end.
author: Gabrielle Zevin

text: Music is enough for a lifetime, but a lifetime is not enough for music.
author: Sergei Rachmaninoff

text: You know what's truly weird about any financial crisis? We made it up. Currency, money, finance, they're all social inventions. When the sun comes up in the morning it's shining on the same physical landscape, all the atoms are in place.
author: Bruce Sterling

text: I don't do regrets. Regrets are pointless. It's too late for regrets. You've already done it, haven't you? You've lived your life. No point wishing you could change it.
author: Lemmy Kilmister

text: Never try to do anything that is outside of who you are. A forced smile is a sign of what feels wrong in your heart, so recognize it when it happens. Living a lie will reduce you to one.
author: Ashly Lorenzana

text: Some people read palms to tell your future, but I read hands to tell your past. Each scar makes a story worth telling. Each callused palm, each cracked knuckle is a missed punch or years in a factory.
author: Sarah Kay

text: If you don't want anyone to know about your existence, you might as well kill yourself. You're taking up space, air.
author: Dave Eggers

text: You gotta take chances in this life or you're alreadydead.
author: Megan McCafferty

text: And I'll tell her that I don't want my life to be samples and scraps. A taste of everything but a meal of nothing.
author: Ally Condie

text: Don't stay in bed, unless you can make money in bed.
author: George Burns

text: Stop searching the world for treasure, the real treasure is in yourself.
author: Pablo

text: Regardless of Sunshine or Rain, Be Thankful for another GREAT day…and treat Life as the ULTIMATE Gift.... Because IT IS :)
author: Pablo

text: Everyday it gets easier to connect with an electronic device that it is to connect with real people.
author: Pablo

text: The best things aren't perfectly constructed. They aren't illusions. they aren't larger than life. They are life.
author: Nina LaCour

text: When you lost sight of your path, listen for the destination in your heart (Allen Walker, D-gray Man)
author: Katsura Hoshino

text: Courage is the price that life exacts for granting peace.
author: Amelia Earhart

text: Forget regret, or life is yours to miss. No other path, no other way, no day but today.
author: Jonathan Larson

text: In the morning there is meaning, in the evening there is feeling.
author: Gertrude Stein

text: Life's just a bunch of accidents, connected by one perfect end.
author: Daniel C. Tomas

text: When people walk away from you, let them go. Your destiny is never tied to anyone who leaves you, and it doesn't mean they are bad people. It just means that their part in your story is over.
author: Tony McCollum

text: It was almost like a reverse nightmare, like when you wake up from a nightmare, you're so relieved. I woke up into a nightmare.""And what is that nightmare, Craig?""Life.
author: Ned Vizzini

text: Peace is not the absence of conflict but the presence of creative alternatives for responding to conflict -- alternatives to passive or aggressive responses, alternatives to violence.
author: Dorothy   Thompson

text: Be patient and open-minded in your interactions with others
author: find the positive attributes that they possess.

text: Applaud my idiocy.
author: Conan O'Brien

text: The untold want, by life and land ne'er granted,Now, Voyager, sail thou forth, to seek and find.
author: Walt Whitman

text: An apology is the superglue of life! It can repair just about anything!!
author: Lynn Johnston

text: I had a dream about you last night.. You were playing with chicken livers and told me everyone was in quarantine.
author: Amy Sommers

text: Perhaps it takes a purer faith to praise God for unrealized blessings than for those we once enjoyed or those we enjoy now.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: Its important to be comfortable with uncertainty.
author: Xiaolu Guo

text: Single is not a status, it is a word that describes a person who is strong enough to live and enjoy life without depending on others.
author: prixie

text: Whole life is a search for beauty. But, when the beauty is found inside, the search ends and a beautiful journey begins.
author: Harshit Walia

text: Sometimes it’s heartbreaking to see your siblings as the people they’ve become. Maybe that’s why we all stay away from each other as a matter of course.
author: Jonathan Tropper

text: We no longer believe because it is absurd: it is absurd because we must believe.
author: Julio Cortazar

text: Because the greatest part of a road trip isn’t arriving at your destination. It’s all the wild stuff that happens along the way.
author: Emma Chase

text: For what it’s worth: it’s never too late to be whoever you want to be. I hope you live a life you’re proud of, and if you find that you’re not, I hope you have the strength to start all over again.
author: Eric Roth

text: Timid men prefer the calm of despotism to the tempestuous sea of Liberty.
author: Thomas Jefferson

text: The first step in making rabbit stew is catching the rabbit.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: There's a whole category of people who miss out by not allowing themselves to be weird enough.
author: Alain de Botton

text: In life there are two things which are dependable. The pleasures of the flesh and the pleasures of literature.
author: Sei Shonagon

text: A free people ought not only to be armed and disciplined, but they should have sufficient arms and ammunition to maintain a status of independence from any who might attempt to abuse them, which would include their own government
author: Spurious quote attributed to George Washington but never said by him .

text: The excursion is the same when you go looking for your sorrow as when you go looking for your joy.
author: Eudora Welty

text: Associate with the noblest people you can find
author: read the best books

text: When you're locked up in here for life, you learn to welcome the little freedoms.
author: Alexander Gordon Smith

text: In still moments by the sea life seems large-drawn and simple. It is there we can see into ourselves.
author: Rolf Edberg

text: I had a dream about you last night.. you were trying to give coordinates to an apple
author: Nicole McKay

text: Yesterday is history, tomorrow is a mystery, today is a gift of God, which is why we call it the present.
author: Bil Keane

text: It is never too late to be what you might have been.
author: George Eliot

text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Listen to the mustn'ts, child. Listen to the don'ts. Listen to the shouldn'ts, the impossibles, the won'ts. Listen to the never haves, then listen close to me... Anything can happen, child. Anything can be.
author: Shel Silverstein

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston S. Churchill

text: It’s no use going back to yesterday, because I was a different person then.
author: Lewis Carroll

text: Well-behaved women seldom make history.
author: Laurel Thatcher Ulrich

text: Nothing is impossible, the word itself says 'I'm possible'!
author: Audrey Hepburn

text: I can't give you a sure-fire formula for success, but I can give you a formula for failure: try to please everybody all the time.
author: Herbert Bayard Swope

text: First they ignore you. Then they ridicule you. And then they attack you and want to burn you. And then they build monuments to you.
author: Nicholas Klein

text: Two wrongs don't make a right, but they make a good excuse.
author: Thomas Szasz

text: You can't stay in your corner of the Forest waiting for others to come to you. You have to go to them sometimes.
author: A.A. Milne

text: Who controls the past controls the future. Who controls the present controls the past.
author: George Orwell

text: Talent hits a target no one else can hit. Genius hits a target no one else can see.
author: Arthur Schopenhauer

text: The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven..
author: John Milton

text: Fantasy is hardly an escape from reality. It's a way of understanding it.
author: Lloyd Alexander

text: When you have eliminated all which is impossible, then whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth.
author: Arthur Conan Doyle

text: Never let your sense of morals prevent you from doing what is right.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: I like living. I have sometimes been wildly, despairingly, acutely miserable, racked with sorrow; but through it all I still know quite certainly that just to be alive is a grand thing.
author: Agatha Christie

text: War is peace. Freedom is slavery. Ignorance is strength.
author: George Orwell

text: Don't judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant.
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

text: It's kind of fun to do the impossible.
author: Walt Disney Company

text: A painter should begin every canvas with a wash of black, because all things in nature are dark except where exposed by the light.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Pain is temporary. Quitting lasts forever.
author: Lance Armstrong

text: Clouds come floating into my life, no longer to carry rain or usher storm, but to add color to my sunset sky.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: Stories never really end...even if the books like to pretend they do. Stories always go on. They don't end on the last page, any more than they begin on the first page.
author: Cornelia Funke

text: The Chinese use two brush strokes to write the word 'crisis.' One brush stroke stands for danger; the other for opportunity. In a crisis, be aware of the danger--but recognize the opportunity.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: All the darkness in the world cannot extinguish the light of a single candle.
author: Francis of Assisi

text: Start writing, no matter what. The water does not flow until the faucet is turned on.
author: Louis L'Amour

text: The secret of health for both mind and body is not to mourn for the past, nor to worry about the future, but to live the present moment wisely and earnestly.
author: Bukkyo Dendo Kyokai

text: To see a World in a Grain of Sand And a Heaven in a Wild Flower, Hold Infinity in the palm of your hand And Eternity in an hour.
author: William Blake

text: Once you have tasted flight, you will forever walk the earth with your eyes turned skyward, for there you have been, and there you will always long to return.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: You is kind. You is smart. You is important.
author: Kathryn Stockett

text: Live in the present, remember the past, and fear not the future, for it doesn't exist and never shall. There is only now.
author: Christopher Paolini

text: Better to be strong than pretty and useless.
author: Lilith Saintcrow

text: A ship is safe in harbor, but that's not what ships are for.
author: John A. Shedd

text: Even if you are on the right track, you’ll get run over if you just sit there.
author: Will Rogers

text: The most common way people give up their power is by thinking they don't have any.
author: Alice Walker

text: We are just an advanced breed of monkeys on a minor planet of a very average star. But we can understand the Universe. That makes us something very special.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: The worst part of success is trying to find someone who is happy for you.
author: Bette Midler

text: The important thing to you is not how many years in your life, but how much life in your years!
author: Edward J. Stieglitz

text: When people don't express themselves, they die one piece at a time.
author: Laurie Halse Anderson

text: There was another life that I might have had, but I am having this one.
author: Kazuo Ishiguro

text: Reputation is what other people know about you. Honor is what you know about yourself.
author: Lois McMaster Bujold

text: Remember, darkness does not always equate to evil, just as light does not always bring good.
author: P.C. Cast

text: When I was about eight, I decided that the most wonderful thing, next to a human being, was a book.
author: Margaret Walker

text: May the forces of evil become confused on the way to your house.
author: George Carlin

text: There must be those among whom we can sit down and weep and still be counted as warriors.
author: Adrienne Rich

text: Lack of direction, not lack of time, is the problem. We all have twenty-four hour days.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: Change will not come if we wait for some other person, or if we wait for some other time. We are the ones we've been waiting for. We are the change that we seek.
author: Barack Obama

text: When one tugs at a single thing in nature, he finds it attached to the rest of the world.
author: John Muir

text: In the endthese things matter most:How well did you love?How fully did you live?How deeply did you let go?
author: Jack Kornfield

text: Great heroes need great sorrows and burdens, or half their greatness goes unnoticed. It is all part of the fairy tale.
author: Peter S. Beagle

text: I'd rather be hated for who I am, than loved for who I am not.
author: Kurt Cobain

text: Trees that are slow to grow bear the best fruit.
author: Moliere

text: Once you have read a book you care about, some part of it is always with you.
author: Louis L'Amour

text: What's terrible is to pretend that second-rate is first-rate. To pretend that you don't need love when you do
author: or you like your work when you know quite well you're capable of better.

text: I don't think of all the misery, but of the beauty that still remains.
author: Anne Frank

text: Parents can only give good advice or put them on the right paths, but the final forming of a person's character lies in their own hands.
author: Anne Frank

text: The grand essentials to happiness in this life are something to do, something to love, and something to hope for.
author: George Washington Burnap

text: Do no harm and leave the world a better place than you found it.
author: Patricia Cornwell

text: Others have seen what is and asked why. I have seen what could be and asked why not.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Shoot for the moon. Even if you miss, you'll land among the stars.
author: Norman Vincent Peale

text: Whatever you can do or dream you can, begin it.Boldness has genius, power and magic in it!
author: John Anster

text: The starting point of all achievement is DESIRE. Keep this constantly in mind. Weak desire brings weak results, just as a small fire makes a small amount of heat.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: . . . when it comes down to it, that’s what life is all about: showing up for the people you love, again and again, until you can’t show up anymore.
author: Rebecca Walker

text: Courage isn't having the strength to go on - it is going on when you don't have strength.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

text: Keep your face always toward the sunshine - and shadows will fall behind you.
author: Walt Whitman

text: If you don't go after what you want, you'll never have it. If you don't ask, the answer is always no. If you don't step forward, you're always in the same place.
author: Nora Roberts

text: Everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms—to choose one’s attitude in any given set of circumstances, to choose one’s own way.
author: Viktor E. Frankl

text: There is nothing more dreadful than the habit of doubt. Doubt separates people. It is a poison that disintegrates friendships and breaks up pleasant relations. It is a thorn that irritates and hurts
author: it is a sword that kills.

text: We never know the quality of someone else's life, though we seldom resist the temptation to assume and pass judgement.
author: Tami Hoag

text: A concept is a brick. It can be used to build a courthouse of reason. Or it can be thrown through the window.
author: Gilles Deleuze

text: I have never met a man so ignorant that I couldn't learn something from him.
author: Galileo Galilei

text: It is easy in the world to live after the world's opinion
author: it is easy in solitude to live after our own

text: No matter how your heart is grieving, if you keep on believing, the dreams that you wish will come true.
author: Walt Disney Company

text: Just because you can doesn't mean you should.
author: Sherrilyn Kenyon

text: You might as well answer the door, my child,the truth is furiously knocking.
author: Lucille Clifton

text: We are flawed creatures, all of us. Some of us think that means we should fix our flaws. But get rid of my flaws and there would be no one left.
author: Sarah Vowell

text: Men are from Earth, women are from Earth. Deal with it.
author: George Carlin

text: For me, I am driven by two main philosophies: know more today about the world than I knew yesterday and lessen the suffering of others. You'd be surprised how far that gets you.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: We don't make mistakes, just happy little accidents.
author: Bob  Ross

text: The best thing to hold onto in life is each other.
author: Audrey Hepburn

text: To love and win is the best thing.To love and lose, the next best.
author: William Makepeace Thackeray

text: Spoon feeding in the long run teaches us nothing but the shape of the spoon.
author: E.M. Forster

text: Happiness is like a butterfly which, when pursued, is always beyond our grasp, but, if you will sit down quietly, may alight upon you.
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

text: You'll never find a rainbow if you're looking down
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: He that can have patience can have what he will.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Wanting to be someone else is a waste of who you are
author: Kurt Cobain

text: Change the way you look at things and the things you look at change.
author: Wayne W. Dyer

text: Death is not the greatest loss in life. The greatest loss is what dies inside while still alive. Never surrender.
author: Tupac Shakur

text: Swords can win territories but not hearts, forces can bend heads but not minds.
author: Mirza Tahir Ahmad

text: A Penny Saved is a Penny Earned
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.
author: Thomas A. Edison

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Travel far enough, you meet yourself.
author: David Mitchell

text: There's so much to be grateful for, words are poor things.
author: Marilynne Robinson

text: Every man is a damn fool for at least five minutes every day
author: wisdom consists in not exceeding the limit.

text: Dream as if you will live forever
author: Live as if you will die today.

text: For like a shaft, clear and cold, the thought pierced him that in the end the Shadow was only a small and passing thing: there was light and high beauty for ever beyond its reach.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: We are products of our past, but we don't have to be prisoners of it.
author: Rick Warren

text: It is always the false that makes you suffer, the false desires and fears, the false values and ideas, the false relationships between people. Abandon the false and you are free of pain; truth makes happy, truth liberates.
author: Nisargadatta Maharaj

text: Fiction is art and art is the triumph over chaos… to celebrate a world that lies spread out around us like a bewildering and stupendous dream.
author: John Cheever

text: My heart is warm with the friends I make,And better friends I'll not be knowing,Yet there isn't a train I wouldn't take,No matter where it's going.
author: Edna St. Vincent Millay

text: Do not go gentle into that good night,Old age should burn and rave at close of day;Rage, rage against the dying of the light.
author: Dylan Thomas

text: If you are lazy, and accept your lot, you may live in it. If you are willing to work, you can write your name anywhere you choose.
author: Gene Stratton-Porter

text: What a large volume of adventures may be grasped within the span of his little life by him who interests his heart in everything.
author: Laurence Sterne

text: If your feet are firmly planted on the ground you'll never be able to dance.
author: Iris Johansen

text: Be more concerned with your character than your reputation, because your character is what you really are, while your reputation is merely what others think you are.
author: John Wooden

text: The greatest thing in the world is to know how to belong to oneself.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: For myself I am an optimist - it does not seem to be much use to be anything else.
author: Winston S. Churchill

text: Resentment is like drinking poison and then hoping it will kill your enemies.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: We either make ourselves miserable, or we make ourselves strong. The amount of work is the same.
author: Carlos Castaneda

text: Man cannot discover new oceans unless he has the courage to lose sight of the shore.
author: Andre Gide

text: I never said it would be easy, I only said it would be worth it.
author: Mae West

text: Don’t ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive, and go do it. Because what the world needs is people who have come alive.
author: Howard Thurman

text: Never dull your shine for somebody else.
author: Tyra Banks

text: Whatever the mind can conceive and believe, it can achieve.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: Without fear there cannot be courage.
author: Christopher Paolini

text: Life is made of so many moments that mean nothing. Then one day, a single moment comes along to define every second that comes after. Such moments are tests of courage, of strength.
author: Sabaa Tahir

text: With all its sham, drudgery, and broken dreams,it is still a beautiful world.Be cheerful.Strive to be happy.
author: Max Ehrmann

text: Government exists to protect us from each other. Where government has gone beyond its limits is in deciding to protect us from ourselves.
author: Ronald Reagan

text: The universe is big, its vast and complicated, and ridiculous. And sometimes, very rarely, impossible things just happen and we call them miracles. And that's the theory. Nine hundred years, never seen one yet, but this would do me.
author: Steven Moffat

text: Do all the good you can. By all the means you can. In all the ways you can. In all the places you can. At all the times you can. To all the people you can. As long as ever you can.
author: John Wesley

text: Any intelligent fool can make things bigger, more complex, and more violent. It takes a touch of genius — and a lot of courage to move in the opposite direction.
author: Ernst F. Schumacher

text: People don't realize how a man's whole life can be changed by one book.
author: Malcolm X

text: All The Woulda-Coulda-ShouldasLayin' In The Sun,Talkin' 'Bout The ThingsThey Woulda-Coulda-Shoulda Done...But All Those Woulda-Coulda-ShouldasAll Ran Away And HidFrom One Little Did.
author: Shel Silverstein

text: It's the children the world almost breaks who grow up to save it.
author: Frank Warren

text: The rights of every man are diminished when the rights of one man are threatened.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: Life before Death.Strength before Weakness.Journey before Destination.
author: Brandon Sanderson

text: Nothing is permanent in this wicked world, not even our troubles.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: If you have good thoughts they will shine out of your face like sunbeams and you will always look lovely.
author: Roald Dahl

text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
author: Aesop

text: Remember, if you ever need a helping hand, it's at the end of your arm, as you get older, remember you have another hand: The first is to help yourself, the second is to help others.
author: Sam Levenson

text: So many things are possible as long as you don't know they are impossible.
author: Mildred D. Taylor

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: Only he who attempts the absurd is capable of achieving the impossible.
author: Miguel de Unamuno

text: I do not care so much what I am to others as I care what I am to myself.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: Being brave is when you have to do something because you know it is right, but at the same time, you are afraid to do it, because it might hurt or whatever. But you do it anyway.
author: Meg Cabot

text: Write it. Shoot it. Publish it. Crochet it, sauté it, whatever. MAKE.
author: Joss Whedon

text: You save yourself or you remain unsaved.
author: Alice Sebold

text: You must take life the way it comes at you and make the best of it.
author: Yann Martel

text: The dog is a gentleman
author: I hope to go to his heaven not man's.

text: Not knowing you can't do something, is sometimes all it takes to do it.
author: Ally Carter

text: The universe is made of stories, not of atoms.
author: Muriel Rukeyser

text: Music is the literature of the heart
author: it commences where speech ends.

text: Everything's a risk. Not doing anything is a risk. It's up to you.
author: Nicola Yoon

text: Dripping water hollows out stone, not through force but through persistence.
author: Ovid

text: There's nothing wrong with you. There's a lot wrong with the world you live in. And definitely get out of high school and make everyone sorry.
author: Chris Colfer

text: Without leaps of imagination or dreaming, we lose the excitement of possibilities. Dreaming, after all is a form of planning.
author: Gloria Steinem

text: I am a happy camper so I guess I’m doing something right. Happiness is like a butterfly; the more you chase it, the more it will elude you, but if you turn your attention to other things, it will come and sit softly on your shoulder.
author: J. Richard Lessor

text: Oh, what a tangled web we weave...when first we practice to deceive.
author: Walter Scott

text: I read somewhere... how important it is in life not necessarily to be strong, but to feel strong... to measure yourself at least once.
author: Jon Krakauer

text: You're never given a dream without also being given the power to make it true.
author: Richard Bach

text: younot wanting mewasthe beginning of mewanting myselfthank you
author: Nayyirah Waheed

text: The stupid neither forgive nor forget
author: the naive forgive and forget

text: I'm not saying I'm gonna change the world, but I guarantee that I will spark the brain that will change the world.
author: Tupac Shakur

text: When I was growing up I always wanted to be someone. Now I realize I should have been more specific.
author: Lily Tomlin

text: Adapt what is useful, reject what is useless, and add what is specifically your own.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Remember tonight... for it is the beginning of always
author: Dante Alighieri

text: The place to improve the world is first in one's own heart and head and hands, and then work outward from there.
author: Robert M. Pirsig

text: The tears of the world are a constant quantity. For each one who begins to weep somewhere else another stops. The same is true of the laugh.
author: Samuel Beckett

text: Start by doing what is necessary, then what is possible, and suddenly you are doing the impossible.
author: Francis of Assisi

text: Bermimpilah, karena Tuhan akan memeluk mimpi-mimpi itu.
author: Andrea Hirata

text: Things do not change
author: we change.

text: I do not believe in taking the right decision, I take a decision and make it right.
author: Muhammad Ali Jinnah

text: If we ever forget that we're one nation under God, then we will be one nation gone under.
author: Ronald Reagan

text: Keep your eyes on the stars, and your feet on the ground.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: We are saved by faith alone, but the faith that saves is never alone.
author: Martin Luther

text: A creative life cannot be sustained by approval any more than it can be destroyed by criticism.
author: Will Self

text: So early in my life, I had learned that if you want something, you had better make some noise.
author: Malcolm X

text: Instead of cursing the darkness, light a candle.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Do you really want to be happy? You can begin by being appreciative of who you are and what you've got.
author: Benjamin Hoff

text: Even if things don't unfold the way you expected, don't be disheartened or give up. One who continues to advance will win in the end.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: Real magic can never be made by offering someone else's liver. You must tear out your own, and not expect to get it back.
author: Peter S. Beagle

text: If my mind can conceive it, and my heart can believe it - then I can achieve it.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit.
author: Harry Truman

text: Know thyself.
author: Socrates

text: The greatest ideas are the simplest.
author: William Golding

text: The best relationships in our lives are the best not because they have been the happiest ones, they are that way because they have stayed strong through the most tormentful of storms.
author: Pandora Poikilos

text: The way I see it, if you want the rainbow, you gotta put up with the rain!
author: Dolly Parton

text: ...Next time you're faced with a choice, do the right thing. It hurts everyone less in the long run.
author: Wendelin Van Draanen

text: Success is not the key to happiness. Happiness is the key to success. If you love what you are doing, you will be successful.
author: Albert Schweitzer

text: The only limits for tomorrow are the doubts we have today.
author: Pittacus Lore

text: I hated every minute of training, but I said, 'Don't quit. Suffer now and live the rest of your life as a champion'.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: He who would learn to fly one day must first learn to walk and run and climb and dance
author: one cannot fly into flying.

text: Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body.
author: Seneca

text: Hope is important because it can make the present moment less difficult to bear. If we believe that tomorrow will be better, we can bear a hardship today.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: If a man is to shed the light of the sun upon other men, he must first of all have it within himself.
author: Romain Rolland

text: When you judge another, you do not define them, you define yourself.
author: Wayne W. Dyer

text: When you have exhausted all possibilities, remember this - you haven't.
author: Thomas A. Edison

text: Nothing's perfect, the world's not perfect. But it's there for us, trying the best it can; that's what makes it so damn beautiful.
author: Hiromu Arakawa

text: Once you learn to quit, it becomes a habit.
author: Vince Lombardi Jr.

text: The three great essentials to achieve anything worthwhile are, first, hard work; second, stick-to-itiveness; third, common sense.
author: Thomas A. Edison

text: What you are is God's gift to you, what you become is your gift to God.
author: Hans Urs von Balthasar

text: There is no passion to be found playing small - in settling for a life that is less than the one you are capable of living.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Feet, what do I need them forIf I have wings to fly.
author: Frida Kahlo

text: Scars are just another kind of memory.
author: M.L. Stedman

text: They laugh at me because I'm different
author: I laugh at them because they're all the same.

text: Never, ever, let anyone tell you what you can and can't do. Prove the cynics wrong. Pity them for they have no imagination. The sky's the limit. Your sky. Your limit. Now. Let's dance.
author: Tom Hiddleston

text: Pointing to another world will never stop vice among us
author: shedding light over this world can alone help us.

text: Yesterday is not ours to recover, but tomorrow is ours to win or lose.
author: Lyndon B. Johnson

text: Beauty is about being comfortable in your own skin. It's about knowing and accepting who you are.
author: Ellen DeGeneres

text: Maybe this is why we read, and why in moments of darkness we return to books: to find words for what we already know.
author: Alberto Manguel

text: My task, which I am trying to achieve is, by the power of the written word, to make you hear, to make you feel--it is, before all, to make you see.
author: Joseph Conrad

text: The strength of a woman is not measured by the impact that all her hardships in life have had on her
author: but the strength of a woman is measured by the extent of her refusal to allow those hardships to dictate her and who she becomes.

text: Believe there is a great power silently working all things for good, behave yourself and never mind the rest.
author: Beatrix Potter

text: If you can't run, you crawl. If you can't crawl-- you find someone to carry you.
author: Joss Whedon

text: I think that one of these days," he said, "you're going to have to find out where you want to go. And then you've got to start going there. But immediately. You can't afford to lose a minute. Not you.
author: J.D. Salinger

text: Ask yourself these three questions, Tatiana Metanova, and you will know who you are. Ask: What do believe in? What do you hope for? What do you love?
author: Paullina Simons

text: The pure present is an ungraspable advance of the past devouring the future. In truth, all sensation is already memory.
author: Henri Bergson

text: Life's like a movie, write your own ending. Keep believing, keep pretending.
author: Jim Henson

text: What would you do if you weren't afraid?
author: Sheryl Sandberg

text: No man chooses evil because it is evil; he only mistakes it for happiness, the good he seeks.
author: Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley

text: What is now proved was once only imagined.
author: William Blake

text: Everything will turn out right, the world is built on that.
author: Mikhail Bulgakov

text: The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice.
author: Theodore Parker

text: Desperation is the raw material of drastic change. Only those who can leave behind everything they have ever believed in can hope to escape.
author: William S. Burroughs

text: You can't connect the dots looking forward
author: you can only connect them looking backwards. So you have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future.

text: Stop feeling sorry for yourself and you will be happy.
author: Stephen Fry

text: The only difference between you and God is that you have forgotten you are divine.
author: Dan Brown

text: Nothing ever really goes away--it just changes into something else. Something beautiful.
author: Sarah Ockler

text: Happiness is always there. You just have to choose to see it. There's no point dwelling in the dark and ignoring the light of the stars.
author: Carrie Hope Fletcher

text: Wasn't that the point of the book? For women to realize, We are just two people. Not that much separates us. Not nearly as much as I'd thought.
author: Kathryn Stockett

text: Gossip is just a tool to distract people who have nothing better to do from feeling jealous of those few of us still remaining with noble hearts.
author: Anna Godbersen

text: We cannot be sure of having something to live for unless we are willing to die for it.
author: Ernesto Che Guevara

text: I don't paint dreams or nightmares, I paint my own reality.
author: Frida Kahlo

text: If we all did the things we are really capable of doing, we would literally astound ourselves.
author: Thomas A. Edison

text: It doesn't matter if a million people tell you what you can't do, or if ten million tell you no. If you get one yes from God that's all you need.
author: Tyler Perry

text: Self-respect is the root of discipline: The sense of dignity growswith the ability to say no to oneself.
author: Abraham Joshua Heschel

text: In prayer it is better to have a heart without words than words without a heart.
author: John Bunyan

text: I choose gentleness... Nothing is won by force. I choose to be gentle. If I raise my voice may it be only in praise. If I clench my fist, may it be only in prayer. If I make a demand, may it be only of myself.
author: Max Lucado

text: God, grant me the serenity to accept the things I cannot change, the courage to change the things I can, and the wisdom to know the difference.
author: Reinhold Niebuhr

text: It is truth that liberates, not your effort to be free.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Sometimes, as we're stumbling along in the dark, we hit something good.
author: Susan Ee

text: A villain is just a victim whose story hasn’t been told.
author: Chris Colfer

text: The greatest discovery of any generation is that a human can alter his life by altering his attitude.
author: William James

text: Awake, arise or be for ever fall’n.
author: John Milton

text: I am who I am; no more, no less.
author: Terry Goodkind

text: Indeed, learning to write may be part of learning to read. For all I know, writing comes out of a superior devotion to reading.
author: Eudora Welty

text: Only the gentle are ever really strong.
author: James  Dean

text: You can’t go back and make a new start, but you can start right now and make a brand new ending.
author: James R. Sherman

text: Be silent and safe — silence never betrays you; Be true to your word and your work and your friend; Put least trust in him who is foremost to praise you,Nor judge of a road till it draw to the end.
author: John Boyle O'Reilly

text: We should not judge people by their peak of excellence
author: but by the distance they have traveled from the point where they started.
