text: Most of what I say is complete truth. My edit button is broken.
author: Myra McEntire

text: I found power in accepting the truth of who I am. It may not be a truth that others can accept, but I cannot live any other way. How would it be to live a lie every minute of your life.
author: Alison Goodman

text: Lies are neither bad nor good. Like a fire they can either keep you warm or burn you to death, depending on how they're used.
author: Max Brooks

text: Words are like eggs dropped from great heights
author: you can no more call them back than ignore the mess they leave when they fall.

text: One lie has the power to tarnish a thousand truths.
author: Al David

text: ...when two opposite points of view are expressed with equal intensity, the truth does not necessarily lie exactly halfway between them. It is possible for one side to be simply wrong.
author: Richard Dawkins

text: The single best thing about coming out of the closet is that nobody can insult you by telling you what you've just told them.
author: Rachel Maddow

text: Only the hand that erases can write the true thing.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Show me a woman who doesn't feel guilty and I'll show you a man.
author: Erica Jong

text: I believe in God but people are liars. It's those people who say they are appointed by God who I don't believe in.
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer

text: Before you enbark on a journey of revenge, dig two graves.
author: Sara Shepard

text: Some things aren't meant to be known. Only believed.
author: Mary E. Pearson

text: A boy may be as disagreeable as he pleases, but when a girl refuses to crap sunshine on command, the world mutters darkly about her moods.
author: Scott Lynch

text: He moved like a dancer, which is not surprising; a horse is a beautiful animal, but it is perhaps most remarkable because it moves as if it always hears music.
author: Mark Helprin

text: Never apologize for showing your feelings. When you do, you are apologizing for the truth.
author: Jose N. Harris

text: Death is unstoppable. One must face it as a fact of life
author: D. Aswini

text: Knowing the truth is not always a kindness.
author: Rosamund Hodge

text: The truth is like salt. Men want to taste a little, but too much makes everyone sick.
author: Joe Abercrombie

text: It has been said, 'the truth will make men free.' The truth alone has never made anyone free. It is only doubt which will bring mental emancipation.
author: Anton Szandor LaVey

text: What cannot be said above all must not be silenced but written.
author: Jacques Derrida

text: What would happen if one woman told the truth about her life?The world would split open.
author: Muriel Rukeyser

text: When the time comes to die, make sure that all you have to do is die!
author: Jim Elliot

text: Truth is a matter of the imagination.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: There are many things in your heart you can never tell to another person. They are you, your private joys and sorrows, and you can never tell them. You cheapen yourself, the inside of yourself, when you tell them.
author: Greta Garbo

text: The simple step of a courageous individual is not to take part in the lie. "One word of truth outweighs the world.
author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

text: Knowledge is a destination. Truth, the journey.
author: Terry Goodkind

text: People are always complaining that life's not fair, but that simply isn't true. Life is extraordinarily fair. It's just not centered on you.
author: Lynn Marie Sager

text: How many people long for that "past, simpler, and better world," I wonder, without ever recognizing the truth that perhaps it was they who were simpler and better, and not the world about them?
author: R.A. Salvatore

text: When everything gets answered, it's fake.
author: Sean Penn

text: If you have no intention of loving or being loved, the whole journey is pointless.
author: Kate DiCamillo

text: At times to be silent is to lie. You will win because you have enough brute force. But you will not convince. For to convince you need to persuade. And in order to persuade you would need what you lack: Reason and Right
author: Miguel de Unamuno

text: I cannot love a man who cannot protect me.
author: Anne Bronte

text: Truth is ever to be found in the simplicity, and not in the multiplicity and confusion of things.
author: Isaac Newton

text: When one realises one is asleep, at that moment one is already half-awake.
author: P.D. Ouspensky

text: In the cherry blossom's shadethere's no such thingas a stranger.
author: Kobayashi Issa

text: The laughter of the world is merely loneliness pathetically trying to reassure itself.
author: Neal A. Maxwell

text: Proof is boring. Proof is tiresome. Proof is an irrelevance. People would far rather be handed an easy lie than search for a difficult truth, especially if it suits their own purposes.
author: Joe Abercrombie

text: Lying is easy. But it's lonely.""What do you mean?""When you lie to everyone about everything, what's left? What's true?""Nothing," I say."Exactly.
author: Victoria Schwab

text: You speak from your heart, young Firepaw. This will make you a stronger warrior one day." said LionheartTigerclaw growled. "Or it might make him give in to kittypet weakness right at the moment of attack
author: Erin Hunter

text: Falsehood is never in words
author: it is in things.

text: Some of us walk around with a necklace of hope, an armour of sanity, but at the end of the day, they always come off. We reveal our naked, vulnerable, real selves.
author: Karen Quan

text: Truth, she thought. As terrible as death. But harder to find.
author: Philip K. Dick

text: A great writer reveals the truth even when he or she does not wish to.
author: Tom Bissell

text: Night is a time of rigor, but also of mercy. There are truths which one can see only when it’s dark
author: Isaac Bashevis Singer

text: Kumikirot ang tyan? Kumikirot ang ulo? Correlation? I therefore conclude na ang utak ay parang tyan, sumasakit kapag walang laman.
author: Eros S. Atalia

text: That which can be destroyed by the truth should be.
author: P.C. Hodgell

text: The conflict between the will to deny horrible events and the will to proclaim them aloud is the central dialectic of psychological trauma.
author: Judith Lewis Herman

text: That is all very senseless, but this senselessness has a pretty mouth, and it smiles.
author: Robert Walser

text: Hell is truth seen too late.
author: Thomas Hobbes

text: If you are out to describe the truth, leave elegance to the tailor.
author: Ludwig Boltzmann

text: I’ve never had anyone’s approval, so I’ve learned to live without it.
author: Pat Conroy

text: Feelings can be like wild animals-we underrate how fierce they are until we've opened their cage
author: Richard Paul Evans

text: The naked truth is always better than the best-dressed lie.
author: Ann Landers

text: We become slaves the moment we hand the keys to the definition of reality entirely over to someone else, whether it is a business, an economic theory, a political party, the White House, Newsworld or CNN.
author: B.W. Powe

text: It takes a certain ingenuous faith - but I have it - to believe that people who read and reflect more likely than not come to judge things with liberality and truth.
author: A.C. Grayling

text: Understanding is a three edged sword: your side, their side, and the truth.
author: J. Michael Straczynski

text: The best way to show that a stick is crooked is not to argue about it or to spend time denouncing it, but to lay a straight stick alongside it
author: Dwight L. Moody

text: Discussion is impossible with someone who claims not to seek the truth, but already to possess it.
author: Romain Rolland

text: To love truth for truth's sake is the principal part of human perfection in this world, and the seed-plot of all other virtues.
author: John Locke

text: If only they would all just leave me alone with my books and my letters, I would be content to let life, and the world pass me by
author: Alison Weir

text: Before speaking, consult your inner-truth barometer, and resist the temptation to tell people only what they want to hear.
author: Wayne W. Dyer

text: Of all the weapons she had commanded, Elizabeth knew the least of love; and of all the weapons in the world, love was the most dangerous.
author: Seth Grahame-Smith

text: Because this is the way things are meant to be.(Press Tilton)
author: D.J. MacHale

text: They'll say you are bador perhaps you are mador at least you should stay undercover.Your mind must be bareif you would dareto think you can love more than one lover.
author: David Rovics

text: Don't change your mind just because people are offended
author: change your mind if you're wrong.

text: Alive or dead, the truth won't rest. Rise up while you can.
author: Mira Grant

text: A faith that cannot survive collision with the truth is not worth many regrets.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: Everything is permitted, Nothing is true.
author: Oliver Bowden

text: Truthfulness so often goes with ruthlessness.
author: Dodie Smith

text: We have to endure the discordance between imagination and fact. It is better to say, “I am suffering,” than to say, “This landscape is ugly.
author: Simone Weil

text: Science, my boy, is made up of mistakes, but they are mistakes which it is useful to make, because they lead little by little to the truth.
author: Jules Verne

text: ...we will stand amazed to see the topside of the tapestry and how God beautifully embroidered each circumstance into a pattern for our good and His glory.
author: Joni Eareckson Tada

text: Never do anything that you can't admit doing, because if you are that ashamed of whatever it is, it's probably wrong.
author: Ashly Lorenzana

text: People who pride themselves on their "complexity" and deride others for being "simplistic" should realize that the truth is often not very complicated. What gets complex is evading the truth.
author: Thomas Sowell

text: Don't worry about being effective. Just concentrate on being faithful to the truth.
author: Dorothy Day

text: She was reflecting back on a truth she had learned over the years: that people heard what they wanted to hear, saw what they wanted, believed what they wanted.
author: Jeffery Deaver

text: I am not afraid to die
author: I am only afraid of saying goodbye to you forever.

text: Totoo pala na kulang ang salita para sa lahat ng nararamdaman.
author: Eros S. Atalia

text: More people would learn from their mistakes if they weren't so busy denying them
author: Harold J. Smith

text: I like the truth, even when it does trouble me.
author: Juliet Marillier

text: Faith and Reason are like two wings of the human spirit by which is soars to the truth.
author: John Paul II

text: A lie, sometimes, can be truer than the truth, which is why fiction gets written.
author: Tim O'Brien

text: What are heavy? sea-sand and sorrow.What are brief? today and tomorrow.What are frail? spring blossoms and youth.What are deep? the ocean and truth.
author: Christina Rossetti

text: You will never be entirely comfortable. This is the truth behind the champion - he is always fighting something. To do otherwise is to settle.
author: Julien Smith

text: Today I bent the truth to be kind, and I have no regret, for I am far surer of what is kind than I am of what is true.
author: Robert Brault

text: I've nothing against people who love truth. Apart from the fact that they make dull companions.
author: Diane Setterfield

text: If you're not gonna tell the truth, then why start talking?
author: Gene Wilder

text: A thing can be true and still be desperate folly, Hazel.
author: Richard Adams

text: Politics determines who has the power, not who has the truth.
author: Paul Krugman

text: He spoke again as if he hadn’t just shattered his promise and her security. Her house of glass now lay in a pile of shards.
author: Aleatha Romig

text: Well and good if all things change, O Lord God, provided I am rooted in You.
author: San Juan de la Cruz

text: A woman has to be intelligent, have charm, a sense of humor, and be kind. It's the same qualities I require from a man.
author: Catherine Deneuve

text: If what he said was the truth, it broke her heart. If what he said was a lie, it was broken anyway.
author: Christine Feehan

text: ...oppression is as American as apple pie...
author: Audre Lorde

text: Because even if the lie is beautiful, the truth is what you face in the end.
author: Lauren DeStefano

text: They're just words. And words alone don't really mean anything. It's what you feel and what you believe when you say them that matter.
author: Dana Reinhardt

text: When one experiences truth, the madness of finding fault with others disappears.
author: S.N. Goenka

text: There is an inmost center in us all, where truth abides in fullness;....and, to know, rather consists in opening out a way where the imprisoned splendor may escape, then in effecting entry for a light supposed to be without.
author: Robert Browning

text: I didn't want the sun to rise. I didn't want the world to continue turning. I wanted the whole planet put into stasis until I could catch up.
author: Anna Sheehan

text: Submit to God and be at peace with him
author: in this way prosperity will come to you.

text: Never assume the obvious is true.
author: William Safire

text: Herein lies the tragedy of the age: not that men are poor, — all men know something of poverty; not that men are wicked, — who is good? not that men are ignorant, — what is Truth? Nay, but that men know so little of men.
author: W.E.B. Du Bois

text: you have to be the prude or the slut, and if you pick one, other people hate you for it, and you can’t trust anyone anymore, because they’re all after the same thing, and you see that you can never go back to how was before…
author: Ned Vizzini

text: The perfect killer has no friends. Only targets.
author: Brent Weeks

text: Truth is the only safe ground to stand on.
author: Elizabeth Cady Stanton

text: News flash, Bozo. Don't ever tell a girl to relax. It only makes us madder.
author: Jess Rothenberg

text: Once in a while it really hits people that they don't have to experience the world in the way they have been told to.
author: Alan Keightley

text: People resist a census, but give them a profile page and they'll spend all day telling you who they are.
author: Max Barry

text: All cynics are disappointed idealists. The more stars in the eyes, the harder the fall.
author: Josh Lanyon

text: I have a theory that as long as you have one good friend, one real friend, you can get through anything.
author: Dana Reinhardt

text: There were a hundred shades between a truth and lie, and she knew them all.
author: V.E. Schwab

text: If a man has so much heat he burns your skin when he touches you, he's the devil. Run away
author: Sarah Addison Allen

text: Martha: Truth or illusion, George; you don't know the difference.George: No, but we must carry on as though we did.Martha: Amen.
author: Edward Albee

text: Good writing is remembering detail. Most people want to forget. Don't forget things that were painful or embarrassing or silly. Turn them into a story that tells the truth.
author: Paula Danziger

text: I'd rather hear an ugly truth, rather than an obscure lie.
author: Ana Monnar

text: Antonin Artaud wrote on one of his drawings, "Never real and always true," and that is how depression feels. You know that it is not real, that you are someone else, and yet you know that it is absolutely true.
author: Andrew Solomon

text: The poet's job is to put into words those feelings we all have that are so deep, so important, and yet so difficult to name, to tell the truth in such a beautiful way, that people cannot live without it.
author: Jane Kenyon

text: Michael: There's nothing here to fear.Lucifer: Well, there's always the truth.
author: Mike Carey

text: Love without truth is sentimentality
author: it supports and affirms us but keeps us in denial about our flaws. Truth without love is harshness

text: He said the truth is like that water: it doesn't matter how hard you try to bury it
author: it'll always find some way back to the surface. It's resilient.

text: Becoming hurts.
author: Kat Howard

text: In wine, there's truth.
author: Pliny the Elder

text: It's funny whom we end up choosing to love and who ends up choosing to love us. It's rarely the people we think it should be.
author: Robin Jones Gunn

text: He looked at me intently, from what seemed behind the veil of a grave experience. Then slowly and prophetically, he said the scariest thing I'd ever heard: "Because the answer to a heartfelt question, Jack, will always break your heart.
author: Thomas H. Cook

text: Those who understand history are condemned to watch other idiots repeat it.
author: Peter Lamborn Wilson

text: I don’t think there’s anything wrong with telling the truth. I know it isn’t fashionable.
author: Craig Ferguson

text: Don't live by my words, don't die by them, chew them slowly digest them, and smile if they give nourishment to your soul.
author: Stanley Victor Paskavich

text: The First Splendid Truth: To be happy, I need to think about feeling good, feeling bad, and feeling right, in an atmosphere of growth.
author: Gretchen Rubin

text: To know the history of science is to recognize the mortality of any claim to universal truth.
author: Evelyn Fox Keller

text: You want me to pin my entire operation, the entire revolution on some teenaged love story? I can't believe this.
author: Victoria Aveyard

text: The first casualty when war comes is truth.
author: Hiram Johnson

text: An opinion should be the result of thought, not a substitute for it.
author: Jef Mallett

text: All you know is what you think you know, but that isn't always what's real.
author: Carrie Vaughn

text: To hate others is ugly.To hate yourself is uglier.
author: Hlovate

text: The distance between insanity and genius is measured only by success.
author: Bruce Feirstein

text: No matter what you do, someone always knew you would.
author: Ami McKay

text: If you are neutral in situations of injustice, you have chosen the side of the oppressor.
author: Desmond Tutu

text: The Universe doesn't like secrets. It conspires to reveal the truth, to lead you to it.
author: Lisa Unger

text: Sex is mathematics. Individuality no longer an issue. What does intelligence signify? Define reason. Desire - meaningless. Intellect is not a cure. Justice is dead.
author: Bret Easton Ellis

text: Over time, hidden truths morph in the dark soil of deceit into something much worse.
author: Patti Callahan Henry

text: The truth is the kindest thing we can give folks in the end.
author: Harriet Beecher Stowe

text: They dispute not in order to find or even to seek Truth, but for victory, and to appear the more learned and strenuous upholders of a contrary opinion. Such persons should be avoided by all who have not a good breastplate of patience.
author: Giordano Bruno

text: The person you hurt the most in this world was the person that brought you into it.
author: Vannary Rang

text: In all debates, let truth be thy aim, not victory, or an unjust interest.
author: William Penn

text: In the end, your past is not my past and your truth is not my truth and your solution - is not my solution.
author: Zadie Smith

text: It's only words... unless they're true.
author: David Mamet

text: I'm not mean, I'm honest. Nobody is ever straightforward. But sometimes people need to hear the truth.
author: Jessica Warman

text: Why do you so earnestly seek the truth in distant places?Look for delusion and truth in thebottom of your own heart.
author: Ryokan

text: The truth is more important than the facts.
author: Frank Lloyd Wright

text: This is what comes of having a heart, even a very small and young one. It causes no end of trouble, and that’s the truth.
author: Catherynne M. Valente

text: Mothers were the only ones you could depend on to tell the whole, unvarnished truth.
author: Margaret Dilloway

text: For people who say they hate being lied to, just start telling them nothing but the pure truth--about everything. That will teach them
author: Seth Adam Brown

text: Those who are enslaved to their sects are not merely devoid of all sound knowledge, but they will not even stop to learn!
author: Galen

text: They're so broke that they've actually cut essential services. In many places, they've cut policemen, because, who the fuck needs them? Or firemen, son of a bitch, it's much more fun watching something burn down.
author: Lewis Black

text: They don't actually want you to do your own thing, not unless it's their thing too.
author: Robert Cormier

text: In this treacherous worldNothing is the truth nor a lie.Everything depends on the colorOf the crystal through which one sees it
author: Pedro Calderon de la Barca

text: when you feel like hope is gone, look inside you and be strong and you'll finally see the truth- that hero lies in you.
author: Mariah Carey

text: But the truth doesn't need to be known, or believed, to be true.
author: Chris Crutcher

text: But my memories are like a fire in winter—whenever I'm cold I can warm my hands at them.—Ditta
author: Madeleine L'Engle

text: I have no ambition to govern men
author: it is a painful and thankless office.

text: Contentment and happiness didn't exist in my life for more than a few moments at a time, and they were really only illusionary. There was always something hidden. Lying in wait to spring up and ruin everything.
author: Sylvia Day

text: Leowin: Don't worry Luthiel. 'Truth's existence never depended upon belief.
author: Robert Fanney

text: Don’t spoil me with your lies, love me with your truth.
author: T.F. Hodge

text: My mom said I was an escapist at heart.... that I preferred imaginary worlds to the real one
author: Amy Plum

text: What is important is not what you hear said, it's what you observe.
author: Michael Connelly

text: For my part, whatever anguish of spirit it may cost, I am willing to know the whole truth; to know the worst and to provide for it.
author: Patrick Henry

text: I only feel like myself when I am alone.
author: Peter Cameron

text: There is no truth on this island of yours. Rather, there are as many truths as there are stars in the sky; and every one of them different.
author: Juliet Marillier

text: Accidents happen. Our bones shatter, our skin splits, our hearts break. We burn, we drown, we stay alive.
author: Moira Fowley-Doyle

text: It's one thing to think you're worthless, and quite another for somebody else to tell you that you are.
author: Jennifer Echols

text: In Western Civilization, our elders are books.
author: Gary Snyder

text: But just because you love something, I added to myself, doesn't mean you'll ever be great. Not if you don't work. Most people stink at the things they love.
author: Amy Chua

text: The ego lusts for satisfaction. It has a prideful ferocious appetite for its version of "truth". It is the most challenging aspect to conquer; the cause for most spiritual turmoil.
author: T.F. Hodge

text: Emotional states are fairly quick bursts of neuronal gossip. Traits, on the other hand, are more like the neuronal equivalent of committed relationships.
author: Yongey Mingyur

text: For my true thoughts have spent more time in your company than in anyone else's, these last two or three months, and where my thoughts are, there am I, in truth".
author: A.S. Byatt

text: The past cannot be changed. The future is yet in your power.
author: Mary Pickford

text: Beware:Ignorance Protects itself.IgnorancePromotes suspicion.SuspicionEngenders fear.Fear quails,Irrational and blind,Or fear looms,Defiant and closed.Blind, closed,Suspicious, afraid,IgnoranceProtects itself,And protected,Ignorance grows.
author: Octavia E. Butler

text: It is impossible to enslave, mentally or socially, a bible-reading people. The principles of the bible are the groundwork of human freedom.
author: Horace Greeley

text: The author who benefits you most is not the one who tells you something you did not know before, but the one who gives expression to the truth that has been dumbly struggling in you for utterance.
author: Oswald Chambers

text: Too many people out there tell us what we can and cannot do but…they don’t know who we are, what’s put in us.
author: Alex Rogers

text: We cannot decide to love. We cannot compel anyone to love us. There's no secret recipe, only love itself. And we are at its mercy--there's nothing we can do.
author: Nina George

text: IF her life had taught her anything, it was that you never really knew what people had going on beneath the surface. People were shit. The only difference between them and animals was people felt the need to hide it.
author: Stacia Kane

text: Experience has taught us that we have only one enduring weapon in our struggle against mental illness: the emotional discovery and emotional acceptance of the truth in the individual and unique history of our childhood.
author: Alice  Miller

text: The Truth is found when men (and Women) are free to pursue it.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: I can be forced to live without happiness, but I will never consent to live without honor.
author: Pierre Corneille

text: Truth is naturally universal...and shines into many different windows, though many are clouded.
author: Anya Seton

text: That man is best who sees the truth himself. Good too is he who listens to wise counsel. But who is neither wise himself nor willing to ponder wisdom is not worth a straw.
author: Hesiod

text: Power is not a means
author: it is an end. One does not establish a dictatorship in order to safeguard a revolution

text: I suppose that we women are such cowards that we think a man will save us from fears, and we marry him.
author: Bram Stoker

text: The truth doesn't always make a good story, does it?
author: Lev Grossman

text: Institutionalized rejection of difference is an absolute necessity in a profit economy which needs outsiders as surplus people.
author: Audre Lorde

text: Do you want this to be a love story?
author: Jenny Downham

text: It is error only, and not truth, that shrinks from inquiry.
author: Thomas Paine

text: Sometimes when I see a bad performance and people still clap... I wonder if they're clapping because they liked what they saw or because they're happy it's over?
author: Kanye West

text: I tried.Can't do it.Brain's empty.
author: Sharon Creech

text: Nothing is easier than self-deceit.For what every man wishes,that he also believes to be true.
author: Demosthenes

text: The aim of science is to discover and illuminate truth. And that, I take it, is the aim of literature, whether biography or history... It seems to me, then, that there can be no separate literature of science.
author: Rachel Carson

text: For those who like that sort of thing," said Miss Brodie in her best Edinburgh voice, "That is the sort of thing they like.
author: Muriel Spark

text: I am an inconsistent creature. Perhaps it is the pressure of my past, and not my own perverse mind, that has made me into this contradictory being. I am all too well aware of this fault in myself. You must forgive me.
author: Soseki Natsume

text: If you always attach positive emotions to the things you want, and never attach negative emotions to the things you don't, then that which you desire most will invariably come your way.
author: Matt D. Miller

text: If what you seek is Truth, there is one thing you must have above all else.” “I know. An overwhelming passion for it.” “No. An unremitting readiness to admit you may be wrong.
author: Anthony de Mello

text: Freedom means the supremacy of human rights everywhere. Our support goes to those who struggle to gain those rights and keep them. Our strength is our unity of purpose. To that high concept there can be no end save victory.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: the truth will set u free but first it will piss u off
author: Albert Borris

text: You must accept the truth from whatever source it comes.
author: Maimonides

text: It's a strange truth that no matter how persuaded we might be of our own correctness, the discomfiting realization that others disagree with us causes a paralyzing inability to argue the case convincingly.
author: Brittney Ryan

text: Then I realized that most of the world's problems stemmed from macho dickheadism, and if I cold defeat that I could save the world.
author: Carrie Vaughn

text: The secret of high finance...if you really need a loan, you won't qualify. And if you don't need a loan, all the lenders will line up to give you money.
author: Joanne Fluke

text: By their actions, they will show you who they are.
author: Leah Remini

text: In fact, the truth cannot be communicated until it is perceived.
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

text: History is idle gossip about a happening whose truth is lost the instant it has taken place.
author: Gore Vidal

text: Satire's nature is to be one-sided, contemptuous of ambiguity, and so unfairly selective as to find in the purity of ridicule an inarguable moral truth.
author: E.L. Doctorow

text: There is no other way to determine the difference between the will of God and the crafts of satan... Jesus is the way, the truth and the life... The Holy Spirit of God is the Comforter...
author: Israelmore Ayivor

text: You can resolve to live your life with integrity. Let your credo be this: Let the lie come into the world, let it even triumph. But not through me.
author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

text: Don't doubt yourself, that's what haters are for.
author: Turcois Ominek

text: Humility is nothing but truth, and pride is nothing but lying.
author: St. Vincent de Paul

text: I have never killed anybody, it is true, but it is because I lacked the courage or the time, not because I lacked the desire
author: Eduardo Galeano

text: The truth of the story lies in the details.
author: Paul Auster

text: There isn't any questioning the fact that some people enter your life, at the exact point of need, want or desire - it's sometimes a coincendence and most times fate, but whatever it is, I am certain it came to make me smile.
author: Nikki Rowe

text: I'll figure out how to be truer: to let people go if they need to be let go of, and to hold on tight if that's what's called for. I will pay attention, so I can cross each human heart that comes across my path, cross it as true as I can.
author: Laura Pritchett

text: The only faithfulness people have is to emotions they're trying to recapture
author: Norman Mailer

text: There are trivial truths and there are great truths. The opposite of a trivial truth is plainly false. The opposite of a great truth is also true.
author: Niels Bohr

text: I would have offered you a forest of truth, but you wish to speak of a single leaf
author: David Gemmell

text: The truth that makes men free is for the most part the truth which men prefer not to hear.
author: Herbert Agar

text: There is nothing so powerful as truth - and often nothing so strange.
author: Daniel Webster

text: His whole life was a sham, a fairy tale. The truth hidden behind a wall of lies, each lie another brick in the wall until he probably couldn't see the truth anymore.
author: Shaun Jeffrey

text: I would rather die an individual than live my life as a clone.
author: Cristina Marrero

text: One truth doesn't refute another. Truth doesn't lie in the object, but in how we see it.
author: Michael J. Sullivan

text: We are all wonderful, beautiful wrecks. That's what connects us--that we're all broken, all beautifully imperfect.
author: Emilio Estevez

text: To cheapen the lives of any group of men, cheapens the lives of all men, even our own. This is a law of human psychology, or human nature. And it will not be repealed by our wishes, nor will it be merciful to our blindness.
author: William Pickens

text: A revolution is coming – a revolution which will be peaceful if we are wise enough
author: compassionate if we care enough

text: When you have money in hand,only you forget who are you .But when you do not have any money in your hand,the whole world forget who you are.It's life.
author: Bill Gates

text: Could it have been anyone, or was it destiny? When I'm considering this I find it helpful to quote the wisdom of my father, who once told me, "Who knows why the fuck anything happens?
author: Ben Aaronovitch

text: A woman who surrenders her freedom need not surrender her dignity.
author: Wally Lamb

text: Purity of heart is what enables us to see.
author: Pope Benedict XVI

text: Fanatics can justify practically any atrocity to themselves. The more untenable their position becomes, the harder they hold to it, and the worse the things they are willing to do to support it.
author: Mercedes Lackey

text: What the world stigmatizes as romantic is often more nearly allied to the truth than is commonly supposed.
author: Anne Bronte

text: She realized all at once that Doon, thin, dark eyed Doon, with his troublesome temper and his terrible brown jacket, and his good heart---- was the person she knew better than anyone now. He was her best friend.--City of Ember--
author: Jeanne DuPrau

text: In essentials, unity; in non-essentials, liberty; in all things, charity.
author: Rupertus Meldenius

text: If you are unable to find the truth right where you are, where else do you expect to find it?
author: Dogen

text: If my work has a theme, I suspect it is a simple one: that most human beings are inescapably alone, and therein lies their tragedy.
author: Richard Yates

text: What a sad story, I thought for so long. Not that I now think it was happy. But I think it is true, and thus the question of whether it is sad or happy has no meaning whatever.
author: Bernhard Schlink

text: He who has truth at his heart need never fear the want of persuasion on his tongue.
author: John Ruskin

text: Why do relationships have to be so complicated?
author: Erin Hunter

text: The stories people tell you about themselves seem to retain the possibility of being false. But what you discover about them by yourself seems to be the truth.
author: John Verdon

text: We learn to make a shell for ourselves when we are young and then spend the rest of our lives hoping for someone to reach inside and touch us. Just touch us—anything more than that would be too much for us to bear.
author: Bill Russell

text: There are two paths of which one may choose in the walk of life; one we are born with, and the one we consciously blaze. One is naturally true, while the other is a perceptive illusion. Choose wisely at each fork in the road.
author: T.F. Hodge

text: See now the power of truth; the same experiment which at first glance seemed to show one thing, when more carefully examined, assures us of the contrary.
author: Galileo Galilei

text: I think most are too afraid to see the truth.
author: Morgan Rhodes

text: She had the best kind of courage, or maybe the worst kind, the kind that gets you into trouble.
author: Alistair MacLean

text: Brilliance is impossible without a touch of insanity.
author: Skyla Madi

text: The beauty of truth is that it need not be proclaimed or believed. It skips from soul to soul, changing form each time it touches, but it is what it is, I have seen it, and someday you will, too.
author: Mark Helprin

text: We don't receive wisdom we must discover it for ourselves.
author: Marcel Proust

text: Give me the facts and I will determine my own truths.
author: Tanjlisa Marie

text: Whatever demon invented stiletto-heeled boots should roast in hell...
author: Cherise Sinclair

text: Miles and years become suddenly invisible when you find yourself back where you started from, as if you've learned nothing and you are once again the person you once were.
author: Karen   White

text: If you like someone, you tell the truth because that, to me, is respect. If you hate someone, you tell them the truth because what do you have to lose?
author: Tara Kelly

text: It was so much easier to be angry. Being angry made him feel strong, even though-- and this contradiction did nothing to diminish his anger-- he was angry only because his position was so weak.
author: Lev Grossman

text: I believe that ignorance is the root of all evil.And that no one knows the truth.
author: Molly Ivins

text: There is a tragic clash between Truth and the world. Pure undistorted truth burns up the world.
author: Nikolai A. Berdyaev

text: You can’t live your life thinking death is going to come all the time. You have to live life and if it comes, then we can only hope we were able to do all the things we wanted. He has to want to do something. Everyone does.
author: Shelena Shorts

text: Because the truth is, it isn't worth loving something if you aren't going to love it all the way.
author: Goldy Moldavsky

text: Love doesn't always come when you want it to. Sometimes it just happens, despite your will.
author: V.C. Andrews

text: I wasn't mean; I wasn't evil. I was nice. And let me tell you, a hesitant man is the last thing in the world a woman needs. She needs a lover and a warrior, not a Really Nice Guy.
author: John Eldredge

text: hindi dahil sa hindi mo naiintindihan ang isang bagay ay kasinungalingan na ito. at hindi lahat ng kaya mong intindihin ay katotohanan.
author: Bob Ong

text: The essence of science is that it is always willing to abandon a given idea for a better one
author: the essence of theology is that it holds its truths to be eternal and immutable.

text: In practical life we are compelled to follow what is most probable
author: in speculative thought we are compelled to follow truth.

text: The truth is revealed when you are ready to receive it when you need it in order to move forward to take the next step in your journey. to move on toward your destiny.
author: Alyson Noel

text: That wasn't love
author: that was stupidity.

text: Never run from the truth. It is always there
author: it never changes – save your energy.

text: But thoughts don't care about truth and shit. They sit up in your mind and fuck with you whenever.
author: Richard Pryor

text: Don't let the fear of losing them keep you from loving them.
author: Nicole  Williams

text: Truth, like love and sleep, resents approaches that are too intense.
author: W.H. Auden

text: You never know when the truth will come home. You can’t choose the time. The time chooses you.
author: Rick Yancey

text: The scientist only imposes two things, namely truth and sincerity, imposes them upon himself and upon other scientists.
author: Erwin Schrodinger

text: The truth has become an insult.
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie

text: There's always a but. It's a magical word. You can say anything you want, go on for as long as you want, and then all you have to do is add the magic word and instantly everything you said is erased, turned meaningless, just like that.
author: Charles Benoit

text: God is not interested in your art but, your heart.
author: Ifeanyi Enoch Onuoha

text: Every concept of reality is all in our head, belief is what makes truth.
author: Christy Leigh Stewart

text: Reality is what trips you up when you walk around with your eyes closed.
author: F. Paul Wilson

text: A trusting heart will follow, only those who truly follow their heart.
author: Anthony Liccione

text: What was my truth worth, if I was prepared to defend the entire world, but not those who were close to me? If I subdued hate, but wouldn't give love a chance?
author: Sergei Lukyanenko

text: Creationists eagerly seek a gap in present-day knowledge or understanding. If an apparent gap is found, it is assumed that God, by default, must fill it.
author: Richard Dawkins

text: When you think of the long and gloomy history of man, you will find more hideous crimes have been committed in the name of obedience than have ever been committed in the name of rebellion.
author: C.P. Snow

text: Numbers do not feel. Do not bleed or weep or hope. They do not know bravery or sacrifice. Love and allegiance. At the very apex of callousness, you will find only ones and zeros.
author: Amie Kaufman

text: Fuck you! I hope you die!""Everybody Dies," I said. "So fuck you.
author: Lawrence Block

text: And just when I though things were starting to get better, everything had gone wrong again.
author: Rachel Ward

text: The perfect killer has no conscience.
author: Brent Weeks

text: I don't think I could ever work in such a blatantly hierarchical corporate setting. I know that everyone in this world is not equal, but I can't bear environments that make this truth so obvious.
author: Peter Cameron

text: Tell people the hammered truth, and it will ring like steel against an anvil.
author: Elizabeth Haydon

text: The fruit of empty hopes is more bitter than the saddest truth.
author: Angel Wagenstein

text: Nothing but truth is immortal.
author: Robert G. Ingersoll

text: Underneath, he was a good cat. Loyal and loving. But no one ever bothered to look past his rough exterior, because in reality, looks meant more than everyone wanted to admit.
author: Cambria Hebert

text: Now I know what success is: living your truth, sharing it.
author: Kamal Ravikant

text: If it ever occurs to people to value the honour of the mind equally with the honour of the body, we shall get a social revolution of a quite unparalleled sort.
author: Dorothy L. Sayers

text: If I am right, Thy grace impartStill in the right to stay;If I am wrong, O, teach my heartTo find that better way!
author: Alexander Pope

text: I would rather be a devil in alliance with truth, than an angel in alliance with falsehood.
author: Ludwig Feuerbach

text: Scepticism is the first step towards truth.
author: Denis Diderot

text: The moment you make someone promise anything is the same moment you ask them to lie to you.
author: Molly Ringwald

text: It's easier to fake it. When you fake it for sixteen years, it becomes part of you, something you don't think about.
author: Alex Flinn

text: And where, I ask you, can a man escape to, when he hasn’t enough madness left inside him? The truth is an endless death agony. The truth is death. You have to choose: death or lies. I’ve never been able to kill myself.
author: Louis-Ferdinand Celine

text: Man keeps looking for a truth that fits his reality. Given our reality, the truth doesn't fit.
author: Werner Erhard

text: I play with the fire of my own truth," she told me, "I will burn for the things I love.
author: Mia Hollow

text: She’s a manner of speaking.Even the flowers don’t come back, or the green leaves.There are new flowers, new green leaves.There are other beautiful days.Nothing comes back, nothing repeats itself, because everything is real.
author: Alberto Caeiro

text: If we act like prey, they’ll act like predators
author: Alyxandra Harvey

text: Everything in life has some good in it. And when something awful happens, the goodness stands out even more--it's sad, but that's the truth.
author: Banana Yoshimoto

text: It is a profound and necessary truth that the deep things in science are not found because they are useful
author: they are found because it was possible to find them.

text: And the truth must finally lie in that which every oppressed individual feels within himself but hasn't the courage to express
author: Wilhelm Reich

text: The right to search for the truth implies also a duty
author: one must not conceal any part of what one has recognized to be the truth.

text: For to speak the truth, there are but few that care thus to spend their time, but choose rather to be speaking of things to no profit.
author: John Bunyan

text: the squeeky wheel gets the grease.
author: Josh Billings

text: Where we fall are the stepping-stones for our journey.
author: Lolly Daskal

text: Whether your pleasing or pissing everybody off, your doing something wrong.
author: David Cook

text: The truth was stranger than the official fiction.
author: Dean Koontz

text: We have to know the truth about the past to discover out future.
author: Patti Callahan Henry

text: When you procrastinate you become a slave to yesterday.
author: Stephen McCranie

text: Power is not revealed by striking hard or often, but by striking true.
author: Honore de Balzac

text: I hear my silence talked of in every lane
author: The suppression of a cry is itself a cry of pain.

text: The line between the public life and the private life has been erased, due to the rapid decline of manners and courtesy. There is a certain crudeness and crassness that has suddenly become accepted behavior, even desirable.
author: Fannie Flagg

text: If a man confessed anything on his death bed, it was the truth; for no man could stare death in the face and lie.
author: Richard Wright

text: The human person finds his perfection "in seeking and loving what is true and good.
author: Pope Benedict XVI

text: May be its mine bad-luck Or yours not to get me But I still have hope Of being yours
author: Hasil Paudyal

text: No human being will ever know the truth, for even if they happened to say it by chance, they would not know they had done so.
author: Xenophon

text: Despite his money and his looks and all the good-on-paper attributes he possessed, he was not a reader, and, well, let's just say that is the sort of nonsense up with which we will not put.
author: Eleanor Brown

text: Mika: Were you happy?Hiro: I was so happy.
author: Ibuki Haneda

text: A lot of young girls think that the opposite of fake is rudeness. And just as ugly as fake is, so is saying whatever is on your mind because it's the truth.
author: Tyra Banks

text: I am powerful in my love for myself. The truth of my being is I love myself exactly as I am. I honor my empowered heart.
author: Amy Leigh Mercree

text: A truth is what it is. A lie, a thought out deception more brutal than a truth could ever be.
author: Charlotte Armstrong

text: Never hold resentments for the person who tells you what you need to hear; count them among your truest, most caring, and valuable friends.
author: Mike  Norton

text: From the mouths of the innocents flows truth.
author: Rae Carson

text: Everything in life is speaking in spite of its apparent silence.
author: Hazrat Inayat Khan

text: The truth is sometimes a poor competitor in the market place of ideas – complicated, unsatisfying, full of dilemmas, always vulnerable to misinterpretation and abuse.
author: George F. Kennan

text: The truths that matter most to us come always half spoken.
author: Baltasar Gracian

text: I've managed to stay alive out here in the Ruin because I'm a realist. I allow the truth to be the truth, no matter how much I might want it to be something else.
author: Jonathan Maberry

text: To say that the first casualty of war is truth is to miss the rather more important point that a principal weapon of war is lies.
author: Harry M. Collins

text: The truth is like a lion
author: you don’t have to defend it. Let it loose

text: Hell is truth known too late.
author: J.C. Ryle

text: Talking to a therapist, I thought, was like taking your clothes off and then taking your skin off, and then having the other person say, "Would you mind opening up your rib cage so that we can start?
author: Julie Schumacher

text: Truth is a hard master, and costly to serve, but it simplifies all problems.
author: Ellis Peters

text: There is no rest for the humble except in despising the great, whose only thought of the people is inspired by self-interest or sadism.
author: Louis-Ferdinand Celine

text: In order that all men may be taught to speak truth, it is necessary that all likewise should learn to hear it.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: I am the truth, since I am part of what is real, but neither more nor less than those around me.
author: Wallace Stevens

text: lies, truth, loveI have always loved truth so passionately that I have often resorted to lying as a way of introducing it into the minds which were ignorant of it's charms.
author: Giacomo Casanova

text: If I don't eat junk, I don't gain weight.
author: Paulina Christensen

text: Within every elaborate lie, a kernel of truth.
author: Marisha Pessl

text: Hope is for people who can't see the Truth.
author: Jeff Lindsay

text: Personal perception of perfection is like that. You see what you want to see. After awhile you just see what you need to.
author: Matthew Good

text: Sand lines my soul which is filled with the breath of the ocean.
author: A.D. Posey

text: When something ought to be true then it proves to be a very powerful truth.
author: Marilynne Robinson

text: I offer my opponents a bargain: if they will stop telling lies about us, I will stop telling the truth about them
author: Adlai E. Stevenson II

text: There are only two ways of telling the complete truth--anonymously and posthumously.
author: Thomas Sowell

text: The number of people who believe a thing has no bearing upon its truth.
author: Marie Sexton

text: You cannot believe everything you hear
author: Jude Morgan

text: It is only possible to live happily ever after on a daily basis.
author: Margaret Wander Bonanno

text: What's even more messed up than funerals, is the way people treat you after the funeral. Like you're diseased or something.
author: Denise Jaden

text: The idea of celibate vampires is ridiculous. To me, vampires are sex. I don't get a vampire story about abstinence. I don't care about high school students. I find them irritating and uninformed.
author: Alan Ball

text: I was saying the right things, but not the really true things.
author: Katherine Hannigan

text: How you approach birth is intimately connected with how you approach life
author: William Sears

text: Each of us is shaped by the truth of our natures.Von to Silver
author: Adrian Phoenix

text: What you see is what you see
author: Frank Stella

text: The terrible thing about the quest for truth is that you find it.
author: Remy de Gourmont

text: It is the tragedy of a distinguished mind and a generous nature that have gone unappreciated in a conventional, unimaginative world. A victim of men's incomprehension of women, a symptom of women's mistrust of men.
author: Francis Wyndham

text: Better the comfort of a lie than the absurdity of the truth.
author: Tanya Huff

text: The truth doesn't mind being told every once in a while.
author: Alysha Speer

text: I will never compromise Truth for the sake of getting along with people who can only get along when we agree.
author: D.R. Silva

text: I'm planning, you see, to try to confine myself to the truth. That's hard for an old, inveterate fantasy martyr and liar who has never hesitated to give truth the form he felt the occasion demanded.
author: Ingmar Bergman

text: Questers of the truth, that’s who dogs are; seekers after the invisible scent of another being’s authentic core.
author: Jeffrey Moussaieff Masson

text: A strange thing, words. Once they're said, it's hard to imagine they're untrue.
author: Lauren DeStefano

text: Truth cannot be constructed. To live in ideology is, as Havel so eloquently reminds us, inevitably to live in a lie. Truth can only be revealed. We cannot be creators, only receptors.
author: James W. Sire

text: I come from Des Moines. Somebody had to.
author: Bill Bryson

text: History was a series of decisions about what to tell and a series of accidents about what survived after telling. Not truth, but a historian could search for truth, and the search was as worthy as any other human activity.
author: David Drake

text: the worst thing about being lied to is knowing you weren't worth the truth.
author: Turcois Ominek

text: I've often mused over the idea that madness is actually a sane reaction to an insane world.
author: Stephanie Ericsson

text: Sometimes playing stupid opens your eyes to the truth.
author: Anthony Liccione

text: Anything not worth doing is worth not doing well. Think about it.
author: Elias Schwartz

text: Think? Why think! We have computers to do that for us.
author: Jean Rostand

text: The story is about being loyal to the truth as a nation, that citizens of a democracy are collectively responsible for what their troops do in war, good or bad.
author: Kevin Sites

text: Decide what your truth is. Then live it.
author: Kamal Ravikant

text: Enlightenment is not a goal to be attained, it is a state-of-being to be regained.
author: Kim Chestney

text: People just don't seem to get me. Don't understand that I need my space. Always telling me what to do. They think rules and routines and clean hands and your p's and q's will make everything all right. They haven't got a clue.
author: Rachel Ward

text: Complaining is truly my strongest weakness.
author: Evinda Lepins

text: George, she says it's the truth that matters. We live and die for the chance to maybe tell a little bit of the truth, maybe shame the Devil just a little bit before we go.
author: Mira Grant

text: Superficiality is the curse of the modern world.
author: Matthew Kelly

text: The Memory Of You Is Like A Drug To Me
author: Jeremy Aldana

text: We all know we're one day closer to the end when we wake up in the morning. We just kid ourselves that it's not happening.
author: Rachel Ward

text: The words he said, too, must be human enough to bleed.
author: Cameron Conaway

text: I never truckled. I never took off the hat to Fashion and held it out for pennies. I told them the truth. They liked it or they didn't like it. What had that to do with me? I told them the truth.
author: Frank Norris

text: This is the basis, and I am not being tried for whether I am a Communist, I am being tried for fighting for the right of my people, who are still second-class citizens in this United States of America
author: Paul Robeson

text: For truth is always strange
author: stranger than fiction.

text: If truth doesn't set you free, generosity of spirit will.
author: Katerina Stoykova Klemer

text: Without balance, a life is no longer worth the effort.
author: Olen Steinhauer

text: I ask myself: is every story that has ever been written in this world, a story of suffering and affliction?
author: Clarice Lispector

text: If truth is not acceptable, it becomes the imagination of others.
author: Patrick White

text: A day without laughter is like living in darkness; you try to find your way around, but you can't see clearly.
author: Emily Mitchell

text: truth is a friendthat asks for loyaltyand acceptancethen it enters our heartsdissolving the boundariesfreeing us from lonliness
author: Nirmala

text: She hadn't lied. She hadn't betrayed anyone's trust; still, she felt she had done something wrong. Or rather, she had not yet done the right thing. Was there a difference between these two sins?
author: Michael David Lukas

text: You'd challenge me and lose. You know it, I know it, but you'd still do it. Sometimes your sense of honor confuses the hell out of me.
author: Seanan McGuire

text: In the pit of her stomach she realized that everything she raged against on Saturday night-- the restrictions, rules, and guidelines-- was born of an ancient fervor. Every rule ever established, from the beginning of time, invited mutiny.
author: Robin Jones Gunn

text: Doon was touched. Kenny looked like a tiny little wisp, but there was something strong inside him. --People of Sparks--
author: Jeanne DuPrau

text: Friends ask you questions
author: enemies question you.

text: The bittersweet about truth is that nothing could be more hurtful, yet nothing could be more helpful.
author: Mike  Norton

text: The most inefficient and self-harming thing a person can do is go out looking for love. Let it find you when the time is right and you're out doing what you love to do. Only then will you find it in its truest form.
author: A.J. Darkholme

text: The search for truth takes you where the evidence leads you, even if, at first, you don't want to go there.
author: Bart D. Ehrman

text: Truth is confirmed by inspection and delay
author: falsehood by haste and uncertainty.

text: What comes, when it comes, will be what it is.
author: Alberto Caeiro

text: To speak and act truth with constancy and precision is nearly as difficult, and perhaps as meretorious, as to speak it under intimidation or penalty
author: John Ruskin

text: Who says that fictions only and false hairBecome a verse? Is there in truth no beauty?Is all good structure in a winding stair?
author: George Herbert

text: Justice is itself the great standing policy of civil society; and any eminent departure from it, under any circumstances, lies under the suspicion of being no policy at all.
author: Edmund Burke

text: A statement of truth, and that is:Everything I have accomplished is Thanks to the Lord above, and without him my success would have been impossible.
author: Felicia Martin

text: what matters is not the enclosure of the work within a harmonious figure, but the centrifugal force produced by it -- a plurality of language as a guarantee of a truth that is not merely partial.
author: Italo Calvino

text: Anything that smiles often needs to be reminded that the world is a cruel, dark place.
author: Matthew Inman

text: There are always consequences to the truth.
author: Patti Callahan Henry

text: Truth will always be truth, regardless of lack of understanding, disbelief or ignorance.
author: W. Clement Stone

text: She has given you something of value: the truth in her heart.
author: Lloyd Alexander

text: I encounter one example after another of how relative truth is.
author: Raoul Wallenberg

text: Propaganda is a sprinter, but truth is a long-distance runner.
author: Ernest Partridge

text: Reproach is infinite, and knows no endSo voluble a weapon is the tongue;Wounded, we wound; and neither side can failFor every man has equal strength to rail.
author: Homer

text: O how we call each other namesYou call me schizophrenicI call you GodBut we do agree on oneDeluded are we both.
author: Sunil Vidyarthi

text: The truth will set you free. Unless you're guilty.
author: Keith Ablow

text: You're the only one that can put pressure on yourself... No one else can put pressure on you. It's self-inflicted. For me, I just want to go out and play football.
author: Maurice Jones-Drew

text: There is only one kind of shock worse than the totally unexpected: the expected for which one has refused to prepare.
author: Mary Renault

text: Life is just a short period of time in which you are alive.
author: Philip Roth

text: In war, the first casualty is truth.
author: Terry Hayes

text: Grief, no matter how you try to cater to its wail, has a way of fading away.
author: V.C. Andrews

text: You have to be able to recognize your truths in the daylight before you can find them in the dark.
author: Kelli Jae Baeli

text: The key to wisdom is this -- constant and frequent questioning ... for by doubting we are led to question and by questioning we arrive at the truth.
author: Pierre Abelard

text: We need to be fit and ready for anything that might come our way.
author: Aaron B. Powell

text: Defeat anger, stop using it as a shield against truth, and you will find the compassion you need to forgive the people you love.
author: Glenn Beck

text: Truth persuades by teaching, but does not teach by persuading.
author: Tertullian

text: When God is ready for you to move, He will make your situation uncomfortable.
author: Germany Kent

text: One has a greater sense of intellectual degradation after an interview with a doctor than from any human experience.
author: Alice James

text: The scientific spirit is of more value than its products, and irrationally held truths may be more harmful than reasoned errors.
author: Thomas Henry Huxley

text: No matter how smart we may think we are, no matter how committed we are to our truth, we can lose our way.
author: Kamal Ravikant

text: To forgive is wisdom, to forget is genius. And easier. Because it's true. It's a new world every heart beat.
author: Joyce Cary

text: Proclaim the truth and do not be silent through fear.
author: Catherine of Siena

text: Isn't strength the ability to renounce every lie in your heart?
author: Bisco Hatori

text: Remember, any lie you are told, even deliberately, is often a more significant fact than a truth told in all sincerity.
author: Halldor Laxness

text: It is what it is. Isn't that how these things always go? They are what they are. We just get to cope.
author: Mira Grant

text: You want to tell a story? Grow a heart. Grow two. Now, with the second heart, smash the first one into bits.
author: Charles Yu

text: I'm only telling you on the truth," he said. "If you can't stand the truth, don't ask for it.
author: Colum McCann

text: You need to understand that truth is stranger than fiction. Listen: people are willing to swallow any old tripe as long as you say it without flinching. They want to be told stuff. And they don't want to doubt you either. It's too hard.
author: Craig Silvey

text: When did swearing become so easy? You still would never swear in front of your parents or most adults, but when you're with your friends it's like every fifth word. Why couldn't learning Spanish be that easy?
author: Charles Benoit

text: My mother is a firm believer in the long pause, useful in interrogations, proclamations of truth, and the occasional cutting dead of someone without their knowing it.
author: Suzanne Finnamore

text: Fifty percent of something is better than than one hundred percent of nothing.
author: Chuck Barris

text: She took refuge on the firm ground of fiction, through which indeed there curled the blue river of truth.
author: Henry James

text: The truth about an animal is far more exciting and altogether more beautiful than all the myths woven about it.
author: Konrad Lorenz

text: A story can always break into pieces while it sits inside a book on a shelf; and, decades after we have read it even twenty times, it can open us up, by cut or caress, to a new truth.
author: Andre Dubus

text: Men don't need linguistic talent
author: they just need courage and words.

text: It is not by believing but by doubting that one can attain to the truth, which is ever changing form and condition.
author: Augusto Roa Bastos

text: All true histories contain instruction; though, in some, the treasure may be hard to find, and when found, so trivial in quantity, that the dry, shriveled kernel scarcely compensates for the trouble of cracking the nut.
author: Anne Bronte

text: There is no 'the truth,' 'a truth'--truth is not one thing, or even a system. It is an increasing complexity.
author: Adrienne Rich

text: In the world there exists no aesthetic plane, not even the aesthetic plane of goodness.
author: Clarice Lispector

text: What we believe about God is the most important truth we believe, and it's the one truth that does the most to shape us. God is the Sun too bright for us to see. Jesus is the Prism who makes the colors beautiful and comprehensible.
author: Michael Spencer

text: The truth is the only thing worth having, and, in a civilized life, like ours, where so many risks are removed, facing it is almost the only courageous thing left to do.
author: Edward Verrall Lucas

text: The nasty little apes that call themselves human beings can do nothing except run and hide.
author: Michael Crichton

text: Mother’s particular devils had remained mysterious to me for decades. So had her past. Few born liars ever intentionally embark in truth’s direction, even those who believe that such a journey might axiomatically set them free.
author: Mary Karr

text: The truth was only another mask, even if it was the best fit, the closest to skin and all that lay beneath
author: Michelle Sagara West

text: Why could we say more to each other when it counted less?
author: Laura Dave

text: We live in a world where unfortunately the distinction between true and false appears to become increasingly blurred by manipulation of facts, by exploitation of uncritical minds, and by the pollution of the language.
author: Arne Tiselius

text: Chase after the truth like all hell and you’ll free yourself, even though you never touch its coat tails.
author: Clarence Darrow

text: And yet, sometimes facts are no more than pitiful consequences, because guilt does not reside in our acts but in the intentions that give rise to our act. Everything turns on our intentions.
author: Sandor Marai

text: Don't be afraid to be afraid. Have fear, and then conquer the shit out of it.
author: D. Antoinette Foy

text: Every war is ironic because every war is worse than expected. Every war constitutes an irony of situation because its means are so melodramatically disproportionate to its presumed ends.
author: Paul Fussell

text: A true friend doesn't have guts
author: they beat you up and later plead with you to beat them back.

text: The truth doesn't get you very far on the streets, or in a group home, or even in high school. That's probably why the idea of Liars, Inc. appealed to me. Everybody lies. You might as well get paid for it.
author: Paula Stokes

text: It is almost impossible to carry the torch of truth through a crowd without singeing somebody's beard.
author: Georg Christoph Lichtenberg

text: ....Kebenaran tidak perlu di iklankan...
author: Fidel Castro

text: Dream Song:The heavens Go with me.
author: Frances Densmore

text: I’m glad I see with my eyes and not the pages I’ve read.
author: Alberto Caeiro

text: Life without any wonder left in it is flat and stale.
author: David Eddings

text: If we don’t honor our truth, how can we expect anyone else to do so?
author: Alexandra Katehakis

text: It takes more effort to avoid the truth than it does to face it.
author: Robert J. Braathe

text: I wished to tell the truth, for truth always conveys its own moral to those who are able to receive it.
author: Anne Bronte

text: But please remember: this is only a work of fiction. The truth, as always, will be far stranger.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: And though there’s a grain of truth in every rumor, I’ve found that the worst gossip usually starts with something harmless.
author: Kathleen O'Dell

text: Physicists have come to realize that mathematics, when used with sufficient care, is a proven pathway to truth.
author: Brian Greene

text: Despite how entertaining certain stories were, at the bottom of every item of gossip there was someone getting hurt.
author: Sherwood Smith

text: It was amazing how it worked: the tiniest bit of truth made credible the greatest lies.
author: Jeffrey Eugenides

text: A lie's true power cannot be accurately measured by the number of people who believe its deception when it is told, it must be measured by the number of people who will go out after hearing it trying to convince others of its truth.

text: Queequeg was a native of Kokovoko, an island far away to the West and South. It is not down in any map; true places never are.
author: Herman Melville

text: DNA tells you all the secrets of life,’ he used to say. Except for one—how to live it.
author: Jennifer Donnelly

text: I'm afraid to have a boyfriend. I don't know how to do that and not lose who I want to be. And I'm afraid of what it means to be close to a guy, a guy I might really like."There it was: the truth.
author: Julia Karr

text: Verily, knowledge is a lock and its key is the question.
author: Imam Ja'Far Al-Sadiq

text: The truth is often one's best shield.
author: James Rollins

text: This thought strengthened in me my belief that all men, without exception, deserve to be pitied, if only because they are alive.
author: Alberto Moravia

text: So do flux and reflux--the rhythm of change--alternate and persist in everything under the sky.
author: Thomas Hardy

text: I didn't care about truth
author: I cared about beauty. It took me many years--it took the experience of lived time--to realize that they really are the same thing.

text: Statistically, the probability of any one of us being here is so small that the mere fact of our existence should keep us all in a state of contented dazzlement.
author: Lewis Thomas

text: The beauty and gift of truth, is that it is not dependent upon your belief in order to remain true
author: A. Dragonblood

text: We the people have no excuse for starry-eyed sycophantic group-think in the Information Age. Knowledge is but a fingertip away.
author: Tiffany Madison

text: In those moments it's hard to remember that an angry voice is an invisible thing incapable of drawing blood.
author: Amanda Howells

text: I dedicate this to all those who did not live to tell it. And may they please forgive me for not having seen it all nor remembered it all, for not having divined all of it - from The Gulag Archipelago
author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

text: I offer no apologies to those whom I may have rendered uncomfortable with my open and honest assertions. The truth is often harsh and uncomfortable to embrace.
author: Casper Odinson Crowell

text: The way of truth is like a great road. It is not difficult to know it. The evil is only that men will not seek it.
author: Mencius

text: To become a lifelong reader, one has to do a lot of varied and interesting reading.
author: Kelly Gallagher

text: There are few things in life worse than a long-winded lawyer.
author: John Grisham

text: The truth, my friend, is an awesome thing, to be handled with wisdom and with courage denied to ordinary people. Most of us must make do with illusions. Or else -- or else we could not endure.
author: Ruth Tessler Goldstein

text: It is as if Little Red Riding Hood had asked the wolf: "Dear Grandmother, what is the truth for?" And the wolf had replied: "The truth helps me tell you better lies.

text: confront your greatest fearsvoice your biggest problemsacknowledge your tiniest issuesthe longer you stay silentthe louder they becomethey won't disappear if you ignore themthey will spread and affect all aspects of your life
author: Connor Franta

text: The Buddha taught complete honesty, with the extra instruction that everything a person says should be truthful and helpful.
author: Sylvia Boorstein

text: One swallows the lie that flatters, but sips the bitter truth drop by drop.
author: Denis Diderot

text: When you start putting pen to paper, you see a side of your personal truth that doesn't otherwise reveal itself in conversation or thought.
author: Anthony Kiedis

text: For every good reason there is to lie, there is a better reason to tell the truth
author: Bo Bennett

text: The truth is, it's really only okay to be yourself if that self is within an accepted range of 'normal'.
author: Stacey Kade

text: The truth is that judgment and fear will never stop, but they don't actually do anything.
author: Julien Smith

text: They're the ones inside a soap bubble. Not me.
author: Antonia Michaelis

text: Maybe I could dole out the truth in tiny pieces that, once assembled, would make a picture that resembled a reality in which I hadn’t done anything wrong.
author: Paula Stokes

text: Why should I struggle through hundreds of pages of fabrication to reach half a dozen very little truths?''For fun?''Fun!' He pounced on the word. 'Words are for truth. For facts. Not fiction.
author: John Fowles

text: Ask yourself: what is it, that if I believed it down to my core, would change everything?
author: Kamal Ravikant

text: The truth is usually left for us to hunt and gather independently, if we are so inclined.
author: Raquel Cepeda

text: My mother always says people should be able to take care of themselves, even if they're rich and important.
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett

text: People only lie because they are afraid of the truth
author: Madison Reil

text: Knowing belongs to man's intellect or reason
author: loving belongs to his will. The object of the intellect is truth

text: I almost cried. But I didn't, because if you're in seventh grade and you cry while wearing a blue floral cape and yellow tights with white feathers on the butt, you just have to curl up and die somewhere in a dark alley.
author: Gary D. Schmidt

text: My mother told me that truth is like my skin, a beautiful, protective covering, and the things that people say or do can be easily changed or discarded. She told me truth comes from the heart.
author: Stewart Lewis

text: I don't reckon misery loves any damn thing at all.
author: Bruce Machart

text: It is better to be divided by truth than united in error.
author: Bruce Bickel

text: The truth is, the Devil's job is easy.
author: Tyler Edwards

text: Comments are free but facts are sacred.
author: C. P. Scott

text: They should know that just because things get difficult, it's no reason to believe God is on vacation.
author: Harry H. Harrison Jr.

text: You have to notice the truth in order to be able to avoid it.
author: Sarah Schulman

text: A humble man will always receive the best that others have to offer
author: for he recognizes the truth

text: Thus we have on stage two men, each of whom knows nothing of what he believes the other knows, and to deceive each other reciprocally both speak in allusions, each of the two hoping (in vain) that the other holds the key to his puzzle.
author: Umberto Eco

text: Dementia was like a truth serum.
author: Amy Tan

text: We must think things not words, or at least we must constantly translate our words into the facts for which they stand, if we are to keep to the real and the true.
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes Jr.

text: Just because other people think something, that doesn't make it true.Maybe there's some truth in that, but it's unsatisfying, bitter-tasting truth.
author: Cynthia Lord

text: A man may say, "From now on I'm going to speak the truth." But the truth hears him and runs away and hides before he's even done speaking.

text: Bitter though it may be to many, Cadfael concluded, there is no substitute for truth, in this or any case.
author: Ellis Peters

text: And if we do not stand now, then when will a stand be taken against evil?
author: Bodie Thoene

text: And whatever my life had been up to that day, it was another life after that. And that is the gospel truth.
author: Sebastian Barry

text: It's amazing how easy the truth is to accept...No matter how strange.
author: K.C. Randall

text: Yawns are not the only infectious things out there besides germs.Giggles can spread from person to person.So can blushing.But maybe the most powerful infectious thing is the act of speaking the truth.

text: The life that I have lived was no more than a mask covering the real me. What has happened was not to kill me but to reveal me.
author: E'yen A. Gardner

text: Good is never accomplished except at the cost of those who do it, truth never breaks through except through the sacrifice of those who spread it.
author: John Henry Newman

text: There are a lot of people like me, people who need books the way they need air.

text: This is the moment when something once more begins visibly to happen, something truly new and unique...something truly historical, in the sense that history again demands to be heard.
author: Vaclav Havel

text: Truth has to be credible or it makes as much trouble as any lie!
author: Sidney Howard

text: There are times when I think that the ideal library is composed solely of reference books. They are like understanding friends—always ready to meet your mood, always ready to change the subject when you have had enough of this or that.

text: You can't get decent Mexican food in DC.
author: Michael Crichton

text: Truth does not surround itself with lies.
author: John Christopher

text: Although the pure truth has never been stated, nevertheless it has never been lost. Its existence does not depend upon human statement but upon human sensitivity. In this it is unlike all other knowledge.
author: Paul Brunton

text: She's my daughter. She's my sister.
author: Robert Towne

text: All art is abstract, because art is an abstraction of the truth.
author: Milford Zornes

text: Schmerz ist Wahrheit
author: alles andere wird angezweifelt.

text: I willingly accept Cassandra's fateTo speak the truth, although believed too late.
author: Anne Killigrew

text: ... the four noble truths: that there is suffering, that it has an origin, that there is a cessation of suffering, and that there is a path to that cessation.
author: Sid Brown

text: If you want the answer—ask the question.
author: Lorii Myers

text: To be haunted is to glimpse a truth that might best be hidden.
author: James Herbert

text: Spiritually is finding the truth in you.
author: Wes Adamson

text: The plain truth can be the hardest thing to see when it's about yourself. If you don't want to know the truth, you'll do anything to disguise it.
author: Sara Pennypacker

text: The truth, the all moving magic, the single handed spark of it all wasI found art. And this saved my life.
author: Mia Hollow

text: In the underworld, reality itself has elastic properties and is capable of being stretched into different definitionsof the truth.
author: Roderick Vincent

text: The way to be invisible - is to truly be imaginary. But since you cannot imagine yourself, you have to clone your imagination into being an image of yourself. Imagine that.
author: Will Advise

text: Too many kings can ruin an army
author: Homer

text: Our true nature is one of innocence and freedom to choose how we live. We need to be brave enough not to give that away. Don't give up on your right to, and sense of, TRUTH, Justice, and GRACE.
author: Jay Woodman

text: And here's to the blues, the real blues— where there's a hint of hope in every cry of desperation.

text: I tell you this true story just to prove that I can. That my frailty has not yet reached a point at which I can no longer tell a true story.
author: Joan Didion

text: Truthfully, wicked people reveal themselves in words first, to inhibit speech would inhibit us seeing the wicked before they act.
author: Leviak B. Kelly

text: The clash of ideas is not weakness.Truth reaches its place when tussling with error.

text: Like all who search for truth out of fear, I desperately wanted someone else to tell me exactly what to do.
author: Rachel Held Evans

text: See with the eyes of love and a thing becomes beautiful. See with the eyes of hate and things are ugly.
author: Joy Cowley

text: We should gather all the good and true principles in the world and treasure them up, or we shall not come out true Mormons.

text: Some people would rather be right than free.
author: Vivian Amis

text: That's the thing with keeping up appearances, Rose; people will do anything to get one and anything to keep one.

text: You taste like sugar," he pants through a smile, still out of breath."Somehow I doubt that. But I appreciate the thought.

text: Personal experience is the basis of all real literature.
author: George Henry Lewis

text: Love consists of a commitment which limits one's freedom - it is a giving of the self, and to give oneself means just that: to limit one's freedom on behalf of another.

text: For a man who walks in the light, to stay humble is not to walk in the dark; you don't need to project yourself to be thought an honest man.
