text: I was starting to see that what looks like garbage from one angle might be art from another. Maybe it did take a crisis to get to know yourself
author: maybe you needed to get whacked hard by life before you understood what you wanted out of it.

text: but even the facts do not always tell the truth
author: Paul Auster

text: A novel must show how the world truly is, how characters genuinely think, how events actually occur. A novel should somehow reveal the true source of our actions.
author: Kevin Hood

text: The truth is .. I'm okay with people not liking me. I'm not okay with them pretending that they do. Keep it real. Everybody ain't for everybody.
author: Nakia R. Laushaul

text: The answers are what they are. Just because you don't like them doesn't mean they aren't true.

text: Truth must necessarily be stranger than fiction
author: for fiction is the creation of the human mind and therefore congenial to it.

text: The truth, as much as people acted like they wanted to hear it, was sometimes too cruel and harsh.

text: There is fact in every fiction and truth in every lie.

text: The cruelest thing you can do to an artist is tell them their work is flawless when it isn't
author: Yahtzee Croshaw

text: I know you have it in you, Guy," Anne said suddenly at the end of a silence, "the capacity to be terribly happy.
author: Patricia Highsmith

text: Life is difficult.
author: M. Scott Peck

text: There is some relationship between the hunger for truth and the search for the right words. This struggle may be ultimately indefinable and even undecidable, but one damn well knows it when one sees it.
author: Christopher Hitchens

text: Just because an idea is true doesn't mean it can be proved. And just because an idea can be proved doesn't mean it's true.

text: I tried to manipulate and control people and I harbored resentment. I wanted to be forgiven but I wouldn't forgive others.

text: When all of this music sounds like you know what you want to say, then it will have been of all worth, ever. You will be something complete unto yourself, present and unique.
author: Jeff Buckley

text: The world is to be carried forward by truth, which at first offends, which wins its way by degrees, which the many hate and would rejoice to crush.

text: The truth is in the heart
author: out there is true darkness.

text: Blessed is he who believes in the illusion because thus he can find his great truth!
author: Sorin Cerin

text: What's the truth? The truth is what people WANT. Liars are basically idealists, liars are saints and prophets. Jesus was a liar.

text: I prefer the keen edge of truth to the dull comfort of lies.
author: Sondra Allan Carr

text: Cunning grows in deceit at seeing itself discovered, and tries to deceive with truth itself.

text: If there is to be reconciliation, first there must be truth.
author: Timothy B. Tyson

text: God is willing to walk the earth again incarnate in us.
author: Eugenia Price

text: When an old truth ceases to be applicable, it does not become any truer by being stood on its head.

text: Toutes les opinions ne se valent pas, et il ne faut pas confondre l'éloquence d'une parole avec la justesse d'une pensée.
author: Tzvetan Todorov

text: We stumble on, thinks Jaslyn, bring a little noise into the silence, find in others the ongoing of ourselves. It is almost enough.
author: Colum McCann

text: I don’t trust the answers or the people who give me the answers. I believe in dirt and bone and flowers and fresh pasta and salsa cruda and red wine. I don’t believe in white wine
author: I insist on color.

text: So obscure are the greatest events, as some take for granted any hearsay, whatever its source, others turn truth into falsehood, and both errors find encouragement with posterity.
author: Tacitus

text: The best fiction is true.
author: Kinky Friedman

text: Sometimes before your luck can be good,someone else's has to be bad
author: Maisie Mosco

text: I know certain truths about life.
author: Dana Reinhardt

text: ...at last I understood that writing was this: an impulse to share with other people a feeling or truth that I myself had.
author: Brenda Ueland

text: Sometimes, the things They say, the laws They make, the way the world spins doesn't make any sense at all...Which is exactly why you have to ask "Why?" and keep on asking until you get the TRUTH...
author: Joe Kelly

text: What you choose also chooses you.
author: Kamand Kojouri

text: The worst part of being okay is that okay is far from happy.
author: Anna Todd

text: Truth must be found in reality, not systems.
author: Joseph Beuys

text: The most popular labor saving device is still money.
author: Phyllis George

text: Basically all the religions,sciences and powers of the world boil down to a simple truth. The Best Story Teller will win in the end!
author: Stanley Victor Paskavich

text: A man says something. Sometimes it turns out to be the truth, but this has nothing to do with the man who says it.
author: Karen Joy Fowler

text: Just because they're a story doesn't mean they're not real.
author: Heather Bouwman

text: It did not seem odd to Max that what he had imagined about Stumps was really true, because this was exactly how games you made up worked. Of course they were true. In your mind.
author: Pauline Clarke

text: However unlikely it may seem, it is the truth and, therefore, one hundred percent likely.
author: Catherynne M. Valente

text: Think about it this way--our actions speak truth our words cannot.
author: Na

text: Don't buy into a superficial reality. It has an expiration date.
author: Truth Devour

text: Poverty is not a disgrace, but it's terribly inconvenient
author: Milton Berle

text: I don’t need words, I just need you to come back.” - Taryn
author: Suzanne Wright

text: He gave himself to them completely, with no guilt, no shame, no reservation, and in that surrender he found a quiet, shining pearl he had never known existed - himself. He was Sam. He was Sunshine. He was Peaches.
author: Heidi Cullinan

text: The truth had lacerated him to the bone, had punctured his heart, and had ripped through his soul. The truth had slain him and tended to his wounds. The truth had hated him and loved him. The truth had opened his eyes to his own faults.
author: Brenda Sutton Rose

text: Truth may be stranger than fiction, but fiction is truer.
author: Frederic Raphael

text: Justice, love, truth, peace and harmony, a serene unity with science and the laws of the universe.
author: Luther Burbank

text: Be positive. Be true. Be kind.
author: Roy  Bennett

text: I say what other people only think, and when all the rest of the world is in a conspiracy to accept the mask for the true face, mine is the rash hand that tears off the plump pasteboard, and shows the bare bones beneath.
author: Wilkie Collins

text: You must first of all think justly. Don’t sit in judgment over others when you don’t know the truth of the matter.
author: Pramoedya Ananta Toer

text: Perhaps conscience did not always produce cowards. Sometimes it made a man feel better about himself.
author: Robert Ludlum

text: Trust the story ... the storyteller may dissemble and deceive, the story can't: the story can only ever be itself.
author: James Robertson

text: Intellectuals are judged not by their morals, but by the quality of their ideas, which are rarely reducible to simple verdicts of truth or falsity, if only because banalities are by definition accurate.
author: Perry Anderson

text: You can always tell the heart of man by what he do, and by what he don't do...
author: Steven J. Carroll

text: This thing I am feeling, I’m almost certain, is the closest I’ll ever come to standing somewhere in between truth and reconciliation.
author: Raquel Cepeda

text: Rule number one: Why is it that the one time a cute guy talks to you, you have a friend who’s in crisis?
author: Candace Bushnell

text: We tend to be judged because of our actions.
author: Roxy Writer

text: One way or another, I think we are all destined to learn the same lessons in life. Universal truths are universal truths. They cannot be changed.
author: Richelle E. Goodrich

text: I never talk back. I listen and always remember your every word, so come pen or mouse, never forget that I will treasure your thoughts forever. Yours truly, Paper.
author: L.M. Fields

text: The true value of a man is not determined by his possession, supposed or real, of Truth, but rather by his sincere exertion to get to the Truth.
author: Gotthold Ephraim Lessing

text: We both know that, my friend, but a man's perception soon becomes his reality.
author: Tim LaHaye

text: Imani ni nia ya kujua kisichoweza kujulikana na hupeleka watu kwenye ukweli. Kuamini hupotosha, na ni nia ya kudharau kinachoweza kujulikana.
author: Enock Maregesi

text: Many Christians are "devoured" by worldly interests that distract them from spiritual service to God.
author: Victor H Ernest

text: Tonight started innocently enough. Most terrible things do, right?
author: Na

text: Šausmīgi daudz taisnību. Nojukt var, ja nav savējās.
author: Imants Ziedonis

text: Scarlet watched a leaf fall to the ground, lying dead amoung the other leaves on the forest floor. "A brief life seems pointless."Tristan thought for a moment. "Isn't that what life is, though? A brief opportunity to exist? A short gift?
author: Chelsea Fine

text: Men occasionally stumble over the truth, but most of them pick themselves up and hurry off as if nothing had happened.
author: Winston  Churchill

text: The worst thing about talk ... is that there's no way to lay it to rest. Every fresh breeze brings a new speculation.
author: Susan Wittig Albert

text: There are two kinds of truth
author: The truth that lights the way and the truth that warms the heart. The fist of these is science and the second is art.

text: Any actor who tells you that they have become the people they play, unless they’re clearly diagnosed as a schizophrenic, is bullshitting you.
author: Gary Oldman

text: The poor themselves can create a poverty-free world. All we have to do is to free them from the chains that we have put around them!
author: Muhammad Yunus

text: And remember that within every lie, there is a truth hidden. Know the truth to defeat the lie.
author: T.A. Miles

text: Curiosity is the most powerful thing you own. Imagination is a force that can actually manifest a reality.
author: James Cameron

text: The truth is not good or bad, it is just the truth.
author: J.B.  Thomas

text: But it is a writer's duty to write and speak and record the truth, always the truth, no matter whom may be offended.
author: Edward Abbey

text: Women were created gullible. It they weren't no babies would be born.
author: Dakota Dawn

text: When you recognize or experience the truth about yourself you automatically feel love, because love is a quality of your true consciousness and true ideas.
author: Tae Yun Kim

text: The thirst for knowledge is like a piece of ass you know you shouldn't chase; in the end, you chase it just the same.
author: George Pelecanos

text: Truths are as much a matter of questions as answers.
author: Ozzie Zehner

text: Shit, money, and the World, the three American truths, powering the American mobility, claimed the Slothrops, clasped them for good to the country's fate. But they did not prosper... about all they did was persist
author: Thomas Pynchon

text: Me truthful... you mendacious.. !!
author: Abhijeet Sawant

text: If history is written by the victorious, what if the victors lied?
author: Michael Duncan

text: Telling the truth can be a dirty job.
author: Francesca Marciano

text: Nothing can stop a lie whose fashion has come...
author: Thomas Williams

text: Confession is always weakness. The grave soul keeps its own secrets, and takes its own punishment in silence.
author: Dorothy Dix

text: Damn it, how will I ever get out of this labyrinth?
author: Simon Bolivar

text: Art-making is not about telling the truth but making the truth felt
author: Christian Boltanski

text: Perception is the lie that we convince ourselves exists
author: Melissa Bradley

text: What a myth never contains is the critical power to separate its truth from its errors.
author: Walter Lippmann

text: ..we all do it. We talk about people we don't like until they become famous. Then we love 'em.
author: Daniel Black

text: One doesn't intentionally to alter the truth, just enhance it and make it more memorable.
author: John H. Alexander

text: It's not an easy thing to tell a true story.
author: Allen Morris Jones

text: i realize you cant just throw real gems at ppl...because they think cubic zirconia is the real thing lol
author: Fee Scott

text: Humor is the whole truth.
author: Frigyes Karinthy

text: When you love someone, they deserve to know the truth
author: Lorna Seilstad

text: The number of those who have nothing to recommend them and of those in whom nothing but good can be found is probably equal
author: Murasaki Shikibu

text: no matter what you call him...we all pray to the same source
author: Fee Scott

text: It’s a truth I love you,It’s a hope you do,And may I live and die with it,Not knowing that you don’t.
author: Amit Abraham

text: He didn't want to play football. He wanted to be told the truth.
author: John Boyne

text: Truth is what your contemporaries let you get away with.
author: Richard M. Rorty

text: The teachers complain that the students today are all lazy, ignorant, and stupid. But the truth is that you're smarter than they are. You're not even old enough to drive and you already know that none of this matters.
author: Charles Benoit

text: Nothing factual that I write or say will be as truthful as my fiction.
author: Nadine Gordimer

text: With the beginning of life, comes the thirst for truth, whereas the ability to lie is gradually acquired in the process of trying to stay alive.
author: Gao Xingjian

text: The media—stenographers to power.
author: Amy Goodman

text: Neatness, madam, has nothing to do with the truth. The truth is quite messy, like a wind blown room.
author: William J. Harris

text: We are the decisive factor in the affairs of the universe.
author: Derek Prince

text: An Idea is nothing but Information, It won't do us any harm until we accept it as perception of truth in our mind, which in time will potentially evolve and construct major events in history.
author: Djayawarman Alamprabu

text: Ellie: "You could lie to me. You could tell me to be encouraged, that good will triumph over evil."Richard: "Good will triumph over evil."Ellie: "Liar.
author: Joan Bauer

text: The truth is not so important as the leaving of it behind.
author: Clare Francis

text: There was a way in which my grandmother's true self was not these guests' business
author: no one's true self was the business of more than a very small number of family members or close friends.

text: A person obsessed with ultimate truth is a person asking to be relieved of money.
author: Robert B. Laughlin

text: I never thought of clothes as having a life of their own -but they do. We all wear an outer layer to hide who we really are.
author: Dee White

text: Nothing exists without its opposite.
author: Chris Crutcher

text: Even today, I've no idea what the truth is, or what I did with it.
author: Luis Bunuel

text: One special advantage of the skeptical attitude of mind is that a man is never vexed to find that after all he has been in the wrong.
author: William Osler

text: When you perceive a truth, look for the balancing truth.
author: John Emerich Edward Dalberg-Acton

text: Pender laughed. "Verify? In this day and age? Who cares about verifying anything? It's all about the speed. Who gets there first defines the truth. You know that as well as any man living.
author: David Baldacci

text: Diets are essentially training courses in how to feel fat and feel like a failure.
author: Paul McKenna

text: We are such idiots, We think everyone else has it all figured out. But we're all stumbling around in dark rooms bumping into furniture and stifling our cries so no one will know.
author: Joan Ryan

text: They will set aside what they have believed about the world, divesting themselves of all preconceptions, all judgments.... They will do it, then they will send their minds out to seek the truth.
author: Sheri S. Tepper

text: Nature alone can lead to the understanding of art, just as art brings us back to nature with greater awarness. It is the source of all beauty, since it is the source of all life.
author: Eugene Carriere

text: The relationship between truth and reason:"Truth cannot be reached by reason alone!
author: Maurice Blondel

text: May I have a few friends and many books - both true.
author: Arthur Ernest Cowley

text: Sometimes the truth feels good.
author: Carson Kressley

text: Fury...sexual, Oedipal, political, magical, brutal....drives us to our finest heights and coarsest depths
author: Salman Rushdie

text: If you don't love yourself, you won't be happy with yourself. If you can't love yourself, you can't love anyone else. You can't give the love you do not have. You can't make anyone love you without loving yourself first.
author: Kemi Sogunle

text: Ridicule is the best test of truth.
author: Philip Dormer Stanhope

text: Truth is immortal
author: error is mortal.

text: .. the truth is what you can't see but are certain of anyway.
author: Rachel Klein

text: Beware! said the peridxis's voice in her head. Don't let It's shadowy little truth overwhelm the greater one.
author: Diane Duane

text: Seit wir den Glauben und damit die Wahrheit verloren haben, liegt zwischen Heuchelei und Ehrlichkeit der letzte Unterschied, der uns bleibt.
author: Juli Zeh

text: Letters couldn't care less whether what is written with them is true or false.
author: Augusto Roa Bastos

text: Truth, like a little dog, will always find its way out into the street.
author: Michael Mejia

text: If there's one person in the world you should be with, there must be one person in the world you shouldn't be with.
author: Amy Zhang

text: People would rather live in a community with unreasonable claims, than face loneliness with their truth
author: Bangambiki Habyarimana

text: As long as he deceived himself about the truth, he could blame fortune and have confidence in the future. Now the clouds of madness were closing round his mind.
author: Hermann Bahr

text: The things said most confidently by advanced persons to crowded audiences are generally those opposite to the fact
author: it is actually our truisms that are untrue.

text: In God's eyes, we're all the same.
author: Mark Willis

text: Die Wahrheit wird nicht im Zorn gesprochen. Die Wahrheit, wenn sie denn gesprochen wird, wird im Geist der Liebe gesprochen.
author: J.M. Coetzee

text: Stop trying to explain yourself. People only understand things from their level of perception
author: within the parameters of their agreement with reality. Save your energy.

text: If you once tell a lie, the truth is ever after your enemy.
author: Eliezer Yudkowsky

text: We all have a voice. Some a whisper, some a roar. If you can roar, roar for others. If you can only whisper, keep trying. Every roar started small.
author: M.L. Shanahan

text: How initially 'to get her in the sack' and subsequently to avoid 'her giving you the sack' are not identical dilemmas faced by the male species, but they sure have a bizarre habit of being bedfellows
author: Alex Morritt

text: Being single is definitely better than being with the wrong person
author: Hassan Choughari

text: There are no lungs like the ones that breathe poetry.
author: D. Antoinette Foy

text: We too often forget that not only is there 'a soul of goodness in things evil,' but very generally also, a soul of truth in things erroneous.
author: Herbert Spencer

text: There's nothing that keeps its youth, so far as I know, but a tree and truth.
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes Sr.

text: Chainschains that hold me to the groundchains that keep me solidly boundchains that tether my heart to youchains that only one truth...
author: Muse

text: Telling one lie almost always requires another, and before the storyteller knows it, they will be caught inside of their own web.
author: Jenna Alatari

text: Holding on to the past will hold you down in life. Learn from it but move on.
author: Jonathan Anthony Burkett

text: Worrying is stupid. It's like walking around with an umbrella waiting for it to rain.
author: Wiz Khalifa

text: Love life comes last in my life. I put real life first.
author: Cyc Jouzy

text: In search of Truth the hopeful zealot goes,But all the sadder tums, the more he knows!
author: H.P. Lovecraft

text: People need to stop accepting the evidence of reality, and start questioning it.
author: Lionel Suggs

text: God existed before every religion
author: He exists in spite of all religions.

text: That was the best of all. To speak the truth and be attended.
author: Carson McCullers

text: It is not always a problem finding the truth, it is however sometimes a problem accepting it.
author: sunny-drunk

text: Peer beyond the wall of doubt to embrace the possibilities.
author: Truth Devour

text: He recognized it and knew it. In others—clients, witnesses, or sometimes adversaries, he had seen or heard it: A gesture, a phrase, or a tone which exposed unintended truth in the beat of a second.
author: Jackson Burnett

text: Never submit we are all equals
author: Heather Nelson

text: Face the truth or the truth will face you!
author: Mehmet Murat ildan

text: The fact is not kill entire populations is able to infect entire regions of land and control the only cure.
author: Ted Dekker

text: Somehow the idyllic existence I envisioned never quite came to pass, but aside from the occasional culinary disaster, my marriage wasn't bad.
author: Alexandra Bogdanovic

text: You can't be spontaneous within reason.
author: Brittany DeLaBarrera

text: Dirty or clean, crumpled or finely creased, you are still priceless to those who Love you.
author: Norlito Baclayen

text: time does stop sometimes, you see everyone passing by, following their destiny and the destination it is leading, and you conclude its better where you are then moving again....often history repeats itself before it changes.....
author: Prashant

text: One's rebuke engraves itself upon the mind more than one's praise.
author: Seth D.

text: It won't hurt until you feel it.
author: Lovely Goyal

text: if its gets rough, you need to get tough.
author: Lovely Goyal

text: Challenging the truth is like playing tennis against the wall! The defeat is inevitable!
author: Mehmet Murat ildan

text: Our power lies in our small daily choices, one after another, to create eternal ripples of a life well lived.
author: Mollie Marti

text: It's better not to know authors personally, because the real person never corresponds to the image you form of him from reading his books.
author: Italo Calvino

text: Your value lies not in status or title, but in the roots of your character and depth of your compassion.
author: Mollie Marti

text: Truth and integrity must be so rare these days that it confuses people when they hear it.
author: Donna Lynn Hope

text: Your greatest path of influence is love.
author: Mollie Marti

text: But try to remember that a good man can never die. You will see your brother many times again-in the streets, at home, in all the places of the town. The person of a man may go, but the best part of him stays. It stays forever.
author: William Saroyan

text: Ah, it is impossible.""No, it is only very difficult - so very difficult that I shall be sure to accomplish it!
author: E.D.E.N. Southworth

text: What seems real one moment is fiction the nextand gone out of existence the moment after that.Nostalgia is the greatest enemy of truth,and change our only constancy.
author: David Budbill

text: My life is my testimony.
author: L.M. Fields

text: The cold rationalism simply covers for raw, wounded emotion. The more driven people are by the mind, the more they feel and further encode their feelings. The thickness of the tarpaulin cover is as the size of the emotion.
author: Dalit Orbach

text: Things difficult - almost to impossibility - can always be accomplished. Write that upon your tablets, for it is a valuable truth.
author: E.D.E.N. Southworth

text: Its not how strong you are, its how firm your footing is.
author: Clive Ollies

text: Whenever you don't know someone and you overhear their problems they always sound so trivial.
author: Dallas Athent

text: Heads will fly all over the place, others will explode by themselves, everyone will have their chance to betray, and those who don't betray will be betrayed.
author: Sandro Veronesi

text: It is rare for people to be asked the question which puts them squarely in front of themselves
author: Arthur Miller

text: And in that moment, everything I knew to be true about myself up until then was gone. I was acting like another woman, yet I was more myself than ever before.
author: Robert James Waller

text: The illusion which exalts us is dearer to us than ten thousand truths.
author: Anton Chekhov

text: I am in revolt against the age-old lie that the majority is always right.
author: Henrik Ibsen

text: A corner is important. It provides privacy and an anchor and lets you exist independently of the room.
author: Roger Ebert

text: Most of us have nicknames—annoying, endearing, embarrassing.But what about your true name? to respond when called.Ever wonder why?.
author: Vera Nazarian

text: Rekao je: Nisu za griješnicima plakali ni nebo ni zemlja. A ja sam mislio: teško čovjeku ako su mu mjera nebo i zemlja.
author: Mesa Selimovic

text: War kills everybody, including the ones who live.
author: Hallgrimur Helgason

text: Truth and justice are commonly found in the personality of the paranoid delusional
author: Russian

text: Look after goodness and truth, beauty will look after herself.
author: Eric Gill

text: A PRIME TRUTH - MAN IS SELFISH(Name of chapter)
author: William J. Federer

text: A lie that could help someone focus on his life was better than a hundred simple truths.
author: Faraaz Kazi

text: The whispering of the truth is stronger than the thunderstorm of the untruth!
author: Mehmet Murat ildan

text: What would I look like if everything that I am, no secrets, was put on paper? Would I be horrifying?
author: Jackie Blue

text: I tell you, you will not see the new beauty and the truth, until you make up your minds to spit.
author: Kazimir Malevich

text: With faith, you may get stuck in a very big lie and may lose the truth forever; but with doubt, you can always reach the truth!
author: Mehmet Murat ildan

text: Nothing forces us to knowWhat we do not want to knowExcept pain
author: Aeschylus

text: I suppose that a lifetime spent hiding one's erotic truth could have a cumulative renunciatory effect. Sexual shame is in itself a kind of death.
author: Alison Bechdel

text: Our love grows soft if it is not strengthened by truth, and our truth grows hard if it is not softened by love.
author: John R.W. Stott

text: That's how it is with legends. The greater they sound, the more must've got left out.
author: Tim Tharp

text: Unfortunately it's also true to say that good management is a bit like oxygen - it's invisible and you don't notice its presence until it's gone, and then you're sorry.
author: Charles Stross

text: Semiotics is in principle the discipline studying everything which can be used in order to lie. If something cannot be used to tell a lie, conversely it cannot be used to tell the truth: it cannot in fact be used "to tell" at all.

text: When enough insane people scream in harmony that they really are healthy, they can actually start to believe themselves. Or put even more simply: people with overlapping delusions get along wonderfully.

text: As in music, when we hear the crescendo building, suddenly if the music stops, we begin to hear the silence as part of the music.

text: Behind the hieroglyphic streets there would either be a transcendent meaning, or only the earth.
author: Thomas Pynchon

text: Poetry is the art of uniting pleasure with truth.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: The only truth was whatever you could make someone believe.
author: Megan Chance

text: …In the end we are all just searching for truth, that which is greater than ourselves.
author: Dan Brown

text: The nostalgia of a moment's love can be an illusionary precipice from which we fall from truth; in heartbreak, what we escape to in the past is what tortures us in the present.
author: Mike  Norton

text: How many times have I told you not to believe everything you hear? Seek truth for yourself.
author: Isabel Allende

text: I am going to die, but that is of no importance.
author: Muriel Barbery

text: Who you gonna believe, bitch? Me? or your lying eyes?
author: Richard Pryor

text: Masks camouflage the faces of both good and evil. Keeps hidden what is a truth and what is a lie.
author: Patti Roberts

text: Usually, if you think something's wrong, it probably is...
author: Jayce O'Neal

text: One thing I love about politicians
author: they won't allow the truth to be obscured by a bunch of facts.

text: True art is thoughtful, emotional examination of how human themes impact the overall experience of existing. The rest is kitsch.
author: Tiffany Madison

text: Among all the nonsense, mistakes, and bad ideas we come up with, maybe some truth will sneak in.
author: Henning Mankell

text: Come to close?No one wants to come to close.If it's done for them,they accept it,even while they condemn it.Why not?But no one wants to know what it's like.Turn a blind eye.Maybe it will go away.
author: Iris Johansen

text: I do not want to drive across a bridge designed by an engineer who believed the numbers in structural stress models are relative truths.
author: R.C. Sproul

text: From this point of view, science - the real game in town - is rhetoric, a series of efforts to persuade relevant social actors that one's manufactured knowledge is a route to a desired form of very objective power.
author: Donna J. Haraway

text: Fact and fiction are different truths.
author: Patricia MacLachlan

text: Art does not exist only to entertain -- but also to challenge one to think, to provoke, even to disturb, in a constant search for the truth.

text: Where else, but from the industrialized world, did the suicide hijackers learn that the huge explosions and death above a city skyline are a peculiar and effective form of communication? They have mastered the language.
author: Chris Hedges

text: Storytellers seldom let facts get in the way of perpetuating a legend, although a few facts add seasoning and make the legend more believable.
author: John H. Alexander

text: There must be repressed truth even in lies.
author: Stanisław Ignacy Witkiewicz

text: Time turns our lies into truths.
author: Gene Wolfe

text: Aye me, how many perils do enfoldThe righteous man, to make him daily fall?Were not, that heavenly grace doth him uphold,And steadfast truth acquite him out of all.
author: Edmund Spenser

text: This is perhaps the hardest truth of any to grasp. Do we wake up every morning amazed that we are loved by God?
author: David Ford

text: Thoreau was an idiot.
author: Bill Bryson

text: To utter a word? no that will only heavy the air you breath, for your eyes speak more then your pretty mouth
author: William Brade

text: A good speech is like a miniskirt--long enough to over all the vital parts, short enough to entice and captivate listeners.

text: Indeed a lie is often more plausible than the truth. "Almost" always. The truth, of course, is never very plausible.

text: Cruel', O'Kelly laughed, 'it's cruel to tell children the truth. If anything convinces me of God's mercy, then it's his gift of making us unable to lie.

text: As time goes on, new and remoter aspects of truth are discovered which can seldom be fitted into creeds that are changeless.

text: Memory is a sly devil that pretends to wear the cloak of truth, but deceives us both in our youth and our age.

text: The truth is not believable to someone who has not lived it in his muscles.
author: Jean Hatzfeld

text: Since history is not an objective reality, but only an imaginative reconstruction of vanished events, the pattern that appears useful and agreeable to one generation is never entirely so to the next.
author: Carl Lotus Becker

text: ...the wise words of a friend and guide rang in my head. 'How would you distinguish a true servant of God from a traitor?...You should take especial notice of how a person speaks, not of other things, but of God.
author: Harry Blamires

text: We speak the truth because we live in truth.
author: Jose Luis Ruiz

text: There is nothing to fear except the persistent refusal to find out the truth.
author: Dorothy   Thompson

text: The other side of the "sacred" is the sight of your beloved in the underworld, dripping with maggots.
author: Gary Snyder

text: We should've been different breeds. There can only be one lion in a small cage. It's not important if the other lion is sleeping or crouching in the corner. The only important thing is that...it's a lion.
author: Seyoung Kim

text: I am no faint-heart when it comes to the unpleasant truth. Indeed I have always taken a bracing sort of pleasure in facing it.

text: Truth resists being projected into the realm of knowledge.
author: Walter Benjamin

text: Luz's manner of speaking made it clear that she had no idea what she might say next. It wasn't that she made things up, strictly speaking--only that facts were merely a point of departure for her.

text: Sunlight is the best disinfectant.
author: William O. Douglas

text: Error is indeed our enemy, but it alone points to the truth and therefore deserves our respectful treatment.
author: Allan Bloom

text: Sometimes a clearly defined error is the only way to discover the truth
author: Benjamin Wiker

text: ...the best way to know the Truth or Beauty is to try to express it. And what is the purpose of existence Here or Yonder but to discover truth and beauty and express it, i.e., share it with others?
author: Brenda Ueland

text: When the dead departed, they took away any falsehoods that they might have allowed us to believe while alive; we who are left behind have to embark on a different life, since the dead are no longer here to help us deceive ourselves.
author: Yiyun Li

text: The truth of course was otherwise, but Lecha had never felt she owed anyone the truth, unless it was truth about their own lives, and then they had to pay her to tell them.
author: Leslie Marmon Silko

text: Books do pretend ...but squeezed in between is even more that is true—without what you may call the lies, the pages would be too light for the truth, you see?
author: Matthew Pearl

text: Righteousness is the truth revealed and working in you.
author: Sherry K. White

text: Deep down, everyone wants the truth.
author: Keith Ablow

text: We are often jealous of our little secrets, though to another ear they generally convey neither profit nor entertainment.
author: Eden Phillpotts

text: The wheel of life has many spokes yet so few people ever leave the hub.
author: Stanley Victor Paskavich

text: Take away health, wealth, and material success and all you have is each other. Maybe that's the place we should start.
author: Charles Herrick

text: Bigotry dwarfs the soul by shutting out the truth.
author: Edwin Hubbell Chapin

text: The thing about memories wasn't that many of them inevitably faded, but that repeated recall of the ones you remembered burnished them into shining, gorgeous lies
author: Dexter Palmer

text: Es gibt immer einen Punkt dabei, wo man nicht mehr weiß, ob man lügt oder ob das, was man erfunden hat, wahrer ist als man selber.
author: Robert Musil

text: Any clod can have the facts, but having opinions is an art.
author: Charles McCabe

text: He, who thought it necessary to maintain himself in her good graces, strove to console her under her disappointment by committing a little violence upon truth.
author: Matthew      Lewis

text: Questions of absolute good and evil are much better not opened to public debate these days, when so few people are sure of their absolutes
author: Sidney Howard

text: History is a better guide than good intentions.
author: Jeane Kirkpatrick

text: Boshafte Wahrheiten sind nur dann gut, wenn kein Fremder sie liest.
author: Mikhail Sholokhov

text: You have to be transparent so you no longer cast a shadow but instead let the light pass through you.
author: Kamand Kojouri

text: It doesn't matter what you think about consciousness, higher truths, or different dimensions - it's what you do and what you are that counts.
author: Belsebuub

text: Was man wünscht, das glaubt auch jeder.
author: Demosthenes

text: The principles you know determines what you get
author: Sunday Adelaja

text: Blessings always come in the silence of learning at the hand of God
author: John M Sheehan

text: It takes courage to become authentic. So many talk about the light but not enough speak the truth about the struggles it takes to get there and the tools to overcome it all.
author: Nikki Rowe

text: Civilization is a myth. That is the truth this world has taught us. We have not risen above our baser instincts... That is what always has and always will drive us.
author: Robert Kirkman

text: Those who scorn you taunt only themselves -- I knew this without reading one word
author: because in reading one is reminded of the truth man is given at birth -- by man I mean man and woman.

text: Making love with strangers is how you get hurt.
author: Dominic Riccitello

text: If you do not have control over your mouth, you will not have control over your future.
author: Germany Kent

text: Don't be so loyal to your mind
author: the mind is not loyal to You.

text: People often associate complexity with deeper meaning, when often after precious time has been lost, it is realized that simplicity is the key to everything.
author: Gary   Hopkins

text: You have to understand yourself before you can understand anything else.
author: Amy Zhang

text: ...how horrible and beautiful it is, that our eyes blur the truth when we can't bear to see it.
author: Juliette

text: Oh, Sweetie. No one ever gets through their TBR list. For every book you finish, you'll add five more. That's just the way it works.
author: Leisa Rayven

text: The truth which sets men free is the truth which most men fear to accept.
author: Amit Abraham

text: Truthfulness is a luxury, perhaps the greatest, and let me assure you, the most expensive luxury in our life—and happy the man who has been able to enjoy it from his very child hood.
author: Friedrich Max Muller

text: It's not getting it right the first time--it's learning to do it right and then not getting lazy.
author: Susan Mallery

text: Finally, finally, this is what bravery looks like. This is what courage looks like. It has nothing to do with dominating the day, every single day. It has to do with showing up and speaking truth. One true sentence after one true sentence.
author: Hannah Brencher

text: You must feed yourself the truth because the world is sure to serve you lies.
author: Serina Hernandez

text: Knowing the Truth is not based on knowledge, but on being "it".
author: Vivian Amis

text: Your personal truth is your gift to the world.
author: Jennifer Elisabeth

text: You never know who you're inspiring or uninspiring. People notice more than you think.
author: Amaka Imani Nkosazana

text: Never settle for half the story, and make-up and imagine the rest. Get the full Story!
author: RYCJ

text: Truth is worse than soap in the eyes.
author: Suzanne Palmieri

text: If we forget our story we were doomed to repeat it
author: Heather Nelson

text: Not all things can be expressed through words, not all the words reflect the truth.
author: Ary Hidayat

text: Intentions of a man reflects in his actions.
author: Sukant Ratnakar

text: The path for those who go with the truth is not is not that smooth. Guns, crosses and hangmen in the gallows are waiting for them!
author: Kandathil Sebastian

text: The Amorous Shepherd is a fruitless interlude, but those few poems are among the world’s greatest love poems, because they’re love poems about love, not about being poems. The poet loves because he loves, not because love exists.
author: Alvaro de Campos

text: A joke is a good camouflage. Next best comes sentiment... But the best camouflage of all - in my opinion - is the plain and simple truth. Because nobody ever believes it.
author: Max Frisch

text: But vain to popularize profundities, and all truth is profound.
author: Herman Melville

text: It was strange how she found out, One moment she didn't know; the next minute she did. One moment her mind was as blank as the desert; the next minute the snake of suspicion had slithered into her thoughts and raised its poisonous head.
author: Thrity Umrigar

text: Sometimes you have to do something ugly so that something beautiful can grow.
author: Cedric Nye

text: After years of searching, I have found my soulmate, and it is myself. The bachelor is content. Oh, he still dates women from time to time, and he listens to the wedding marches sometimes too. But only because he likes them.
author: Marci Shimoff

text: Silence is a powerful tool.
author: Na

text: Tell the truth no matter who it hurts.
author: Zarina Bibi

text: You lie to her, you lie to me, you lie to yourself. Blind girl blind you.
author: Daniel Wallace

text: We're all so multifaceted, and it's impossible to see all the sides at once.
author: Na

text: Memories that you remember are Truth of your life .....
author: Adil Adam Memon

text: Heart - FamilyBody - LazyMind - WorkSoul - Happy
author: Adil Adam Memon

text: Facts, like living things, have a value in and of themselves and demand respect. Yet some people use them as means to an end, and dismiss them as soon as they are done using them.
author: James Rozoff

text: Life would again have to make superhuman efforts, "as in a battle," to break open for himself a path through the truths created by the sciences which "dream of being but cannot see it in waking reality.
author: Lev Shestov

text: Success will not come when you read success Quotations .It won't leave once you inspired and apply it.
author: Pradeep

text: True ideas seem to suffer from repetition, while stupid ones tend to flourish.
author: James Rozoff

text: Elizabeth Bachinsky, Darren Bifford, Jason Camelot, Rachel Cyr, Tara Flanagan, Lilly Fiorentino, John Goldbach, David McGimpsey, Evan Munday, Sachiko Murakami, Ian Orti, Marisa Grizenko, Christina Palassio, Mike Spry, Darren Wershler.
author: Jon Paul Fiorentino

text: A thousand truths can be assembled in such a way as to create a colossal lie.
author: James Rozoff

text: The fishhook catches the fish
author: the truth catches the lie

text: As time and space are bent by gravity, so too is truth bent by power.
author: James Rozoff

text: Update your truth! There is no soul
author: there is only mind and body! Update your truth or the truth will update you! The truth and the science are the greatest updaters!

text: Truth is only what we like to hear....
author: Adil Adam Memon

text: Quid quo pro - you cant get something from nothing .
author: Carlous Ruiz Zafon

text: The problem with escaping is that we leave behind us, even among those we love, different versions of the truth and everything we couldn’t bring ourselves to say.
author: Frederick Weisel

text: The mistakes we regret the most are the ones we were too scared to make

text: Rather he consoled himself with the fact that, in the real world, when he looked closely into the darkness he might find the presence of a light, damaged and bruised, but a little light all the same.

text: The whole war between the atheist and the theist comes down to this: the atheist believes a 'what' created the universe
author: the theist believes a 'who' created the universe.

text: God is alive, real, and wants to heal you in every aspect of your life where you are hurting.
author: Robert A. Schuller

text: Cynicism is when a small mind and a hurt heart rejects the hope, love, and truth of a big and caring God.
author: Jayce O'Neal

text: I am unaware of his plans but I shall never stop believing in them because I cannot fathom them and I prefer to mistrust my own intellectual capacities than his justice.
author: Alexis de Tocqueville

text: God doesn't bless us just to make us happy
author: He blesses us to make us a blessing.

text: Theological formation is the gradual and often painful discovery of God's incomprehensibility. You can be competent in many things, but you cannot be competent in God.
author: Henri J.M. Nouwen

text: If you are a Christian, you are not a citizen of this world trying to get to heaven; you are a citizen of heaven making your way through this world.
author: Vance Havner

text: It may not always be easy, convenient or politically correct to stand for truth and right, but it is the right thing to do. Always.
author: M. Russell Ballard

text: The greatest thing a man can do in this world, is to make the most possible out of the stuff that has been given him. This is success, and there is no other.
author: Orison Swett Marden

text: Men always makes gods in their own image.
author: Xenophanes

text: Believe what you believe and it will be, believe what others believe and they will consume you!
author: Stanley Victor Paskavich

text: A foe to God was never true friend to man
author: Edward Young

text: I have no such powers. If I did I would be more merciful than God, believe me.
author: Mario Puzo

text: There's no safety outside of God.
author: Beth Nimmo

text: Spirituality is not about being fixed
author: it is about God's being present in the mess of our unfixedness. (Messy Spirituality)

text: We are all butterflies. Earth is our chrysalis.
author: LeeAnn Taylor

text: The sorrow of God lies in our fear of Him, our fear of life, and our fear of ourselves. He anguishes over our self-absorption and self-sufficiency... God's sorrow lies in our refusal to approach Him when we sinned and failed.
author: Brennan Manning

text: I feel the healinghands of Godtouch my heartand kiss my soul.
author: Harley King

text: A friend once said, and I found to be true,That everyday people, they lie to God too,So what makes you think, that they won't lie to you.
author: Lauryn Hill

text: The Maker of the universe with stars a hundred thousand light-years apart was interested, furious, and very personal about it if a small boy played baseball on Sunday afternoon.
author: Sinclair Lewis

text: If the Almighty directs our steps, we've no need to look back over our shoulders.
author: Liz Curtis Higgs

text: I think music is what language once aspired to be. Music allows us to face God on our own terms because it reaches beyond life.
author: Simon Van Booy

text: If you wish to befriend someone, look for a person who loves first God then themselves. If they love God, they will be able to love their neighbor, too.
author: Petar Dunov

text: Sometimes God calms the storm and sometimes He calms his child.
author: John H. Groberg

text: How do you know when you're God?" "When I pray to him I find I am talking to myself.
author: Peter Barnes

text: I know I'm not God, are you? Don't be silly.God? God? Everybody's God? Don't be silly.
author: Allen Ginsberg

text: Science Fiction: Any scientific acclaim that omits God.
author: Johnny Hart

text: The worse I am, the more I need God. I can't shut myself out from His mercy. That is what it would mean; starting a life with you, without Him.
author: Evelyn Waugh

text: Ô, Wanderess, WanderessWhen did you feel your most euphoric kiss? Was I the source of your greatest bliss?
author: Roman Payne

text: I told him I had once lost everything I had, too, and that I think that can be God’s way of building walls around us to force us to look up at Him.
author: Kimberly Novosel

text: Marriage isn't about Winning - It's about Lasting
author: Mark Gorman

text: Bare heights of loneliness...a wilderness whose burning winds sweep over glowing sands, what are they to HIM? Even there He can refresh us, even there He can renew us.
author: Amy Carmichael

text: I swear to God . . .”“God doesn’t come to this part of the house, so swear to me.” -Liam C
author: J.J. McAvoy

text: In monasteries, seminaries, retreats and synagogues, they fear hell and seek paradise. Those who know the mysteries of God never let that seed be planted in their souls.
author: Omar Khayyam

text: All God's giants have been weak men who did great things for God because they reackoned on God being with them.
author: James Hudson Taylor

text: I decided I would fill the emptiness in me with God and with paint.
author: Kimberly Novosel

text: The Bible is the greatest of all books; to study it is the noblest of all pursuits; to understand it, the highest of all goals.
author: Charles C. Ryrie

text: CONCERNED BUT NOT CONSUMED!
author: Ron Sanders

text: The world is charged with the grandeur of God.
author: Gerard Manley Hopkins

text: We are beautiful because we are sons and daughters of God, not because we look a certain way.
author: Kate Wicker

text: God made you the way He wanted you to be and He does not make mistakes. He has a plan for your life that is much bigger than you can imagine.
author: Michele Woolley

text: I don't know whether I believe in God or not. I think, really, I'm some kind of a Buddhist. But the essential thing is to put oneself in a frame of mind which is close to that of prayer.
author: Henri Matisse

text: But no matter how much evil I see, I think it’s important for everyone to understand that there is much more light than darkness.
author: Robert Uttaro

text: You and I were created by God to be so much more than normal…Following the crowd is not a winning approach to life. In the end it’s a loser’s game, because we never become who God created us to be by trying to be like everybody else.
author: Tim Tebow

text: To love someone is to desire and work toward their becoming the best version of themselves. The one person in all the universe who can do this perfectly for you is God.
author: John Ortberg Jr.

text: Charity even for one person does not make sense except in terms of an effort to love all Creation in response to the Creator's love for it.
author: Wendell Berry

text: It's time we capture our imaginations for Christ
author: Peter E. Gillquist

text: Define the word exist, and you'll know whether God exists.
author: Bill Gaede

text: Christianity is not about how much faith you have. Its about Who your faith is in.
author: Mike Donahey

text: If there's one thing I've learned, it's that God is a very creative author, and He writes a different story for every person. No two lives or stories alike.
author: Robin Jones Gunn

text: Never doubt God in the darkness what he has given us in the light.
author: Francine Rivers

text: You must know that you are worth much to me whether you accomplish anything or not. Even if you are rejected in the world's eyes, you are valuable to me.
author: Stormie Omartian

text: I think God is a callous bitch not making me a lesbian. I'm deeply disappointed by my sexual interest in men.
author: Diamanda Galas

text: God made everything out of nothing. But the nothingness shows through.
author: Paul Valery

text: I testify that He is utterly incomparable in what He is, what He knows, what He has accomplished and what He has experienced. Yet, movingly, He calls us His Friends
author: Neal A. Maxwell

text: The fear of God reigning in the heart is the beauty of the soul.
author: Matthew Henry

text: God is the reason I’m surviving, but he’s sure used you in my life wonderfully. I love you.
author: Esther Earl

text: Only the hopeless love God.
author: Jennifer Donnelly

text: Happiness is inseparably connected with decent, clean behavior.
author: Boyd K. Packer

text: Supposing that originally there was nothing but one creator, how could ordinary binary sexual relations come into being?
author: Neal Stephenson

text: Open your eyes that you might see that all things are possible through you (with God's help)
author: Robert A. Schuller

text: Could there be any doubt that the Jews would seek to harm the Son of God again, knowing that his body was now readily accessible in the form of defenseless crackers?
author: Sam Harris

text: God does not patch up the old life,or make certain repairs on the old life;He gives a new life, through the new birth.
author: Kathryn Kuhlman

text: When we lift our hands in praise and worship, we break spiritual jars of perfume over Jesus. The fragrance of our praise fills the whole earth and touches the heart of God.
author: Dennis Ignatius

text: For in Paris, whenever God puts a pretty woman there (the streets), the Devil, in reply, immediately puts a fool to keep her.
author: Jules Barbey d'Aurevilly

text: Your current circumstances are part of your redemption story He is writing.
author: Evinda Lepins

text: One must love God first, and only then can one love one's closest of kin and neighbors. We must not be idols to one another, for such is not the will of God.
author: Thaddeus of Vitovnica

text: As fallible humans, we usually slip too far over one edge or the other - all wrath and judgment or all grace and love.
author: Eric Wilson

text: We live in a world that is beyond our control, and life is in a constant flux of change. So we have a decision to make: keep trying to control a storm that is not going to go away or start learning how to live within the rain.
author: Glenn Pemberton

text: O child of God, be more careful to keep the way of the Lord, more concentrated in heart in seeking His glory, and you will see the loving-kindness and the tender mercy of the Lord in your life.
author: Charles Haddon Spurgeon

text: I see the glass half full and thank God for what I have.
author: Ana Monnar

text: A fish cannot drown in water,A bird does not fall in air.In the fire of creation,God doesn't vanish:The fire brightens.Each creature God mademust live in its own true nature;How could I resist my nature,That lives for oneness with God?
author: Mechthild of Magdeburg

text: God is so immense that if he were 'too visible,' people would give forced compliance without expressing their heart. So God made it possible, in enormous love, for us to live as if he were not there.
author: John Ortberg Jr.

text: When I see God I see Beauty, I see Love, I see Joy, I see Peace and I see You. God loves us let us love one another.
author: E'yen A. Gardner

text: On my honor, I will do my bestTo do my duty To God and my country
author: Boy Scouts of America

text: I want to help you to grow as beautiful as God meant you to be when He thought of you first.
author: George MacDonald

text: If God were not only to hear our prayers, as he does ever and always, but to answer them as we want them answered, he would not be God our Saviour but the ministering genius of our destruction.
author: George MacDonald

text: All was in God's plan, and he had to accept even as he didn't understand.
author: Barbara Cameron

text: One by one, these disciples would infect the nations with grace. It wasn't a call to take the sword or the throne and force the world to bow. Rather, they were to live the contagious love of God, to woo the nations into a new future.
author: Shane Claiborne

text: was it scripted by God or I am playing with my life.
author: Durgesh Satpathy

text: Those with less curiosity or ambition just mumble that God works in mysterious ways. I intend to catch him in the act.
author: Damien Echols

text: Let my worship be within the heart that rejoices, for behold, all acts of love and pleasure are my rituals.Therefore, let there be beauty and strength, power and compassion, honor and humility, mirth and reverence within you.
author: Doreen Valiente

text: I know there is a God because in Rwanda I shook hands with the devil. I have seen him, I have smelled him and I have touched him. I know the devil exists and therefore I know there is a God.
author: Romeo Dallaire

text: This is one reason people need to believe in God—because we want someone to know us, truly, all the way through, even the worst of us.
author: Tim Kreider

text: In seven days God had created the Earth. In a single day mankind had turned it upside down.
author: Kristina McMorris

text: One must pray first, but afterwards one must help oneself. God does not care for cowards.--"Wanda
author: Ouida

text: You are a child of mine,Born of my own design,And you bear the hard of life.No matter where you go,Oh, you will always know,That you've been made free in Christ,And you are a child of mine!
author: Mark Schultz

text: And if there is no god?You act as if there is, and it's the same thing.
author: Janet Fitch

text: In a well-functioning democracy, the state constitution is considered more important than God's holy book, whichever holy book that may be, and God matters only in your private life.
author: Ayaan Hirsi Ali

text: Even when you don't see Him working, He's busy working on your behalf. Forever grateful for God's grace!
author: Yvonne Pierre

text: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth . . . Then he said, "Let there be light." Which means he made the entire universe in the dark! How fucking good is that? He's brilliant.
author: Ricky Gervais

text: Every word, every image used for God is a distortion more than a description.
author: Anthony de Mello

text: Coincidence is God's way of being anonymous.
author: Laura Pedersen

text: It is only through us that God lives. When we abandon him, he dies.
author: David Eagleman

text: Even now, there are still days so beautiful, I almost believe in God.
author: Ann Hood

text: If in my life I fail completely to heed others, solely out of a desire to be 'devout' and to perform my 'religious duties', then my relationship with God will also grow arid. It becomes merely 'proper', but loveless.
author: Pope Benedict XVI

text: God is dead and I am his replacement.
author: J.D. Robb

text: Jeder für sich und Gott gegen alle
author: Werner Herzog

text: What you cant forget... God cant remember!
author: John F. MacArthur Jr.

text: The day came when she discovered sex, sensuality, and literature; she said, 'I submit! Let my life be henceforth ruled by poetry. Let me reign as the queen of my dreams until I become nothing less than the heroine of God.
author: Roman Payne

text: God's Will is what we would choose if we knew what God knows.
author: Nancy Leigh DeMoss

text: Break my heart for what breaks yoursEverything I am for Your Kingdom's causeAs I walk from earth into eternity
author: Hillsong United

text: Cynicism places the cynic at the center seat of judgement with the self appointed authority to criticize and condemn.
author: Jayce O'Neal

text: The mind of God is a mystery and none can understand it.
author: Rae Carson

text: God gave us the gift of life
author: it is up to us to give ourselves the gift of living well.

text: Prayers are to men as dolls are to children.
author: Samuel Butler

text: I have realized that whatever good comes to me, comes from God, whatever bad comes to me, God has allowed it, and I choose to be thankful for both.
author: Jeff Goins

text: Embedded in the larger story of redemption is a principle we must not miss: God uses ordinary people to do extraordinary things in the lives of others.
author: Paul David Tripp

text: Two or three angelsCame near to the earth.They saw a fat church.Little black streams of peopleCame and went in continually.And the angels were puzzledTo know why the people went thus,And why they stayed so long within.
author: Stephen Crane

text: You don't have to stop thinking and asking questions to believe in God, child. If He'd wanted a flock of eight billion sheep, He wouldn't have given us opposable thumbs, much less free will.
author: Hillary Jordan

text: To be a child means to owe one's existence to another, and even in our adult life we never quite reach the point where we no longer have to give thanks for being the person we are.
author: Hans Urs von Balthasar

text: Remember: He WANTS your fellowship, and He has done everything possible to make it a reality. He has forgiven your sins, at the cost of His own dear Son. He has given you His Word, and the priceless privilege of prayer and worship.
author: Billy Graham

text: When Lincoln was asked if God was on the Union’s side, Lincoln’s unvarying response was that what was really important was whether they were on God’s side.
author: Joe L. Wheeler

text: No one can sum up all God is able to accomplish through one solitary life, wholly yielded, adjusted, and obedient to Him.
author: Dwight L. Moody

text: Never pity missionaries; envy them. They are where the real action is -- where life and death, sin and grace, Heaven and Hell converge.
author: Robert C. Shannon

text: How was she created? I'm not sure if you realize this, but it was in God's image. How can anybody dare to speak ill of something which bears such a noble imprint?
author: Christine de Pizan

text: Our intellect is not intended to be an end in itself, but only a means to the very mind of God.
author: Ravi Zacharias

text: I won't put my ignorance on an altar and call it God. It feels like idolatry, like the worst kind of idolatry.
author: Robert Charles Wilson

text: Ah, how many Marahs have been sweetened by a simple, satisfying glimpse of the Tree and the Love which underwent its worst confict there. Yes, the Cross is the tree that sweetens the waters. 'Love never faileth.
author: Jim Elliot

text: Thank God I am not God! Thank God I am not God!
author: Allen Ginsberg

text: I wish we had the technology to fight God on an equal basis. To beard him in his den. To fight back for all of the injustices heaped on humanity. To allow him to alter his smug arrogance or be blown to hell.
author: Dan Simmons

text: If you search for God you will find him and He will not give up on you".
author: Michele Woolley

text: The Divine Voice is not always expressed in words. It is made known as a heart-consciousness.
author: A.J. Russell

text: There is no sin too great for God's grace. There is no habit too big for his healing. There is no label too strong for his love.
author: Craig Groeschel

text: Don’t worry when you meet opposition for obeying God. Worry when you don’t have opposition, because you’re probably not obeying God.
author: Craig Groeschel

text: Before I could turn to look up, a voice boomed from the heavens: "What the heck is going on down there?
author: Kat Falls

text: God is here. This truth should fill our lives, and every Christmas should be for us a new and special meeting with God, when we allow his light and grace to enter deep into our soul.
author: Josemaria Escriva

text: God's ways are higher than our ways not because he is less compassionate than we are but because he is more compassionate than we can ever imagine.
author: Rachel Held Evans

text: We don't know the Devil's side of the story, because God wrote all the books.
author: J.A. Konrath

text: I have always considered "Pascal's Wager" a questionable bet to place, since any God worth believing in would prefer an honest agnostic to a calculating hypocrite.
author: Alan M. Dershowitz

text: My God is love and sweetly suffers all.
author: Sri Aurobindo

text: It is straightforward—and never mind, for now, about plagues and famines: if God existed, and if he cared for humankind, he would never have given us religion.
author: Martin Amis

text: God lives in the place of praise. If we want to be where He is, we need to go to His address.
author: Nancy Leigh DeMoss

text: Worshiping the Devil is no more insane than worshiping God...It is precisely at the moment when positivism is at its high-water mark that mysticism stirs into life and the follies of occultism begin.
author: Joris-Karl Huysmans

text: The world is a goddamned evil place, the strong prey on the weak, the rich on the poor; I’ve given up hope that there is a God that will save us all. How am I supposed to believe that there’s a heaven and a hell when all I see now is hell.
author: Aaron B. Powell

text: Don't allow the opinions of other people to shape your concept of him. Get to know him yourself, and let the goodness of God change you from the inside out.
author: Judah Smith

text: To me, God is like this happy bus driver.
author: Jerry Stahl

text: We're more concerned about our own "victory" over sin than we are about the fact that our sins grieve God's heart.
author: Jerry Bridges

text: I believe that people who are devoutly religious, within any specific religion, have no true respect for the ultimate vastness that is God.
author: Clair Huffaker

text: If we had more sleepless nights in prayer, there would be fewer souls to have a sleepless eternal night in hell.
author: Leonard Ravenhill

text: The paradox in Christian truth is invariably due to the fact that it is the truth that exists for God. The standard of measure and the end is superhuman
author: and there is only one relationship possible: faith.

text: And fragile is thy tenure of this worldStill haunted by the monstrous ghost of God.("To Science")
author: George Sterling

text: Worthy character is best forged from a life of consistent, correct choices centered in the teachings of the Master.
author: Richard G. Scott

text: I don't belive in God. I believe in...Al Pacino.
author: Javier Bardem

text: We need god to prosper those without him will not.
author: Nostradamus

text: We will never be happy until we make God the source of our fulfillment and the answer to our longings. He is the only one who should have power over our souls.
author: Stormie Omartian

text: And why does this same God tell me how to raise my children when he had to drown his?
author: Robert G. Ingersoll

text: A cannonball travels only two thousand miles an hour
author: light travels two hundred thousand miles a second. Such is the superiority of Jesus Christ over Napoleon.

text: The art of life is to live in the present moment and to make that moment as perfect as we can by the realization that we are the instruments and expression of God himself.
author: Emmet Fox

text: Do you remember? Do you remember the world before the poison?
author: Amy Reed

text: Don't sit back and wait for God to do it all. Ask for His advice, but be prepared to do the hard yards yourself.
author: Morris Gleitzman

text: What a comfort to know that God is a poet.
author: Rachel Held Evans

text: Bidden or unbidden, God is present.
author: Erasmus

text: With or without God, good decisions can be made; But only with God will great decisions be made.
author: Damon Thueson

text: It takes a lot of strength to hold onto and care for the things we love, so why is it that god seems to have made humans unable to conjure up that degree of power and love?
author: Ai Yazawa

text: It did not look like the work of God, but it might have represented the handicraft of a God with a joyous sense of humor, a dancing God who loved mischief as much as prayer, and playfulness as much as mischief.
author: Pat Conroy

text: Whoever possesses God in their being has Him in a divine manner, and He shines out to them in all things; for them all things taste of God and in all things it is God's image that they see.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Many may have stopped believing in you, but God hasn't.
author: Anita R. Sneed-Carter

text: What the spiritual journey is all about is uniting our will with God's will, wanting what He wants, loving what He loves, living a life that in all its aspects honors Him and gives Him glory.
author: Ralph Martin

text: There better not be a God because I'll be in big trouble.
author: Patricia Marx

text: Yes. There is one God, and he lives in the sky, and he hears all of us. It is just that here on earth, we are confused about how to believe in him. But what does it matter, as long as we trust he is there?
author: Kristin Harmel

text: Because you’re a creation of God, you reflect the Divine qualities of creativity, wisdom, and love.
author: Doreen Virtue

text: Self-sufficiency is the enemy of salvation. If you are self-sufficient, you have no need of God. If you have no need of God, you do not seek Him. If you do not seek Him, you will not find Him.
author: William Nicholson

text: I want to encourage you by letting you know that there’s hope for you and your situation whatever you are dealing with. God is intimately involved with every detail of your future and His desire is for you to be an overcomer.
author: Sue Augustine

text: If you wait, your heavenly Father will pick you up, carry you out into the night, and make your life sparkle. He wants to dazzle you with the wonder of his love.
author: Paul E. Miller

text: Among all the creatures of creation, the gods favor us: We are the only ones who can empathize with their problems.
author: David Eagleman

text: This heart belongs to you alone.
author: Skillet

text: When you are convinced that everything that happens is the will of God, what is there to do but wait until God has mercy?
author: Maaza Mengiste

text: From all of our beginnings, we keep reliving the Garden story.
author: Ann Voskamp

text: The most important lesson that we're supposed to be learning right now is how completely lost we are without God. If we don't learn this lesson, then our lives are going to have zero meaning. (Stronger: Forty Days of Metal and Spirituality)
author: Brian Welch

text: Is it folly to believe in something that is intangible? After all, some of the greatest intangibles are Love, Hope, and Wonder.Another is Deity.The choice to be a fool is yours.
author: Vera Nazarian

text: God’s sovereign will is not at the whim and mercy of our person and individual responses to it.
author: R.C. Sproul

text: God sends meat and the devil sends cooks.
author: Thomas Deloney

text: Ultimately a hero is a man who would argue with the gods, and so awakens devils to contest his vision. The more a man can achieve, the more he may be certain that the devil will inhabit a part of his creation.
author: Norman Mailer

text: There's a major fault in Western society. It makes room for only one god, and in only one gender. There's no balance, no co-existence, no partnership.
author: Tomson Highway

text: Your Excellency, I have no need of this hypothesis.
author: Pierre-Simon Laplace

text: God can give or take away, Well who would make such an awful rule.
author: Josh Ritter

text: Perhaps the Creator of this strange place knows us better than we know ourselves. Perhaps humanity was meant to eternally ponder the purpose and importance of our own existence. If we were assured of either, we’d be intolerable creatures.
author: Tiffany Madison

text: If God didn't want you to be a dreamer, He wouldn't go around handing out dreams! That's a clue!
author: Mark Gorman

text: Others can't always define your true identity. They see what they 'want' to see but beyond your mess, God sees the beauty He created and still thinks it's good!
author: Chinonye J. Chidolue

text: Without God, life has no purpose, and without purpose, life has no meaning. Without meaning, life has no significance or hope.
author: Rick Warren

text: Only God can take our failures and turn them into victories.
author: Evinda Lepins

text: You are not on a journey to God
author: you are on a journey WITH God.

text: But in the desert, in the pure clean atmosphere, in the silence – there you can find yourself. And unless you begin to know yourself, how can you even begin to search for God?
author: Father Dioscuros

text: Statistics, likelihoods, and probabilities mean everything to men, nothing to God.
author: Richelle E. Goodrich

text: I bet you've seen the fundamentalist bumper sticker that says, "God said it! I believe it! That settles it!" It must be a typo because what the driver really means is, "I said it! God believes it! That settles it!
author: Robert M. Price

text: Oh my God ... YOU are real.Oh my God ... You ARE real.OH MY GOD ... You are REAL!Oh my God ... You're really real!
author: Pattie Mallette

text: The unconditional love of God leads to a life of freedom and transforms each day into a potentially wild adventure.
author: Randy Elrod

text: Sometimes the Lord allows us to go through tough times so we learn to rely upon Him.
author: Shirley Corder

text: Never say you are five feet nine whenyou are five feet eight and a half" was the first one I encountered.Another was, "Always say some prayers at night because it might turnout that there is a God.
author: John Knowles

text: Admire and adore the Author of the telescopic universe, love and esteem the work, do all in your power to lessen ill, and increase good, but never assume to comprehend.
author: John  Adams

text: I don't believe in angels and I have trouble with the whole God thing. I don't want to say I don't believe in God but I don't think I do. But I believe in people who do.
author: Billy Connolly

text: These black times go as they come and we do not know how they come or why they go. But we know that God controls them, as he controls the whole vast cobweb of the mystery of things.
author: Elizabeth Goudge

text: Yevgeny Yevtushenko: 'You atheist?'"Kingsley Amis: 'Well, yes, but it's more that I hate him.
author: Kingsley Amis

text: From today onwards, I am going to strive for the greatest purity of soul, that the rays of God's grace may be reflected in all their brilliance.
author: St. Faustina Kowalska

text: You can't just know about God. You have to KNOW God!
author: Carman Licciardello

text: People keep looking for the perfect one, but perfection is an attribute of God alone.
author: Farahad Zama

text: God is A.W.E.S.O.M.E! HE is Always Working Extraordinary Supernatural On-time Miracles Everyday
author: Sommer Ellison

text: It is of great significance if there is a person who truly prays in a family. Prayer attracts God's Grace and all the members of the family feel it, even those whose hearts have grown cold. Pray always.

text: There either is a god or there is not
author: there is a 'design' or not.

author: he should proselytize because the Gospel being shared is the ultimate act of love: because he thinks he can love everybody.

text: Maybe I will always have to love the idea of love or a concept of God more than I can love a person.

text: Most women had the one thing in common: they had great pain when they gave birth to their children. This should make a bond that held them all together
author: it should make them love and protect each other against the man-world. But it was not so. It seemed like their great birth pains shrank their hearts and their souls. They stuck together for only one thing: to trample on some other woman... whether it was by throwing stones or by mean gossip. It was the only kind of loyalty they seemed to have. Men were different. They might hate each other but they stuck together against the world and against any woman who would ensnare one of them.

text: How many people you bless is how you measure success
author: Rick Ross

text: Not all men (and especially the wisest) share the opinion that it is bad for women to be educated. But it is very true that many foolish men have claimed this because it displeased them that women knew more than they did.
