text: They are called, "SELF-worth" and "SELF-esteem" for a reason... we can't let others decide what we are worth, that is so dangerous! Empower yourself!
author: Jaeda DeWalt

text: Just because you're in a situation, doesn't mean you have to be that situation. You're not the situation you're in!
author: K.M. Johnson

text: Your life is always under construction. It is your job to learn howto untangle the threads and weave a tapestry that matches your desires.
author: Dannye Williamsen

text: The essence of wisdom is to know when to be doing, and when it's useless even to try
author: Mary  Stewart

text: For us lads of eighteen they ought to have been mediators and guides to the world of maturity, the world of work, of duty, of culture, of progress -- to the future.
author: Erich Maria Remarque

text: I mean, reality sucks. The world is a cancer, and shits so bad it’s scary. Everything’s filthy. But you know what? One day, it’s not going to be here. So be glad you know what life is. You’re alive. Live.
author: Mitch Lucker

text: Wisdom. . .is knowing what you have to accept.
author: Wallace Stegner

text: You see, to find the brightest wisdom one must pass through the darkest zones. And through the darkest zones there can be no guide. No guide, that is, but courage
author: Adam Gidwitz

text: The more you know, the less you need.
author: Yvon Chouinard

text: Perhaps wisdom is simply a matter of waiting, and healing a question of time. And anything good you've ever been given is yours forever.
author: Rachel Naomi Remen

text: The more you look into and understand yourself, the less judgmental you become towards others.
author: Tariq Ramadan

text: Everyone's allowed to be in love with the wrong person at some point. In fact, it's a mistake not to be.
author: Harriet Evans

text: It is born in mind that the tragedy of life does not lie in not reaching the goal. The tragedy of life lies in having no goal to reach.
author: Benjamin E. Mays

text: The fear of God is not the beginning of wisdom. The fear of God is the death of wisdom. Skepticism and doubt lead to study and investigation, and investigation is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Clarence Darrow

text: Sure, it was your idea and your fly, but he caught the big fish. Remember, fairness is a human idea largely unknown in nature.
author: John Gierach

text: The idea of authority, which they represented, was associated in our minds with a greater insight and a more humane wisdom.
author: Erich Maria Remarque

text: He who binds to himself a joyDoes the winged life destroy
author: But he who kisses the joy as it fliesLives in eternity's sun rise.

text: If we only fell in love with people who were perfect for us...then there wouldn't be so much fuss about love in the first place.
author: Amor Towles

text: There are two kinds of fools. One says, "This is old, and therefore good." And one says, " This is new, and therefore better.
author: John Brunner

text: A visitor is a friend, he brings news, good or bad, which is bread to the hungry minds in lonely places. A real friend who comes to the house is a heavenly messenger, who brings the panis angelorum.
author: Karen Blixen

text: Lady Wisdom will be your close friend
author: and Brother Knowledge will be your pleasant companion.

text: Grown people know that they do not always know the way of things, and even if they think they know, they do not know where and how they got the proof.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: what's right? If you want something, you have the right to take it. If you want to do something, you have the right to do it.
author: Bret Easton Ellis

text: In life, the hardest decisions often have to be made more than once. But each time, it gets easier.
author: Matthew J. Kirby

text: The past is our definition. We may strive with good reason to escape it or to escape what is bad in it but we will escape it only by adding something better to it.
author: Wendell Berry

text: Only a female could be as calculating and have the foresight that destiny requires,' the gypsy explained. 'Now fate is a man,' he went on, 'no planning, just go with the flow and see what tomorrow brings.
author: Traci Harding

text: learn from the past live in the present plan for the future
author: Audrey Farrell

text: They say you should know your limits and work within them. But how can you really know your limits unless you try to expand them?
author: Jonathan Cainer

text: The world’s greatest achievers have been those who have always stayed focussed on their goals and have been consistent in their efforts.
author: Roopleen

text: The weak are dominated by their ego, the wise dominate their ego, and the intelligent are in a constant struggle against their ego.
author: Hamza Yusuf

text: I observe and remain silent.
author: Elizabeth I

text: It is because of its emptiness that the cup is useful.
author: Carole Wilkinson

text: Misfortunes make us wise
author: Mary Norton

text: Ordinary people merely think how they shall 'spend' their time
author: a man of talent tries to 'use' it.

text: It is a common sentence that knowledge is power
author: but who hath duly considered or set forth the power of ignorance? Knowledge slowly builds up what ignorance in an hour pulls down.

text: While differing widely in the various little bits we know, in our infinite ignorance we are all equal.
author: Karl R. Popper

text: Don't assume the world evolved in the order in which you discovered it.
author: Raymond E. Feist

text: Are we being good ancestors?
author: Jonas Salk

text: Most people know nothing about learning
author: many despise it. Dummies reject as too hard whatever is not dumb.

text: What we have to learn, in both meditation and in life, is to be free ofattachment to the good experiences, and free of aversion to the negative ones.
author: Sogyal Rinpoche

text: Success equals goals
author: all else is commentary.

text: Tell no man anything, for no man listensYet hold thy lips ready to speak.
author: Carl Sandburg

text: Be beautiful if you can, wise if you want to, but be respected - that is essential.
author: Anna Gould

text: Wisdom tends to grow in proportion to one's awareness of one's ignorance.
author: Anthony de Mello

text: When you come to see you are not as wise today as you thought you were yesterday, you are wiser today.
author: Anthony de Mello

text: Forget the years, forget distinctions. Leap into the boundless and make it your home!
author: Zhuangzi

text: Wordsare powerfulforces of nature.they are destruction.they are nourishment. they are flesh. they are water.they are flowers and bone.they burn. they cleansethey erase. they etch. they can eitherleave youfeelinghomelessor brimmingwith home.
author: Sanober  Khan

text: Our wisdom comes from our experience,and our experience comes from our foolishness.
author: Sacha Guitry

text: Remember, young man, experience is not the best teacher. Other people's experience is the best teacher. By reading about the lives of great people, you can unlock the secrets to what made them great.
author: Andy Andrews

text: Lord, grant me the serenity to accept the things I cannot change, the courage to change the things I can, and the wisdom to hide the bodies of those people I had to kill because they pissed me off!
author: Sarah Ferguson

text: When every situation which life can offer is turned to the profit of spiritual growth, no situation can really be a bad one.
author: Paul Brunton

text: Before, you are wise; after, you are wise. In between you are otherwise
author: David Zindell

text: Perhaps the hardest lesson to learn is not to be attached to the results of your actions.
author: Joan Borysenko

text: There are questions I'm still not wise enough to answer, just wise enough to no longer ask.
author: George Jones

text: Perhaps there is supranatural: reason beyond the normal definitions of fact or data-based logic
author: something that only makes sense if you can see a bigger picture of reality. Maybe that is where faith fits in.

text: Burn worldly love, rub the ashes and make ink of it, make the heart the pen, the intellect the writer, write that which has no end or limit.
author: Guru Nanak

text: Pull the string and it will follow wherever you wish. Push it, and it will go nowhere at all.
author: Dwight D. Eisenhower

text: The notion of freedom proclaimed by the modern world is anti-discipline. But true freedom cannot be separated from discipline.
author: Matthew Kelly

text: A day unemployed is like a bagel- even when it's bad, it's still pretty good...
author: CrimethInc.

text: why can't we control our anger? because we love perfection. make a little room for imperfection in our lives.
author: Sri Sri Ravi Shankar

text: Losing an illusion makes you wiser than finding a truth.
author: Ludwig Borne

text: Whatever you want to be in life, you're training for it right now.
author: Michael Anderson

text: If you rest, you rust.
author: Helen Hayes

text: Wisdom says we are nothing. Love says we are everything. Between these two our life flows.
author: Jack Kornfield

text: Nobody is wired wrong, because there’s no wrong and right in the way we are.
author: Hannah Hart

text: Each person you meetis an aspect of yourself,clamoring for love.
author: Eric Micha'el Leventhal

text: I've been the oldest child since before you were born
author: E.L. Konigsburg

text: It is the destiny of the weak to be devoured by the strong.
author: Otto von Bismarck

text: Incredibly intelligent people always seem odd to those who are not as sharp.
author: Alexei Maxim Russell

text: People talk to old people like they're children.'Oh you're very old aren't you?' Yeah I'm old. I'm not stupid.
author: Craig Ferguson

text: When did you get so clever?""When I realized I wasn't as clever as I thought.
author: John Connolly

text: We tend to live up to our expectations.
author: Earl Nightingale

text: Never read a book through merely because you have begun it.
author: John Witherspoon

text: It's not enough to be nice in life. You've got to have nerve.
author: Georgia O'Keeffe

text: Don't believe everything you think. Thoughts are just that - thoughts.
author: Allan Lokos

text: For we have been socialized to respect fear more than our own needs for language and definition, and while we wait in silence for that final luxury of fearlessness, the weight of that silence will choke us.

text: some winterswill never meltsome summerswill never freezeand some things will only... live in poems.
author: Sanober  Khan

text: The very old and the very young have something in common that makes it right that they should be left alone together. Dawn and sunset see stars shining in a blue sky; but morning and midday and afternoon do not, poor things.

text: You think money can solve any problem, but all it s good for is buying the things it can, and leaving you free to pursue the things it can't.
author: Ann Herendeen

text: Wherever you are, however you got there, if it's good, you're meant to be there either because you earned it or life led you there and you were smart enough to hold on.

text: The closer you come to knowing that you alone create the world of your experience, the more vital it becomes for you to discover just who is doing the creating.
author: Eric Micha'el Leventhal

text: Summer grasses,All that remainsOf soldiers' dreams
author: Basho Matsuo

text: A moment of consideration often prevents a thousand apologies
author: Kevin J. Anderson

text: Forever is a really long time, you know? What do you do with forever?""The same thing you do when you don't have forever." He smiled wanly. "Live.

text: It requires wisdom to understand wisdom: the music is nothing if the audience is deaf.
author: Walter Lippmann

text: Every time we start thinking we're the center of the universe, the universe turns around and says with a slightly distracted air, 'I'm sorry. What'd you say your name was again?

text: It is not what he has, nor even what he does, which directly expresses the worth of a man, but what he is.

text: Kehidupan lebih nyata daripada pendapat siapapun tentang kenyataan.

text: Ethical veganism represents a commitment to nonviolence.
author: Gary L. Francione

text: Knowledge is like an endless resource; a well of water that satisfies the innate thirst of the growing human soul. Therefore never stop learning... because the day you do, you will also stop maturing.

text: To know how to put what knowledge in which place is wisdom (hikmah). Otherwise, knowledge without order and seeking it without discipline does lead to confusion and hence to injustice to one's self.

text: He is beginning to master wisdom when he tries to learn how not to try.
author: Paul Brunton

text: Habit rules the unreflecting herd.
author: William Wordsworth

text: To believe yourself brave is to be brave
author: it is the one only essential thing.

text: Just remember this, my girl, when you look up in the sky you can see the stars and still not see the light.
author: Jack Tempchin

text: Reading is at the threshold of the spiritual life
author: it can introduce us to it. It does not constitute it ... There are certain cases of spiritual depression in which reading can become a sort of curative discipline ... reintroducing a lazy mind into the life of the Spirit.

text: Before the fruits of prosperity can come, the storms of life need to first bring the required rains of testing, which mixes with the seeds of wisdom to produce a mature harvest.
author: Lincoln Patz

text: Boy, the solid things you can hold in your hands are never all you've got. They're the least of what belong to you. The qualities inside you, those are what you've really got to defend yourself with.

text: The failure mode of clever is “asshole.
author: John Scalzi

text: Wind is renewable. Turbines are not.

text: Mirrors that hide nothing hurt me. But this is the hurt of purging and precious renewal - and these are the mirrors of dangerous grace.

text: Believe in yourself. Under-confidence leads to a self-fulfilling prophecy that you are not good enough for your work.
author: Roopleen

text: The fox knows many things, but the hedgehog knows one big thing.

text: Para ser grande, sê inteiro: nadaTeu exagera ou exclui.Sê todo em cada coisa. Põe quanto ésNo mínimo que fazes.Assim em cada coisa a Lua todaBrilha, porque alta vive.
author: Ricardo Reis

text: All those years I thought that it was power that brought responsibility. It's not. I was wrong.It's responsibility that brings power. It's knowing what needs to be done that brings strength. And courage.
author: Peter Parker

text: it's a rare day when she speaks in anything but platitudes--all those exhausted phrases and hand-me-down ideas that cram the dump sites of contemporary wisdom
author: Paul Auster

text: Those who try to juggle wisdom, power and greed, drop one of the balls, every time.”—Zarost
author: Greg Hamerton

text: The most important thing in life is to dare. The most complicated thing in life is to be afraid. The smartest thing in the world is to try to be a moral person.
author: Shimon Peres

text: For this is Wisdom; to love, to liveTo take what fate, or the Gods may give.To ask no question, to make no prayer,To kiss the lips and caress the hair,Speed passion's ebb as you greet its flowTo have, -to hold -and -in time, -let go!
author: Laurence Hope

text: Childbirth is normal until proven otherwise.
author: Peggy Vincent

text: But neither infinite power nor infinite wisdom could bestow godhood upon men. For that there would have to be infinite love as well.

text: The Christian religion asks us to put our trust not in ideas, and certainly not in ideologies, but in a God Who was vulnerable enough to become human and die, and Who desires to be present to us in our ordinary circumstances.

text: ...ducks and geese are foolish things, and must be looked after, but girls can take care of themselves.
author: Washington Irving

text: If love goes too far, it turns into cruelty.
author: Haruo Shirane

text: Life after love was possible. I was proof. Loss taught you about yourself, burrowed in the dark parts of your soul and ate you from the inside out.
author: Hailey Edwards

text: We judge others by their actions and ourselves by our intentions. We need to offer grace to all, because we all need a little grace.

text: That is how we become wise, by living each day attending to the lessons God puts in our path.
author: Emma Campion

text: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom in learning from books as well as from nature.
author: Mortimer J. Adler

text: he who is greedy is always in want
author: Horace

text: The intelligent have plans
author: the wise have principles.

text: A great benefit of Sabbath keeping is that we learn to let God take care of us — not by becoming passive and lazy, but in the freedom of giving up our feeble attempts to be God in our own lives.
author: Marva J. Dawn

text: Color is the overpowering of black
author: white – the final victory over black.

text: Spend your leisure time in cultivating an ear attentive to discourse, for in this way you will find that you learn with ease what others have found out with difficulty.
author: Isocrates

text: Wisdom is knowing what to do next; Skill is knowing how to do it, and Virtue is doing it.

text: The least strained and most natural ways of the soul are the most beautiful
author: the best occupations are the least forced.

text: The one thing I do remember is that as I retraced my steps through all the familiar streets of my life, Inow felt completely lost.

text: Humans will never be in charge of this world, as long as dust and weeds do as they please.

text: Not what happens to youBut how you accept itIs of paramount importance.
author: Sri Chinmoy

text: Follow your heart, listen to your inner voice, stop caring what others think.
author: Roy  Bennett

text: I have learned that the point of life's walk is not where or how far I move my feet but how I am moved in my heart.

text: Nothing in this world is difficult, but thinking makes it seem so. Where there is true will, there is always a way.
author: Wu Cheng'en

text: There are genuine mysteries in the world that mark the limits of human knowing and thinking. Wisdom is fortified, not destroyed, by understanding its limitations. Ignorance does not make a fool as surely as self-deception.

text: Wisdom cannot be stolen - it can only be shared.

text: If Peter Pan had been real, he would've gone mad and killed everyone in Neverland.

text: Since living is believing, no one can be completely lacking in faith.
author: Kentetsu Takamori

text: Wisdom does not make me full. It fills me with hunger.

text: Tell the truth. Sing with passion. Work with laughter. Love with heart. 'Cause that's all that matters in the end.
author: Kris Kristofferson

text: Fear is a state of nervousness only fit for children

author: Piet Hein

text: Constitutional democracy, you see, is no romantic notion. It's our defense against ourselves, the one foe who might defeat us.
author: Bill Moyers

text: You have to be ill if you want to get better.
author: Colin Firth

text: ...some nights I'd sneak out and listen to the radio in my Dad's old Chevy - children need solitude - they don't teach that in school...
author: John Geddes

text: No one is defined by a single act," Frederic said. "Whether it was years ago or weeks ago. We're all given chances to change, to make up for things we've done wrong. It's how we handle those opportunities that really matters.
author: Christopher Healy

text: I said, but I have to go, there are so many places calling my name.
author: Nikki Rowe

text: Wisdom with an inheritance is good, but wisdom without an inheritance is better than an inheritance without wisdom.
author: Anne Bradstreet

text: Time is the wisest of all things that are
author: for it brings everything to light.

text: Some people today are wandering generalities instead of meaningful specifics because they have failed to discover and mine the wealth of potentials in them.

text: I tended to hate people that hit me in the head without warning.
author: Amanda Hocking

text: Knowledge without application is simply knowledge. Applying the knowledge to one’s life is wisdom — and that is the ultimate virtue

text: Accept yourself, love yourself, and keep moving forward.
author: Roy  Bennett

text: Fighting in the name of freedom has too high a price," Merlin sighed as he leaned into his mother's arms.
author: Dee Marie

text: For years I've been searching for a homeland, finally I found it in you..
author: Seja Majeed

text: In the hearts of men lies both good and evil. The two coexist. Some men become good, and others become evil. It is the way of this world.-Kikyo
author: Rumiko Takahashi

text: We make Idols of our concepts, but Wisdom is born of wonder
author: Pope Gregory I

text: A person of little knowledge Grows old as a plough-ox grows old.His fleshes increases
author: His wisdom does not increase.

text: The life and passion of a person leave an imprint on the ether of a place. Love does not remain within the heart, it flows out to build secret tabernacles in a landscape.
author: John O'Donohue

text: People can have the best of intentions when they tell their loved ones how they should be living their lives. But often times, when we are in struggle, we are seeking to be supported, not solved.
author: Jaeda DeWalt

text: Man masters nature not by force, but by understanding
author: Jacob Bronowski

text: The most talented people are always the nicest.
author: James Caan

text: Success means having the courage, the determination, and the will to become the person you believe you were meant to be.
author: George Sheehan

text: Don't be cool. Like everything.
author: Shaky Kane

text: What people want, mainly, is to be told by some plausible authority that what they are already doing is right. I don't know know of a quicker way to become unpopular than to disagree.
author: John Brunner

text: Be generous with your smile and try not to frown.And you will see my children
author: your smile will never let you down ☺

text: Success is getting what you want. Happiness is wanting what you get.
author: Ingrid Bergman

text: When mind exists undisturbed in the Way, nothing in the world can offend, and when a thing can no longer offend it ceases to exist in the old way. When no discriminating thoughts arise, the old mind ceases to exist.
author: Sengcan

text: We gaze up at the same stars, the sky covers us all, the same universe encompasses us. What does it matter what practical system we adopt in our search for the truth? Not by one avenue only can we arrive at so tremendous a secret.
author: Quintus Aurelius Symmachus

text: Clear nights are sometimes the coldest.
author: Luanne Rice

text: Respect yourself and others will come to respect you. That is true about all of us, but especially true about women. That is the meaning of honour.
author: Abdulrazak Gurnah

text: Read the dictionary from A to Izzard today.Get a vocabulary. Brush up on your diction.See whether wisdom is just a lot of language.
author: Carl Sandburg

text: People as a rule do mean much more than they understand.
author: Glenway Wescott

text: No mortal man has ever served at the same time his passions and his best interests.
author: Sallust

text: It's other people who make us wise, and I haven't known nearly enough.
author: Mary E. Pearson

text: You are going to find that buying the car is much less satisfying than working for it.
author: Thomas M. Sterner

text: You do not own the molecules. They are stardust. They belong to God. What you do own is your soul. Nobody can take that away from you.
author: Hugh MacLeod

text: If you dwell in the darkness, you can see in the dark and look into the light. But in choosing the darkness, you know you are destined to walk alone.
author: Colin Bateman

text: Morality is the weakness of the mind.
author: Arthur Rimbaud

text: Seek to learn constantly while you live
author: do not wait in the faith that old age by itself will bring wisdom.

text: When it comes to life and love, why do we believe our worst reviews?
author: Sarah Jessica Parker

text: The only thing more dangerous than ignorance is the pretense of intelligent ignorance. The former is teachable
author: the latter is not.

text: A moment’s beginning ends in a moment
author: Munia Khan

text: It is better to listen to a crow that lives in trees than to a learned man who lives only in ideas.
author: Kate Horsley

text: We are all small-minded people, creeping about the earth grubbing for our own advantage and making the very mistakes for which we want to humiliate our neighbors.
author: Helen Simonson

text: Books! tis a dull and endless strife:Come, hear the woodland linnet,How sweet his music! on my life,There's more of wisdom in it.
author: William Wordsworth

text: The work goes on, the cause endures, the hope still lives and the dreams shall never die.
author: Edward M. Kennedy

text: I almost think there is no wisdom comparable to that of exchanging what is called the realities of life for dreams
author: Hugh Walpole

text: No plaque reminds the passer-by of these glories, although there should be one; for those who invent biscuits bring great pleasure to many.
author: Alexander McCall Smith

text: Fools, their wisdom weak,are their own enemiesas they go through life,doing evilthat bearsbitter fruit.
author: Juan Mascaro

text: There can be as many wrong reasons to do the right thing as there are stars in the sky. There might even be more than one legitimate right reason. But there is never a right reason to do the wrong thing. Not ever.
author: Donita K. Paul

text: As long as you know who you are, and see what makes you happy, it doesn't matte how others see you
author: Wendy Mass

text: I came to set fire to the earth. And I am watchful that the fire grow.May the fire of love grow in our hearts.
author: May the fire of transformation glow in our movements.

text: Happiness is attained, not through self-interest, but through unconditional fidelity in endless love of eternal light.
author: Aaron Cohen

text: Be a craftsman in speech that thou mayest be strong, for the strength of one is the tongue, and speech is mightier than all fighting.
author: Ptah-Hotep

text: Stories last longer: but only by becoming only stories.
author: John Crowley

text: Just be honest with yourself. That opens the door.
author: Vernon Howard

text: Peace is not an easy prospect--it requires greater bravery than does conflict.
author: Ozzie Zehner

text: Progress is a natural result of staying focused on the process of doing anything.
author: Thomas M. Sterner

text: You shouldn't imagine things that you haven't a shred of proof happened...
author: Susan Meissner

text: It's only hard until it becomes easy.
author: David A. Smith

text: I am quite a wise old bird, but I am no desert hermit who can only prophesy when his guts are knotted with hunger. I am deep in the old man’s puzzle, trying to link the wisdom of the body with the wisdom of the spirit until the two are one.
author: Robertson Davies

text: Integrity is the lifeblood of democracy.Deceit is a poison in its veins.
author: Edward M. Kennedy

text: You don't trade in the devil you know for the one you don't know.
author: Ann Rinaldi

text: Compassion for others is a mark of wisdom.
author: Tadahiko Nagao

text: In the endeavor to establish a difficult habit, for example, mere wishing will never carry it to a successful conclusion; our wishing must rise to the level of WANTING if this goal is to be achieved.
author: Ralph Alfred Habas

text: Do not, under any circumstances, put this book down and turn on the teevee.The teevee debilitates our culture.
author: Inga Muscio

text: Quand on parle des vices d’un homme, si on vous dit : “Tout le monde le dit” ne le croyez pas ; si l’on parle de ses vertus en vous disant encore : “Tout le monde le dit”, croyez-le.
author: Francois-Rene de Chateaubriand

text: I am in the world to change the world
author: Kathe Kollwitz

text: A statement made from the heart has an aroma, it has lights, it has earth andsun and moon. It is colorful and it grasps the attention of those around you,like the sunset.
author: Beth Johnson

text: all appears to change when we change
author: Henri-Frederic Amiel

text: There is no greater power than the one others do not believe you possess.
author: Luis Marques

text: You don't always need to know your destination when you set out on a journey.
author: Carole Wilkinson

text: Physical strength doesn't mean a damn thing when you're all cut up and bleeding inside.
author: Cameron Dane

text: It is not really hard to do nothing. Many can. The hard part is doing nothing without feeling guilty about it.
author: Haim Shapira

text: Wisdom doesn't automatically come with old age.Nothing does --- except wrinkles.It's true,some wines improves with age.But only if the grapes were good in the first place
author: Abigail Van Buren

text: I thought about the difference between a mama's girl and a daddy's girl. I decided that a daughter who belongs to her daddy expects gifts, while a daughter who belongs to her mama expects a lot more. Not from her mama. From herself.
author: Victoria Bond

text: Definitions from Mulla Do-PiazaA fool:
author: A man trying to be honest with the dishonest.

text: Snobbery might sometimes look cool, like smoking, but the end result is usually a repelling one.

text: What whispers from the center of the soul is an innocence—so loving, so pure, so divine—that the sage bows and the wise man weeps at the sound of its soft singing.
author: Heather K. O'Hara

text: Find something about yourself to laugh about every day.
author: Mensah Oteh

text: NATURE: Where people go to recharge
author: Darlene Dawne

text: To find true fulfilment, you must live your life’s purpose.
author: Mensah Oteh

text: Don't ever let the Man get you.
author: Sophia Amoruso

text: Life is not about running away from your problems. It is about facing the problem, fixing it and then keep going until you stumble to another problem. The most important thing is never stopping.
author: Prabidh

text: A flower does not use words to announce its arrival to the world
author: it just blooms.

text: Smiling at someone can have significant health consequences.
author: Sharon Salzberg

text: Whatever begins will end, in time.
author: Muhammed Muazu Mukaddas

text: While ignorance might be bliss, apathy can be deadly.
author: Todd Stocker

text: Never feel ashamed of your longing for happiness.
author: Sharon Salzberg

text: Perfection is fragile
author: interacting with something that seems perfect puts it in peril.

text: Clinging to our ideas of perfection isolates us from life and is a barrier.
author: Sharon Salzberg

text: It’s important to take God’s love personally, though it may not be an easy thing to do. Sure, God loves the world, but he also loves each one of us individually.
author: Tim Tebow

text: life doesn't have to be perfect to be enjoyed
author: Thabiso Monkoe

text: Courage isn’t instinctive or genetic
author: it has to be learned and practiced. Why not start today?

text: Planning and perseverance will bring your mission to fruition.
author: Rob Liano

text: Madness is freedom and some may only perceive its Insanity.
author: J. Limbu

text: A cheap writer has an expensive thoughts.
author: J. Limbu

text: Let your work speak,Let your work scream,Let your work live,Forever.
author: Akash Lakhotia

text: Tu puedes, tu SIEMPRE puedes
author: Mi madre

text: It's time we allow our youth to de tangle life with ease.
author: Judith H Specht

text: All goals are not created equal
author: the size of the goal matters but it isn’t as important as the passion behind the goal.

text: Joy is a fruit of the Holy Spirit, which is most greatly experienced in the heart of those willing to surrender all self-sufficiency: self-promotion, self-reliance, self-protection, and self-absorption: it is a gift given.
author: Robin M. Bertram

text: Moving on feels heavy because you have been weighed down by fear
author: Dr. Jacinta Mpalyenkana

text: We stand on the shores of a Mystery, carried by the beauty of the horizon into fantasy that gives birth to our reality. So live like each moment is filled with gold.
author: Tessa Taylar

text: Our joy is not strapped by the confines of our present situation
author: it is deeply rooted in the confidence we have in Jesus Christ. It cannot be moved.

text: Sacrifice doesn’t mean giving up something for nothing
author: it means giving up one thing for another of a greater perceived value.

text: A star doesn’t need words to tell the world what it does
author: it just shines.

text: Shine the light of awareness on your disempowering beliefs and choose to substitute them with empowering beliefs. This way you will choose love over fear and your path to purpose becomes clear.
author: Sharon Kirstin

text: Dare to declare who you are. It is not far from the shores of silence to the boundaries of speech. The path is not long, but the way is deep. You must not only walk there, you must be prepared to leap.
author: St. Hildegard of Bingen

text: Our labor here is brief, but the reward is eternal. Do not be disturbed by the clamor of the world, which passes like a shadow. Do not let false delights of a deceptive world deceive you.
author: St. Clare of Assisi

text: An Angel can illuminate the thought and mind of man by strengthening the power of vision and by bringing within his reach some truth which the Angel himself contemplates.
author: St. Padre Pio

text: However softly we speak, God is so close to us that he can hear us; nor do we need wings to go in search of him, but merely to seek solitude and contemplate him within ourselves, without being surprised to find such a good Guest there.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: Christ has no body now but mine. He prays in me, works in me, looks through my eyes, speaks through my words, works through my hands, walks with my feet and loves with my heart.
author: St. Teresa Of Avila

text: Talent alone does nothing
author: dreams and incredible ideas alone do nothing. Action is needed to create change.

text: Time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.
author: Marthe Troly-Curtin

text: Let us be grateful to the people who make us happy
author: they are the charming gardeners who make our souls blossom.

text: Success is getting what you want, happiness is wanting what you get
author: W.P. Kinsella

text: I'm happy. Which often looks like crazy.
author: David Henry Hwang

text: Man only likes to count his troubles
author: he doesn't calculate his happiness.

text: Happiness quite unshared can scarcely be called happiness
author: it has no taste.

text: Happiness is an accident of nature, a beautiful and flawless aberration.
author: Pat Conroy

text: The secret of happiness is freedom, the secret of freedom is courage.
author: Carrie Jones

text: Very little is needed to make a happy life
author: it is all within yourself in your way of thinking.

text: There are as many styles of beauty as there are visions of happiness.
author: Stendhal

text: We all live with the objective of being happy
author: our lives are all different and yet the same.

text: The only time you fail is when you fall down and stay down.
author: Stephen Richards

text: It is sometimes easier to be happy if you don't know everything.
author: Alexander McCall Smith

text: Woman is not made to be the admiration of all, but the happiness of one.
author: Edmund Burke

text: Let us dance in the sun, wearing wild flowers in our hair...
author: Susan Polis Schutz

text: It's a helluva start, being able to recognize what makes you happy.
author: Lucille Ball

text: It is a happiness to wonder
author: -- it is a happiness to dream.

text: Happiness is not achieved by the conscious pursuit of happiness
author: it is generally the by-product of other activities.

text: Happiness depends on being free, and freedom depends on being courageous.
author: Marie Rutkoski

text: Ahhh. Bed, book, kitten, sandwich. All one needed in life, really.
author: Jacqueline Kelly

text: The reason people find it so hard to be happy is that they always see the past better than it was, the present worse than it is, and the future less resolved than it will be.
author: Marcel Pagnol

text: Having a low opinion of yourself is not 'modesty.' It's self-destruction. Holding your uniqueness in high regard is not 'egotism.' It's a necessary precondition to happiness and success.
author: Bobbe Sommer

text: Happiness only real when shared.
author: Christopher McCandless

text: One is happy as a result of one's own efforts once one knows the necessary ingredients of happiness: simple tastes, a certain degree of courage, self denial to a point, love of work, and above all, a clear conscience.
author: George Sand

text: If you've got nothing to dance about, find a reason to sing.
author: Melody Carstairs

text: I think the saddest people always try their hardest to make people happy because they know what it’s like to feel absolutely worthless and they don’t want anyone else to feel like that.
author: Robin Williams

text: Anne was always glad in the happiness of her friends
author: but it is sometimes a little lonely to be surrounded everywhere by happiness that is not your own.

text: Happiness, like unhappiness, is a proactive choice.
author: Stephen R. Covey

text: Success will be within your reach only when you start reaching out for it.
author: Stephen Richards

text: Judgment is a negative frequency.
author: Stephen Richards

text: Create all the happiness you are able to create; remove all the misery you are able to remove. Every day will allow you, --will invite you to add something to the pleasure of others, --or to diminish something of their pains.
author: Jeremy Bentham

text: Joy came always after pain.
author: Guillaume Apollinaire

text: It’s not about working for money, it’s about having money work for you.
author: Stephen Richards

text: When we are collecting books, we are collecting happiness.
author: Vincent Starrett

text: Laughter is a sunbeam of the soul.
author: Thomas Mann

text: Even in Siberia there is happiness.
author: Anton Chekhov

text: Happiness is good health and a bad memory.
author: Ingrid Bergman

text: Control of consciousness determines the quality of life.
author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi

text: I NEVER UNDERSTOOD THE POINT OF BEING SADWHEN I COULD CHOOSE TO BE HAPPY
author: Megan McCafferty

text: The true measure of a man is how he treats you when others are not looking.
author: Alessandra Torre

text: Recipe For Happiness Khaborovsk Or Anyplace'One grand boulevard with treeswith one grand cafe in sunwith strong black coffee in very small cups.One not necessarily very beautifulman or woman who loves you.One fine day.
author: Lawrence Ferlinghetti

text: Hapiness is as exclusive as a butterfly, and you must never pursue it. If you stay very still, it may come and settle on your hand. But only briefly. Savour those moments, for they will not come in your way very often.
author: Ruskin Bond

text: Human happiness and moral duty are inseparably connected.
author: George Washington

text: No one asked you to be happy. Get to work.
author: Colette

text: We've got everything we need right here, and everything we need is enough.
author: Jack Johnson

text: I think people would be happier if they admitted things more often. In a sense we are all prisoners of some memory, or fear, or disappointment - we are all defined by something we can’t change.
author: Simon Van Booy

text: There are a few moments in your life when you are truly and completely happy, and you remember to give thanks. Even as it happens you are nostalgic for the moment, you are tucking it away in your scrapbook.
author: David Benioff

text: Today only the person who no longer believes in a happy ending, only he who has consciously renounced it, is able to live. A happy century does not exist; but there are moments of happiness, and there is freedom in the moment.
author: Ernst Junger

text: I'm living a dream I never want to wake up from.
author: Cristiano Ronaldo

text: When I die, don't come, I wouldn't want a leafto turn away from the sun -- it loves it there.There's nothing so spiritual about being happybut you can't miss a day of it, because it doesn't last.
author: Frank O'Hara

text: If you write it down, you can make it happen.
author: Staness Jonekos

text: We are a noisy and blessed little family
author: Nancy E. Turner

text: Unhappy the land that is in need of heroes
author: Bertolt Brecht

text: ...The world is full of happy people but no one ever hears of them. You have to fight and make a scandal to get in the papers. No one knows about all the happy people...
author: Gene Stratton-Porter

text: When she absently worried her bottom lip with one of her adorable little fangs, he sighed..Dear gods, it’d finally happened to him.Happiness.Then his own fangs sharpened.I will kill anyone who tries to take this feeling away from me.
author: Kresley Cole

text: To be happy--one must find one's bliss
author: Gloria Vanderbilt

text: Money can't buy happiness, but it certainly is a stress reliever.
author: Besa Kosova

text: Be Sure To Notice When You're Happy
author: Brenda Farrar-ejemai

text: A woman's happiness is in throwing everything away to live for love.
author: Ai Yazawa

text: The secret of happiness is not doing what we like but in liking what we do.
author: J.M. Coetzee

text: How simple and frugal a thing is happiness: a glass of wine, a roast chestnut, a wretched little brazier, the sound of the sea. . . . All that is required to feel that here and now is happiness is a simple, frugal heart.
author: Nikos Kazantzakis

text: You don't have to be part of a couple to be happy, you know.
author: Phyllis Reynolds Naylor

text: Happiness is the most tired word in any language.
author: Erol Ozan

text: Sky is not a limit for me
author: because I have no limit for myself in life. Because life is a world full of risk taking and possibilities. No matter how hard or easy life is

text: Happiness is not a state to arrive at, but a manner of traveling.
author: Margaret Lee Runbeck

text: Success is getting what you want
author: happiness is wanting what you get.

text: In pursuit of happiness, smart people often end up dumbing down themselves.
author: Erol Ozan

text: The decision is mine, and I choose happiness
author: Malori Howell

text: Lord, make me nowAs happy as the field.With flowers enriched...
author: Eileen A. Soper

text: Action may not always bring happiness
author: but there is no happiness without action.

text: Here at last is her smile: burn it into your memory
author: you won't see it often.

text: Work harder to appreciate your ordinary day.
author: Gretchen Rubin

text: There's a difference between living and just surviving. Do something you love, and find someone to love who loves that you love what you do.It is really that simple.And that hard.
author: John Connolly

text: It's all about the smiles!
author: Emmy Swain

text: Happiness is the best revenge
author: Jacki Kane

text: A woman laughing is a woman conquered.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

text: Talking about someone who makes you happy actually makes you happy.
author: Elizabeth Scott

text: I’d rather be miserable loving you than happy with anyone else.
author: Angela Morrison

text: Monsters don’t get happy endings.
author: Paula Stokes

text: May those who follow their fate be granted happiness
author: may those who defy it be granted glory

text: Happiness is what you choose, what you follow, not what follows you. These are the things I have seen, these are the things I now know, these are the things I will carry with me as I go.
author: Allison Winn Scotch

text: You don’t have to be happy to smile.
author: Daniel Willey

text: When you win and the other fellow loses, what do you see? A losing face. There is great joy in losing and making the other person win and have a happy face. Who will be the happiest person? The one who brings happiness to others.
author: Swami Satchidananda

text: We are young, but We already know that in life's great game those who are most unhappy are those who haven't taken the risk to be happy. And I don't want to be one of those
author: Guillaume Musso

text: Sometimes it just feels really really wonderful to be alive.
author: Doug Coupland

text: Happiness must be grown in one's own garden.
author: Mary Engelbreit

text: Today is Your Day to Dance Lightly with Life. It Really Is.
author: Jonathan Lockwood Huie

text: You can be right or you can be happy.
author: Gerald G. Jampolsky

text: If we live our lives looking for the excitement and exhilaration that change can bring, we will be much happier than when we are eventually forced to accept it anyways.
author: Daniel Willey

text: Friendship is thinking of the other person first.
author: George Alexiou

text: I would cling to unhappiness because it was a known, familiar state. When I was happier, it was because I knew I was on my way back to misery. I've never been convinced that happiness is the object of the game. I'm wary of happiness.
author: Hugh Laurie

text: When a small child, I thought that success spelled happiness. I was wrong, happiness is like a butterfly which appears and delights us for one brief moment, but soon flits away.
author: Anna Pavlova

text: The happiness of love is in action
author: its test is what one is willing to do for others.

text: Helping others is the secret sauce to a happy life.
author: Todd Stocker

text: You and your purpose in life are the same thing. Your purpose is to be you.
author: George Alexiou

text: If you want good luck
author: you must go out there and search for that luck!! Because luck is waiting for you to look for it.

text: You are so intense. Like a storm. It's shocking how intense you are.
author: Francesca Lia Block

text: Today, just take time to smell the roses, enjoy those little things about your life, your family, spouse, friends, job. Forget about the thorns -the pains and problems they cause you - and enjoy life
author: Bernard Kelvin Clive

text: I learned you pay for your happiness. That's why I don't expect to be happy all the time. I'd rather be surprised by one moment every so often to remind me that joy is possible, even if I have to pay for it later.
author: Esmeralda Santiago

text: I like living, breathing better than working...my art is that of living. Each second, each breath is a work which is inscribed nowhere, which is neither visual nor cerebral, it's a sort of constant euphoria.
author: Marcel Duchamp

text: Those who wish to sing always find a song.
author: Swedish Proverb

text: if it makes you happy, then why the hell are you so sad?
author: Sheryl Crow

text: Then the world will be for the common people, and the sounds of happiness will reach the deepest springs. Ah! Come! People of every land, how can you not be roused.
author: Karl Marx

text: ... too much brooding, not enough doing.
author: Timothy Findley

text: Happy is the soul that has a friend. Happier is the soul that trusts in the truth of the heart of a friend. Happiest is the soul that knows the solemnity of friendship and honours its laws.
author: Ogwo David Emenike

text: The secret to happiness is the journey, not the destination.
author: Claire Chilton

text: I wanted to hold happiness in reserve, like a bottle of champagne. I postponed it because I was afraid, because I overvalued it, and then I didn't want to use it up, because what do you wish for then?
author: Curtis Sittenfeld

text: And so it turned out that only a life similar to the life of those around us, merging with it without a ripple, is genuine life, and that an unshared happiness is not happiness.
author: Boris Pasternak

text: Happiness had never been like this before. Now it came like sun showers, the sun and the rain together. Happiness was happier than it had been - sharp, piercing, and snatched, like a breath while swimming in surf.
author: Elizabeth Knox

text: One recipe for happiness is to have to sense of entitlement.' To this she added a star and noted at the bottom of the page: 'This is not a lesson I have ever been in a position to learn.
author: Alan Bennett

text: too much happiness always overflowed into tears of sorrow.
author: Amy Tan

text: Everyone who is not happy must be shot.
author: John le Carre

text: Honest, open communication is the only street that leads us into the real world... We then begin to grow as never before. And once we are on this road, happiness cannot be far away.
author: John Powell

text: Action and personal happiness have no truck with each other
author: they are eternally at war.

text: And all the best words together couldn't hold the happiness.
author: Katherine Hannigan

text: And hard times are good in their own way, too. Because the only way you can achieve true happiness is if you experience true sadness as well. It's all about light and shade. Balance.
author: Gabrielle Williams

text: Happiness is a big joke
author: let us laugh at it loud.

text: The key to ultimate happiness and fulfillment lies within our own transformation. The more we learn and grow and evolve as individuals, the more we will find happiness and satisfaction in relationships, work and life.
author: Kristi Bowman

text: The secret to happiness is low expectations.
author: Barry Schwartz

text: There is no happily-ever-after to run to. We have to work for happiness.
author: Mary Balogh

text: Whoever said happiness needs a plan?
author: Robert Holden

text: Happy are those who evolve from within.
author: Amit Abraham

text: These fucking women really piss me off,' April said. 'Because instead of being elated by the thought of making their own happiness and chasing some crazy dream, all they want to do is narrow their options and do something safe.
author: J. Courtney Sullivan

text: Happiness isn't always easy," he'd said. "But it's a priority.
author: Goldy Moldavsky

text: Stop trying to 'fix' yourself
author: you're NOT broken! You are perfectly imperfect and powerful beyond measure.

text: A truly radical resolution would be to embrace existence just as it is, as the only thing that matters, to proclaim that the world itself is heaven, made for our total enjoyment and fulfillment...
author: CrimethInc.

text: Man does not strive for happiness
author: only the Englishman does that.

text: Le bonheur est cette danse où l'on s'approche et l'on s'écarte sans se perdre. Il est même fait des larmes des longues séparations à condition que viennent les retrouvailles.
author: Timothee de Fombelle

text: If you're not getting happier as you get older, then you're fuckin' up
author: Ani DiFranco

text: The pursuit of happiness is a most ridiculous phrase
author: if you pursue happiness you'll never find it.

text: Happiness is the pleasantest of emotions; because of this, it is the most dangerous. Having once felt happiness, one will do anything to maintain it, and losing it, one will grieve.
author: Kij Johnson

text: Sadness is never bad," said Amparo. "Sadness is the mirror of being happy
author: Tim Willocks

text: His hand lay across my stomach as he slept soundly. I entwined my fingers with his and breathed through the warmth that seeped through my chest. Such a simple, sweet thing to do, yet holding hands in bed was incredibly intimate.
author: N.R. Walker

text: The brave who focus on all things good and all things beautiful and all things true, even in the small, who give thanks for it and discover joy even in the here and now, they are the change agents who bring fullest Light to all the world.
author: Ann Voskamp

text: But I am king. And the well-being of my kingdom depends on my sound judgment and clear head. And those things depend on my state of happiness. And I have known for a long time that my state of happiness depends on you.
author: Sharon Shinn

text: Happiness is not in things
author: happiness is in you.

text: I think now that maybe true sweetness can only happen in limbo.
author: Peter Heller

text: To be happy, you have to risk being unhappy.
author: Ogwo David Emenike

text: It's impossible to be unhappy while wearing a poncho!
author: Noel Fielding

text: Sooner or later in life everyone discovers that perfect happiness is unrealizable, but there are few who stop to consider the antithesis; that perfect unhappiness is equally unattainable.
author: Primo Levi

text: The bitterness of joy lies in the knowledge that it cannot last. Nor should joy last beyond a certain season, for, after that season, even joy would become merely habit.
author: Tanith Lee

text: Happiness is like a cat. If you try to coax it or call it, it will avoid you. It will never come. But if you pay no attention to it and go about your business, you'll find it rubbing up against your legs and jumping into your lap.
author: William J. Bennett

text: Try to be happy within the context of the life we are actually living. Happiness is not a situation to be longed for or a convergence of lucky happenstance. Through the power of our own minds, we can help ourselves.
author: Wayne Coyne

text: The only means by which one could attain complete happiness is to avoid living in constant expectation of it. It's the expectation that causes our unhappiness and consequent bitterness about life
author: Mohammed Naseehu Ali

text: Those who are happiest are those who do the most for others.
author: Booker T. Washington

text: The universe doesn't give you what you want in your mind
author: it gives you what you demand with your actions.

text: There is no happiness in love, except at the end of an English novel.
author: Anthony Trollope

text: Unhappiness is caused when we cannot let go of our memories.
author: Jed Rubenfeld

text: These men are worth your tears. You are not worth their merriment.
author: Wilfred Owen

text: Happiness is simple. Everything we do to find it is complicated.
author: Karen Maezen Miller

text: If you see the world and yourself through a lens smudged by negativity then you’ll find much misery. If you look outwards and inwards through lens brightened by positivity you’ll find much to be happy and appreciative about.
author: Henrik Edberg

text: Remember the botched brothel-visit in L’Education sentimentale and remember its lesson. Do not participate: happiness lies in the imagination, not the act. Pleasure is found first in anticipation, later in memory.
author: Julian Barnes

text: He tapped my chest. 'Happy is here.' He tapped his own chest. 'Here.'I looked down past my chin. 'Inside?''Inside.'It was getting crowded in there. First angel. Now happy. It seemed there was more to me than cabbage and turnips.
author: Jerry Spinelli

text: Not that happiness is dull. Only that it doesn't tell well. And of our consuming diversions as we age is to recite, not only to others but to ourselves, our own story.
author: Lionel Shriver

text: A lifetime of lonely regret or happiness with some pain along the way.
author: Melissa Nathan

text: Nothing in the world can make you happy
author: everything in the world can encourage you to be happy

text: The key to knowing joy is being easily pleased.
author: Mark Nepo

text: This idea of perpetual happiness is crazy and overrated, because those dark moments fuel you for the next bright moments; each one helps you appreciate the other.
author: Brad Pitt

text: I wonder if people always choose what will make them unhappy.
author: Megan Whalen Turner

text: I know money isn't everything. but it certainly is something.
author: Thisuri Wanniarachchi

text: I wished upon a star and the heavens sent me you
author: Sonya Watson

text: I don't know whether it is beautiful or sad, that I find such blissful happiness within the confines of my own mind, more so than I do in the reality that surrounds me.
author: Kendal Rob

text: Gratitude always comes into play; research shows that people are happier if they are grateful for the positive things in their lives, rather than worrying about what might be missing.
author: Dan Buettner

text: Be like a branch of a tree
author: flex your body to face 'wind of sorrow'

text: that's exactly the good thing about the Injun life--you don't have to stop and think about whether or not you're 'happy'--which in my opinionis a highly overrated human condition invented by white folks
author: Jim Fergus

text: Why do I want to run from happiness?
author: Mary Balogh

text: The happiness of a family is such a complex matter. Like a table laid out with a tea service, it looks so ordinary until it's threatened. Then it becomes infinitely precious.
author: Lynda Williams

text: A withered maple leaf has left its branch and is falling to the ground
author: its movements resemble those of a butterfly in flight. Isn't it strange? The saddest and deadest of things is yet so like the gayest and most vital of creatures?

text: Why sadness was created?So we could rest from laughing.
author: Nelson M. Lubao

text: The secret of happiness is something to do
author: John Burroughs

text: I want to be careful not to throw all this away. This is happiness. I think this is what happiness is. I haven't got it yet, but I can sense it out there. I feel I'm close to it. Some days, I'm so close I can almost smell it.
author: Sebastian Faulks

text: It's hard to say. Sometimes people have had terrible childhoods. And sometimes they just haven't found their special place in life. And sometimes they're dogs from hell and must be destroyed.
author: Charles Addams

text: Happiness is not a state of being. Happiness is a vector, it is movement.
author: Neal Shusterman

text: We live in a community of people not so that we can suppress and dominate eachother or make each other miserable but so that we can better and more reliably satisfy all life's healthy needs.
author: Wilhelm Reich

text: Happiness is the lucky pane of glass you carry in your head. It takes all your cunning just to hang on to it, and once it's smashed you have to move into a different sort of life.
author: Carol Shields

text: Reading messed with my brain in an unaccountable way. It made me happy
author: or something.

text: Through strength I found loveIn time I found myself in happiness with you
author: Alexis Jordan

text: As I watch him, a sudden recognition comes over me: Tonight, I can be anyone I want… maybe being happy only means living in the moment, appreciating the exact moment you're in and not thinking about the worries of the future.
author: Laura Fitzgerald

text: Many of us seem to live our lives looking for happiness. That is not always a bad thing, but it can be if we continually believe that happiness is someplace else and not in the present.
author: Barbara Barrington Jones

text: It is sad that people need alcohol to make them happy.
author: Habeeb Akande

text: Try to be happy in this present moment, and put not off being so to a time to come--as though that time should be of another make from this which has already come and is ours.
author: Thomas Fuller

text: I willingly allow that money does not guarantee happiness
author: but it must also be allowed that it makes happiness a great deal easier to achieve.

text: The good life doesn't knock on the door. Joy is a job.
author: Lionel Shriver

text: I had decided to stop chasing the money, and start chasing the passion.
author: Tony Hsieh

text: Wanna be always Happy? Always carry some chocolate with you
People with the most interests live, not only longest, but happiest.
author: George Matthew Allen

text: We were so happy.
author: Liane Moriarty

text: Those who decide to use leisure as a means of mental development, who love good music, good books, good pictures, good company, good conversation, are the happiest people in the world.
author: William Lyon Phelps

text: The disempowered mind believes dreams come true
author: the empowered mind knows you bring your dreams to life.

text: Cynicism is the calling card of unhappy people.
author: Iimani David

text: Es dauerte einen Moment, bis er ihr antwortete:Nein, ich spreche von... von eurer Freiheit, glaube ich. Von dem Glück, das ihr habt, für euch zu leben und auf alles andere zu pfeifen.
author: Anna Gavalda

text: I feel as though the world is a friendly boy walking along in the sun.
author: Robert Rauschenberg

text: But there was, she knew, something else. Happiness, she supposed. Whatever that might be. What, exactly, she wondered, was happiness. Very positively she wanted it.
author: Nella Larsen

author: Maquita Donyel Irvin

text: The point is, you have family and friends who love you. You have a world out there just waiting for you to conquer it. You have a life that will be anything you make it. That's the point.
author: Malorie Blackman

text: But everything I have to give, I'll give to you
author: Ray Lamontagne

text: He who plants a tree, plants a hope.
author: Lucy Larcom

text: You were standing in the wake of devastation And you were waiting on the edge of the unknown And with the cataclysm raining down Insides crying "Save me now" You were there, impossibly alone
author: Linkin Park

text: But human beings are not machines, and however powerful the pressure to conform, they sometimes are so moved by what they see as injustice that they dare to declare their independence. In that historical possibility lies hope.
author: Howard Zinn

text: Mind led bodyto the edge of the precipice.They stared in desireat the naked abyss.If you love me, said mind,take that step into silence.If you love me, said body,turn and exist.
author: Anne Stevenson

text: Things go away to return, brightened for the passage
author: A.R. Ammons

text: Below me Rontu was running along the cliffs barking at the screaming gulls. Pelicans were chattering as they finished the blue water. But suddenly I thought of Tutok, and the island seemed very quiet.
author: Scott O'Dell

text: Destiny isn't something you make as you're told. By overcoming difficulties, life and this whole world can advance forward. That's what destiny is for. A world that has lost the power to advance forward, will be destroyed - Aladdin
author: Shinobu Ohtaka

text: Deep down under where his heart resided, strangled up in thorny vines of guilt, anger, fear and longing, there lay something deeper in him, something that he couldn’t see but she could.
author: Carol Oates

text: Hope is a psychological mechanism unaffected by external realities.
author: Gene Wolfe

text: That was how it always was with Colleen: No matter how sad she felt, there was always this little bit of hope - like a speck of glitter caught in your eyelash - that never went away, no matter what.
author: Lauren Tarshis

text: A dried plant is nothing but a sign to plant a new one
author: Priyansh Shah

text: The path i walk lights up in flames
author: Roberta Karim

text: Nor dread nor hope attendA dying animal
author: A man awaits his endDreading and hoping all.

text: The sun is still there... even if clouds drift over it. Once you have experienced the reality of sunshine you may weep, but you will never feel ice about your heart again.
author: Elizabeth Goudge

text: Everything happens for a reason. Just be patient.
author: Michael Christos Zeis

text: Denial. It’s the only thing that keeps most of us from losing our sanity.
author: Heena Rathore P.

text: We must be content to grow slowly. Most of us will still barely be at the beginning of our recovery by the time we die. But that is better than killing ourselves pretending to be healthy.
author: Simon Tugwell

text: God can inject hope into a absolutely hopeless situation.
author: Mark  Evans

text: The least I can do is speak out for the hundreds of chimpanzees who, right now, sit hunched, miserable and without hope, staring out with dead eyes from their metal prisons. They cannot speak for themselves.
author: Jane Goodall

text: You put a new heart in Emma a long time ago, it just wasn’t the kind you were thinking of.” He laughed to himself. “Hope is an amazing thing. I saw it in Emma, saw it with my own eyes.
author: Charles Martin

text: In honor of Oprah Winfrey: Even greater than the ability to inspire others with hope is the power to motivate them to give as much to the lives of others as they would give to their own
author: and to empower them to confront the worst in themselves in order to discover and claim the best in themselves.

text: He cursed himself for having assumed the weather would be sunny. Perhaps it was the result of evolution, he thought--some adaptive gene that allowed the English to go on making blithe outdoor plans in the face of almost certain rain.
author: Helen Simonson

text: All this hoping for nothing-or someone-that's maybe hopeless
author: Rachel Cohn

text: You are one woman in an endless line of women who were chosen to love more passionately than others, be committed to justice more fervently than others, and seek our Maker’s higher expressions more reflectively than others.
author: Mandy Nachampassack-Maloney

text: Hope is faith holding out its hand in the dark.
author: George Iles

text: A man is a fool to live in hopes of a better tomorrow. I have a thousand, better ways today to spend what time remains ahead of me, and I have brighter, lighter and more pleasant places in which to spend it.
author: Jack Whyte

text: Human life is under the absolute dominion of two mighty principles, fear and hope, and that any one who can make these serve his ends may be sure of rapid fortune.
author: Lucian of Samosata

text: Unforgiveness denies the victim the possibility of parole and leaves them stuck in the prison of what was, incarcerating them in their trauma and relinquishing the chance to escape beyond the pain.
author: T.D. Jakes

text: The Rest of Our Life Is Going To Be The Best of Our Life!
author: Darrell Yarbrough

text: Hope and desire,All unfulfilled,Have more than ropeAnd hangman killed.
author: Stevie Smith

text: Live for the lovers, not the haters!
author: Scylar Tyberius

text: Have faith in your beliefs
author: Michael Christos Zeis

text: Hope is a seed that grows inside us from the moment we are born
author: J.D. Willd

text: Do we ever have to abandon all hope? Is it not perhaps a good thing that by refusing to give in to the evidence, the dreams that lie half awake in us all may persist?
author: Theodore Monod

text: Forget yesterday ... Tomorrow still holds happy surprises for us.
author: David S. Mark

text: NOT, I’ll not, carrion comfort, Despair, not feast on thee;Not untwist—slack they may be—these last strands of manIn me ór, most weary, cry I can no more. I can;Can something, hope, wish day come, not choose not to be.
author: Gerard Manley Hopkins

text: Oh no," I said, because if our life is just one endless song about hope and regret, then "oh no" is apparently that song's chorus, the words we always return to.
author: Brock Clarke

text: The morning was full of sunlight and hope.
author: Kate Chopin

text: Hope can be imagined as a domino effect, a chain reaction, each increment making the next increase more feasible... There are moments of fear and doubt that can deflate it.
author: Jerome Groopman

text: Wherever the hope of glory lives, hopelessness passes away.
author: Nadine C. Keels

text: Faith doesn't always come naturally but as we take those steps of faith, we notice little by little that our faith has grown and it isn't as hard as we first thought.
author: Jacqueline Winslow

text: Never loose hope. Stay strong, you never know what tomorrow brings.
author: Magith Noohukhan

text: I cared about them. I wanted them to feel better, to live better lives. And then it occurred to me - I cared about myself. I wanted me to live a better life, too Caring about myself was allowing me to care about others.
author: Cate Tiernan

text: We’re a couple of shooting stars, Biggs, and we’ll never be stopped.
author: George Lucas

text: When you Love.. you Expect.. Once u start Expecting.. u have Hope.. Hopes in return makes you to put Trust.. and Trust makes u prone to Betrayal.. and whn u r Betrayed.. Hell falls over U...!!!
author: Abhijeet Sawant

text: Uncertainty is the refuge of hope.
author: Henri-Frederic Amiel

text: When our dreams don’t come true, sometimes we can hand them down to our children and hope that they will come true for them. The joy can be immeasurable just the same.
author: Isabel  Lopez

text: Worry looks back, Hope looks forward, but Faith always looks up.
author: Umair Naeem

text: He wore his hope like a winter coat, layered over the top of all of him.
author: Cora Carmack

text: You can never run from your past; it defines who you are today. But, use your Awo given intelligence and enjoy the future…because THAT only comes once.’ VAALBARA The Land of Shadows
author: Michelle Horst

text: We are preaching hope, standing on the bones of the past.
author: John Rucyahana

text: Old folks live on memory, young folk live on hope.
author: Gayla Reid

text: Action is what makes hope a reality.
author: T.M. Brenner

text: Your emotional state has a tremendous amount to do with sickness, health and well-being. For years, my husband and I lived on -- and because of -- hope. Hope continues to give me the mental strength to carry on.
author: Dana Reeve

text: Hope, how she had grown to hate the word. It was an insideious seed planted inside a person's soul, surviving covertly on little tending, then flowering so spectacularly that none could help but cherish it.
author: Kate Morton

text: God is writing His story on the pages of our lives.
author: Rick  Anderson

text: It’s like we have the bones of animals and the hearts of angels.
author: Baxter Clare Trautman

text: When in darkness....strike a match
author: John Paul Warren

text: If we want a better world, we must never cease to think about what kind of world we want... and we must not be afraid to do whatever it takes to make the world the way it should be. It is not enough just to talk. We must act!
author: Kate Forsyth

text: Yet some men say in many parts of England that King Arthur is not dead, but had by the will of our Lord Jesu into another place; and men say that he shall come again, and he shall win the holy cross.
author: Thomas Malory

text: It doesn't matter where you've been, only where you are going.
author: L.M. Fields

text: Her shoes were comfortable. They reflected her hope for the evening.
author: William Peter Blatty

text: The child was slender as fleeting hope.
author: William Peter Blatty

text: There have been bleak nights along my way, many of my own making, but life is all the brighter for them now. To the human eye, without the darkness there are no stars.
author: Sumangali Morhall

text: Give up isn't going to helpKeep going on will help
author: Kristopher Raines

text: You may find that knowledge is not so great a thing as belief or hope.
author: Mark Rude

text: The need in me beautiful is to look back on you forever!!
author: Abhijeet Sawant

text: Rafe made people find something in themselves...(he) made me dream, he saw what I could hope to be, and helped me hope it. He did that to everyone he knew - especially the ones he knew the best." - Danny
author: Randall Wallace

text: Try to see the good in all things...
author: M. Howson

text: While there is tea, there is hope.")
author: Arthur Wing Pinero

text: We can be redeemed only to the extent to which we see ourselves.
author: Martin Buber

text: The idea of the future, pregnant with an infinity of possibilities, is thus more fruitful than the future itself, and this is why we find more charm in hope than in possession, in dreams than in reality.
author: Henri Bergson

text: Farming is a profession of hope
author: Brian Brett

text: Healing Follows Forgiveness
author: Pat Bluth

text: There is a great deal of ruin in a nation.
author: Adam Smith

text: You can curse the moonCurse the day your’re bornBut the pilot of your plane is youWe’re all in this world, by a greater plan Look up, lift your wings, Because you canThe Light Said (The First Song album)
author: Phyllis Wheaton

text: Man must use what he has, not hope for what is not.
author: G.I. Gurdjieff

text: Please don't take my wings...
author: Randall Wallace

text: Faith without a measure of doubt ain't worth a brass farthin'.
author: Howard Frank Mosher

text: People die from lack of shared empathy and affinity. By establishing social connectedness, we give hope a chance and the other can become heaven. ( "Le ciel c'est l'autre" )
author: Erik Pevernagie

text: Hope is a straw hat hanging beside a window covered with frost.
author: Margaret George

text: Do you have any idea how frightening it is to care for someone who dreams? They infect you with their optimism. And soon you start to dream together. You begin to dare to hope.
author: Lee  Winter

text: I hope your heart feels better. I hope your mind is set right.I hope your love is connected to others, and you don't feel so lost and locked up.I hope everything is okay for you. And by okay, I mean greater than ever.
author: Shailee J-N

text: And that's what love is, I suppose. The one thing that is most worth hoping for, and the one thing that's most surprising when it lands. Because it's better. It exceeds hope, makes hope nearsighted.
author: Jessica Soffer

text: With every choice you create the life you’ll live
author: with every decision you design it.

text: A very small degree of hope is sufficient to cause the birth of love
author: Stendhal

text: Take a chance..., change a 'NO' to a 'YES'today.
author: M. Howson

text: No rain can't get the rainbow.
author: ayumi hamasaki rainbow

text: Your dreamers. You ridiculous children. You dancing grinning fuckups. Here is your bright future. Your earnest, saccharine hope. How does it taste dripping from the neck of everyone you love?
author: Isaac Marion

text: I can always look up at the cosmos and marvel, no matter what happens. And when I look up at it, I feel as though my problems are small. I don't know why, but it always makes me feel better.
author: Matthew Quick

text: We all flee in hope of finding some ground of security
author: M.T. Anderson

text: I could smack her, punch her in the face, but then I see what she can't hide from me. I've seen it before-the desperation, the agony, the need to find a reason to go on, and the inability to find it.
author: Mary Beth Miller

text: In the meantime, I’llwish it upon a star.'- Michael Cooper
author: Julie Ann Knudsen

text: Never take today for granted, tomorrow might never come.
author: Matt Vella

text: And hope is but a dream of those that wake.
author: Matthew Prior

text: Hope comes not from the people around you, but from yourself.
author: Alegna G. Granados

text: The future is an infinite succession of presents, and to live now as we think human beings should live, in defiance of all that is bad around us, is itself a marvelous victory.
author: Howard Zinn

text: There is a period for hope and one for mourning.
author: Federico Chini

text: From the mud of adversity grows the lotus of joy
author: Carolyn Marsden

text: Whoso beset him roundWith dismal storiesDo but themselves confound
author: His strength the more is.

text: No point carrying useless ballast. It won't change a thing.
author: Joanne Harris

text: An opportunity is a miracle waiting to happen.
author: Rue Allyn

text: Despair wishes their hope diminishes.
author: Sherina Gandia

text: What will you do now with the gift of your left life?
author: Carol Ann Duffy

text: We must look to Mary's example to know how to deal with the glorious impossibilities of God. Look how she turned the world upside down by making one simple statement ...
author: Calvin Miller

text: They that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
author: Patricia Christian Punches

text: It took more than science to make hope real.
author: Jerome Groopman

text: There it must be, I think, in the vast and eternal laws of matter, andnot in the daily cares and sins and troubles of men, that whatever ismore than animal within us must find its solace and its hope. I hope, or Icould not live.
author: H.G. Wells

text: ... but I must reluctantly observe that two causes, the abbreviation of time, and the failure of hope, will always tinge with a browner shade the evening of life.
author: Edward Gibbon

text: But, luckily, he kept his wits and his purple crayon.
author: Crockett Johnson

text: Don’t allow your crisis to become a crisis of faith.

text: When my worldwas nothing but darknessI set my soul on fire.
author: Liz  Newman

text: Your purpose will fuel your hope.
author: Akiroq Brost

author: Frederick Douglass

text: Lifeless life is the very thing Jesus came to rescue us from.
author: Erin M. Straza

text: The world needs more beautiful things.
author: Paula Stokes

text: Hope could not outlast the breather. Love, however . . . Love was something not even death could conquer, because at the end of everything, even life, he was hers.
author: Ryan Graudin

text: Fear is the greatest prison.Hope is the greatest freedom.
author: Filipe de Lima Coelho

text: ...hope. Simple hope.It was something we all deserved.
author: E. M. Crane

text: At the end of the night awaits the white morning: showered in sunlight.
author: Gerrit Achterberg

text: Sometimes, however, there is more than hope.Sometimes there is reason.
author: Elizabeth Haydon

text: When the days become darkThe nights become coldAnd all there is, is an empty soulBelieve you can escape your plightEven when relief is out of sightDon't you dare give up the fightDon't you dare
author: Evy Michaels

text: the hope of a better future belongs to us!
author: Manuel Corazzari

text: You must believe in yourself, otherwise we are all lost to darkness
author: Robert Ian Wilson

text: Hope because the Universe hears you. Hope because the Universe works for you. Hope because the Universe speaks to you. Hope because the Universe will finish what it began in you.
author: Drishti Bablani

text: Everything will work out... don't give up hope, remain calm.
author: Jonathan Franklin

text: Some things cannot be fixed. They can only be carried.
author: Megan Devine

text: Hope says: one dayyou will see her, if you will only wait.Despair says:all you have left of her is your bitterness.Beat, heart…The earthhas not swallowed everything.
author: Antonio Machado
