author: Aditi Khorana

text: Denial is the inevitable consequence of those who are presented with disastrous scenarios without being offered an avenue for success. That is why we need to maintain a hopeful attitude.
author: Ashavan

text: There is always hope if we keep an unsolved problem fairly in view
author: there’s none if we pretend it’s not there.

text: Over the years I have felt many things leave me.	People do not change, but yet somehow time alters everything.	As the years roll on people are not quite as forgiving, nor quite as kind, hopeful, loving.
author: Ross  Turner

text: Perhaps love and hope are one and the same.
author: Cameron Dokey

text: Hope is never lost more than we are
author: Slaven Vujic

text: This world is filled with critics already
author: being an encourager is what we need to be.

text: The corpse opened his eyes.
author: Donato Carrisi

text: What else is a child, but hope? Hope for the future, hope for one's own salvation, hope for a tomorrow that shines as bright and warm as the best yesterday you can remember.
author: Emily Littlejohn

text: Sometimes, crazy is exactly what we need.
author: Dennis Canfield

text: Even more than a heart, hope was a necessary traveling companion.
author: Julianne Donaldson

text: Even the worst days have an ending, and the best days have a beginning.
author: Jennifer Coletta

text: Hope on you is worthless because you make a mark to forgot
author: ascertain solo

text: Education is the realization of hope for the future.
author: Victor Tam

text: Hope is a curious thing. You always seem to loose it when you most need it.
author: P.B. McIlrath

text: You see, if we have no hope, we have nothing
author: Tarun Betala

text: Suddenly I heard Virginia’s voice calling to me from the sitting room window: “Hitler is making a speech.” I shouted back, “I shan’t come. I’m planting iris and they will be flowering long after he is dead.
author: Leonard Woolf

text: The rain begins with a single drop
author: Manal Al-Sharif

text: We cannot hunt for love. We can only surrender and become love’s prey.
author: Kamand Kojouri

text: shit happens, miracles don't
author: Mohamed el airou

text: i’m going to love again so quietlyno one will know i’m home.
author: stephanie   roberts

text: It's making me so sad that you are unreachable. My desire for your touch, and your lips. It's breaking me inside. I'm so scared you are the only one i will ever love. I'm so scared because I know there is no other you.
author: Salome Steinz

text: Love must risk all or perish.
author: Anthony  Ryan

text: When we love something we never give up.
author: Beverley Lee

text: De bomen komen uit de gronden uit hun stam de twijgen.En iedereen vindt het heel gewoondat zij weer bladeren krijgen.We zien ze vallen naar de gronden dan opnieuw weer groeien.Zo heeft de aarde ons geleerddat al wat sterft zal bloeien.
author: Toon Hermans

text: You will trip up and fall sometimes. It doesn't take away from how great you are. You will not always say the right thing. It doesn't revoke the light within you.
author: Stalina Goodwin

text: Love doesn't need your permission, that's what hope is for.
author: R.A.Delmonico

text: Don’t allow anyone to shame you with your life story. Walk in your truth knowing that you are the better because of it. It is because of all the things you've been through that you are able to be who you are today.
author: Stalina Goodwin

text: Poetry flows from our hearts. It is like that mass of snow we call a glacier and when it starts its slow movement, it thaws all the icicles, which keep mounting within us, in the hope of receiving the warmth of words.
author: Balroop Singh

text: Dear newlywed wife, somebody out there is determined to shape your view of intimacy and marriage. You get to choose who will shape your opinion; the Author of marriage or offended humans?
author: Ngina Otiende

text: God has a passion for brand new beginnings.
author: Ngina Otiende

text: Sure is a word of hope not the word of trust
author: Dattasmita HV

text: Choose the Path to Enlightenment over the path of least resistance
author: Brian Coletta

text: Hope is a gift. Use it wisely.
author: Harley King

text: Surround yourself with what matters.
author: hlbalcomb

text: She lives fearlessly in the light of her own truth.
author: H.L. Balcomb

text: Hope is an undiscovered disappointment
author: Unknown

text: The biggest act of rebellion right now is remaining defiantly hopeful.
author: Rupert Dreyfus

text: As long as there's a maybe there will be a hopr
author: Hadil Diaf

text: Hope can live in a vacuum.
author: Greg Rucka

text: I imagined it was far better to be optimistic, to proceed assuming wherever you could that you had cared enough, that you'd made a difference, that you would again. Dwelling on the worst was no way to live.
author: Gwenda Bond

text: I'm not going to lie: there are no good options here.
author: Jackson Lanzing

text: No one is ever really lost as long as their story still exists.
author: R.M. Romero

text: Because desperate people cling to hope like sailors to their wreaks.
author: Kate Morton

text: Nurture your dreams to fruition, refuse to let the rot in reality.
author: Tyconis D. Allison Ty

text: Success is not got in twinkling of an eye.
author: Akanksha Agrawal

text: If someone gives you tough situation, it's better to make a distance.
author: Avinash Advani

text: Hope?" She eyed Cassian dubiously. "Is that the best the Rebel Intelligence can do?" Cassian might as well have shrugged. "Rebellions are built on hope," he said
author: Alexander Freed

text: This is what I know
author: people's hopes go on forever.

text: If where we are now and whatever we are going through does not motivate us to leave this world better than the way we met it, we are in this world for wrong reasons.
author: Bamigboye Olurotimi

text: Even if it's meaningless . . . sometimes, nice things happen.
author: Tsukumizu

text: While we must always strive to grow beyond those who come before us, we must revere and learn from those that broke the ground originally, endeavor to honor their legacy, and then continue in our own vision.
author: Alexandra Silber

text: I want to live. Doesn't everyone feel the same way?
author: Yua Kotegawa

text: Real affection does not see anything in terms of 'rights', nor does it even care if it is reciprocated. It merely waits by the door and hopes."From 'A Walking Shadow
author: E.M. Swift-Hook

text: If you did not live lovingly and love deeply, you would not feel the pain of separation. But neither would you feel the joy, passion, and happiness that living fully and loving deeply bring.
author: Susan Barbara Apollon

text: Hope is a fine killing thing.
author: Kirryn Lia Todd

text: You are the future that will never become my past.
author: Luna Marym

text: Remember: Rain doesn't need watering.
author: Efrat Cybulkiewicz

text: She had hoped. And hope broke more hearts than any man ever could.
author: Cora Carmack

text: Even the tiniest of flowers can have the toughest roots.
author: Shannon  Mullen

text: Sometimes hope is like wearing someone else’s coat. You know it can shield and protect you, just as it did them, but it still doesn’t feel right. You’re just not used to it.
author: Karina Halle

text: There is an old phrase, ‘hiding in plain sight.’ This is where we find the loved one we miss so much. All we need to do is open our eyes, our minds, and our hearts.
author: Susan Barbara Apollon

text: No matter how dark the moment, love and hope are always possible!
author: George Chakiris

text: Dreams bring hope in life, hope brings dreams alive.
author: Elijah Onyenmeriogu

text: Give yourself permission to see and feel the extraordinary events in your own life. In internalizing them, you also will find your perspective about life and its meaning will change, resulting in growth and expansion of your soul.
author: Susan Barbara Apollon

text: When we learn to attribute meaning to the events in our lives, we connect with our Higher Purpose, Higher Wisdom, or Source; we become Master of the Self. A gradual process, this is often tied to loss and to love.
author: Susan Barbara Apollon

text: Hope because the universe hears you.Hope because the universe works for you.Hope because the universe speaks to you.Hope because the universe will finish what it began in you.
author: Drishti Bablani

text: You only confuse hope with power once in life.
author: Abby Fabiaschi

text: I’d rather not know what lies ahead because I like the dark. I like thinking there is something good in the places I can’t see. And that’s not ignorance. That’s just hope.
author: Caroline George

text: Regret is the strongest anchor that latches on to the ground, and you carry it within you, it is a feeling quite unlike others for it is despair mingled with hope.
author: Saim .A. Cheeda

text: Hope is not a door, but a sense that there might be a door at some point, some way out of the problems of the present moment even before that way is found or followed.
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: Hopefulness is risky, since it is after all a form of trust, trust in the unknown and the possible, even in discontinuity. To be hopeful is to take on a different persona, one that risks disappointment, betrayal...
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: My wishing star glowed slightly and winked back at me. I could almost hear its voice, tinkling like wind chimes and church bells, reassuring me that everything would return to normal.
author: Erica Sehyun Song

text: Violence is the power of the state
author: imagination and non-violence the power of civil society.

text: How can the heart and mind work together? The mind wants logic and to travel in straight lines, while the heart wants to be free and travel upward in spirals to dizzying heights.
author: Gillian Duce

text: Initially they waited with hope, but as each hour passed, hope slipped away like the wind, the wind that as a small boy Ethan had once tried to capture with his tiny fingers.
author: K. Martin Beckner

text: I'm still looking for someoneWho said they were here for me,And I thought I was once there for you.But when troubles are nearer than friends,And the road comes to an end,What could I do?I wish I could fly away like you.
author: Margo T. Rose

text: I believe that life is full of tragedy. Some lives more than others. But I also believe that comfort can be found with the people that love you . . . if you're willing to let them give it.
author: Eliza Maxwell

text: Wherever you are and whatever you do, I hope you find the time to dwell in your favourite memories. It is one of the most beautiful and relaxing things we can do, no matter when and no matter where.
author: James Andrew Crosby

text: Hope is only the love of life.
author: Sally Brampton

text: No matter how bleak and black her existence became, the familiar sight of the moon restored something within her, small as it was—like tiny fluttering wings of flame beating back the darkness.
author: Shona Moyce

text: The naively cynical measure a piece of legislation, a victory, a milestone not against the past or the limits of the possible, but against their ideas of perfection...
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: today my heart was ripped in twoone half for me, one half for you take a half and keep it neari have mine and will always be here crossing paths before the endonce a lover, forever a friend
author: Connor Franta

text: It was hope that was the problem. Hoping feelings wouldn't be hurt, hoping love would blossom, that was painful. But committing yourself to misery, that was just a dead feeling. It was pulling the Band-Aid and embracing the pain.
author: Stephen Emond

text: Speranța e doar în infinitul posibilităților.
author: Alexandru Lamba

text: He who has health, has hope; and he who has hope, has everything
author: Thomas Carlyle

text: Never give up hope, no matter how dark things may seem.
author: A. T.

text: All that is required of you is an open mind and a little patience.
author: F.K. Preston

text: I keep dying and hoping you notice me. But you’re too busy living.
author: F.K. Preston

text: The greatest happiness is a quiet kind. It’s the tender understanding that we’re living in a very strange place full of strange creatures. And there’s quite a bit of wonder in that.
author: F.K. Preston

text: I live in one of the best places, bar none, to appreciate the wild natural environment. I also live in one of the most politically difficult places to work on its behalf: Alaska.
author: Kate Troll

text: I will wait for you until the gold from the sun dwindles away.
author: H.S. Crow

text: I gave up drinking before my twentieth birthday. I haven’t touched the stuff since. And I’ve discovered that not everyone who does horrible things is a horrible person.
author: Brent  Jones

text: See, you’ve got to understand, son. There’s two types of guys in this world. There’s guys . . . who think they’re in control, and guys like us who live in the moment. Who accept life as it is.
author: Brent  Jones

text: It feels like I’m stuck in one spot. It’s been this way for a long time. I know you understand, but now you’re moving on without me. And I—I’m not ready to be alone.
author: Brent  Jones

text: After you hear and listen. First must come desire. Second must come willingness.Third should come understanding.Fourth should come progression and with progression will come more understanding.
author: Hyrum Yeakley

text: After you hear and listen. First must come desire. Second must come willingness.Third should come understanding.Fourth should come progression and with progression will come more understanding..
author: Hyrum Yeakley

text: Good Evening to inline expectations have not materialized, I hope tomorrow you can be hugged with the smile that tick, under the morning light through the sweet lips.

text: Hopes are so well constructed, so monstrously dashed!

text: SALLY: Grab what you want and hope for the best.

text: There is never a guarantee of survival, no matter your strength or skill or wit. There is only the day you are conceived and the day you die, and all else is a series of moments you either embrace or endure.

text: Hope is something God gives us. Hope is...Hope is a bright light on a dark night. If your hope is guiding you into this shore, then this is where you should drop your anchor.

text: He pondered his turmoil, wondering which he feared most—losing his father or being alone in the world. Both were inevitable. Neither could be stopped or slowed down. All he could do now was brace for impact.

text: I know it is difficult to believe in your own courage or fortitude when everything inside of you feels weak and shattered. But do not believe what you feel. You will not be easily broken.

text: We weren’t that different after all, our souls laden with the same ache of loss, our hearts bursting with the same longing to save what remained of all we loved.
author: Rachel L. Schade

text: We will not die cornered and cowering in the ruins of a dead city.
author: Rachel L. Schade

text: Hope is a strange thing, not to mention a common prostitute name,...
author: Andy Paine

text: Posture is Paramount.
author: Cindy Ann Peterson

text: Dreams of success often get in the way of actual success.

text: A jewelled garden the envy of Aladdin's cave spilled across the courtyard.
author: Felix Long

text: Jin looked both ways for the Quicksilver Dragon before crossing the canal.
author: Felix Long

text: The two seasons; deepest winter from above and nascent spring from below, created a juxtaposition that drew the scene into the land of legend sitting only a heartbeat away behind a translucent river of time.

text: I must give you this warning: your journeys will rarely ever go as you plan. You will make mistakes, and you will feel lost. Whenever that happens, look to the light and keep moving forward in faith.

text: The best of what we are lies in what we hope to be

text: Hope means you keep holding on to things that won't ever be so again.

text: Hope in the shadow of fear is the world's most powerful motivator.

text: Hope is a gift we give ourselves in our darkest despair.

text: Money isn't success. Success is the character with which you make it.

text: Greatness is not a term of success, but of endurance.

text: Hope is human natural right but they have to keep it.

text: It takes time;It will take time, But it will happen

text: I pray for insight where the path is veiled, fortitude where the obstacles appear insurmountable, endurance where the destination seems unattainable, and equanimity when all that remains is to accept the journey.

text: The question is not whether our life is purposeful, but whether that purpose leads to a hope which is proper to our nature as human beings, for only then will life be meaningful.

text: A single thread of hope is stronger than all the chains that bind you.

text: W“e’ve taken our love of crows and glitter to the point of no return. Our best advice is don’t get caught up in the details… just enjoy the over-the-top romance.

text: Imagination belongs to hope. It’s the creative dance of possibility.
author: Sharon Weil

text: Hope dreams into being what is possible but not yet formed.
author: Sharon Weil

text: Winners gather together to fuel hope
author: losers gossip together to fuel hate.

text: You know what they say about hope, it breeds eternal misery
author: Spencer Hastings

text: That’s the hope, isn’t it? To see your family in your time of dying.
author: Saim .A. Cheeda

text: Hope requires waiting. Waiting requires patience.

text: Embrace the pain
author: Jude Gibbs

text: Don't be afraid that things are changing, because indeed they always are.

text: Per qualche motivo anche solo assistere mi aveva fatto venir voglia di uccidere qualunque parte di me provasse del desiderio. Qualunque parte di me stesse ancora aspettando qualcosa da qualcuno, speranzosa.

text: Go be absolutely, positively, fucking angelic.

text: Some stories won't have a happy ending, but there's always hope that the next one will. Hope is everything. Even when there's nothing else. Especially when there's nothing else.

text: Somewhere, excitement waited for me like an uncut cake.

text: Hope is like sunshine, which is still there, shielded by cloudsHope is that light, which beckons to brighten our daysThe breezy buoyancy of hope stirs us to hold handsThe darkest of clouds disintegrate, says Mother Nature.

text: With enough 'ifs' we could put Paris in a bottle.
author: - French proverb

text: Hope is made of air, and wishes. An empty box wrapped in shiny paper.

text: Do not focus on the broken pieces, focus on the whole picture

text: Hope can be the strongest if you believe it to be.
author: Callie L.

text: I thank God every day for this life, and I want there to be more, though that’s not known. What is known is that I’m alive today, this minute. And that’s pretty much what we all have – this day, this moment.

text: We all die. Not all of us live.
author: Edie Littlefield Sundby

text: A long walk is a slow remembering of how profound and wonderful life is
author: God is everywhere and in everything. Wherever I look I am looking at God.

text: Acceptance of death and cancer did not mean I intended to give up, just the opposite. I was prepared to fight cancer not out of fear of dying, but out of joy of living.
author: Edie Littlefield Sundby

text: I started to walk the day I was told I was dying of cancer. I believe walking has kept me alive. I live with a constant, pressing awareness of death. Once I start to walk, I am not afraid anymore; all is well.

text: Hatred doesn’t need company. It stews in its own loneliness and can grow still. Love needs companions. Love alone is daunted. Love together is unbound and unrestricted.

text: Your happiness is a beacon of light in my life. Although you may not realize so now, your bright disposition will smooth many paths ahead for you. Whatever happens—don’t ever let that light go out.

text: Glimpse is a meeting of dreamers in the silent night sky.

text: Everything has its opposite within it.Love can be very destructive if you mishandle it.Pain can help you find ways to grow and change.Fear can eventually lead you to a path of hope.
author: Jay Woodman

text: ...nothing helps a lie float like a hopeful listener.
author: Joshilyn Jackson

text: Liza with her acceptance could take care of tragedy
author: she had no real hope this side of Heaven.

text: I sometimes find myself sitting here not watching an uninteresting movie and contemplating the future. I don't always know what to do, but I have faith that I'll figure it out somehow.

text: As the last curtain falls on a spent year, a new year pirouettes to center stage with the elegance and charm of a prima ballerina – and delivers the promise of peace, hope, love and joy.

text: While there is life, there is hope.
author: Jules Verne

text: There was no public to humiliate him here. They already knew he was a lunatic. They expected it. He could burst into howls of insanity, and they would only smile those gentle smiles at him and wrestle him into the chains.

text: Only to close these eyesto sleep in the lap of loveand stay there forever.

author: Kilroy J. Oldster

text: Lessons from the past create hope for the future and action in the present.

text: A Hero: A Moment When You’re Bigger Than Yourself.

text: Walking through darkness with thoughts full of colors".

text: Religiousity is unself glory not purity

text: I'm just a broken thing you're familiar with.

text: you giver of light.you lover of love.you beautifulbeautifulhuman being you.

author: but it's always there. Hope is not invincible

text: Hope is a form of conscious dream making.
author: Kilroy J. Oldster

text: To the wonders of all things, we fall and then we are reborn

author: maybe I'm one of those who were born with wings and the destiny to fly.

author: God's gift can only bring you joy when they are joined with your gratitude.

author: Dr. Jacinta Mpalyenkana

author: don’t read the Red Herring prospectus before investing in hope.

text: Life is cold

text: Walk gently, and be brave.

author: Herbert Hoover

text: The dead can survive as part of the lives of those that still live.

text: To live in hearts we leave behind is not to die.
author: Thomas  Campbell

text: Men should think twice before making widowhood women's only path to power.

text: In time, in time they tell me, I'll not feel so bad. I don't want time to heal me. There's a reason I'm like this.I want time to set me ugly and knotted with loss of you, marking me. I won't smooth you away.I can't say goodbye.

text: I was always holding onto people, and they were always leaving.
author: Lili St. Crow

text: The phoenix must burn to emerge.
author: Janet Fitch

text: Be ashamed to die until you have won some victory for humanity.

text: I know, too, that death is the only god who comes when you call.

text: How could the death of someone you had never met affect you so?

text: Honor from death,” I snap, “is a myth. Invented by the war torn to make sense of the horrific. If we die, it will be so that others may live. Truly honorable death, the only honorable death, is one that enables life.

text: There is no such thing as inner peace. There is only nervousness and death.

text: My heart has joined the Thousand, for my friend stopped running today.

text: No sense of the irony of human experience, that we are the highest form of life on earth, and yet ineffably sad because we know what no other animal knows, that we must die.

text: Now, I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds.
author: J. Robert Oppenheimer

text: Summertime is always the best of what might be.
author: Charles Bowden

text: Death is contagious
author: it is contracted the moment we are conceived.

text: I know that it's easier to look at death than it is to look at pain, because while death is irrevocable, and the grief will lessen in time, pain is too often merely relentless and irreversible.
author: Robert Goolrick

text: People who get up early in the morning cause war, death and famine.
author: Banksy

text: Yer a good lad, Atticus, mowin’ me lawn and killin’ what Brits come around.
author: Kevin Hearne

text: Conscience is no more than the dead speaking to us.
author: Jim Carroll

text: There are always two deaths, the real one and the one people know about.
author: Jean Rhys

text: And we wept that one so lovely should have a life so brief
By daily dying, I have come to be.
author: Theodore Roethke

text: The power of the dead is that we think they see us all the time. The dead have a presence. Is there a level of energy composed solely of the dead? They are also in the ground, of course, asleep and crumbling. Perhaps we are what they dream.
author: Don DeLillo

text: No drowning man can know which dropOf water his last breath did stop
author: Charles Sedley

text: The cries of the dead are terrible indeed
author: you should try not to hear them.

text: He would reach for me in the middle of the night, nearly every single night, wrapping one of those solid arms around my waist and pulling me in close. So. Close.
author: Chelsie Shakespeare

text: Time doesn’t heal all wounds. We both know that’s bullshit
author: it comes from people who have nothing comforting or original to say.

text: A tragedy need not have blood and death
author: it's enough that it all be filled with that majestic sadness that is the pleasure of tragedy.

text: Death is the fairest thing in the world. No one's ever gotten out of it. The earth takes everyone - the kind, the cruel, the sinners. Aside from that, there's no fairness on earth.
author: Svetlana Alexievich

text: Usually time alters and affects everything, but when someone you love dies time cannot change that, no amount of time will ever change that, so time stops having any meaning.
author: Rosamund Lupton

text: Stay with me to-night; you must see me die. I have long had the taste of death on my tongue, I smell death, and who will stand by my Constanze, if you do not stay?

text: Today will die tomorrow.
author: Algernon Charles Swinburne

text: I'm not afraid of death. Death's afraid of me.
author: Lee Child

text: What glitters may not be gold
author: and even wolves may smile

text: But on paper, things can live forever. On paper, a butterfly never dies.

text: Each of us has his own rhythm of suffering.
author: Roland Barthes

text: Here is a list of terrible things,The jaws of sharks, a vultures wingsThe rabid bite of the dogs of war,The voice of one who went before,But most of all the mirror's gaze,Which counts us out our numbered days.
author: Clive Barker

text: Not forever does the bulbul singIn balmy shades of bowers,Not forever lasts the springNor ever blossom the flowers.Not forever reigneth joy,Sets the sun on days of bliss,Friendships not forever last,They know not life, who know not this.
author: Khushwant Singh

text: How do you mourn something that never really belonged to you?
author: Rebecca Serle

text: When the dead do walk seek water's run,for this the Dead will always shun. Swift river's best or broadest laketo ward the dead and have and make.If water fails thee, fire's thy friend, if neither guards it will be thy end.

text: Death is easy when you've already tried to find it.
author: Pierce Brown

text: You know how cats do. They hide to die. Dogs come home.
author: Thomas Harris

text: The first kiss can be as terrifying as the last.
author: Daina Chaviano

text: when you came you cried and everybody smiled with joy
author: when you go smile and let the world cry for you.

text: Somebody should tell us, right at the start of our lives, that we are dying. Then we might live life to the limit, every minute of every day. Do it! I say. Whatever you want to do, do it now! There are only so many tomorrows.
author: Michael Landon Jr.

text: Pain was good. If I hurt, it meant I wasn't dead.
author: Kristin Cast

text: The dead know everything but they don't give a damn.
author: Joanne Harris

text: Death is only the end if you assume the story is about you.

text: The evil that men do lives after them
author: The good is oft interred with their bones.

text: Everyone dies. It is how one lives that matters.

text: Not only had my brother disappeared, but--and bear with me here--a part of my very being had gone with him. Stories about us could, from them on, be told from only one perspective. Memories could be told but not shared.

text: The whole life of the individual is nothing but the process of giving birth to himself; indeed, we should be fully born when we die - although it is the tragic fate of most individuals to die before they are born.

text: We're all ghosts. We all carry, inside us, people who came before us.

text: Death is an inevitability, isn't it? You become more aware of that when you get to my age. I don't worry about it. I'm ready for it. When I go, I want to go doing what I do best. If I died tomorrow, I couldn't complain. It's been good.

text: I wish I had a memory of that first violent shove, the shock of cold air, the sting of oxygen into new lungs. Everyone should remember being born. It doesn't seem fair that we only remember dying.

text: If there be light, then there is darkness; if cold, heat; if height, depth; if solid, fluid; if hard, soft; if rough, smooth; if calm, tempest; if prosperity, adversity; if life, death.
author: Pythagoras

text: Grief is love turned into an eternal missing
author: Rosamund Lupton

text: Death always wins.
author: Kelly Creagh

text: Energy is neither created nor destroyed. It just changes shape.
author: Sheri Reynolds

text: Death is more universal than life
author: everyone dies but not everyone lives.

text: You don't have friends in here, you'll soon come to understand that. You get attached to someone, then you'll just lose them. They'll get shanked or they'll jump or they'll be taken one night.
author: Alexander Gordon Smith

text: An eye for an eye my friend.
author: Tsugumi Ohba

text: Suicide sometimes proceeds from cowardice, but not always; for cowardice sometimes prevents it; since as many live because they are afraid to die, as die because they are afraid to live
author: Charles Caleb Colton

text: Everyone you have ever loved in your life becomes a part of your soul. They never leave. They're always inside you, and you can bring them out whenever you want.
author: Nate Kenyon

text: They say that somewhere in Africa the elephants have a secret grave where they go to lie down, unburden their wrinkled gray bodies, and soar away, light spirits at the end.
author: Robert McCammon

text: I am only alive because I have not yet died.
author: Anthony Doerr

text: He could not stand. It was notThat he could not thrive, he was bornWith everything but the will –That can be deformed, just like a limb.Death was more interesting to him.Life could not get his attention.
author: Ted Hughes

text: I love the name of honor more than I fear death.
author: Gaius Julius Caesar

text: It's a little hard not to worry when my best friend keeps on dying.
author: Kristin Cast

text: It's a time of sorrow and sadness when we lose a loss of life.
author: George W. Bush

text: ...Matt. I didn't think they'd kill you... I'm so sorry...
author: Tsugumi Ohba

text: You're probably wondering what's going to happen to you. That's easy. The same thing is going to happen to you that has happened to every other human being who has ever lived. You're going to die. We all die. That's just how it is.
author: Ernest Cline

text: I have nothing now but praise for my life. I'm not unhappy. I cry a lot because I miss people. They die and I can't stop them. They leave me and I love them more...
author: Maurice Sendak

text: Death is nature’s way of saying, ‘Your table is ready.
author: Robin Williams

text: Reproduction is more pleasurable than death.
author: Herman E. Daly

text: But a kingdom that has once been destroyed can never come again into being
author: nor can the dead ever be brought back to life.

text: Now comes the mystery! (last words)
author: Henry Ward Beecher

text: A girl always remembers the first corpse she shaves.
author: Caitlin Doughty

text: Pain can kill, all on its own: the body goes into shock and shuts down.
author: Teri Terry

text: Only those few who are able to surpass their fear of death completely can fully experience the highest forms of life; not the mundane life of the mortal, but the godly life of the resurrected.
author: Zeena Schreck

text: Death is not the endDeath can never be the end.Death is the road.Life is the traveller.The Soul is the Guide...Our mind thinks of death.Our heart thinks of lifeOur soul thinks of Immortality
author: Sri Chinmoy

text: Cover her face
author: mine eyes dazzle. She died young.

text: Time moves on for us, for you it stands still. You will be forever ageless as we grow old, your smile will never wrinkle, nor will that shine in your eyes fade..
author: Kendal Rob

text: I became quietly seized with that nostalgia that overcomes you when you have reached the middle of your life and your father has recently died and it dawns on you that when he went he took some of you with him.
author: Bill Bryson

text: The only certainty is death.
author: Guy de Maupassant

text: It was not my intention to collapse; no, I would die standing.
author: Knut Hamsun

text: The light at the end of the tunnel is just the light of an oncoming train.
author: Robert Lowell

text: We carry the dead with us only until we die too, and then it is we who are borne along for a little while, and then our bearers in their turn drop, and so on into the unimaginable generations.
author: John Banville

text: He was dead, all right. He had been shot, poisoned, stabbed, and strangled. Either somebody had really had it in for him or four people had killed him. Or else it was the cleverest suicide I'd ever heard of.
author: Richard S. Prather

text: She already felt dead in everything but name. What remained to be taken from her? She longed to be enfolded, welcomed, into the earth - to breathe no more, love no more, hurt no more
author: Alan Brennert

text: It hurts when they're gone. And it doesn't matter if it's slow or fast, whether it's a long drawn-out disease or an unexpected accident. When they're gone the world turns upside down and you're left holding on, trying not to fall off.
author: Walter Mosley

text: For death is life. It is only living that is lifeless.
author: Mervyn Peake

text: Bu dağlarda bin kere ölmeden, bir kere dirilemezsin.
author: Yasar Kemal

text: Death is the Graduation of the Soul
author: Sylvia Browne

text: (On his gravestone): "I told you I was ill".
author: Spike Milligan

text: Cada suspiro es como un sorbo de vida del que uno se deshace.
author: Juan Rulfo

text: The dead know only one thing, it is better to be alive
author: Stanley Kubrick

text: Damn it! How will I ever get out of this labyrinth?
author: Simon Bolivar

text: I love you Anna Covey,' he said, his voice barely audible. And slowly, clumsily, he leant forward, and his lips found hers, and Anna felt him kiss her awkwardly, she knew that she wasn't a Surplus any more. And nor was Peter.
author: Gemma Malley

text: No man should go to Valhalla with brothel rash.
author: Mark  Lawrence

text: Sorry to hear about your Dad."He shrugged. "He was seventy, and we always told him fast food would kill him.""Heart attack?""He was hit by a Pizza Express truck.
author: J.A. Konrath

text: Now I am about to take my last voyage, a great leap in the dark
author: Thomas Hobbes

text: Someone once said that death is not the greatest loss in life. The greatest loss is what dies inside of us while we live. I could tell you who said it, but who the hell really cares.
author: One tree hill haley james scott

text: Death is harder on those who are left behind.
author: Robert La Fosse

text: Girls are always saying things like, “I’m so unhappy that I’m going to overdose on aspirin,” but they’d be awfully surprised if they succeeded. They have no intention of dying. At the first sight of blood, they panic.
author: Rachel Klein

text: I've changed my ways a little, I cannot nowRun with you in the evenings along the shore,Except in a kind of dream, and you, if you dream a moment,You see me there.
author: Robinson Jeffers

text: Pentru mine, moartea este doar hotarul unde încetează să mai existe "mâine". Numai până acolo poți să iubești, să visezi, să regreți. Brusc, tot ce n-ai făcut va rămâne pentru totdeauna nefăcut.
author: Octavian Paler

text: Death! Strange that there should be such a word, and such a thing, and we ever forget it; that one should be living, warm and beautiful, full of hopes, desires and wants, one day, and the next be gone, utterly gone, and forever!
author: Harriet Beecher Stowe

text: Never send to know for whom the bells tolls
author: it tolls for thee.

text: But my point, you see is that death is misunderstood. The loss of one's life is not the greatest loss. It is no loss at all. To others, perhaps, but not to oneself.
author: Tom Rachman

text: After sixty-one years together, she simply clutched my hand and exhaled.
author: Sara Gruen

text: I mentally bless and exonerate anyone who has kicked a chair out from beneath her or swallowed opium in large chunks. My mind has met their environment, here in the void. I understand perfectly.
author: Suzanne Finnamore

text: Life is too fleet for onomatopoeia.
author: Mervyn Peake

text: And starward drifts the stricken world,Lone in unalterable gloomDead, with a universe for tomb,Dark, and to vaster darkness whirled.(“The Testimony of the Suns”)
author: George Sterling

text: I want to look at you as you kill me.
author: Curtis Jobling

text: I basked in you;I loved you, helplessly, with a boundless tongue-tied love.And death doesn't prevent me from loving you.Besides, in my opinion you aren't dead.(I know dead people, and you are not dead.)
author: Franz Wright

text: The Only Way Out Is To DIE!!!!
author: Bullet for My Valentine

text: I would rather live than die. I would rather die than survive as a monster.
author: Robert Fanney

text: Death is as unexpected in his caprice as a courtesan in her disdain
author: but death is truer – Death has never forsaken any man

text: I got out on the street and started crying the kind of hysterical tears made justifiable only by turning off one’s cell phone, putting it to the ear, and pretending to be told of a death in the family.
author: Sloane Crosley

text: I am not a coward, but I am so strong. So hard to die.
author: Meriwether Lewis

text: If men only felt about death as they do about sleep, all terrors would cease. . . Men sleep contentedly, assured that they will wake the following morning. They should feel the same about their lives.
author: Richard Matheson

text: Do not mourn the dead. They know what they are doing.
author: Clarice Lispector

text: Never underestimate the will of a creature who begins to die at birth.
author: Lyn Benedict

text: A society that doesn't know any longer how to observe every death with proper rituals, that does not know that death is not the end, but only part of the journey, has lost its way, has had the very heart of its humanity torn out.
author: Marina Warner

text: No popularity exists when tragedy strikes. All that's left are human hearts and love and ache. We all love each other, deep down, and when we see another soul in pain we can't help but hurt too.
author: Maya Van Wagenen

text: Once a flower is picked it immediately begins to die.
author: Nenia Campbell

text: But hey, if there’s one bright side to your dying, it’s that you aren’t around to tell me things I don’t like hearing. I’m sorry. That was a dickhead thing to say. I need a condom for my mouth.
author: Adam Silvera

text: Every cataclysm is welcomed by somebody
author: there is always someone to rejoice at disaster and see in it the prospect of a new beginning and a better world.

text: Sometimes the only answer to death is lunch.
author: Jim Harrison

text: Survival is the celebration of choosing life over death. We know we're going to die. We all die. But survival is saying: perhaps not today. In that sense, survivors don't defeat death, they come to terms with it.
author: Laurence Gonzales

text: Death is the reward for living
author: Sylvia Browne

text: No mother should lose her child.
author: Ann Hood

text: She couldn't tell where his pupils ended and the irises began
author: looking into those eyes was like looking into a well where children had drowned.

text: So if there is something on the planet that is worth living for, I'd better not miss it, because once you're dead, it's too late for regrets, and if you die by mistake, that is really, really dumb.
author: Muriel Barbery

text: I lost my father this past year, and the word feels right because I keep looking for him. As if he were misplaced. As if he could just turn up, like a sock or a set of keys.
author: Mark Slouka

text: Death is inevitable passion is eternal
author: Allison van Diepen

text: Life is a long agonized illness only curable by death.
author: Spike Milligan

text: I'm not scared of death, I just don't want to seek it out.
author: Thomas James Higgins

text: If death, said my father, reasoning with himself, is nothing but the separation of the soul from the body;--and if it is true that people can walk about and do their business without brains,--then certes the soul does not inhabit there.
author: Laurence Sterne

text: It is always consoling to think of suicide
author: it's what gets one through many a bad night.

text: After all, what is every man? A horde of ghosts – like a Chinese nest of boxes – oaks that were acorns that were oaks. Death lies behind us, not in front – in our ancestors, back and back until...
author: Walter de la Mare

text: Perhaps because it seems so appropriate, I don't notice the rain. It falls in sheets, a blanket of silvery thread rushing to the hard almost-winter ground. Still, I stand without moving at the side of the coffin.
author: Michelle Zink

text: Death always leaves one singer to mourn.
author: Katherine Anne Porter

text: The knowledge of death seemed present in both sisters—it was something about the way they carried themselves, something that had broken too soon and had not mended, marking them in spite of their lightheartedness.
author: Jhumpa Lahiri

text: The first time is always the hardest
author: Sarwat Chadda

text: Tell her thisAnd more,—That the king of the seasWeeps too, old, helpless man.The bustling fatesHeap his hands with corpsesUntil he stands like a childWith surplus of toys.
author: Stephen Crane

text: Hope is a horrible thing, you know. It's a plague. It's like walking around with a fishhook in your mouth and someone just keep pulling it and pulling it."STATE OF WONDER
author: Ann Patchett

text: I write and that way rid myself of me and then at last I can rest.
author: Clarice Lispector

text: Sleep: the stepchild of Death.
author: Nancy A. Collins

text: Most people don't want to die, but they don't want to live either. I am speaking about men now as much as women. They look for a third way, but there is no third way.
author: Jonathan Rosen

text: There are women whose love only ends with death.
author: Georges Rodenbach

text: I was made to rule the darkness.
author: Rae Hachton

text: And let me ask you this: the dead, where aren't they?
author: Franz Wright

text: Too easy to lose the way.Too hard to keep from unraveling when there’s nothing to remind you of who youare and where you should be.Another eternity passes in the flick of an eyelash.
author: Diana Rowland

text: It is life that hurts you not death.
author: Martine Leavitt

text: In the end, living is defined by dying.
author: Bernard Beckett

text: Anyone in this world can have the power of life and death over someone else. It's horrible, but true. All you need to do is take it. And once you have -- there is no going back. (The Killer's Cousin)
author: Nancy Werlin

text: What we call 'time' isn't chronological but spatial
author: what we call 'death' is merely a transition between different kinds of matter.

text: The healing power of even the most microscopic exchange with someone who knows in a flash precisely what you're talking about because she experienced that thing too cannot be overestimated.
author: Cheryl Strayed

text: Everyone thought that things were getting back to normal. They had no idea that normal didn’t exist for me any more. Normal had been smashed on the rocks beneath the bridge.
author: Cat Clarke

text: Vanye: You're asking me to kill you.Luthiel: I'm asking you to save her.
author: Robert Fanney

text: … for overstrong was the command to hold fast to each smallest particle of time, to the smallest particle of every circumstance, and to embody all of them in memory as if they could be preserved in memory through all deaths for all times.
author: Hermann Broch

text: And in such bliss does devastation grow.
author: Roshani Chokshi

text: ...there's not enough of anything to go around except people and death.
author: Sasa Stanisic

text: When you attempt suicide, the counselors try to talk you out of trying it again by asking you about other people, which is good prevention if you care about other people.
author: Albert Borris

text: A flower bloomed already wilting. Beginning its life with an early ending.
author: R.J. Gonzales

text: A penny for my thoughts, oh no, I'll sell them for a dollarThey're worth so much more after I'm a gonerAnd maybe then you'll hear the words I been singin'Funny when you're dead how people start listenin
author: Kimberly Perry

text: Death truly does have life, and walks with and lives through us everyday.
author: Nicholas A. McGirr

text: I am Death. I come quickly, I come slowly, but I am not cheated.
author: Erika Johansen

text: We think of mortality so little these days...I thought of the stern Victorian determination to keep death in mind, the uncompromising tombstones.Remember, pilgrim, as you pass by,As you are now so once was I:As I am so will you be...
author: Tana French

text: The day after Paul Newman was dead, he was twice as dead.
author: Maurice Sendak

text: I'm Allen Walker!"My life....is over...I'm going to die....
author: Katsura Hoshino

text: What I have learned lately is that people deal with death in all sorts of ways. Some of us fight against it, doing everything we can to make it not true. Some of us lose our selves to grief. Some of us lose ourselves to anger.
author: Carrie Jones

text: There is not a day or night but a doe offers her life for her kittens, or some honest captain of Owsla his life for his Chief Rabbit's. Sometimes it is taken, sometimes it is not. But there is no bargain, for here, what is, is what must be.
author: Richard Adams

text: I think...I think its always been you who was dying,"- Delaney
author: Megan Miranda

text: What is life? It departs covertly. Like a thief Death took him.
author: John Gunther

text: His life spells living. Your life or my life, apart from Him, spells death.
author: Ravi Zacharias

text: Had I truly thought I would not die when he kissed me? But I did. For a moment the breath and life went out of me and there was no time and no tomorrow but only my lips against his.
author: Martine Leavitt

text: Death and destruction are necessary to the health of the world, and therefore as natural, and lovable, as birth and life. Only priests and born cowards moan and weep over dying. Brave men face it with approving nonchalance.
author: Ragnar Redbeard

text: Life dies but forever will there be music. Always.
author: Nicholas A. McGirr

text: The field of the soul must be watered by the rain with tears of love
author: otherwise it will become a desert.

text: In so many senseless deaths, beauty is to blame.
author: Suzanne Finnamore

text: Please not yet. Those are the three eternal words. Please not yet.
author: John D. MacDonald

text: Achieving the state of SABLE is not, as many people who live with these knitters believe, a reason to stop buying yarn, but for the knitter it is an indication to write a will, bequeathing the stash to an appropriate heir.
author: Stephanie Pearl-McPhee

text: Elaborate burial customs are a sure sign of decadence.
author: J.G. Ballard

text: The question of how to spend my life, of what my life is for, is a question posed only to me, and I can no more delegate the responsibility for answering it than I can delegate the task of dying.
author: Anthony T. Kronman

text: The grave's a fine and private place,But none, I think, do there embrace.
author: Andrew Marvell

text: To be alive, it seemed to me, as I stood there in all kinds of sorrow, was to be both original and reflection, and to be dead was to be split off, to be reflection alone.
author: Teju Cole

text: I don't think that science and the paranormal have to be at war; in fact, it's crucial that they work together. It seems naïve to believe that the world is exactly as it seems.
author: Chelsie Shakespeare

text: I looked at the stained-glass image of the lamb in the window above me, but that only reminded me that lambs are famous for being led to slaughter, or sometimes hanging out with lions in ill-advised relationships.
author: Maureen Johnson

text: I have every reason to be sad, but I don't have any reason to mourn. People grieve when things end. Nothing has ended tonight. One of us has simply gone ahead as we always knew it would have to be.
author: Samantha Sotto

text: Now that her spirit had left, I’d thought she would feel light. Then I realized it was the spirit that carries the weight of the body and not the other way around.
author: Jessica Maria Tuccelli

text: The most ruthless of all humans are the ones cornered in by death.
author: Yuu Watase

text: Red lips are not so red as the stained stones kissed by the English dead.
author: Wilfred Owen

text: The foolish rush to end their lives.Only the steadfast soul survives.
author: Christine de Pizan

text: You've got to think of the fine times you had with your mate, not the moment of his perishin'. Every tear you shed now only wets his windin' sheet and disturbs his rest
author: L.A. Meyer

text: It's important to attend funerals. It is important to view the body, they say, and to see it committed to earth or fire because unless you do that, the loved one dies for you again and again.
author: Ann-Marie MacDonald

text: In my world death will come chasing. In your world it will start whispering in your ear to destroy yourself. I know this because it started whispering to me when I was in the detention center.
author: Chris Cleave

text: Death ends a life, but it does not end a relationship, which struggles on in the survivor’s mind toward some final resolution, some clear meaning, which it perhaps never finds.
author: Robert Woodruff Anderson

text: The calla lilies are in bloom again. Such a strange flower—suitable to any occasion. I carried them on my wedding day, and now I place them here in memory of something that has died.
author: Katharine Hepburn

text: Dante: Evergreens aren't supposed to die
author: Yvonne Wood

text: I am dying now, but I still have many things to say.
author: Roberto Bolano

text: The candle glimmers but an hour. The nightLooms in its ancient hunger. Would you knowThe tragedy of human love and need?Gaze on the stars, then on a brother's face!
author: George Sterling

text: I've grieved enough for his life cut short and for mine for running on for so long with so little in it. It's weakness now, but I suppose I am crying out of a general sense of loss. Maybe I am mourning for the human condition.
author: Rosie Thomas

text: Would that the dead were not dead! But there is grass that must be eaten, pellets that must be chewed, hraka that must be passed, holes that must be dug, sleep that must be slept.
author: Richard Adams

text: Watch birth and death:The lotus has alreadyOpened its flower.
author: Soseki Natsume

text: Where they burn books, at the end they also burn people
author: Heinrich Heine

text: Heaven is a place where all the dogs you've ever loved come to greet you.
author: Oliver Gaspirtz

text: The fame of his likes circulates briskly but soon grows heavy and stale
author: and as for history it will limit his life story to the dash between two dates.

text: When the sun shall be folded up
author: and when the stars shall fall

text: Every man believes to some extent that the world began when he was born and, at the moment of leaving it, suffers at having to let the Universe remain unfinished.
author: Maurice Druon

text: I'm not courting death
author: I've far too many books left to read.

text: I'll never be ready. Yet at the same time, you always want to reach the end. You can't fly to a destination and linger in the air. I want to reach the end of this thing, and I feel terrible about it.
author: Kaui Hart Hemmings

text: I wish I could recommend the experience of not being killed to everyone.
author: Tim Kreider

text: When it's my time, and the reaper calls my name, there will be no stink of fear on me, and my only wish will be to die with grace, covered in the blood of my enemies.
author: Cedric Nye

text: Death is a part of Life, they are dancing together the dance of infinity in front of the gates of Time. We can live our dreams as we are dreaming our future. Time is the Endless Consciousness
author: Grigoris Deoudis

text: Hell, I don't want to grow old at all. I never want to die.
author: Kate Millett

text: Fear leads to death as the window to the courtyard. Jump!
author: Grigoris Deoudis

text: In fact living is dying.
author: Guy de Maupassant

text: When it comes, you’ll be dreamingthat you don’t need to breathe;that breathless silence isthe music of the darkand it’s part of the rhythmto vanish like a spark.
author: Wisława Szymborska

text: We live, we love, we let it go.
author: Kevin Dalton

text: Beastly of him to die before you realized he might be fascinating.
author: Tasha Alexander

text: Immortality like this is about as useful as sunscreen on a submarine.
author: Elizabeth Marx

text: It doesn't matter how much his mother loves him
author: love is not enough to keep any of us alive.

text: Time has transfigured them intoUntruth. The stone fidelityThey hardly meant has come to beTheir final blazon, and to proveOur almost-instinct almost true:What will survive of us is love.
author: Philip Larkin

text: There are only two things worse then an empty canvas: death and taxes.
author: Ragnar Tørnquist

text: The young open the paper to forget about life by reading the funny strips. The old do it to forget about death by reading other people's obits. My advice: don't open the paper and go on with your life.
author: Gabriel Ba

text: The sharp knife of a short life, wellI've had, just enough time.
author: Kimberly Perry

text: You smile upon your friend to-day,To-day his ills are over;You hearken to the lover's say,And happy is the lover.'Tis late to hearken, late to smile, But better late than never:I shall have lived a little whileBefore I die for ever.
author: A.E. Housman

text: That's it then. This is how it ends. I haven't even read Proust.
author: James Turner

text: You are going to end up as one of those sad old men who poke around in rubbish bins.”“I’m going to end up in a hole in the ground... And so are you. So are we all.
author: J.M. Coetzee

text: Forget it. Never explain
author: never apologize. You can either write posthumously or you can't.

text: Just know I amNot there to catch youBut I am there for you
author: Caleb Warta

text: die daily.hourly, if you'vegot the energy.
author: Sherwin Tjia

text: seeing a woman cough today made me sense a vague fear of death
author: Brandon Scott Gorrell

text: You can't drown yourself that simply. All good suicides involve speed and irreversibility, because the body will always move to protect itself against the sicko mind trying to do it in.
author: Josephine Humphreys

text: Birth and death - what could be more monstrous than that? We like to deceive ourselves and call it wondrous and beautiful and majestic, but it's freakish, let's face it.
author: Rohinton Mistry

text: When I die, nieces, I want to be cremated, my ashes taken up in a bush plane and sprinkled onto the people in town below. Let them think my body is snowflakes, sticking in their hair and on their shoulders like dandruff.
author: Joseph Boyden

text: Little sleep's-head sprouting hair in the moonlight,when I come backwe will go out together,we will walk out together among,the ten thousand things,each scratched too late with such knowledge, the wages of dying is love.
author: Galway Kinnell

text: Time erodes us all.
author: Meg Rosoff

text: The world begins anew with every birth, my father used to say. He forgot to say, with every death it ends. Or did not think he needed to. Because for a goodly part of his life he worked in a graveyard.
author: Sebastian Barry

text: The dead do not harm us, only the alive.
author: Rosie Thomas

text: Poets are interested primarily in death and commas.
author: Carolyn Kizer

text: But at my back I always hear Time’s wingèd chariot hurrying near
author: Andrew Marvell

text: Death is a scandal. The machine is functioning, we are all hostages
author: Elias Canetti

text: It is my wish to die of unique causes, perhaps in a high-speed tricycle crash, a bizarre stapling incient, or as a result of inadvertently sucking my brains out through my ear while trying to untwist the vacuum hose.
author: Paula Poundstone

text: If I should go before the rest of youBreak not a flower nor inscribe a stone, Nor when I'm gone speak in a Sunday voice But be the usual selves that I have known. Weep if you must, Parting is hell, But life goes on, So sing as well.
author: Joyce Grenfell

text: She was made mostly of coffee and empty spaces.
author: Adelise M. Cullens

text: There is no cure for birth and death save to enjoy the interval
author: George Santayana

text: While death and darkness girdle meI grope for immortality.
author: Lionel Pigot Johnson

text: With The Dread, first kiss was the beginning. Second kiss was the end.
author: Luke  Taylor

text: Once the game is over, the King and the pawn go back in the same box.
author: Italian proverb

text: The fact of having been born is a bad augury for immortality.
author: George Santayana

text: You smell so good,""No, I don't, I smell like death.""You're crazy, you're not dead. You do not smell like death.""I was dead a long time.""And now you're not. Hence the alive smell.
author: Amy Tintera

text: Not that there's anything wrong with just lying around on your back. In it's way, rotting is interesting too, as we will see. It's just that there are other ways to spend your time as a cadaver.
author: Mary Roach

text: A man who takes into consideration the feelings of others even when arranging the manner of his own death shows a nobility of character which compels the admiration of all classes.
author: Flann O'Brien

text: Those who live in memories are never really dead.
author: Kate Morton

text: And now let us love and take that which is given us, and be happy; for in the grave there is no love and no warmth, nor any touching of the lips. Nothing perchance, or perchance but bitter memories of what might have been.
author: H. Rider Haggard

text: She had storms all her life, but she died peacefully.
author: Janet Flanner

text: …the longer I think about it the more it seems to me that we who are still alive are unreal in the eyes of the dead, that only occasionally, in certain lights and atmospheric conditions, do we appear in their field of vision.
author: W.G. Sebald

text: Die, die we all pass away, But don't wear a frown coz it's really okay,And you might try to hide, And you might try to pray,But we all end up remains of the day.
author: Danny Elfman - The Corpse Bride

text: If you truly live, you will never truly die!
author: Melanie M. Grace

text: If there is nothing else there is this: to be inundated, consumed.
author: Peter Heller

text: Losing a mate to death is devastating but it's not a personal attack like divorce. When somebody you love stops loving you and walks away, it's an insult beyond comparison.
author: Sue Merrell

text: Everything- even me, and one day even Charlotte- must come to end.
author: Avery Williams

text: Life is the death of all of us.' - Tristan
author: Rowan Speedwell

text: People aren't often asked to make life or death decisions. There are no causes to die for. You can go through life never knowing which of your friends would really come through for you
author: Bella Pollen

text: The kiss. Oh, the kiss. What a perfect, unnerving, luscious kiss. He made me feel unhinged . . . like he could take me apart and put me back together again and again.
author: Chelsie Shakespeare

text: It’s natural to die. The fact that we make such a big hullabaloo over it is all because we don’t see ourselves as part of nature. We think because we’re human we’re something above nature.
author: Morrie Schwartz

text: And this evening when I close my eyes against the darkness and think about her, I'll imagine iridescent wings fluttering, if only for a moment, against cloudless blue skies.
author: Nancy Stephan

text: What is birthday, but a celebration of death.
author: Aphole

text: Death will be a great relief. No more interviews.
author: Katharine Hepburn

text: Yet for quixotic reasons--namely, that I enjoyed writing obits--I had decided to scale back on articles about city life in order to write exclusively about the city's dead. For even less money. It was a strange and inexplicable career move.
author: Avi Steinberg

text: A dead Christ I must do everything for
author: a living Christ does everything for me.

text: To learn to die is to learn to live. Death is the giver of life.
author: Michael Foley

text: How am I going to tell the kids? How do I tell the man that I love, the man that I swore I’d grow old with that we won’t have that happy ending that he and I have worked so hard for? How do I say goodbye to all of you? How do I let go?
author: Nicole Ireland

text: Gone, but only until we get there
author: Jo Royston

text: The only cure for a real hangover is death.
author: Robert Benchley

text: That kind of imagination is why we're not dead.
author: Rebecca McKinsey

text: Life isn't fair. It's just fairer than death, that's all.
author: William Goodman

text: Dr. Beall gave him the first shot, followed closely by the second.He said, "I'll check for a heartbeat."I said, "You don't need to. I can see it in his eyes."Dewey was gone.
author: Vicki Myron

text: Wherever you look there is so much loss and folly to contemplate.
author: Rosie Thomas

text: It is when we are faced with death that we turn most bookish.
author: Jules Renard

text: As he watches the sun rise, what grieves him is that he failed her. He thinks of the terror she felt. They tell him it was quick, as if that will somehow confine the horror.
author: Nancy Horan

text: Corpses sour you. They are bad for objectivity.
author: Bertolt Brecht

text: I believe there are ways whose ends are life instead of death.
author: William Saroyan

text: The Angel of Death is the invisible Angel of Life.
author: Henry Mills Alden

text: Death always knew how to connect vice with misfortune.
author: Jindrich Styrsky

text: The sum of his life was a unique melody, hauntingly beautiful and powerful.
author: Ann Marston

text: It was Mina this whole time, wasn’t it?" I give him the only thing I can: the cold, hard truth. The one that’ll rewrite every memory he has - of him and me, her and me, the two of them, all three of us: "It’ll always be Mina.
author: Tess Sharpe

text: You can’t avoid the inevitability of death. It comes at you one way or another, and takes us all to the same place in the end. To apologize for it is to apologize for the sun shining or the rain falling. It is what it is.
author: Catherine Doyle

text: You can’t get the blood out.
author: Steven Ramirez

text: No man kills himself unless there is something wrong with his life.
author: Al Alvarez

text: Every suicide is a solution to a problem.
author: Jean Baechler

text: Most of us are naturally inclined to struggle against the restrictions our friends and family impose upon us, but if we are so unfortunate as to lose a loved one, what a difference then! Then the restriction becomes a sacred trust.
author: Susanna Clarke

text: The menopause of Sarah became her menostart
author: this is feminine beauty! The death plot against Mordecai became his life spring

text: Neighbours complaining about someone’s dog making an awful racket. You could hardly blame the poor beast, its owner had died in her bed at least a fortnight before and there hadn’t been much left of the old girl worth eating.
author: James  Oswald

text: Death had been more pitiful to them than longer life would have been. It had taken the one in the loyalty of love, and the other in the innocence of faith, from a world which for love has no recompense and for faith no fulfillment.
author: Ouida

text: She did not have time to wonder about his being late. He died bent over the sidewalk sign that stood out in front of the hardware store... He had not even had time to get into the store...
author: Alice Munro

text: For as much as I hate the cemetery, I’ve been grateful it’s here, too. I miss my wife. It’s easier to miss her at a cemetery, where she’s never been anything but dead, than to miss her in all the places where she was alive.
author: John Scalzi

text: Time and death sleep side by side.
author: Garth Nix

text: The first time I died, I didn't see God. No light at the end of the tunnel. No haloed angels. No dead grandparents. To be fair, I probably wasn't a solid shoo-in for Heaven. But, honestly, I kind of assumed I'd make the cut.
author: Megan Miranda

text: Please bury me in the library With a dozen long-stemmed proses
author: J. Patrick Lewis

text: Every living thing requires love to survive for without it, we die.
author: Sanjo Jendayi

text: No thought is born in me that does not bear the image of death.
author: Michelangelo Buonarroti

text: Death is not in the nature of things
author: it is the nature of things. But what dies is the form. The matter is immortal.

text: I'm going to enjoy this.”“What?” I asked. “Hell?
author: J.X. Burros

text: Death stalked toward us on padded feet.       And it was not alone.
author: Melika Dannese Lux

text: I didn't want to die. I just found death soothing to think about.
author: Natalie Standiford

text: No amount of wishing will bring back the dead.
author: Natalie Standiford

text: The country people, indeed, did not always clearly distinguish between the Fairies and the dead. They called them both the 'Silent People'; and the Milky Way they thought was the path along which the dead were carried to Fairyland.
author: Hope Mirrlees

text: a penny for my thoughts oh no i'll sell em for a dollar their worth so much more after im a goner and then maybe you'll hear the words ive been singing funny when your dead how people start listening
author: The Band Perry

text: For some death is an art, for others it is merely an inevitability.
author: Sadie S. Forsythe

text: The dead leave their shadows, an echo of the space within which once they lived. They haunt us, never fading or growing older as we do. The loss we grieve is not just their futures but our own.
author: Kate Mosse

text: Sólo yo entiendo lo lejos que está el cielo de nosotros; pero conozco cómo acortar las veredas. Todo consiste en morir, Dios mediante, cuando uno quiera y no cuando Él lo disponga. O, si tú quieres, forzarlo a disponer antes de tiempo.
author: Juan Rulfo

text: His name was Theo.
author: Cinda Williams Chima

text: The back door of every tomb opens on a hilltop.
author: George MacDonald

text: Cure the symptoms, cure the disease.
author: Michael Critchton

text: As she cried, I could feel growing there, as had once before, a presence between us: the tiny perfect form of Sherry nestled between her parents' bodies. Our bodies were shaped by her absence, by the almost unbearable weight of her loss.
author: Robert J. Wiersema

text: When I shut my eyes on this world I'll finally have peace.
author: Kevin  Walker

text: How did it die?" he asked."Short circuit," I said. "Old and frayed wires."He looked at me like I was senile."Could have been disease. Violence. Or, sometimes, things die because we don't love them enough.
author: Jonathan Messinger

text: The crickets still sing in October. And lilly, she's trying to bloom. Tho she's resting her head on the shoulder of death, she still shines by the light of the moon.
author: Kevin Dalton

text: Death is my lover and he wants to move in.
author: Sarah Kane

text: To Suspect your Own Mortality is to Know the Beginning of Terror
author: To Learn Irrefutably that you are mortal is to Know the End of Terror.

text: When I die, I plan to be laughing hysterically.
author: Minari Endou

text: Live now, die later!
author: Stewart Home

text: It's your life, not your death, that defines you.
author: J.J. Brown

text: Cine n-ar dori să moară visând că moare?
author: Sasa Pana

text: It's life. You deal with it or you don't.
author: Pinder Buckley

text: What's it like?""Death? It's like being on holiday with a group of Germans.
author: Rob Grant

text: A fool without fear is sometimes wiser than an angel with fear.
author: Lady Nancy Astor

text: As I remember his laugh, there was nothing mad about it, it was more like the laugh of someone who has been the victim of a practical joke, a farce in which he had believed until suddenly he realized his folly.
author: Guy Sajer

text: Como siempre, la consideración de su muerte lo serenaba tanto como lo turbaba la muerte de los demás. Tal vez porque, a fin de cuentas, su muerte era el final del mundo.
author: Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa

text: Funerals aren't scheduled.
author: Benjamin J. Carey

text: The chanting went on, the musicians giving in to the rhythm of their own being, finding healing in touching that rhythm, and healing in chanting about death, the only real god they knew.
author: Karl Marlantes

text: He'd learned something. Life was booby-trapped and there was no easy passage through. You had to jump from colour to colour, from happiness to happiness. And all those possible explosions in between. It could be all over any time.
author: Rupert Thomson

text: It came to Mary now that her mother had been right, after all; Mary had been born for this. In sixteen years she'd shot along the shortest route she could find between life and death, as the crow flew.
author: Emma Donoghue

text: The thing about oracles is that they talk back.
author: Darin Bradley

text: La verdad, es que en el fondo soy un fatalista. Si a uno le llega la hora, da lo mismo un Boeing que la puntual maceta que se derrumba sobre uno desde un séptimo piso
author: Mario Benedetti

text: Taggle looked up at her, his amber eyes as deep as the loneliness Kate had felt before he became her friend."The traditional thing," he said slowly, "involves the river and a sack.
author: Erin Bow

text: Dying's a fearful popular activity these days so we often double 'em up.
author: John Marsden

text: As if the dead really do persist, even in a bottle of wine.
author: Thomas Pynchon

text: Fife... simply walked off by himself, into the jungle to look at all the things which would continue to exist after he had ceased to. There were a lot of them. Fife looked at them all. They remained singularly unchanged by his scrutiny.
author: James  Jones

text: Mostly I couldn't bear... the paltry notion that memory was all that eternal life really meant, and I spent too much time wondering where people got the fortitude or delusion to keep on moving past the static dead.
author: Gail Caldwell

text: When will the deathOf Death ever come?from "The Siege
author: Visar Zhiti

text: May be able to die those who didn't know how to live
author: Manuel Maria Barbosa du Bocage

text: I want you to tell all these people that I wanted more time to spend with them. Tell them I meant to, tell them I wanted to hear what they said and tell them what was on my mind.
author: Kage Baker

text: I should not really object to dying were it not followed by death.
author: Thomas Nagel

text: Life versus Death becomes, as Montaigne pointed out, Old Age versus Death.
author: Julian Barnes

text: As he walked, the sad faded leaves were driven pitilessly around him by the wind, and under the mingling influences of autumn and evening, a craving for the quietude of the grave … overtook him with unwanted intensity.
author: Georges Rodenbach

text: Life is short, death is forever.
author: Marsha Qualey

text: Curiosity is a good thing, like onion soup. But too much onion soup makes your breath smell terrible. And too much curiosity can make your whole body smell terrible, if it causes you to be dead.
author: Michael Reisman

text: Only fools insist upon life at any cost.... Others would say that life may be laid down when it becomes too heavy. Where does it go, after all, but into the keeping of the Powers who gave it and will give it once again?
author: Sheri S. Tepper

text: I think about death sometimes. Analytically, of course.
author: Lynne Truss

text: I know I should look away, let him grieve in private, but I cannot. The sweet girl that I knew only briefly is the key to this gentle beast who has captured my heart.
author: Robin LaFevers

text: Life makes beggars out of those who have joyful hearts, taxing the living with hardship and tribulation, but the charity of companionship, the currency of shared and unmitigated love, alleviates all disconsolation.
