text: I not afraid of dying, I'm afraid of suffering
author: Raheem Kirlew

text: Let's go to Valhalla with the sun on our faces.
author: Mark  Lawrence

text: Don't call it death
author: call it moving to Heaven.

text: He wasn't supposed to die,' he cried out, somewhat desperately, petulantly, like a spoiled child. But I could hear other thoughts racing between us.Neither are you.Neither am I.
author: Patti Smith

text: As he walked along the runway, he came upon a United Airlines pilot. “He tried to sit up,” Martz said. “I saw a huge triangular hole in his forehead and I told him to just lie still and that help was on the way, but it was too late for him.
author: Laurence Gonzales

text: This is how most stories end in the hospital. Not with crash carts and sirens and electric shocks to the chest, but with an empty room, a crisp white bed, silence.
author: Jacob M. Appel

text: Not every loss was confirmed by an officer at the door. Nor a telegram with the power to sink a fleet. Loss, often the worst kind, also arrived through the deafening quiet of an absence.
author: Kristina McMorris

text: Carmen will always be free.
author: Prosper Merimee

text: Tombstones covered the dale, the smooth marble surfaces bright. She had spent days here as a teenager, though not out of any awareness of mortality. Like every adolescent, she intended to live forever.
author: Thomm Quackenbush

text: Strangest problems of life seem clearing
author: but clouds sweep between--Is my journey’s end coming?

text: I have faced death this night, and I have called his bluff.
author: Lucien LaCroix

text: Jango danced
author: and the tune that he danced to was Death.

text: Time owed an alliance to the undying.
author: Thomm Quackenbush

text: If you Have ever Fainted, You Are a stranger in the land of the dead!
author: Antoni Omeihe

text: Only the dead has seen th end of war
author: Mirza Tabish

text: Maybe one did have to be smart in order to kill.
author: Markelle Grabo

text: One day u will be the message...a message for those who still alive...
author: Fayrouz Yasser

text: You can't make me do nothing but die!
author: Richard Wright

text: Death is an endless night so awful to contemplate that it can make us love life and value it with such passion that it may be the ultimate cause of all joy and all art.
author: Paul Theroux

text: ...it does not matter if we are forgotten; what matters is the effect we have on those around us and those who come after us. What matters is how our own lives affect the larger, perpetual community of the living.
author: Lynn Schooler

text: We must learn to accept ourselves in the painful experiment of living. We must embrace the spiritual adventure of becoming human, moving through the many stages that lie between birth and death.
author: Johann Baptist Metz

text: This isolation, this deep pit in the bottom of my stomach, didn't have to hurt so much." - Aquamarine Rosabelle bonus included in Dreams, Smiles, and Bloody Tears
author: Chamera Sampson

text: There are far more reasons for death than there are for life.
author: Sergei Lukyanenko

text: Adam pressed his hand to his face. Sighed. "Right. It's just that… He died. And I'm so freaking pissed off, I swear I'd punch him in the face if he were standing right here.
author: Kristina McBride

text: Do the thing you love to do. Hank Williams died at the ripe old age of twentynine. Stevie Ray Vaughan at thirty-five. Jesus at thirtythree. Don’t think you’re special and the Lord’s gonna bless you with time.
author: Jill S. Alexander

text: The only way to find out if you're immortal is to make it to the end of time and look around to see if you're still alive. Until you've done that, all you know is that you haven't died yet.
author: John Patrick Lowrie

text: When I lived here and woke up from the fog in my head, I would walk by myself to the grave site set aside for me, so that I could feel comfortable if I lived there after death.
author: Kyung-Sook Shin

text: I was no longer troubled when he pulled out a machete in a crowded bar, tried to pick up schoolgirls, or threatened to scalp us, then rip off our heads and scoop out our brains.
author: Tahir Shah

text: I looked at him on the bed. He coughed once and a trail of brownish dead blood came out of his mouth and ran down the side of his chin. Then he stopped breathing. And I thought, I'll make sure I never end up here, either.
author: Sebastian Faulks

text: She had been born with a different name, to a woman with laughing eyes and warmly whispered words of love who’d died degraded and afraid on a misty Irish morning.
author: C.S. Harris

text: I have read of the great wars of ages past, and men slaughtered by the tens of thousands. And we give but fleeting consideration to their deaths, for it is our nature to banish such thoughts.
author: Seth Grahame-Smith

text: Put a man on the brink of the abyss and - in the unlikely event that she doesn't fall into it - he will become a mystic or a madman... Which is probably the same thing!
author: Apostolos Doxiadis

text: A dead man is the worst enemy alive, I thought. You can't alter his power over you. You can't alter what you love or owe. And it's too late to ask him for his absolution. He has beaten you all ways.
author: John le Carre

text: That which is alive hath known death, and that which is dead can never die, for in the Circle of the Spirit life is naught and death is naught. Yea, all things live forever, though at times they sleep and are forgotten.
author: H. Rider Haggard

text: why we fear death... life is more scary.....
author: Raheel Habib

text: There's a bug inside you. Growing in you, eating everything you've forced yourself to forget, everything you don't want to think about. Your bug will decide your fate one day. And... chances are, you will die because of it.
author: Kouhei Kadono

text: Iam not scared of death because I don'twant to live this useless life.
author: Raffid

text: There was a pretty young woman I used to see pegging out sheets and I worried that she would grow old there and that no one would know how beautiful she was. And maybe she would die without ever having really lived.
author: Sebastian Faulks

text: It is said that men condemned to death are subject to sudden moments of elation; as if, like moths in the fire, their destruction were coincidental with attainment.
author: John le Carre

text: Who embalms the Undertaker when he dies?
author: Alison Bechdel

text: I once feared death. It is said that death begins with the absence of life. And life begins when death is no longer feared. I have stared death in the eye and survived.
author: Cheryl Kaye Tardif

text: His will to live was waning, and it made him almost transparent, as though rather than dying, he might just disappear one day, leaving behind only a vague scent of regret.
author: Ian Morgan Cron

text: What a curious power words have.
author: Tadeusz Borowski

text: In quoting others, we cite ourselves.
author: Julio Cortazar

text: ...we're dealing with two devils who both want to rule hell.
author: Ruta Sepetys

text: Though mountains melt and oceans burn,The gifts of love shall still return.
author: Rosamund Hodge

text: He can’t take his eyes off the stars, but I can’t take mine off his face.
author: Amie Kaufman

text: I hope to arrive at my death, late, in love, and a little drunk.
author: Atticus Poetry

text: Each day is a new opportunity. I chose to make this day a great one.
author: Louise L. Hay

text: The moment you start arguing with an ignorant fool, you have already lost.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: There is an ocean of silence between us… and I am drowning in it.
author: Ranata Suzuki

text: Which came first — the observer or the particle?
author: Vanna Bonta

text: Your life is a movie. You are the main character. You say your scripts and act to your lines. Of course you do your lines in each scene. There is a hidden camera and a director who you can ask for help anytime up above.
author: Diana Rose Morcilla

text: Sing me a love song in a slow, southern drawl to the tune of sunny days...
author: Kellie Elmore

text: The last time I felt alive – I was looking into your eyes.Breathing your air…. touching your skin…… Saying goodbye….The last time I felt alive…. I was dying.
author: Ranata Suzuki

text: She will blaze through you like a gypsy wildfire. Igniting you soul and dancing in its flames. And when she is gone, the smell of her smoke will be the only thing left to soothe you.
author: Nicole  Lyons

text: He was both everything I could ever want…And nothing I could ever have…
author: Ranata Suzuki

text: I think perhaps I will always hold a candle for you – even until it burns my hand.And when the light has long since gone …. I will be there in the darkness holding what remains, quite simply because I cannot let go.
author: Ranata Suzuki

text: She knew there were only small joys in life--the big ones were too complicated to be joys when you got all through--and once you realized that, it took a lot of the pressure off.
author: Lorrie Moore

text: You do not exist to serve the illusion. The illusion exists to serve you.
author: Lauren Zimmerman

text: I think, to be happy is to be interested and engaged.
author: Aimee Mann

text: God created all things. It's men who decide which ones are mistakes.
author: Beautiful Creatures

text: Poetry is breathing words that gives a reader pause.
author: Ankita Singhal

text: My toes are going to come,"-MariannThe Night Owl
author: Emma Holly

text: If not in the moment, where do you propose to live?
author: Na'ama Yehuda

text: Truth is irrelevant
author: what matters is what people believe.

text: If you want something done, you'll have to dream it yourself.
author: Corey Aaron Burkes

text: You can't Google life
author: Colin Tegerdine

text: The biggest cause of divorse in my opinion is marriage
author: Paul V. Walters

text: Writers don't get mad they get even in their novels.
author: Candace C. Bowen

text: Love is relentless, and so am I ;)
author: Keisha Keenleyside

text: Change the world, I know I won’t,Enthralling as always I hope it remains,A kaleidoscope of joy, sorrow and pain.But my only wish as I take this jaunt,Is for my words on you to impress upon,A smile, a tear or even an angry frown.
author: Anurag Anand

text: Maybe love and pain are synonyms.
author: Vanshika Dhyani

text: I was feeling rational and restless, which is horrible for watching movies
author: Sinclair Lewis

text: I think all artists struggle to represent the geometryof life in their own way, just like writers deal witharchetypes. There are only so many stories that you cantell, but an infinite number of storytellers.
author: Henry Mosquera

text: Balanced emotions are crucial to intuitive decision making.
author: Michael D. Eisner

text: There wasn't an anhydrous lacrimal gland in the room...
author: Mary Roach

text: If time is money and you wasted my time, then give me back my money!
author: Ljupka Cvetanova

text: A genius is a lunatic who made it!
author: Steve K. Smy

text: Dont regret that it just got over, but instead Move on for More!
author: Steven Singh Babbar

text: Be the winner in your life!
author: Tae Yun Kim

text: You will not do incredible things without an incredible dream.
author: John Eliot

text: Life makes fools of us all sooner or later. But keep your sense of humor and you'll at least be able to take your humiliations with some measure of grace. In the end, you know, it's our own expectations that crush us." -- from Skippy Dies
author: Paul Murray

text: The great correspondent of the seventeenth century Madame de Sevigne counseled, "Take chocolate in order that even the most tireome company seem acceptable to you," which is also sound advice today!
author: Barrie Kerper

text: People who believe they have bad luck create bad luck. Those who believe they are very fortunate, that the world is a generous place filled with trustworthy people, live in exactly that kind of world.
author: Chris Prentiss

text: She's an old soul with young eyes, a vintage heart, and a beautiful mind.
author: Nicole  Lyons

text: Famous people steal my quotes all of the time without knowing
author: none of it is ever very interesting though.

text: If you have to choose between any two, first choose time to think.
author: Mohith Agadi

text: So I don’t think I’ll make Poet Laureate,but I swear I’m not twisted and bitter,If finely-wrought talentsdon’t weigh in the balance,I can always write haiku on Twitter.
author: Rosy Cole

text: There’s no better way to get to know a city than to walk its streets. A place will reveal its soul through its sights, sounds and smells, and eventually, it’ll teach you its rhythm.
author: Henry Mosquera

text: Stories You Can Feel!
author: Corey Aaron Burkes

text: People that love quotes are those short on time, but long on big ideas.
author: Bruce Edwin

text: My father once told me that it’s not enough for a man to be lucky
author: that a guy has to know when that streak is on for him.

text: I love when people quote me. It makes me feel that my words have meaning. People give words power. When they speak out for things they feel, we all become alive.
author: Jennifer Megan Varnadore

text: Sleep is death enjoyed.
author: Friedrich Hebbel

text: At the end to death I must go,Finishing a real tragic and funny show.
author: Mohit K. Misra

text: Só a quero porque não a posso ter.Como um rapaz imberbe
author: Madeline Hunter

text: Where have all the Fembots gone?
author: Ren Garcia

text: Anybody can fail.
author: M.G. Hardie

text: They are ever-changing. They are unlike immortals who stay young and beautiful forever—who have time under their control. Humans, they only have a few years to live, and yet there is beauty in the way time controls them.
author: Kaye Allen

text: Gentle whispers from above Awakened me more Than ever did This loud world
author: Holly Ducarte

text: Feels so good to be quoted
author: Malebo Sephodi

text: Sunshine brings a new day,moonlight ends the harsh light.
author: Cara Beatrice

text: Ko kiti priplavajo v vaše srce, ga nikoli več ne zapustijo.
author: Ziva Vivianaia Dor

text: You can't break the broken.
author: Margarete Cassalina

text: No matter how far I go, I'm still your person. We stand together now.
author: Kendare Blake

text: What does life give me in the end but sorrow?What do love's good and evil send but sorrow?I've only seen one true companion - pain,And I have known no faithful friend but sorrow.
author: Hafez

text: Peace within us starts when we learn to forgive and let go
author: Mimi Novic

text: Inside the scrawny adolescent was a soul that defied extinction.
author: Yen

text: Don't aim for perfection. Aim for 'better than yesterday'.
author: Izey Victoria Odiase

text: ...This wasn’t just Kylie. This was the woman she had chosen above every other person in the world. This woman was the most important person in her universe, and she was determined to show her how true that was.
author: Susan X. Meagher

text: Like Cupid,your promiseswere just misses.
author: Timothy Joshua

text: Everyone has a story to tell, so why not let me put it on paper!
author: Maija Monreaux

text: You will meet great beings when you come out of the well and see the world.
author: Mohith Agadi

text: An intelligent investor sees an opportunity in dip than risk.
author: Mohith Agadi

text: You will hear thunder and remember me, And think: she wanted storms. The rim Of the sky will be the colour of hard crimson, And your heart, as it was then, will be on fire.
author: Anna Akhmatova

text: So sweet and delicious do I become,when I am in bed with a manwho, I sense, loves and enjoys me,that the pleasure I bring excels all delight,so the knot of love, however tightit seemed before, is tied tighter still.
author: Veronica Franco

text: Nobody wanted your dance,Nobody wanted your strange glitter, your flounderingDrowning life and your effort to save yourself,Treading water, dancing the dark turmoil,Looking for something to give.
author: Ted Hughes

text: We sit and talk,quietly, with long lapses of silenceand I am aware of the streamthat has no language, coursingbeneath the quiet heaven ofyour eyeswhich has no speech
author: William Carlos Williams

text: A poem begins with a lump in the throat
author: a homesickness or a love sickness. It is a reaching-out toward expression

text: HereI'm here-the snow falling.
author: Kobayashi Issa

text: My eyes are vague blue, like the sky, and change all the time; they are indiscriminate but fleeting, entirely specific and disloyal, so that no one trusts me. I am always looking away. Or again at something after it has given me up.
author: Frank O'Hara

text: The purpose of poetry is to remind ushow difficult it is to remain just one person,for our house is open, there are no keys in the doors, and invisible guests come in and out at will.
author: Czesław Miłosz

text: Perfumes are the feelings of flowers.
author: Heinrich Heine

text: I am God, la de dah.
author: Anne Sexton

text: But my heart is an old house(the kind my mothergrew up in)hell to heat and cooland faulty in the wiringand though it’s nice to look atI have no businessinviting lovers in.
author: Clementine von Radics

text: So here is my story, may it bringSome smiles and a tear or so,It happened once upon a time,Far away, and long ago,Outside the night wind keens and wails,Come listen to me, the Teller of Tales!
author: Brian Jacques

text: We live in an old chaos of the sun.
author: Wallace Stevens

text: I walked a mile with Pleasure;She chatted all the way;But left me none the wiserFor all she had to say.I walked a mile with Sorrow;And ne’er a word said she;But, oh! The things I learned from her,When Sorrow walked with me.
author: Robert Browning Hamilton

text: He could build a city. Has a certain capacity. There’s a niche in his chestwhere a heart would fit perfectlyand he thinks if he could just maneuver one into place –well then, game over.
author: Richard Siken

text: . . . All artists’ work is autobiographical. Any writer’s work is a map of their psyche. You can really see what their concerns are, what their obsessions are, and what interests them.
author: Kim Addonizio

text: I see all of us reading ourselves away from ourselves,  straining in circles of light to find more light until the line of words becomes a trail of crumbs that we follow across a page of fresh snow
author: Billy Collins

text: I'm at that place I grew up to leave.
author: Adrian C. Louis

text: Does something which exists on the edge have no true relevance to the stable center, or does it, by being on the edge, become a part of the edge and thus a part of the boundary, the definition which gives the whole its shape?
author: Lucy Grealy

text: McGough: I'm sorry. I'm afraid I've caught poetry. Mr Bones: Oh really? Well, don't worry, sir - I used to suffer from short stories.McGough: Really? When?Mr Bones: Oh, once upon a time ...
author: Graham Chapman

text: This is perhaps the most noble aim of poetry, to attach ourselves to the world around us, to turn desire into love, to embrace, finally what always evades us, what is beyond, but what is always there – the unspoken, the spirit, the soul.
author: Octavio Paz

text: You should always be trying to write a poem you are unable to write, a poem you lack the technique, the language, the courage to achieve. Otherwise you're merely imitating yourself, going nowhere, because that's always easiest.
author: John Berryman

text: Sing your life
author: any fool can think of words that rhyme.

text: Valentine's Day is the poet's holiday.
author: Ted Kooser

text: If it ain't a pleasure, it ain't a poem.
author: William Carlos Williams

text: I'll say I love you,Which will lead, of course,to disappointment,but those words unsaidpoison every next moment.I will try to disappoint youbetter than anyone else has.
author: Stephen Dunn

text: Poetry is life distilled.
author: Gwendolyn Brooks

text: If you cannot be a poet, be the poem.
author: David Carradine

text: The night has a thousand eyes,And the day but one; Yet the light of the bright world dies With the dying sun. The mind has a thousand eyes, And the heart but one: Yet the light of a whole life dies When love is done.
author: Francis William Bourdillon

text: But some nights, I must tell you,I go down there after everyone has fallen asleep.I swim back and forth in the echoing blackness.I sing a love song as well as I can,lost for a while in the home of the rain.
author: Billy Collins

text: No one wants to read poetry. You have to make it impossible for them to put the poem down--impossible for them to stop reading it, word after word. You have to keep them from closing the book.
author: Muriel Rukeyser

text: ...you look at me like an emergency
author: Adrienne Rich

text: All I know is a door into the dark
author: Seamus Heaney

text: I've got mixed feelings about poetry cause done well poetry is fantastic. But not many people are capable of doing it well. I think you should have some kind of license to perform poetry. A poetic license perhaps.
author: Craig Ferguson

text: If I am more alive because love burns and chars me,as a fire, given wood or wind, feels new elation,it's that he who lays me low is my salvation,and invigorates the more, the more he scars me.
author: Michelangelo Buonarroti

text: Hinged to forgetfulness like a door,she slowly closed out of sight,and she was the woman I loved,but too many times she slept likea mechanical deer in my caresses,and I ached in the metal silenceof her dreams.
author: Richard Brautigan

text: Love shall be our token
author: love be yours and love be mine.

text: MISERABLERelease the toxic and infectious-Spreaders of misery,Souls destroying souls-And poisonous liars.Awaken from the hallucinations-And take back your heart.Reclaim your self-esteem-And leave the toxic be.
author: Giorge Leedy

text: Rather than words comes the thought of high windows:The sun-comprehending glass,And beyond it, the deep blue air, that showsNothing, and is nowhere, and is endless.
author: Philip Larkin

text: sekali berarti sesudah itu mati
author: Chairil Anwar

text: And medecine, law, business, engineering, these are noble pursuits and necessary to sustain life. But poetry, beauty, romance, love: these are what we stay alive for.
author: Tom Schulman

text: For books are more than books, they are the lifeThe very heart and core of ages past,The reason why men lived and worked and died,The essence and quintessence of their lives.
author: Amy Lowell

text: Nothing is more natural than mutual misunderstanding; the contrary is always surprising. I believe that one never agrees on anything except by mistake, and that all harmony among human beings is the happy fruit of an error.
author: Paul Valery

text: I am a work in progress.
author: Violet Yates

text: When no one is looking,I swallow deserts and cloudsand chew on mountainsknowing they are sweet bones!When no one is lookingand I want to kiss God,I just lift my own hand to my mouth.
author: The Rubaiyat of Hafiz

text: I think we are in rats’ alley
author: Where the dead men lost their bones.

text: Free verse is like free love
author: it is a contradiction in terms.

text: Who you are contributes to your poetry in a number of important ways, but you shouldn't identify with your poems so closely that when they are cut, you're the one that bleeds.
author: Dorianne Laux

text: Poetry is a rich, full-bodied whistle, cracked ice crunching in pails, the night that numbs the leaf, the duel of two nightingales, the sweet pea that has run wild, Creation's tears in shoulder blades.
author: Boris Pasternak

text: The true poem rests between the words.
author: Vanna Bonta

text: True and false fears let us refrain, Let us love nobly, and live, and add again Years and years unto years, till we attain To write threescore: this is the second of our reign.
author: John Donne

text: The poet doesn't invent. He listens.
author: Jean Cocteau

text: A black cat among roses,phlox, lilac-misted under a quarter moon,the sweet smells of heliotrope and night-scented stock. The garden is very still.It is dazed with moonlight,contented with perfume...
author: Amy Lowell

text: The inmost spirit of poetry, in other words, is at bottom, in every recorded case, the voice of pain – and the physical body, so to speak, of poetry, is the treatment by which the poet tries to reconcile that pain with the world.
author: Ted Hughes

text: Every poem is a coat of arms. It must be deciphered. How much blood, how many tears in exchange for these axes, these muzzles, these unicorns, these torches, these towers, these martlets, these seedlings of stars and these fields of blue!
author: Jean Cocteau

text: Failure: the renewable resource.
author: Kay Ryan

text: If you can not be a poet, be the poem.
author: David Carradine

text: Poetry is prose in slow motion.
author: Nicholson Baker

text: Well, now,if little by little you stop loving meI shall stop loving you little by little.If suddenlyyou forget medo not look for me,for I shall already have forgotten you.
author: Pable Neruda

text: I am too full of lifeto be half-loved.
author: Ijeoma Umebinyuo

text: Into my heart an air that killsFrom yon far country blows:What are those blue remembered hills,What spires, what farms are those?That is the land of lost content,I see it shining plain,The happy highways where I wentAnd cannot come again.
author: A.E. Housman

text: The JewelThere is this caveIn the air behind my bodyThat nobody is going to touch:A cloister, a silenceClosing around a blossom of fire.When I stand upright in the wind,My bones turn to dark emeralds.
author: James Wright

text: Love me in actions, not in words.
author: Dee Dee M. Scott

text: Armed I am with love. Disarmed I am.
author: Manuel Alegre

text: When composing a verse let there not be a hair's breath separating your mind from what you write; composition of a poem must be done in an instant, like a woodcutter felling a huge tree or a swordsman leaping at a dangerous enemy.
author: Basho Matsuo

text: Desire, loneliness, wind in the flowering almond—surely these are the great, the inexhaustible subjectsto which my predecessors apprenticed themselves.I hear them echo in my own heart, disguised as convention.
author: Louise Gluck

text: A door jumpsout from shadows,then jumps away. Thisis what I've come to find:the back door, unlatched.Tooled by insular wind, itslams and slamswithout meaningto and without meaning.
author: Li-Young Lee

text: Dear to me is sleep: still more, being made of stone,While pain and guilt still linger here below,Blindness and numbness--these please me alone;Then do not wake me, keep your voices low.
author: Michelangelo Buonarroti

text: loneliness can fly a helicopter through a cut-out shapeof a helicopter the same size as the helicopterand that's it's only skilland it isn't good enoughbut it's still amazing.
author: Tao Lin

text: Balm of the summer night, balm of the ordinary,imperial joy and sorrow of human existence,the dreamed as well as the lived—what could be dearer than this, given the closeness of death?
author: Louise Gluck

text: Silver hidden in the gold,Young man hidden in the old,Laughing lord with weeping eyes,Bring king and ring before sunrise! -Hilarion, The Great and Terrible Quest
author: Margaret Lovett

text: Women Are Not RosesWomen have no beginningonly continualflows.Though rivers flowwomen are notrivers.Women are notrosesthey are not oceansor stars.i would like to tellher this buti think shealready knows.
author: Ana Castillo

text: People with yuan fen are destined to like one another;Friendship develops even if a thousand miles apart.But should yuan fen be absent between two individuals,They will remain strangers despite sitting face-to-face
author: Adeline Yen Mah

text: I offer you what I have myPoverty
author: W.S. Merwin

text: No one here likes a wet dog.
author: Billy Collins

text: Prose: words in their best order
author: poetry: the best words in the best order.

text: World is suddener than we fancy it.
author: Louis MacNeice

text: There's in my mind a...turbulent moon-ridden girlor old woman, or both,dressed in opals and rags, feathersand torn taffeta,who knows strange songsbut she is not kind.
author: Denise Levertov

text: Sweet is the lore which nature brings
author: Our meddling intellectMisshapes the beauteous forms of things

text: Words are what sticks to the real. We use them to push the real, to drag the real into the poem. They are what we hold on with, nothing else. They are as valuable in themselves as rope with nothing to be tied to.
author: Jack Spicer

text: These wrinkles are nothingThese gray hairs are nothing,This stomach which sagswith old food, these bruisedand swollen ankles, my darkening brain,they are nothing.I am the same boymy mother used to kiss.
author: Mark Strand

text: Well in case you failed to notice,In case you failed to see,This is my heart bleeding before you,This is me down on my kneesThese foolish games are tearing me apartYour thoughtless words are breaking my heartYou're breaking my heart
author: Jewel

text: Maybe you're one of those people who writes poems, but rarely reads them. Let me put this as delicately as I can: If you don't read, your writing is going to suck.
author: Kim Addonizio

text: For all the ghosts and corpses that shall never know the breath of our childrenso longfor the sacrifice and endurance of our mothers and the sustained breath of our fatherswe live
author: Saul Williams

text: I can’t help but notice that you keep writing love poetry to my wife. Well, you see, I married her, which makes her my wife. You know what you might want to try? Writing some poems about the sunset. The sunset isn’t fucking married.
author: A.J. Jacobs

text: THERE is something in the autumn that is native to my blood—	 Touch of manner, hint of mood;	 And my heart is like a rhyme,	 With the yellow and the purple and the crimson keeping time.
author: Bliss Carman

text: Had we but world enough, and time
author: Andrew Marvell

text: A poem should not meanBut be.
author: Archibald MacLeish

text: Love WasLove Will BeBut Most of All,Love is.Life Cannot Be Without ItIt is found in the WombIn The WoodsIn The Stars.To Be or Not to BeTo Love, or not to LoveThey Are Equal.My Soul Whispers Into the Spaces.Yes.
author: Cindy Martinusen Coloma

text: Life is a poem most people never read.
author: Laurence Overmire

text: Try to be thoughtful, don't make the poor man say it;see how human he is,he has children of his own,it is your job to ask:And now he can never not nod.And now he can never say no.And now he can never not say.
author: Kathleen Sheeder Bonanno

text: Freud thought that a psychosis was a waking dream, and that poets were daydreamers too, but I wonder if the reverse is not as often true, and that madness is a fiction lived in like a rented house
author: William H. Gass

text: At five in the afternoon. It was exactly five in the afternoon. A boy brought the white sheet at five in the afternoon. A frail of lime ready prepared at five in the afternoon. The rest was death, and death alone
author: Federico Garcia Lorca

text: Give a poet a pen
author: A. Jarrell Hayes

text: E andando nel sole che abbagliasentire con triste meravigliacom'è tutta la vita e il suo travaglioin questo seguitare una muragliache ha in cima cocci aguzzi di bottiglia.
author: Eugenio Montale

text: Almost none of the poetries I admire stick to their labels, native or adopted ones. Rather, they are vagrant in their identifications. Tramp poets, there you go, a new label for those with unstable allegiances.
author: C.D. Wright

text: I think here I will leave you. It has come to seemthere is no perfect ending.Indeed, there are infinite endings.Or perhaps, once one begins,there are only endings.
author: Louise Gluck

text: I don’t need your praiseto survive. I was here first, before you were here, beforeyou ever planted a garden.And I’ll be here when only the sun and moonare left, and the sea, and the wide field.I will constitute the field.
author: Louise Gluck

text: In a pine tree,A few yards away from my window sill,A brilliant blue jay is springing up and down, up and down,On a branch.I laugh, as I see him abandon himselfTo entire delight, for he knows as well as I doThat the branch will not break.
author: James Wright

text: I will meet you on the nape of your neck one day, on the surface of intention, word becoming act.We will breathe into each other the high mountain tales, where the snows come from, where the waters begin.”-In the yellow time of pollen
author: Luke Davies

text: Only poetry isn't shit.
author: Roberto Bolano

text: All a poet can do today is warn.
author: Wilfred Owen

text: Poems are difficult to silence.
author: Stephen Greenblatt

text: ... imaginary gardens with real toads in them ...... if you demand on one hand,the raw material of poetry inall its rawness andthat which is on the other handgenuine, then you are interested in poetry.
author: Marianne Moore

text: Writing poetry is talking to oneself; yet it is a mode of talking to oneself in which the self disappears; and the product's something that, though it may not be for everybody, is about everybody.
author: Richard Wilbur

text: The pure playfulness of certain wholly whimsical portions of (Charles) Cros’s work should not obscure the fact that at the center of some of his most beautiful poems a revolver is leveled straight at us.
author: Andre Breton

text: The fact that Iam writing to youin Englishalready falsifies what Iwanted to tell you.My subject:how to explain to youthat I don't belong to Englishthough I belong nowhere else,if not herein English.
author: Gustavo  Perez Firmat

text: There is such a shelter in each other.
author: Nick Laird

text: That's what I want, that kind of recklessness where the poem is even ahead of you. It's like riding a horse that's a little too wild for you, so there's this tension between what you can do and what the horse decides it's going to do.
author: Li-Young Lee

text: A poet could kill the dead.
author: Cameron Conaway

text: You can make a difference in another person's life and not realize it, just by giving them One Moment of your time, One Memory to recall, One Motion that tells them they are not alone! OM!
author: Deb Simpson

text: So very lovely His blood on her swollen lips His first vampireSo very lovely He would have to remember Each salacious cutHe took her slowly Bled her of secrets and screams He smiled contemplating That vampires bled just like whores.
author: Wrath James White

text: Every poet... finds himself born in the midst of prose. He has to struggle from the littleness and obstruction of an actual world into the freedom and infinitude of an ideal.
author: Thomas Carlyle

text: We must listen to poets.
author: Gaston Bachelard

text: But neither money nor machines can create. They shuttle tokens of energy, but they do not transform. A civilization based on them puts people out of touch with their creative powers.
author: Lewis Hyde

text: Enjoy yourselves. And Hap: Don't let Umber near the arrows and bows; he's liable to shoot himself in the nose." Dodd grinned and snapped the reins, and the carriage rolled away. Umber sniffed. "One of his lesser poems. Come, Hap.
author: P.W. Catanese

text: Poetry is the language of the soul;Poetic Prose, the language of my heart.Each line must flow as in a song,and strike a chord that rings forever.To me, words are music!
author: Lori R. Lopez

text: Everything is nothing.
author: Azaam Yahoo

text: Wherever we go we do harm, forgivingourselves as wheels do cement for wearingeach other out. We set this houseon fire, forgetting that we live within.(from "To a Meadowlark," for M.L. Smoker)
author: Jim Harrison

text: A violent order is disorder
author: and a great disorder is an order.These two things are one.

text: By the craggy hill-side,Through the mosses bare,They have planted thorn-treesFor pleasure here and there.If any man so daringAs dig them up in spite,He shall find their sharpest thornsIn his bed at night.
author: William Allingham

text: What do I care, in the dreams and the languor of spring,That my songs do not show me at all?For they are a fragrance, and I am a flint and a fire,I am an answer, they are only a call
author: Sara Teasdale

text: Slogans are mere wordsuck...
author: Gabriel Thy

text: I do not write poetry
author: I take words and dip them in feelings.

text: How blest am I in this discovering thee!To enter in these bonds is to be free;Then where my hand is set, my seal shall be. Full nakedness! All joys are due to thee,As souls unbodied, bodies unclothed must be
author: John Donne

text: I wanted to write some words you'd remember.Words so alert they'd leap from the paper,crawl up your shoulder, lie by your ears,and purr themselves to you like baby kittens,but it was rainy, so I laid there and daydreamed about you.
author: C.L. Foster

text: Un día habré dormido con un sueño tan largo que ni tus besos puedan avivar el letargo. Un día estaré sola, como está la montaña entre el largo desierto y la mar que la baña.
author: Alfonsina Storni

text: Her belly ruptures full of parasites, Her eyes sink back in her skull Her butchered wrists, dangle From the edge of the bathtubHer children cuddle against her Desperate for love she cannot give
author: Wrath James White

text: I’ve been here before, dreaming myselfbackwards, among grappling hooks of light.True to the seasons, I’ve lived every wordspoken. Did I walk into someone’s nightmare?
author: Yusef Komunyakaa

text: Historical sense and poetic sense should not, in the end, be contradictory, for if poetry is the little myth we make, history is the big myth we live, and in our living, constantly remake.
author: Robert Penn Warren

text: An anaesthetic is a poet-killer.
author: Lewis Hyde

text: Impatience kills quickly.
author: Katerina Stoykova Klemer

text: A word is elegy to what it signifies.
author: Robert Haas

text: Even ivory towers need central heating.
author: Breyten Breytenbach

text: Don't be afraid of poetry.
author: Clifton Fadiman

text: Had we but world enough, and time...
author: Andrew Marvell

text: Hate flows from a broken spirit.
author: Kevin  Walker

text: Live for everything, or die for nothing
author: Nate Spears

text: breathe in experience breathe out poetry
author: Muriel Rukeyser

text: We are spirits clad in veils.
author: Christopher Pearse Cranch

text: Poetry is an orphan of silence.
author: Charles Simic

text: Poetry is the sound of the human animal.
author: Suniti Namjoshi

text: Some women marry houses.
author: Anne Sexton

text: poems open doors
author: that don't even exist.

text: Faith is the poetry of our dreams
author: action is the builder of our reality.

text: say what you mean and mean what you say
author: Angel Silva

text: Dying is a universe of its own.
author: Arlene Ang

text: In summer the empire of insects spreads.
author: Adam Zagajewski

text: If you can't beat em: Poem.
author: David  Rowe

text: LIFE IS SUBVERSIVE
author: Ernesto Cardenal

text: Yo, múltiple, como en contradicción
author: Julia de Burgos

text: A versifier passes through the sound
author: sounds go through a poet.

text: Love doesn’t make you a poet
author: it makes you poetry.

text: I don’t need the facts. I’m a Pisces.
author: Phil Volatile

text: A mí me ha tocado no estar contigo
author: no tengo miradas para encontrarteni hay cosa en que pueda reconocerte.

text: Memory revises me.
author: Li-Young Lee

text: This is life...by all means necessary
author: Natasha Head

text: We are all poets, really.
author: Walter Lowenfels

text: Poetry is not an art, it's a symptom.
author: Michele Brenton

text: Stars are shining, my dreams too
author: لطيفة الحاج

text: The faster you go, the idler you get.
author: Ferreira Gullar

text: Literature is news which stays news.
author: Ezra Pound

text: You say you are a poet of law
author: I saw you are a contradiction in terms. I only wonder there were not comets and earthquakes on the night you appeared in this garden

text: I didn't know how to hold my lips.
author: Lesle Lewis

text: Neither pathway is correct.
author: Azaam Yahoo

text: Do not be afraid
author: our fateCannot be taken from us

text: Doubt isn't the opposite of faith
author: it is an element of faith.

text: Leap, and the net will appear.
author: Julia Cameron

text: We're stronger than we think.
author: Joe Kelly

text: Love only fails when we fail to love.
author: Tyler Edwards

text: Bombs have kill-radiuses
author: churches should have love-radiuses--anyone within twenty miles of a church should know it.

text: Faith is simply whatever is real to us.
author: Elizabeth Kostova

text: Grace saved my life
author: it can revolutionize yours.

text: When has faith ever been about feelings?
author: Camy Tang

text: The only sweeter than union is reunion.
author: Kathleen McGowan

text: Logic cannot comprehend love
author: so much the worse for logic.

text: There are no atheists in the foxhole.
author: Ernie Pyle

text: Stories are verbal acts of hospitality.
author: Eugene H. Peterson

text: Faith is a question of eyesight
author: even the blind can see that.

text: Logic only tells us what's there
author: it can't really address what isn't. Even the most devoted empiricist must admit that we have no hope of understanding the universe. Some things are unknowable.

text: Os ventos do destino são imutáveis.
author: Susana Almeida

text: Religion consists much in holy affection
author: but those exercises of affection which are most distinguishing of true religion are these practical exercises. Friendship between earthly friends consists much in affection

text: Faith Never does seem Feasible
author: Jocelyn Andersen

text: Monogamy is faith in the faithless
author: Virginia Alison

text: In a world full of nouns, be a verb.
author: Kristin McKenzie Rice

text: The death of a man is his sins.
author: Evans Biya

text: For God and for the future generations.
author: Sarah Holman

text: Truth never dies.
author: Ngugi wa Thiong’o

text: Life did come with a manual.
author: Joyce Rachelle

text: Alice released a long exhale. "Showtime.
author: Elizabeth Goddard

text: They are faithful, even if we are not.
author: Ashlee Willis

text: Why I love Jesus Christ
author: He saved me from my sins.

text: You cling on to the truth
author: no one in the world can shake your faith and devotion.

text: Faith is not to understand, but to feel!
author: 1 Minuto na Palma da Mao

text: It is one thing to believe in God
author: it is quite another to believe God.

text: Faith is in your Heart
author: Religion is in your Head.

text: Faith Food Fun
author: dELSON

text: Faith is the greatest magic of all.
author: Farah Oomerbhoy

text: Arise and be all that you dreamed
author: Lacey Sturm

text: Failure is falling up!
author: Dr. Sean McMillan

text: Believe in yourself, always.
author: Sujish Kandampully

text: Sometimes all thats left is faith.
author: Renae A. Sauter

text: Be still and know that I am God!
author: Bible

text: Have faith in the light you carry.
author: Seth Adam Smith

text: To love anyone is to risk.
author: Stephanie Morrill

text: Why trade definite for a maybe?
author: Carlos Wallace

text: You made a writer keep faith in his pen.
author: Avijeet Das

text: Belief attracts something
author: faith makes things happen!

text: Faith is the ability to endure.
author: Michael R. French

text: The answer to most prayers is no.
author: Alex George

text: I have a mustard seed
author: and I am not afraid to use it

text: We don’t change to believe
author: we believe to change! (el)

text: Don't tell me the moon is shining
author: show me the glint of light on broken glass.

text: Ideas aren't magical
author: the only tricky part is holding on to one long enough to get it written down.

text: My books are water
author: those of the great geniuses is wine. Everybody drinks water.

text: I don't profess any religion
author: I don't think it’s possible that there is a God

text: Some books are undeservedly forgotten
author: none are undeservedly remembered.

text: You are what you write.
author: Helvy Tiana Rosa

text: I need solitude for my writing
author: not 'like a hermit' - that wouldn't be enough - but like a dead man.

text: Don't get it right - get it WRITTEN!
author: Lee Child

text: The desire to write grows with writing.
author: Erasmus

text: All words are pegs to hang ideas on.
author: Henry Ward Beecher
